title: USD, Hydra, OpenExec and OpenSubdiv: Pixar’s Open Source Meetup
speakers_or_contributors: Jessica Wang; Katie Herrera
keywords_tags: Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Join the discussion with the developers and users of Universal Scene Description (USD), Hydra, OpenExec, and OpenSubdiv. We'll showcase work from the past year and the plans ahead.

title: Workstations: From the Desk to the Datacenter
speakers_or_contributors: Jon Bach
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Image Processing; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: As creative and technical workflows become more remote, the traditional tower workstation is no longer the only answer. This session explores the spectrum from deskside workstations to rackmount systems, VDI, and cloud-based platforms. We will compare common tools and deployment models, discuss the strengths and tradeoffs of each approach, and examine how the AI boom and memory shortages are changing infrastructure decisions. When does local hardware still win? When does centralized or cloud infrastructure make more sense? Join an open discussion on where workflows are headed and where the long-term equilibrium may settle.

title: Maxon Computer Inc | Preparing Students for Creative Careers: Teaching the Tools and Workflows the Industry Uses
speakers_or_contributors: Steve Forde
keywords_tags: Animation; Digital Twins; Education; Games; Industry Insight; Modeling; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Creative talent opens the door, but it is hands-on experience with professional tools and production workflows that prepares students for successful careers.

Join Steve Forde, Chief Product Officer at Maxon, as he explores how educators can better align teaching with the realities of today's creative industries. Drawing on examples from film, broadcast, motion graphics, visual effects, and game development, Steve will discuss the technologies and collaborative workflows used by studios around the world, and why experience with industry-standard tools continues to be an important advantage for graduates entering the workforce.

The session will also introduce Maxon's growing education initiatives designed to help educators bring students closer to professional practice. These include classroom support from Maxon's Solutions Experts through guest lectures, masterclasses, and curriculum guidance, along with a new skills assessment tool that enables students to measure their progress and build confidence as they develop practical production skills.

Rather than focusing on software features alone, this session looks at how educators can bridge the gap between classroom learning and professional production, giving students the experience, confidence, and industry relevance they need to succeed in creative careers.

title: Art Gallery
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery explores computer graphics and interactive techniques as languages and materials to express artistic visions and experiences. The theme “In-Betweens” highlights the interactions that the twelve installations establish with space, time, and generations. Across media and approaches, including screens, projections, installations, sculptures, retro devices, wearables, bio art, data visualization art, robotics, and space art, the Art Gallery brings together creators whose works address large-scale questions and humanitarian concerns; investigate foundational components such as algorithmic methods and computing hardware; expand how we perceive an increasingly interconnected world; and re-examine and speculate about our place within the layered infrastructures of contemporary technology.

title: SIGGRAPH Thesis Fast Forward
speakers_or_contributors: Ruben Wiersma; Ruben Wiersma; Ryusuke Sugimoto; Siqi Wang; Shivangi Aneja; Nicole Feng; Tahmeena Javaid Adeel; Uday Kusupati; Diana Marin; Josh Vekhter
affiliations: Bournemouth University; ByteDance Inc.; ETH Zurich; Institute of Science and Technology Austria (ISTA); New York University; Stanford University; Technical University Munich; Technische Universität Wien (TU Wien); UT Austin
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The SIGGRAPH Thesis Fast Forward is a forum for Ph.D. students in computer graphics to present and broadcast their research in 3 minutes or less. Students from around the world introduce us to a wide variety of topics spanning research over the last five years.

title: The Last 20%: Getting GenAI to a Deliverable Frame
speakers_or_contributors: Mathieu Mazerolle; Jason Schliefer; Troy Tobin; Jeff Kember
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Visual Effects
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The strongest pipelines pair the speed of AI with the control of traditional workflows, keep artists in command, and hold context as they scale across production, including air-gapped and on-prem. Production with AI is not one shot and a handover. It is continuous loops of iteration: matching color and scene-linear workflows, holding continuity across the frames that carry a story, and moving between AI tools and DCCs until the work is right. Artists, pipeline engineers, and tool builders show how they are building these AI-centric pipelines today, then open the floor: what works, what breaks, and what comes next.

title: DualBrep: A Dual-Field Continuous Representation for B-rep Modelling
speakers_or_contributors: Yilin Liu; Pradeep Jayaraman; Chinthala Reddy; Xiang Xu; Hooman Shayani
description: DualBrep presents a novel continuous representation for CAD models, resolving modeling challenges caused by heterogeneous data structures. It uses dual scalar fields: a Signed Distance Function for geometry and an Unsigned Distance Field for topology. Compressing these into a single latent space enables robust reverse engineering and generative modeling.

title: Face Rigging through Curvenet Parametrization
speakers_or_contributors: Jeremie Talbot; WilliamS Sheffler; Fernando de Goes
description: We describe a new rigging technique that generates face parametrizations by leveraging curvenets as a sparse proxy for authoring weights outlining face contours, which are then interpolated across the surface mesh to drive face articulation. We showcase our method using face rigs built for various characters from Disney/Pixar’s Hoppers.

title: Fast and Exact Winding Numbers for Triangle Meshes
speakers_or_contributors: Peiyuan Xie; Christian Hafner; Chris Wojtan
affiliations: Institute of Science and Technology Austria (ISTA)
description: Generalized winding numbers are a useful measure for inside-outside classification. This paper introduces a novel method for computing winding numbers for triangle meshes, which leverages a ray-mesh intersection and an elementary sum over boundaries. Our algorithm gives the exact answer and is more efficient than all existing techniques in practice.

title: Generative 3D & Editing
Holography & Displays
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Display; Fabrication; Hardware; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation; Virtual Reality

title: Invisible Holographic Window: Full-color 3D Image Reconstruction from Transparent Surface-relief Computer-generated Holograms
speakers_or_contributors: Ryo Higashida; Masato Miura; Teruyoshi Nobukawa; Yuta Yamaguchi; Ken-ichi Aoshima; Nobuhiko Funabashi; Masahiro Yamaguchi
affiliations: Institute of Science Tokyo; Japan Broadcasting Corporation (NHK)
description: We demonstrate an invisible holographic window: a transparent surface‑relief computer-generated hologram formed on glass. Using a scattering‑suppressed encoding scheme and spatial‑frequency band‑division multiplexing, we achieve high‑transmittance, crosstalk‑free full‑color 3D image reconstruction, allowing clear viewing of a photorealistic holographic image overlaid in front of real‑world scenes through the glass.

title: Iris3D: 3D Generation via Synchronized Diffusion Distillation
speakers_or_contributors: Yixun Liang; Weiyu Li; Rui Chen; Fei-Peng Tian; Jiarui Liu; Ying-Cong Chen; Ping Tan; Xiao-Xiao Long
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Light Illusions; Nanjing University; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
description: Iris3D is a novel 3D generation system that combines feedforward reconstruction models with a new Synchronized Diffusion Distillation (SDD) method to produce detailed geometry and vivid textures. By addressing view inconsistency and over-smoothing issues in traditional Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) methods, it enables controllable and high-quality 3D content creation.

title: Learning for CAD
Pull the Strings
keywords_tags: Animation; Dynamics; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Modeling; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Simulation
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education

title: The Perpetual Pipeline – 50 Years of Symbiosis in Computer Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Barbara Ford Grant; Kim Davidson; Rob Bredow; Paul Debevec; Natalya Tatarchuk; Doug Roble
affiliations: Activision; BFG Productions; Lucasfilm; Meta; Netflix; SideFX
keywords_tags: Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: "The Perpetual Pipeline" celebrates 50 years of magic born when researchers and filmmakers collided - from character animation to digital oceans to virtual worlds. Now, with AI accelerating the research-to-screen pipeline, two exceptional panels bridged by a VES fireside chat rally to seize what comes next.

title: Winding Numbers & Surface Representations
Autoregressive B-Rep Shape Generation with Parametric Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Dafei Qin; Rui Xu; Zeyu Shen; Kaichun Qiao; Hongyang Lin; Qixuan Zhang; Huaijin Pi; Lan Xu; Jingyi Yu; Wenping Wang; Taku Komura
affiliations: Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; ShanghaiTech University; Texas A&M University; The University of Hong Kong (HKU); University of Hong Kong
description: ParaCAD is a point-cloud-conditioned autoregressive framework for B-Rep generation that directly predicts native parametric surface types and continuous parameters via a surface-centric tokenization, preserving CAD semantics and downstream editability beyond grid/point-based representations.

title: CubePart: An Open-Vocabulary Part-Controllable 3D Generator
speakers_or_contributors: Yiheng Zhu; Kangle Deng; Jean-Philippe Fauconnier; Inaki Navarro Oiza; Daiqing Li; Ava Pun; Yinan Zhang; Peiye Zhuang; Xiaoxia Sun; Maneesh Agrawala; Kiran Bhat; Tinghui Zhou
description: CubePart is an open-vocabulary 3D generative framework offering explicit semantic part-based control over mesh structure. Guided by a text prompt and custom part schema, it synthesizes coherent multi-part meshes, which are ready for direct integration into game engines and animation pipelines without manual post-processing.

title: PAColorHolo: A Perceptually-Aware Color Management Framework for Holographic Displays
speakers_or_contributors: Chun Chen; Minseok Chae; Seung-Woo Nam; Myeong-Ho Choi; Minseong Kim; Eunbi Lee; Yoonchan Jeong; Jae-Hyeung Park
description: We present PAColorHolo, a perceptually-aware color management framework that enables accurate color reproduction in holographic displays. Our approach jointly addresses system-level color distortions through color space transformation, adaptive illumination control, and a color-restoration neural network. Simulations, optical experiments, and a user study demonstrate improved perceptual color fidelity.

title: Spatially Accelerated Winding Numbers for Curved Geometry
speakers_or_contributors: Jacob Spainhour; Brad Whitlock; Kenneth Weiss
description: Existing winding number methods for curved geometry, such as SVG and CAD datasets, scale linearly with the number of NURBS primitives. We propose a sublinear approach that leverages a BVH to agglomerate winding number contributions from distant boundary components while maintaining accurate point containment decisions on in-the-wild datasets.

title: ASWF Machine Learning Working Group
speakers_or_contributors: Tommy Burnette
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:

title: NVIDIA: How AI is Changing Education
speakers_or_contributors: Jacob Liberman
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Education; Industry Insight
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping how students learn, how educators teach, and how institutions deliver knowledge at scale. This panel brings together innovators at the forefront of AI-driven education — from Studyfetch, a leading AI-powered study platform transforming how students engage with course material, to NVIDIA's Deep Learning Institute and Community Educator programs, which are equipping the next generation of AI practitioners worldwide.
Panelists will explore the ways AI is fundamentally changing how we teach and learn. Generative AI and large language models are personalizing the learning experience, accelerating skill development, and democratizing access to high-quality education. Discussion will cover real-world deployment of AI tools in academic and professional training environments, the challenges of integrating these technologies responsibly, and what the future classroom looks like as AI capabilities continue to advance.
Attendees will leave with a practical understanding of how AI is being applied in education today, key considerations for educators and institutions adopting these tools, and a clear perspective on where human instruction and AI assistance intersect most powerfully

title: SIGGRAPH Asia 2026
speakers_or_contributors: Carrie de Souza; Jarah Lachica
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: SIGGRAPH Asia 2026, the 19th ACM SIGGRAPH conference and exhibition on computer graphics and interactive techniques in Asia, will take place from 1 to 4 December 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre KLCC, Malaysia, its first edition in the country. As the sister conference to SIGGRAPH, it travels across Asia, blending innovation with local culture. This session is designed for those new to SIGGRAPH Asia, offering a clear introduction to what the conference is about, how to participate, and what to expect. It also provides an open platform to ask questions, exchange ideas, and connect with the community.

title: Textiles
speakers_or_contributors: Jenny Lin; Michael Haller; Gilbert Bernstein; James McCann; Adriana Schulz
affiliations: Brown University; Carnegie Mellon University; Free University of Bozen-Bolzano; University of Utah; University of Washington
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Textiles are everywhere in graphics, from simulation and rendering to fabrication and robotics. Come to this Birds of a Feather to meet others interested in the unique challenges and opportunities textiles present to the graphics community. We'll talk about current state of the art techniques, what problems still need to be solved, and where research is going in the future. Cool demos are encouraged!

title: A Generative Motion Rig for Artist-Driven Motion Authoring
speakers_or_contributors: Jakob Buhmann; Dhruv Agrawal; Dominik Borer; Luca Vögeli; Robert Sumner; Martin Guay
description: Recent breakthroughs in generative modeling enable scaling 3D motion via sparse handles. We introduce the Generative Motion Rig (GMR), a Blender plugin bridging research and production. Through "generative keyframing," artists rapidly author or edit sequences using noise sampling and pose manipulation, streamlining the transition from traditional animation to AI-driven workflows.

title: B-repLer: Language-guided Editing of CAD Models
speakers_or_contributors: Yilin Liu; Niladri Shekhar Dutt; Changjian Li; Niloy J. Mitra
affiliations: Autodesk Research; University College London (UCL); University of Edinburgh
description: Current language-guided CAD editing struggles with dataset shortages and relies on construction history. We introduce B-repLer, a novel framework enabling text-based CAD edits in a learned latent space without requiring construction history. We also present BrepEDIT-240K, the first large-scale editing dataset, enabling accurate, complex CAD edits from high-level text commands.

title: Pixal3D: Pixel-Aligned 3D Generation from Images
speakers_or_contributors: Dong-Yang Li; Wang Zhao; Yuxin Chen; Wenbo Hu; Meng-Hao Guo; Fang-Lue Zhang; Ying Shan; Shi-Min Hu
affiliations: Tencent; Tsinghua University; Victoria University of Wellington
description: Pixal3D is a pixel-aligned paradigm that solves the fidelity bottleneck in image-to-3D synthesis. By utilizing a back-projection conditioning scheme to explicitly lift 2D features into view-aligned 3D volumes, it eliminates ambiguity, enabling scalable generative reconstruction of highly accurate 3D assets and scenes from single or multi-view images.

title: Robust Containment Queries over Collections of Trimmed NURBS Surfaces via Generalized Winding Numbers
speakers_or_contributors: Jacob Spainhour; Kenneth Weiss
description: We introduce an accurate and precise generalized winding number method for trimmed NURBS patches based on integrating along boundary curves. For points near a patch, we add a correction term determined via line-surface intersections. The method enables robust inside-outside containment queries that respect curved features in complex CAD models.

title: Single-View Holographic Volumetric 3D Printing with Coupled Differentiable Wave-Optical and Photochemical Optimization
speakers_or_contributors: Felix Wechsler; Riccardo Rizzo; Christophe Moser
description: We introduce Single-View Holographic Volumetric Additive Manufacturing (SHVAM), a layer-free 3D printing technique that creates microscale structures in seconds using holographic light patterns. Our coupled optical-chemical optimization framework enables ~10 μm lateral features from a mechanically simple, single-view system with broad manufacturing applications.

title: Flashover: Spatial Storytelling of Wildfire from Memory to Spatial Experience
speakers_or_contributors: Phillip Wilkinson; Robert Ellis Walton; SeungWon Jeong; Taehyun Rhee
affiliations: University of Melbourne; University of Melburne
keywords_tags: Capture/Scanning; Image Processing; Rendering; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: We present Flashover, a spatial storytelling project that reframes wildfire as lived experience: memory, repetition, and loss. We describe how multi-zone installation design and point-based volumetric imagery invite active perception rather than spectacle, translating traumatic memory into embodied encounter and reflection on the climate crisis.

title: Flatten the Complex: Joint B-Rep Generation via Compositional k-Cell Particles
speakers_or_contributors: Junran Lu; Yuanqi Li; Hengji Li; Jie Guo; Yanwen Guo
description: Generating CAD B-Reps is challenging due to complex topology-geometry entanglement. We solve this by reformulating B-Reps into compositional k-cell particles. Our flow matching framework unifies topological entities, enabling the joint generation, precise 3D reconstruction, and local in-painting of high-fidelity CAD models with superior validity.

title: HoloPathTracer: Fast and Accurate Wave Path Tracing for Holography
speakers_or_contributors: Wenbin Zhou; Xiangyu Meng; Jiankai Xing; Xin Liu; Suyeon Choi; Yifan Peng
affiliations: Stanford University; The University of Hong Kong; The University of Hong Kong (HKU)
description: To bring physically accurate 3D cues to holography, HoloPathTracer moves beyond prior computer-generated holography pipelines that separate radiance rendering from wave propagation. By tracing complex wave paths directly through the scene, it enables phase-only holograms with natural defocus, reflections, refractions, and more faithful visual realism.

title: InvSculpt: Inverse Sculpting Modeling via Controlled 3D Generation and a Vector Displacement Field
speakers_or_contributors: Hengyu Meng; Lanjiong Li; Zhijing Shao; Yingda Yin; Lingting Zhu; Zeyu Hu; Xin Wang; Ligang Liu; Zeyu Wang
affiliations: LIGHTSPEED; Tencent LightSpeed Studio; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou); University of Science and Technology of China
description: We propose InvSculpt, which decomposes a mesh into a base shape and reusable details represented by a vector displacement field. It uses text-guided 2D priors and a 3D flow model for mask-free detail removal while preserving identity. The method enables accurate decomposition and high-fidelity detail transfer.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Profiling with Nsight Systems (Second Offering)
speakers_or_contributors: Dan Binyamin; Uri Shomroni
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: This training lab offers graphics developers a look at the agentic future being built into Nsight Systems, with a focus on holistic analysis of CPU and GPU performance. Participants will work through hands-on scenarios where AI agents assist with performance analysis . The session bridges the gap between emerging model capabilities and the practical realities of real-time and offline graphics pipelines. Developers at all levels will gain actionable insight into how agentic AI workflows can be adopted today and what innovations are on the horizon.

title: The Antipodal Method: Fast, Accurate, and Robust 3D Generalized Winding Numbers
speakers_or_contributors: Cedric Martens; Philip Trettner; Mikhail Bessmeltsev
affiliations: Shaped Code GmbH; Université de Montréal
description: We introduce a new formulation and algorithm for fast, precise computation of generalized winding numbers applicable to both meshes and parametric surfaces. Our approach expresses the winding number as the sum of ray-surface intersections and a boundary integral. It achieves significant speedups over prior precise and approximate methods.

title: ADS: Random Sampling of Occupancy Functions using Adaptive Delaunay Scaffolding
speakers_or_contributors: Suzuran Takikawa; Leo Foord-Kelcey; Oliver Oxford; Nicholas Vining; Alla Sheffer
affiliations: NVIDIA; The University of British Columbia; University of British Columbia
description: We present ADS, a method that delivers both pseudo-random surface samples and isosurface connectivity from occupancy functions, unlike prior approaches that typically provide only one. ADS achieves this with orders of magnitude fewer function evaluations at comparable accuracy than prior approaches, by progressively refining a Delaunay scaffold around the surface.

title: BrepForge: Factorized B-rep Synthesis via Wireframe Composition and Boundary-Conditioned Surface Instantiation
speakers_or_contributors: Jing Li; Yihang Fu; Falai Chen
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China
description: BrepForge is a generative framework for CAD B-rep modeling that factorizes synthesis into wireframe construction and boundary-conditioned surface generation. This two-stage design improves topological validity and geometric precision, enabling the creation of complex and realistic CAD models.

title: Complex-Valued Holographic Radiance Fields
speakers_or_contributors: Yicheng Zhan; Dong-Ha Shin; Seung-Hwan Baek; Kaan Akşit
description: Complex-valued holographic radiance fields optimize holographic 3D scenes without relying on intensity-based intermediaries. Leveraging multi-view images, our method uses complex-valued Gaussian primitives encoding amplitude and phase aligned with scene geometry. This eliminates expensive single-view hologram recalculation, accelerating speed by 30×–10,000× while matching state-of-the-art quality, bridging wave optics and 3D scene geometry.

title: Subframe-Aware Transfer of Chain Simulations to Non-Uniform Scaled Rig Controls
speakers_or_contributors: FunShing Sin; Vinod Melapudi; Parag Havaldar
description: We present solutions to two challenges in transferring simulated motion to rig controls for real-time game engines: computing local Euler rotations under non-uniform scaling, and eliminating artificial subframe swinging caused by Euler interpolation. Our methods are fast, direct, robust, and support hierarchical joint chains.

title: VecSet-Edit: Unleashing Pre-trained LRM for Mesh Editing from Single Image
speakers_or_contributors: Teng-Fang Hsiao; Bo-Kai Ruan; Yu-Lun Liu; Hong-Han Shuai
affiliations: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University
description: We propose VecSet-Edit, a novel pipeline leveraging a pre-trained Large Reconstruction Model for high-fidelity 3D mesh editing. Using only 2D image conditions and strategic token manipulation based on our exploration of VecSet features, it enables precise localized edits while preserving original details.

title: Differentiable Voxelization of Surface Representations
speakers_or_contributors: Tobias Djuren; Ugo Finnendahl; Markus Worchel; Hendrik Meyer; Marc Alexa
description: We provide an efficient technique to differentiate voxel values with respect to the positions of the surface that generated the voxel grid. This allows to solve problems, such as intersection detection, that can be easily solved on regular voxel grids for surface representations such as triangle meshes.

title: Img2CADSeq: Image-to-CAD Generation via Sequence-Based Diffusion
speakers_or_contributors: Shiyu Tan; Zixuan Zhao; Hao Gao; Zhiheng Chen; Xiaolong Yin; Enya Shen
affiliations: School of Software and BNRist, Tsinghua University
description: Img2CADSeq is a multi-stage pipeline converting single-view images into parametrically editable 3D CAD models. Using a coarse-to-fine hierarchical codebook and VQ-Diffusion , it bridges 2D visual features with 3D semantics , generating standard STEP files ready for direct commercial software use.

title: MorphSkein: A Shape-Changing Afterimage Display Preserving Pixel Density During Surface-Area Changes Across Troposkein-Based Shapes
speakers_or_contributors: Maxime Daniel; Shariff AM Faleel; Pourang Irani
affiliations: ESTIA Institute of Technology; University of British Columbia
description: Shape-changing displays typically lose pixel density as surface area expands, limiting usability. We introduce MorphSkein, a shape-changing afterimage display that preserves a minimum density (1.44 px/cm in our prototype) across naturally occurring axisymmetric shapes of varying surface area generated by spinning cables (troposkeins).

title: Prox-E: Fine-Grained 3D Shape Editing via Primitive-Based Abstractions
speakers_or_contributors: Etai Sella; Hao Phung; Nitay Amiel; Or Litany; Or Patashnik; Hadar Averbuch-Elor
affiliations: Cornell Tech; Technion - Israel Institute of Technology; Tel Aviv University
description: Prox-E is a training-free framework for fine-grained 3D shape editing that uses a primitive-based geometric proxy to explicitly control structural changes. By editing this proxy with a VLM and guiding a 3D generative model accordingly, it enables localized, identity-preserving edits that outperform prior 2D-driven and training-based 3D editing approaches.

title: CoherentRaster: Efficient 3D Gaussian Splatting for Light Field Displays
speakers_or_contributors: Gyujin Sim; Seungjoo Shin; Hosung Jeon; Gwangsoon Lee; Hyon-Gon Choo; Sunghyun Cho
affiliations: Electronics and Telecommunications Research Institute (ETRI); Pohang University of Science and Technology
description: We propose CoherentRaster, an efficient 3D Gaussian Splatting-based light field rendering framework for glasses-free 3D displays. CoherentRaster introduces Cross-view Coherent Attribute Reuse to eliminate redundant computations across neighboring viewpoints and View-coherent Remapping to restore warp-level memory efficiency degraded by interlaced subpixel layouts, enabling real-time, high-quality synthesis.

title: Inside DAC: Creative Connections Across SIGGRAPH’s Digital Arts Community
speakers_or_contributors: Rebecca Xu; Victoria Szabo; Bonnie Mitchell; Jan Searleman; Gustavo Rincon; Sanglim Han; Wei He; Peijing Mou; Juergen Hagler; Joyce Pei
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH Art Advisory Group; ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community; ACM SIGGRAPH History Committee; Chewing Theory Studio; Clarkson University; Pratt Institute; UCLA Design Media Arts; University of Applied Sciences Upper Austria
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Looking for ways to connect with SIGGRAPH’s creative side? This session offers an open and inviting introduction to the ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community (DAC), a vibrant network that brings together artists, designers, technologists, and researchers working across digital and computational media arts.

Attendees will get a clear overview of how DAC supports creative exchange through inclusive programs designed to encourage experimentation, dialogue, and collaboration across disciplines. The session highlights key DAC initiatives, including SPARKS Lightning Talks, online and hybrid exhibitions, and the student digital art competition, along with opportunities for global engagement through partnerships with organizations such as ISEA and Expanded Animation.

Whether you are a student, a first-time SIGGRAPH attendee, or someone curious about engaging more deeply with SIGGRAPH’s creative communities, this session provides a friendly entry point into DAC’s activities and a chance to discover how you can share your work, connect with others, and participate in an international network of digital arts practitioners.

title: Interactive Rigging of Art-directed and Stylized Plants
speakers_or_contributors: Alexa Mahajan; James Baker; William Sokoloski; Jacob Zimmer; Jason Dengler; Chris Michael; Jay Gambell; Arunachalam Somasundaram
keywords_tags: Animation; Dynamics; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Simulation
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: Plants in recent films at DreamWorks Animation are complex and stylized and may not conform to hierarchical and physical structures. From just input models of plants, our environment rigging toolset can produce rigs procedurally and interactively allowing the artist to control the process and account for the stylization and complexity.

title: Learning for CAD - Interactive Discussion
description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. Outside the room will be a series of poster boards for authors to gather around with the audience. Authors are invited to bring any material related to their paper that could instigate further conversation such as printouts, posters, demos, or other presentation aids. The interactive discussions provide attendees more interaction with the individual authors.

title: ShapeUP: Scalable Image-Conditioned 3D Editing
speakers_or_contributors: Inbar Gat; Dana Cohen Bar; Guy Levy; Elad Richardson; Daniel Cohen-Or
description: ShapeUp introduces a scalable framework for high-fidelity 3D asset editing. By casting editing as an image-conditioned, latent-to-latent translation, it enables precise local and global transformations while maintaining native 3D consistency. Our approach ensures seamless identity preservation without requiring manual spatial masks.

title: Uncertainty-aware geometry processing on Gaussian Process Implicit Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Baptiste Genest; David Coeurjolly
description: We present a geometry processing framework enabling computations directly on probabilistic representations of shapes. In contrast to classical geometry processing pipelines our approach considers uncertainty in the input data and account for the distribution of plausible geometries, enabling a principled handling of noise and ambiguity for downstream geometry processing tasks.

title: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage
speakers_or_contributors: Jimmz Zhang
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage is an interactive, generative art installation that re-imagines Los Angeles as a living, ever-evolving digital twin. Conceived for SIGGRAPH 2026, the project bridges LA’s rich cinematic legacy with the frontier of spatial computing. By seamlessly integrating Kinect motion tracking and AI-generated point clouds, it invites participants to co-author a collaborative digital monument. As users input their personal memories and desires of LA, individual landmarks dynamically merge, overlap, and fold into a singular, massive 3D cyber environment—blurring the boundaries between physical presence, collective memory, and cybernetic space.

title: The Geometry Gap: Ground-Truth 4D Data for World Models and Physical AI
speakers_or_contributors: Tyler Raciti
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Geometry; Physical AI; Robotics; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: World models and robot policies learn physical dynamics mostly from monocular video and simulation — both geometrically underdetermined, so models approximate depth and structure rather than measure it. When those predictions drive a robot, hallucinated geometry becomes a safety problem. This session convenes graphics, vision, robotics, and world-model researchers to discuss the missing substrate: ground-truth 4D capture (calibrated multi-view capture, 3D/4D Gaussian splatting, and multi-sensor fusion) and how to scale it. We'll discuss and debate where real spatiotemporal ground truth comes from, real2sim versus sim2real efficacy, implicit versus explicit, and what's next for world models.

title: Evolution of Anatomy at Framestore
speakers_or_contributors: Christoph Genzwuerker; Alex Rothwell; Philippe Mongeau; Quentin Sanchez
description: Framestore introduces the Anatomy Toolkit (FAT), replacing legacy deformer workflows with an end-to-end simulation platform. FAT automates constraint construction through geometric labeling and enables the convergence of Rigging and Creature FX workflows. This delivers anatomically accurate, high-fidelity muscle and tissue deformations with superior efficiency and simplified artist experience.

title: Designing Interfaces for Aphantasia
speakers_or_contributors: David Shorey
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Aphantasia affects how people use interfaces that assume visual memory, mental imagery, or the ability to “picture” a previous state. This Birds of a Feather session will discuss UI design patterns that make software more accessible for people who do not visualize internally: persistent previews, visible history, explicit labels, side-by-side comparisons, concrete examples, saved states, clearer spatial cues, and reduced reliance on memory-based workflows. The goal is to identify practical design guidelines for creative tools, AI tools, visualization software, and everyday applications.

title: DAC: SPARKS in Review: Digital Art and SE Asia, Performance, Immateriality, Interabilities, Artificial Life, AI in Filmmaking, and Animism
speakers_or_contributors: Bonnie Mitchell; Bonnie Mitchell; Sanglim Han; Clea Waite; Mona Kasra; Douglas Chan; Patrick Lichty; Zi-wei Wei; Ina Conradi; Mark Chavez
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community; ACM SIGGRAPH History Committee; Ardongdé Films; Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Nanyang Technological University; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou); University of Virginia; Winona State University
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Performance; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This session will present the highlights from the fall 2025 and spring 2026 SPARKS (Short Presentations for the Kindred Spirit) sessions. Speakers from seven previous SPARKS session will show examples of their work and discuss the research behind their work. The "Entangled (Im)materialities" session moderated by Clea T. Waite and Bonnie Mitchell in Nov. explored artworks that challenged the structures of perception, knowledge, memory, and materiality. "Embodied Convergences: Humans, Machines, and Performance", moderated by Mona Kasra in Dec. featured artists and researchers who are redefining the possibilities of human–machine collaboration through embodied, computational, and performative practices. "Beyond the Global North: Southeast Asia’s New Wave of Generative and Interactive Innovation" moderated by Patrick Hartono and Bin You in Jan. included artists who are shaping new trajectories for generative and interactive visual culture in the SE Asia. "Interabilities in 21st Century Arts & Technology", moderated by James Hullick and Melentie Pandilovski in Feb. included neurodiverse artists who are reshaping contemporary society. "Alter Nature: Exploring Life through Computational Arts", moderated by Zi-Wei Wu in Mar. included pioneers and young artists working with the concept of artificial life. "Confluence of Generative AI and the Art of Filmmaking", moderated by Sal Ardongdé and Paul Trillo in Apr., explored how generative AI is transforming workflows and creative outcomes in the film industry. "Animism Revisited: Worlding with Digital and More-than-Human Minds", moderated by Michael Just and Gustavo Alfonso Rincon in May, considered how creative practices are reopening and complicating animist ways of sensing, valuing, and coexisting.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: From Perception to Prediction - Developing Physical AI with Cosmos 3
speakers_or_contributors: Asawaree Bhide
keywords_tags: Generative AI; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
In this lab, we'll work hands-on across three core capabilities of NVIDIA Cosmos 3: reasoning, world generation, and action prediction. We'll use Cosmos 3 Nano Reasoner to caption real robot video, generate augmentation prompts, and validate synthetic footage using chain-of-thought reasoning. We'll then use Cosmos 3 and Cosmos-Transfer2.5 to synthesize and transform photorealistic video from those prompts, scaling training data without new recording sessions.

Finally, we'll apply Cosmos 3 in different action modes to translate scene understanding into embodied control commands, completing the perceive → reason → act loop.

title: SideFX | Learning Matters. Certification Proves It.
speakers_or_contributors: John Moncrief; Melissa Broccolo; Nicole Campbell-Abernethy
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Dynamics; Education; Games; Lighting; Modeling; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
As the demand for skilled Houdini artists continues to grow across the visual effects, animation, and gaming industries, education programs face increasing pressure to prepare students with both technical expertise and industry-recognized credentials. This session will explore the value of certification at every level of the learning journey, including School, Instructor, and Artist Certifications, and how these programs help establish educational quality, validate skills, and create clearer pathways from the classroom to industry careers.
Attendees will gain insight into how certification benefits students, instructors, and institutions by providing measurable standards of achievement and helping align training with industry expectations. We will discuss the role certifications play in building confidence, enhancing employability, and supporting educational excellence.
The session will also highlight the extensive resources available to support educators teaching Houdini. These include free instructor licenses, curriculum reviews, a dedicated learning management system, and an instructor community designed to foster collaboration and knowledge sharing. In addition, we will showcase ready-to-use teaching materials such as Curriculum-in-a-Box, curated presentations, and other educational resources created to help instructors deliver engaging, effective learning experiences while reducing course development time.
Whether you are an educator looking to strengthen your Houdini program or an institution seeking ways to better support student success, this session will provide practical insights into leveraging certifications and educational resources to create meaningful learning outcomes and career opportunities.

title: Controlling the Frame: AI VFX, Generative Video, and the Future of Compositing
speakers_or_contributors: Hoon Kim; Conrad Curtis; Sofia Pensado
affiliations: Beeble AI; Grammatik Agency; The VPX Lab

description:
AI is rapidly reshaping VFX workflows, enabling tasks like relighting, background transformation, and look development to happen after capture. But as these systems become more capable, a central question emerges: how much control do artists actually retain?

This BOF explores the rise of controllable AI VFX and hybrid compositing workflows, where traditional techniques intersect with learned systems.

Drawing on emerging production practices, we invite artists, technologists, and researchers to discuss where these approaches succeed, where they break down, and how workflows are evolving across lighting, consistency, and creative intent.

title: Differentiable Physics for Graphics and AI
speakers_or_contributors: Minchen Li; Chenfanfu Jiang; Yin Yang; Tuur Stuyck; Ming Lin; Tuur Stuyck; Denis Zorin; Miles Macklin; Guying Lin; Yunuo Chen
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); NVIDIA; New York University; The University of Hong Kong; University of California Los Angeles; University of Maryland College Park; University of Utah
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Dynamics; Education; Fabrication; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Industry Insight; Modeling; Physical AI; Robotics; Simulation; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description:
Differentiable Physics for Graphics and AI (DPGA) is a half-day SIGGRAPH technical workshop exploring how differentiable physics-based simulation is transforming graphics and related fields including robotics, 3D vision, design, and fabrication. As simulation becomes tightly integrated with optimization, learning, and generative pipelines, differentiability enables new workflows for inverse problems, system identification, and physics-aware design.

The workshop is organized by researchers from Carnegie Mellon University, UCLA, the University of Utah, and Meta Reality Labs, whose work spans physical simulation, graphics systems, robotics, and AI. Together with invited speakers from academia and industry, they bring perspectives from foundational simulation research to large-scale production systems and real-world applications.

The workshop brings together foundational methods, practical systems, and emerging applications in differentiable simulation across graphics, robotics, 3D vision, design, and fabrication. Through keynote talks, lightning presentations, and a moderated panel, it will showcase both what differentiable physics can already enable and the technical barriers that still limit broader adoption, including efficiency, robustness, contact-rich dynamics, and the gap between application-facing representations and simulation-ready assets.

Attendees can expect an engaging forum that connects developers with researchers and practitioners seeking to use simulation as an optimization and learning primitive, not just a forward model. A key merit of the workshop is its ability to bridge communities that do not often meet in one venue, helping participants identify practical opportunities, clarify open problems, and shape future directions for differentiable simulation in graphics and AI.

description: To meet the demands of massive, diverse worlds, Wardrobe decouples character design from technical rigging. By utilizing automated pipelines, it reduces manual setup from days into minutes. This system enables high-quality, scalable character variation, allowing artists to populate entire worlds while reducing technical overhead.

title: Frontiers Workshop: CTRL-G: Controllable Generative Graphics for Games
speakers_or_contributors: Josef Spjut; Arnav Jhala; Budmonde Duinkharjav; Arnav Jhala; Michael Lentine; Anjul Patney
affiliations: Epic Games; NVIDIA; North Carolina State University
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Performance; Physical AI; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The CTRL-G (Controllable Generative Graphics for Games) workshop explores the critical intersection of generative AI and interactive game development. While generative models are transforming content creation, the central challenge for games remains control: steering these models in real time to align with player intent, production pipelines, and artistic requirements.

This three-hour session brings together AI researchers, graphics/VFX professionals, and ethicists to address two core themes: developing controllable world models—such as adaptive, player-responsive environments—and evolving game engine education to incorporate these new hybrid workflows. By bridging technical innovation with production realities and ethical considerations, CTRL-G aims to define a practical agenda for integrating generative AI into modern game engines. Participants will gain actionable insights into state-of-the-art techniques, integration challenges, and the pedagogical shifts necessary to prepare the next generation of developers for an AI-augmented industry.

keywords_tags: Graphics Systems Architecture; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: GFXReconstruct is an open-source framework for capturing, replaying, and analyzing Vulkan applications. This BOF will explore how developers can use GFXReconstruct as the foundation for debugging, profiling, and performance analysis workflows, both from the command line and through integrated tools. Attendees will learn about recent project updates, use cases, and opportunities to contribute feedback and help guide future development.

title: Hybrid Gaussian Wang Tiles for Class-aware Authoring and Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Yunfan Zeng; Li Ma; Pedro V. Sander
affiliations: Eyeline Labs; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
description: We present a multi-class Gaussian Splatting Wang Tiles framework for synthesizing large, detailed scenes from multiple exemplars. Our method enables smooth transitions via hybrid tile mixing, supports real-time rendering with hybrid CPU–GPU sorting, and provides an interactive authoring tool for editing class distributions and scene structure.

title: MV-S2V: Multi-View Subject-Consistent Video Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Ziyang Song; Xinyu Gong; Bangya Liu; Zelin Zhao
affiliations: Georgia Institute of Technology; The Hong Kong Polytechnic University; The University of Texas at Austin; University of Wisconsin-Madison
description: MV-S2V is the first work to explore video generation with multi-view subject consistency. It presents a simple yet effective framework, including a tailored data curation pipeline and a Temporally-Shifted RoPE (TS-RoPE) specially designed for multi-view reference conditioning, achieving superior 3D subject consistency w.r.t. multi-view reference images and high-quality visual outputs.

title: Topologically Consistent Multi-view 3D Head Reconstruction via Coarse-Guided Layered Surface Sampling
speakers_or_contributors: Timo Bolkart; Daoye Wang; Prashanth Chandran
description: From calibrated multi-view images, SHELLS reconstructs 18k-vertex 3D heads in 0.08 seconds. It aggregates DinoV2 features via projective surface-aware feature sampling, allowing a transformer to predict dense semantic meshes 3.5x faster with 88% less GPU memory than state-of-the-art methods. The result is an efficient, globally consistent multi-view reconstruction framework.

title: gCDT: A Highly Parallel GPU Algorithm for Large-Scale Constrained Delaunay Triangulation
speakers_or_contributors: Peng Fan; Min Tang; Ruofeng Tong; Lili He; Peng Du; Hailong Li
affiliations: Shenzhen Poisson Software Co., Ltd.; Zhejiang Sci-Tech University; Zhejiang University
description: We present a highly parallel GPU algorithm for 2D constrained Delaunay triangulation that robustly handles arbitrary valid constraints. It computes triangulations of up to 10 million vertices in 0.1 seconds on an RTX 4090, substantially outperforming prior GPU methods and widely used CPU implementations across diverse benchmark inputs.

title: Emotion Manipulation for Talking-Head Videos via Facial Landmarks
speakers_or_contributors: Kwanggyoon Seo; Rene Jotham Culaway; ByeongUk Lee; Junyong Noh
affiliations: Amazon Robotics; Burt Intelligence; Flawless AI; KAIST, Visual Media Lab
description: Preserving lip synchronization during emotion manipulation in video is a major challenge. This paper proposes a method using a pretrained StyleGAN and a latent-based landmark editing module. It modifies the latent edit direction with facial landmarks, enabling fast, high-quality emotion changes while sustaining original lip sync.

title: Faster 3D Gaussian Splatting Convergence via Structure-Aware Densification
speakers_or_contributors: Linjie Lyu; Ayush Tewari; Jianchun Chen; Thomas Leimkuehler; Christian Theobalt
affiliations: Cambridge University; Max Planck Institute for Informatics
description: We present a structure-aware densification method for 3D Gaussian Splatting that adapts primitives based on local texture frequency. By combining multi-scale analysis and anisotropic splitting, it preserves fine details while avoiding unnecessary density, enabling faster training and higher-quality novel view synthesis.

title: OmniRoam: World Wandering via Long-Horizon Panoramic Video Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Yuheng Liu; Xin Lin; Xinke Li; Baihan Yang; Chen Wang; Kalyan Sunkavalli; Yannick Hold-Geoffroy; Hao Tan; Kai Zhang; Xiaohui Xie; Zifan Shi; Yiwei Hu
affiliations: Adobe Research; City University of Hong Kong; University of California Irvine; University of California San Diego; University of Pennsylvania
description: This work introduces a controllable panoramic video generation framework designed for long-horizon scene wandering. It utilizes a global-to-local preview-and-refine pipeline to synthesize high-fidelity, spatially and temporally coherent panoramic videos from a single input panoramic image or video.

title: Scalable GPU Construction of 3D Voronoi and Power Diagrams
speakers_or_contributors: Bernardo Taveira; Carl Lindström; Maryam Fatemi; Lars Hammarstrand; Fredrik Kahl
description: We present a GPU algorithm for constructing large-scale 3D Voronoi and power diagrams. By combining directional geometric culling with hierarchical best-first traversal, our method efficiently handles tens of millions of points across diverse spatial distributions. Applied to mesh-based neural rendering, it removes a key computational bottleneck, enabling higher-fidelity scene reconstructions.

title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Omar El Khalifi; Thomas Rossi; Oscar Fossey; Thibault Fouque; Ulysse Mizrahi; Philip Torr; Ivan Laptev; Fabio Pizzati; Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
description: ActCam is a zero-shot method providing joint control over camera trajectories and character acting. It integrates into preexisting artistic workflows by leveraging traditional cinematography skills for fine-grained control. Requiring no additional training, the framework avoids costly finetuning and adapts across different backbone architectures.

title: Disney’s “Zootopia 2” Crowds: Bringing the Desert Festival to Life
speakers_or_contributors: Josh Richards; Josh Meister
description:
In Disney’s “Zootopia 2”, thousands of mammals gather at a Desert Festival. The expansive
layout of the set, alongside the need for art-directed performances, required an efficient,
multi-scale crowds approach. Environment-driven systems for large-scale movement,
automated placement of authored group performances, and procedural character animation, all
proved effective at scale.

title: GPU-accelerated Certified Hausdorff Distance Between Triangle Meshes
speakers_or_contributors: Haopeng Fan; Min Tang; Leonardo Sacht; Qiang Zou; Ruofeng Tong; Peng Du
affiliations: State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University; Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina; Zhejiang University
description: We present a GPU-accelerated certified algorithm for the directed Hausdorff distance between triangle meshes. By replacing recursive branch-and-bound with a parallel wavefront pipeline, it delivers millisecond-scale, tolerance-controlled results on models with millions of triangles, enabling interactive geometric queries while closely matching state-of-the-art CPU accuracy on large, challenging benchmark datasets.

title: Gaussian Point Splatting
speakers_or_contributors: Joris Rijsdijk; Christoph Peters; Michael Weinmann; Ricardo Marroquim
description: We propose Gaussian point splatting, a stochastic method for rendering massive 3DGS scenes. By sampling pixel-sized opaque points, splatting them atomically, and applying stochastic transparency, we eliminate the need for sorting. Our approach distributes workload evenly across GPU threads, enabling the real-time display of hundreds of millions of Gaussians efficiently.

title: Learning a Delighting Prior for Facial Appearance Capture in the Wild
speakers_or_contributors: Yuxuan Han; Xin Ming; Tianxiao Li; Zhuofan Shen; Qixuan Zhang; Lan Xu; Feng Xu
description: A high-quality facial appearance capture method from casual smartphone videos with a powerful delighting prior.

title: Bringing Diversity from Diffusion Models to Semantic-Guided Face Asset Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Yunxuan Cai; Sitao Xiang; Zongjian Li; Haiwei Chen; Yajie Zhao
affiliations: University of Southern California Institute for Creative Technologies
description: This paper introduces a framework for generating high-quality, semantically controllable facial assets. By leveraging a diffusion model's diversity, we develop a face asset synthesis pipeline to produce paired albedo and geometry data with semantic labels. These pairs are then used to train a disentangled GAN, enabling precise latent editing of the generated assets while preserving identity.

title: Foundry | Shaping Future Storytellers: Integrating Advanced 3D, AI and VP Workflows
speakers_or_contributors: Juan Salazar
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Education; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: To prepare students for modern workflows, educators must teach the core concepts behind the craft, not just the technical steps to finish a task. Juan Salazar, Foundry’s Creative Director, will review the latest advancements in Nuke’s 3D system, Nuke Stage, and Griptape. Plus, he will discuss how these tools fit into a broader, future-facing vision for AI and virtual production, offering advice on how to train artists who understand how to truly evaluate and direct their tools.

title: HL-OutPaint: Coarse-to-Fine Video Outpainting for High-Resolution Long-Range Videos
speakers_or_contributors: Jeongeun Park; Janghyeok Han; Geonung Kim; Hyun-Seung Lee; Kyuha Choi; Youngseok Han; Sunghyun Cho
affiliations: POSTECH; Pohang University of Science and Technology; Visual Display Business, Samsung Electronics
description: We introduce HL-OutPaint, a coarse-to-fine framework for high-resolution long-range video outpainting. HL-OutPaint incorporates a novel global-local frame swapping mechanism that enables both long-term and short-term temporal consistency. Experimental results show that our approach produces spatially and temporally coherent results across diverse real-world high-resolution long-range videos.

title: PQ-Free HD: Priority-Queue-Free Hausdorff Distance for Triangle Meshes on GPU
speakers_or_contributors: Zhihao Hu; Renjie Chen
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China
description: We present a method for computing error-bounded Hausdorff distance between triangle meshes on the GPU. By eliminating the serial bottleneck in traditional branch-and-bound algorithms, our approach achieves high-throughput performance and enables geometry processing applications that require guaranteed error control.

title: SHARP-GS: Scalable High-fidelity Accelerated Rendering Pipeline for Ultra-high Resolution 3D Gaussian Splatting
speakers_or_contributors: JunRan Ding; WeiHang Liu; YuKe Li; YiZhou Wang; AnTong Li; QiHan Ding; Xin Lou; JingYi Yu
description: This work introduces a high-performance 3D Gaussian Splatting framework that unlocks real-time 8K rendering for immersive VR. By overcoming architectural bottlenecks, it achieves a massive 2.55× speedup and sustains over 250 FPS, delivering high-fidelity, 120 Hz stereoscopic experiences without compromising visual quality.

title: The State of Creative Coding: Processing Foundation Community Meetup
speakers_or_contributors: Xin Xin; Moon Davé,
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Education; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Math Foundations and Theory; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
For over two decades, Processing Java and p5.js have provided a learning environment and a technical substrate for artists, educators, and developers to create generative art and interaction design through code. This session brings the community together to discuss where the Processing ecosystem stands today — and where it's headed.

Processing Foundation will share 25–26 technical milestones and 2D and 3D demonstrations, followed by an overview of the R&D work currently underway to pave the path for Processing 5. We'll leave ample time for participants to share their work and contribute to the projects through feedback, questions, and discussion.

title: Wētā FX Presents: Avatar: Fire and Ash
speakers_or_contributors: Eric Saindon; Dan Barrett; Nicky Muir; Richard Baneham; Peter Litvack; Pavani Rao Boddapati; Nicholas Illingworth
keywords_tags: Animation; Computer Vision; Lighting; Modeling; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
description:
In Avatar: Fire and Ash, we return to Pandora’s environments and biomes, creatures and clans, and immerse audiences in water, and dazzle them with fire.
The Academy Award winning VFX team from Wētā FX and Lightstorm Entertainment will overview the process of creating the film’s 3,100+ shots including how the teams blended extensive native stereo photography with CG environments and fully digital performance-captured characters. While advanced tools are required to work at such scale, everything was handcrafted - every character, tree, leaf, pore, sweat drop and insect – to ensure Pandora is a believable world.
To open the session, Nicky, and Peter, will outline the logistical challenges Fire and Ash presented: managing, scheduling, and supporting a team of four Visual Effects Supervisors, six Sequence VFX Supervisors, two Animation Supervisors, and more than 1,200 crew over the course of roughly three years. sharing learnings of how 1 and 2 helped chart a smoother path.
Eric will discuss how the film is the product of 20 years of close collaboration and technical achievements. Wētā FX and Lightstorm entertainment, have worked on Avatar projects for the last 20 years, establishing a creative shorthand and shared vocabulary that have enabled the highest quality of storytelling and visual spectacle.
Eric will also discuss how Wētā FX’s philosophy for Fire and Ash was the same as on other past projects: anchoring the visuals in real world science, before infusing human artistry and technical innovations to bring the world of Pandra to life.
Richie will review how the virtual cinematography in the film—and how the film was shot natively in stereo 3D—meant that every shot had to be accurate not just on a flat plane, but also in 3D space as if it was a volume. He will review the shot composition process, and how performance capture, motion and animation is all considered.
Pavani will take a deep dive into the creation and destruction of the Windtrader fleet. She will discuss how we built the complex models and dealt with the challenges of scale, movement, rendering and simulations, and creative strategies used to realize the director's vision.
Nic, dives into some of the new effects challenges in the film—more water, bigger models, and of course, how the team tackled the titular element, fire, created in Kora, a new addition to Wētā FX’s global simulation framework Loki, allowing artists to creatively direct repeatable combustion physics while maintaining physical plausibility.
Finally, Dan will discuss the film’s characters and creatures, highlighting how the team of facial animators took great care to preserve each actor’s performance and creative intent, using technology and human artistry to breathe life into the emotional heart of the film. Wētā FX’s team created over 2,500 facial motion shots for 30 speaking characters, including Oona Chaplin’s Varang, using a proprietary facial system that uses a blend of neural networks to solve muscles embedded in the face volume and allows layers of animator adjustments in a physically plausible manner, preserving the artistic performance of the actor.

title: A LoD of Gaussians: Out-of-Core Training and Rendering for Seamless Ultra-Large Scene Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Felix Windisch; Thomas Köhler; Lukas Radl; Mattia D'urso; Michael Steiner; Dieter Schmalstieg; Markus Steinberger
affiliations: Graz University of Technology; University of Stuttgart
description: Gaussian Splatting struggles with large scenes due to memory limits and chunking artifacts. We introduce "A LoD of Gaussians," a new framework enabling ultra-large scene training and rendering on a single consumer GPU. Through out-of-core streaming and Level-of-Detail, it achieves seamless multi-scale reconstruction without relying on scene partitioning.

title: EchoAvatar: Real-time Generative Avatar Animation from Audio Streams
speakers_or_contributors: Bohong Chen; Yumeng Li; Yinglin Xu; Youyi Zheng; Yanlin Weng; Kun Zhou
affiliations: State Key Laboratory of CAD & CG, Zhejiang University; Zhejiang University
description: We introduce a novel framework designed to generate continuous, coherent full-body motion from arbitrary audio streams with low latency. Our approach is a unified streaming architecture capable of synthesizing continuous motion from incremental audio inputs. And it serves as a plug-and-play solution for transforming voice agents into interactive humanoid avatars.

title: Manifold k-NN: Accelerated k-NN Queries for Manifold Point Clouds
speakers_or_contributors: Pengfei Wang; Qinghao Guo; Haisen Zhao; Shiqing Xin; Shuangmin Chen; Changhe Tu; Wenping Wang
affiliations: Qingdao University of Science and Technology; School of Computer Science and Technology Shandong University; Shandong University; Texas A&M University
description: We present Manifold k-NN, a dynamic programming-based framework for accelerated k-nearest neighbor search on manifold point clouds. By recursively partitioning insertion history via Voronoi successor lists, our method achieves 1–10× speedup over kd-trees in volume-to-surface queries, while supporting zero-overhead prefix queries and dynamic point deletion.

title: Secrets of the Animal Kingdom: Crowds in Disney and Pixar’s Hoppers
speakers_or_contributors: Julia Newhide; J.D. Northrup; Jonah Laird; Psy DeLacy
description: This talk presents a hybrid workflow of procedural systems informing physics-driven simulations used to craft the natural crowds in Disney and Pixar’s Hoppers. We will present details of crowds workflows using both SideFx Houdini, and Pixar’s proprietary Presto PCF toolsets to produce hordes of snakes, butterflies, seagulls, and more!

title: UniVidX: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Versatile Video Generation via Diffusion Priors
speakers_or_contributors: Houyuan Chen; Hong Li; Xianghao Kong; Tianrui Zhu; Shaocong Xu; Weiqing Xiao; Yuwei Guo; Chongjie Ye; Lvmin Zhang; Hao Zhao; Anyi Rao
affiliations: Beihang University; Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence; HKUST; Nanjing University; Stanford University; The Chinese University of Hong Kong; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Tsinghua University
description: UniVidX is a unified multimodal video generation framework that repurposes video diffusion priors to flexibly generate and translate across visual modalities. Instantiated as UniVid-Intrinsic and UniVid-Alpha, it supports tasks such as inverse rendering, relighting, matting, and inpainting, while achieving strong temporal consistency and data efficiency with limited training videos.

title: Locality-Aware Automatic Differentiation on the GPU for Mesh-Based Computations
speakers_or_contributors: Ahmed Mahmoud; Rahul Goel; Jonathan Ragan-Kelley; Justin Solomon
affiliations: MIT CSAIL; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Massachusetts Institute of Technology - EECS
description: We present a GPU system for automatic differentiation for functions defined on triangle meshes that exploits locality and sparsity to deliver efficient derivative computation. From simple user-defined energy terms, the system produces gradients, Jacobians, and sparse Hessians, accelerating simulation and optimization workflows and shifting the computational bottleneck away from differentiation.

title: Mobile3DGS³: Accelerate Mobile 3DGS Rendering via Gradient-Aware Super-Sampling and Frame Interpolation
speakers_or_contributors: Fan Gao; YIbo Zhao; Changhao Song; Jiarui Wen; Yuxuan Song; Youcheng Cai; Ligang Liu
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China
description: We propose Mobile3DGS³, the first mobile 3DGS acceleration paradigm solving high-res real-time rendering pain points via gradient-assisted post-rendering. It features TSGC, GASS, GAFI for alternating super-sampling/interpolation, delivering high-fidelity efficiency.

title: See-through: Single-image Layer Decomposition for Anime Characters
speakers_or_contributors: Jian Lin; Chengze Li; Haoyun Qin; Kwun Wang Chan; Yanghua Jin; Hanyuan Liu; Chun Wang, Stephen Choy; Xueting Liu
affiliations: Saint Francis University; Spellbrush; University of Pennsylvania
description: We introduce a framework that automates the transformation of static anime illustrations into manipulatable 2.5D models. Our approach decomposes a single image into fully inpainted, semantically distinct layers with inferred drawing orders — up to 19 layers including hair, face, eyes, clothing, accessories, and more.

speakers_or_contributors: James Kane
keywords_tags: Art; Games; Generative AI; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: ‘The Shadow of the Czar’ is the jazz-infused, Webby-honored noir game by James C. Kane featuring breakthrough use of 3D gaussian splat generation, reconstruction and rendering techniques in the browser. This demo will show how the hit cross-platform web title was developed solo and open sourced in just six weeks.

title: A Scalable UsdSkel Instancer for Crowds
speakers_or_contributors: Cameron Billingham; Patricio Ricaud Cavazos; Abraham Stolk; Nellie Robinson; Moe El-Ali; Harmony M. Li
description: UsdSkelPointInstancer is a new USD schema for crowds that combines packed arrays for agent data with prototypes built from shareable UsdSkel components. The increased speed and reduced memory of the new representation was used successfully to represent large crowds on Walt Disney Animation Studios' "Zootopia 2."

speakers_or_contributors: Dean Johnson
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Education; Generative AI; Hardware; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:

title: Hands-on Course: Powered By Gaussian Splats: From World Models to 3D Interactive Worlds
speakers_or_contributors: Nigel Joe Al Hartman; Alex Coulombe
affiliations: Agile Lens: Immersive Design; SensAI Hackademy
description: From the SensAI Hackademy team behind Splat Analyzer and the World Model end-to-end workflow kits, this hands-on workshop shows the complete pipeline of working with AI-generated world models and reconstructed Gaussian splat scenes, turning them into fully interactive and playable web experiences using 3D object detection, segmentation, and browser-native workflows

title: AtomSlicer: Constant-Thickness Field-Aligned Non-Planar Slicing and Continuous Toolpaths for FFF
speakers_or_contributors: Giovanni Cocco; Vincent Belle; Eric Garner; Sylvain Lefebvre; Xavier Chermain
affiliations: Université de Lorraine, CNRS, Inria, LORIA
description: AtomSlicer is a 3D printing method for fused filament fabrication that generates non-planar layers and continuous toolpaths aligned with user-defined fields. It enables better control of layer orientation, constant thickness, and near-continuous deposition, helping improve print quality, reduce interruptions, and support advanced multi-axis fabrication.

title: Points as Tori: Fast Pointwise Signed Distance for Point Clouds
speakers_or_contributors: Nicole Feng; Ioannis Gkioulekas; Keenan Crane
description: Signed distance computation has been either fast or robust, but not both. We explain why, using a theory that unifies signed distance with reconstruction. Our method directly estimates signed distance from point clouds, providing fast pointwise evaluation at arbitrary spatial resolution, without requiring discretization, global optimization, or explicit reconstruction.

title: Real-Time Rendering
SIGGRAPH Roundtables: Beyond the Talk
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description:
Engage. Reflect. Connect.

For its second year, SIGGRAPH 2026 hosts the Roundtables — a series of intimate, speaker-led discussions that carry the conversation beyond the stage. Each 90-minute roundtable seats up to 10 participants in a focused, informal setting. Every table centers on a specific topic drawn from a SIGGRAPH 2026 Talk and is led by the speaker who presented it — a chance to share experiences, ask the questions there's never time for after a Talk, and explore emerging ideas alongside peers and contributors from across disciplines.

Seating is first-come, first-served, so we encourage you to arrive early to join the conversation that resonates most.

Session Format:
Each roundtable follows a simple, engaging structure:
* Opening reflection (3–5 minutes) * Participant introductions (10 minutes) * Group discussion (30 minutes) * Optional table transition (10 minutes) * Stay or explore another topic * Continued discussion (25–30 minutes) * Closing summary (10 minutes)

Roundtable Themes:
Table 1: Engine Room - Real-time VFX & Virtual Production Pipelines, Unreal Engine + OpenUSD with He Sun and Tzung-da Tsai

Table 2: Truth in the Age of AI: Governance, Provenance, and the Future of Evidence with Nira Bozkurt and Charlie Fink

Table 3: From Threads to Theory: Computational Design, Materials, and Quantum Rendering with William Samosir and João Ferreira

Table 4:Spatial Storytelling & Digital Worlds: Gaussian Splatting, Twins, and Immersive Capture with Rory Mitchell, Aj Chavar and Nonny de la Pena

Table 5: Sketch & Synthesize: Artist-Driven AI for Animation, Sound, and Real-Time Performance with Prabal Gupta and Jakob Buhmann

Table 6: Educating the Next Generation: Computer Graphics, AI, Art, and the Future of Animation Learning with Damian Thorn-Hauswirth and Lei Yang

Table 7: Slop or Craft? Human Artistry, Automation, and the Future of Animation Integrity with Lauren Morrison and Christopher Barischoff

Table 8: Look Sharp — Stylized Compositing, Lighting & Lens Craft with Rebecca Lin and Chris Berry

Table 9: Material World — Rendering Realism: Shading, Materials & Light Scattering with Mitch Prater and Artem Kryzhanovskyi

title: Sponsored Keynote by NVIDIA: Neil Ashton, Ming-Yu Liu, and Edward Liu; Next Era of Graphics — Neural Rendering, World Models, and Simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Neil Ashton; Ming-Yu Liu; Edward Liu
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Join NVIDIA Research and Engineering leaders to experience the latest advances shaping computer graphics and simulation. We will explore breakthrough neural rendering techniques, progress in developing world models, and simulation methods for AI, built by AI.

Through recent research examples and demos, we’ll look at how these technologies are changing the way digital worlds are built, rendered, and used across every domain — creative tools, industrial design, robotics, and autonomous systems.

title: Streaming of rendered content with adaptive frame rate and resolution
speakers_or_contributors: Yaru Liu; Joseph March; Rafał K. Mantiuk
description: To improve the perceived quality of streamed content while reducing rendering costs, we exploit the spatiotemporal limits of the human visual system and adaptively adjust both frame rate and resolution based on scene content and motion. Those are controlled with a neural network, trained on a dataset of game content.

title: TwinPose: Person-Specific Subspaces for Multi-View 3D Pose Estimation
speakers_or_contributors: Wenwu Yang; Tianyi He; Jiwei Ding; Xun Wang; Rong Zhang; Kun Zhou
description: TwinPose is an observation-driven framework for real-time multi-person 3D motion capture from sparse multi-view inputs in complex scenes. By constructing instance-aware "twin poses" that unify 2D pose semantics and multi-view geometric consistency, it enables robust, efficient, and detector-agnostic 3D human pose estimation, even under occlusions and dense interactions.

title: Wētā FX Presents: Avatar: Fire and Ash - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: AMOR: Airborne Motion Reconstruction via Homotopy-Aware Trajectory Optimization
speakers_or_contributors: Chanha Kim; Jungdam Won
description: Existing Human-Mesh-Recovery methods struggle with dynamic airborne motions, often producing unstable and unrealistic results. We present a refinement approach that enforces physical consistency by selecting reliable motion segments, applying homotopy-aware trajectory optimization. Experiments on in-the-wild and challenging videos show improved realism and stability over prior methods.

title: FreeShell: A Context-Free 4D Printing Technique for Fabricating Complex 3D Triangle Mesh Shells
speakers_or_contributors: Chao Yuan; Shengqi Dang; Xuejiao Ma; Nan Cao
description: FreeShell introduces a robust thermal-shrinkage-actuated 4D printing technique for fabricating freeform thin-shell surfaces. By printing triangular tiles connected by shrinkable connectors using a single material, heating triggers the transformation from flat structures into 3D shells. An optimized mesh layout algorithm ensures geometric accuracy and robustness, reducing material and environment dependency.

title: Gabor Fields: Orientation-Selective Level-of-Detail for Volume Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Jorge Condor; Nicolai Hermann; Mehmet Ata Yurtsever; Piotr Didyk
description: We propose using Gabor kernels as volumetric density primitives to enable compact, cheap and continuous level-of-detail to primitive-based physically-based volume rendering with single and multiple scattering. We show state-of-the-art results in regression quality, runtime performance and introduce novel stochastic techniques for trading quality and performance in volume rendering.

title: SuperSDF:Sparse SDF Super-Resolution for Surface Extraction
speakers_or_contributors: Sagar Panwar; Nissim Maruani; Céline Loscos; Mathieu Desbrun; Pierre Alliez
affiliations: INRIA; INRIA / Ecole Polytechnique; L Research
description: SuperSDF is a learning-based method for signed distance field super-resolution that reconstructs high-fidelity meshes from coarse inputs, without mesh supervision or auxiliary surface representations. Using a sparse voxel network near the surface, our approach learns how to directly refine the input SDF, outperforming prior methods in quality, efficiency, and scalability.
