title: FLASHand: Feed-forward reLightable and Animatable Single-view Hand Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Ling-Xiao Zhang; Lin Gao; Wei-Hong He; Yu-Xuan Yang; Yunbing Xing; Yu-Kun Lai; Yiqiang Chen
affiliations: Cardiff University; East China Normal University; Institute of Computing Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences; South China University of Technology
description: We propose FLASHand, the first feed-forward model to reconstruct high-fidelity, relightable, and animatable 3D hand avatars from a single RGB image. By leveraging NIMBLE priors and mesh-based 2D Gaussian Splatting, FLASHand achieves instant, personalized reconstruction with disentangled appearance, supporting real-time animation and relighting.

title: LightOpt: Lights Optimization for Real-time Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Tuo Chen; Luyan Cao; Kui Wu; Shimin Hu
description: LightOpt is a differentiable optimization framework that automatically reduces the number of lights in real-time game scenes while preserving visual appearance. By optimizing and restructuring real-time light sources, it lowers lighting cost, reduces overlap, and improves cross-platform performance for modern game rendering.

title: Mechanical Cloaking of Halftoned Imagery
speakers_or_contributors: Jonàs Martínez; Brisard Sébastien; Kostas Danas; Eric Garner; Sid Kumar; Sylvain Lefebvre
affiliations: Aix Marseille Université; Inria; LMS, CNRS, Ecole Polytechnique; TU Delft; Université de Lorraine, CNRS, Inria, LORIA
description: We explore a new direction in mechanical cloaking: halftoning an image using a porous structure that behaves like a linear, isotropic material and visually matches an image. For an external observer, this creates the surprising effect where the object appears mechanically homogeneous while its porous structure resembles a target image.

title: SAND: Spatially Adaptive Network Depth for Fast Sampling of Neural Implicit Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Chuanxiang Yang; Junhui Hou; Yuan Liu; Siyu Ren; Guangshun Wei; Taku Komura; Yuanfeng Zhou; Wenping Wang
affiliations: City University of Hong Kong; Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Shandong University; Texas A&M University; The University of Hong Kong (HKU)
description: SAND is an efficient neural implicit surface representation framework that accelerates sampling by adapting network depth to spatial importance and local geometric complexity. By combining a volumetric depth map with a tailed MLP for adaptive termination, it significantly improves inference-time query efficiency while preserving high-fidelity reconstruction and enabling neural level-of-detail.

title: AGILE: Hand-object Interaction Reconstruction from Video via Agentic Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Jin-Chuan Shi; Binhong Ye; Tao Liu; Xiaoyang Liu; Yangjinhui Xu; Junzhe He; Zeju Li; Hao Chen; Chunhua Shen
description: AGILE reconstructs 3D hand-object interactions from monocular videos via agentic generation. It produces complete, simulation-ready object meshes and robustly estimates object pose without SfM, enabling accurate and stable results on challenging real-world data.

title: DPF: Differentiable Polyphase Filtering for Large-Kernel Approximation
speakers_or_contributors: Zhe Cao; Zhizhen Wu; Yuchi Huo; Zhonggui Chen; Rui Wang
affiliations: School of Informatics, Xiamen University; State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University
description: We introduce a novel Polyphase Filtering framework offering the fastest approximation for real-time, large-kernel image and video processing. Overcoming fixed-resolution processing constraints, our method efficiently handles complex, spatially-variant kernels. It significantly outperforms existing techniques, delivering unmatched computational speed and superior visual quality for dynamic post-processing and advanced visual effects.

title: Shellular Metamaterial Design via Compact Electric Potential Parametrization
speakers_or_contributors: Tianyi Huang; Chang Liu; Bohan Wang
description: This work introduces a compact, expressive design space for shellular metamaterials, along with a fast GPU-based homogenization pipeline that evaluates elastic properties in near real time. This enables interactive exploration and inverse design, achieving diverse geometries and a broad range of mechanical behavior at low solid volume.

title: The PhaseTree: Multiphase Signed Distance Fields
speakers_or_contributors: Eric Galin; Pierre Hubert-briere; Marie-Paule Cani; Adrien Peytavie; Eric Guérin; Hugo Schott
affiliations: Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique - Laboratoire d'informatique de l'École Polytechnique (LIX); INSA, Lyon; Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1
description: We introduce the PhaseTree, a novel hierarchical construction tree representation for compactly modeling objects composed of multiple phases or materials. An object is defined as a single tree that combines phase-aware primitives and operators, yielding a unified multiphase signed distance representation that naturally supports complex topologies and non-manifold interfaces.

title: Visualizing the Moon for Artemis II
speakers_or_contributors: Ernie Wright; Kel Elkins; Marie Henderson
description:
Artemis II is the first crewed spaceflight to the Moon, in fact the first beyond Earth orbit, since Apollo 17 in 1972. The four astronauts will fly around the far side of the Moon on the sixth day of their mission, and for a six-hour period during the flyby, they will be observing, photographing, and describing the geology of the side of the Moon never seen from Earth, including areas never seen by human eyes in daylight even by Apollo astronauts.

Cinematic scientific visualization using high-end commercial 3D rendering software has played a crucial role in both planning the observations and in training the astronauts. This kind of visualization combines rendering realism with large NASA datasets to create highly accurate representations of the lighting conditions the astronauts are likely to encounter.

Small differences in Sun angle can reveal or hide topographic, albedo, and color features of the greatest interest to lunar scientists. For this reason, the final observation plan won’t be created until after the launch, when the precise trajectory and lunar arrival time are known. A six-hour realtime visualization of the flyby will be rendered from Mission Control on day 2 of the flight. This will be used by the science team to create the observation plan, which will then be uploaded to the crew as they approach the Moon. The visualization will later be played on a large screen in Mission Control, synchronized with the actual flyby.

The production session will exhibit several visualizations of the Artemis II lunar flyby and explain how they were created and how they were used by lunar scientists and the astronauts. The visualizations draw on publicly available NASA datasets and spaceflight software libraries that will be highlighted and explored.

As of this writing, the 10-day Artemis II mission is scheduled to launch in early March. If it is completed successfully before SIGGRAPH, we will compare the flyby visualizations to photographs and video taken during the mission, and we will hear from the astronauts how useful the visualizations were to them during the flight.

The availability of panelists may be contingent on the success of the mission and on government funding and travel approval.

title: DeepMill++: Neural Guidance Meets Rasterization for Efficient Accessibility Analysis
speakers_or_contributors: Fanchao Zhong; Yao Zhang; Guanze Xin; Peng-Shuai Wang; Lin Lu; Changhe Tu; Haisen Zhao
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Fabrication; Geometry; Modeling
description: We introduce DeepMill++, a conservative and highly efficient framework for cutter accessibility analysis on arbitrary triangular meshes. DeepMill++ reformulates accessibility and occlusion detection as a rasterization-based visibility and depth pooling problem. DeepMill++ achieves up to 9.5× speedup over state-of-the-art geometric methods, while maintaining 97.5% conservative accessibility accuracy.

title: Dual Contouring of Signed Distance Data
speakers_or_contributors: Xiana Carrera; Ningna Wang; Christopher Batty; Oded Stein; Silvia Sellán
affiliations: Columbia University; University of Southern California; University of Waterloo
description: We propose an algorithm to reconstruct explicit polygonal meshes from discretely sampled Signed Distance Function (SDF) data, which is especially effective at recovering sharp features. Building on the Dual Contouring of Hermite Data method, we solve a quadratic optimization problem to place the mesh's vertices. Critically, this optimization relies solely on discretely sampled SDF data, without requiring arbitrary access to the function, gradient information, or training on large-scale datasets. Our method sets a new state of the art in surface reconstruction from SDFs at medium and high resolutions, and opens the door for applications in 3D modeling and design.

title: EgoForce: Forearm-Guided Camera-Space 3D Hand Pose from a Monocular Egocentric Camera
speakers_or_contributors: Christen Millerdurai; Shaoxiang Wang; Yaxu Xie; Vladislav Golyanik; Didier Stricker; Alain Pagani
affiliations: DFKI; Max Planck Institute for Informatics
description: EgoForce enables real-time 3D hand pose, shape, and absolute position recovery from a single head-mounted RGB camera. Designed for lightweight smart glasses, it uses forearm cues and camera-aware ray-space lifting to provide precise tracking across diverse camera models and device setups.

title: Generalized Spherical Harmonics Products using Spherical Grids
speakers_or_contributors: Di An; Jiaqi Wu; Bowen Xu; Lingqi Yan; Kun Xu
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence; Tsinghua University
description: We introduce the Generalized Spherical Harmonics Product, a novel method supporting inputs with different SH orders and flexible output truncation. Using Spherical Grids as an intermediate representation, we reduce SH products to simple point-wise multiplications, achieving 3.5–6.5× speedup over state-of-the-art methods while maintaining accuracy for real-time rendering applications.

title: Bring Your Bunny (or Something) 05: Fabrication Meet-up
speakers_or_contributors: Quentin Becker; Anran Qi; Takeo Igarashi; Koya Narumi; Michael Haller
affiliations: Free University of Bozen-Bolzano; Keio University; The University of Tokyo
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This event will provide an opportunity for those working on fabrication to meet others and share their experiences. We encourage them to bring physical objects (fabrication results) to make it a fun event. We plan to invite people working on fabrication widely using our own connections, but also welcome anybody interested in this topic. This will be the 5th “Bring Your Bunny (or Something)” BoF event with previous events taking place at SIGGRAPH in 2023, 2024, and 2025, and SIGGRAPH Asia in 2024.

title: Dual Contouring over Expanded Cubes (DCx) for Zero-Level Set Extraction from Neural Unsigned Distance Functions
speakers_or_contributors: Qingchao Bao; Xuhui Chen; Jingpeng Yin; Fei Hou; Wencheng Wang; Hong Qin; Ying He
affiliations: Dalian University of Technology; Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Nanyang Technological University; Stony Brook University
description: DCx extends the Dual Contouring method which only extracts manifold surfaces from signed distance fields, to enable the surface extraction from unsigned distance fields. This is achieved by looking up a carefully designed table over expanded 2x2x2 cubes, allowing complex topologies to be extracted, particularly for non-manifold structures.

title: OmniHands: Robust Motion Capture of Interactive Hands via A Versatile Transformer
speakers_or_contributors: Dixuan Lin; Yuxiang Zhang; Mengcheng Li; Wei Jing; Qi Yan; Qianying Wang; Yebin Liu; Hongwen Zhang
affiliations: Beijing Normal University; Lenovo Group Ltd; Tsinghua University
description: OmniHands is a universal architecture for hand reconstruction across diverse scenarios, supporting single-hand reconstruction, two-hand interaction reconstruction, temporal inputs, and multi-view inputs. It achieves this within a unified framework by first tokenizing the features of each hand and then fusing the tokens in a task-dependent manner

title: Uniformly Deployable Kirigami on Arbitrary Planar Graphs
speakers_or_contributors: Aviv Segall; Jing Ren; Olga Sorkine-Hornung
description: We present a method for exploring the space of uniformly deployable hinged kirigami structures, as a constrained embedding space of an arbitrary planar graph. The design space can be used for desired deployment properties, such as conformal behavior and fully-closed deployed configuration, and for inverse design.

title: IsoGami: Rigid-Deployable Kirigami Materials From Isohedral Tilings
speakers_or_contributors: Guo Han; Juan Montes Maestre; Numerow Logan; Ronan Hinchet; Stelian Coros; Bernhard Thomaszewski
description: We present a computational method to design rigid-deployable Kirigami sheets based on isohedral tilings. By exploring combined topology and geometry spaces, our approach identifies self-deployable structures with controlled expansion. Our approach generates diverse designs, demonstrated through an interactive browser and validated with 3D-printed prototypes.

title: Subgrid Marching Tetrahedra
speakers_or_contributors: Hossein Baktash; Mark Gillespie; Keenan Crane
description: Subgrid Marching Tetrahedra is an iso-surfacing method that reconstructs high quality surfaces while capturing fine topological features below grid resolution. Using grid edge intersections locations with the implicit function, it produces manifold meshes with the usual benefits from classical marching methods such as easy parallelism and intersection-free property.

title: Countering Racial Bias in Computer Graphics Research: Five Years Later
speakers_or_contributors: Theodore Kim; Silvia Sellan; Oded Stein; Alvin Shi; Daniel Ritchie
affiliations: Brown University; Columbia University; University of Southern California; Yale University
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This BOF is for attendees interested in discussing racial bias and related issues embedded in computer graphics research. It is a follow-on to similarly titled BOFs at SIGGRAPH 2021, 2022, 2024, and 2025. We will celebrate progress over the last five years, discuss setbacks, and brainstorm paths forward. This will be a friendly, collaborative space for mutual, authentic engagement across difference. Attendees can be at any stage, including learning about, taking steps toward, or enacting change in computer graphics research.

title: Electronic Theater (separate ticket required)
description:
Every year the Electronic Theater highlights a selection of the very best in computer animation and this year's edition continues in this tradition.

The prestigious, Oscar® qualifying festival is your ticket to this best, and soon to be buzziest, short film projects. Opening night also includes the presentation of the festival awards!

title: Frontier Talk: Write Once, Read ALWAYS: Reading Computer Graphics through Critical Code Studies
speakers_or_contributors: Mark Marino
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Most programmers don't want people looking too closely at their code, except the parts where they found that clever hack or ingenious workaround. And now with AI some people don't even read the code they generate. But what if we looked closer at code? This talk introduces Critical Code Studies, a field that uses computer source code as an entrypoint for discussing culture, philosophy, and the way we see and represent the world. Focusing on examples from the world of computer graphics, I will explore what your code may say about you and about the world you are modeling and envisioning.

title: Arm Create - The New Arm Developer Experience
speakers_or_contributors: Gabriel Peterson; Li Guangyao; Lukas Medek; Ning Liu; Peter Hodges; Sergio Alapont; Yuwen Wu; Zhou Jian
affiliations: Arm; NetEase Games; Sumo Digital; Tencent Games
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Games; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Industry Insight; Lighting; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Join Arm to build, experiment, and bring ideas to life at Arm Create at SIGGRAPH 2026.

Neural graphics on mobile are rapidly moving from research into real-world development workflows. At Arm Create Dev Day at SIGGRAPH, developers can explore what that means in practice. Bringing together developers, researchers, and ecosystem partners, the event features technical talks, guided demonstrations, and direct access to Arm experts working at the intersection of graphics, AI, and mobile development.

Attendees will experience Neural Dawn, firsthand and learn how to start building with Neural Graphics today: From integrating neural rendering techniques into existing pipelines to preparing ML + GPU workflows for next-generation mobile hardware.

Arm Create Dev Day showcases what developers can build now and how upcoming Arm technologies will expand future possibilities. The conversation continues throughout SIGGRAPH with additional demos, meetings, technical sessions, and stage presentations throughout the week.

title: Beyond Computational Design: Design Intelligence and the Future of Creative Systems
speakers_or_contributors: Joanna Berzowska; Masa Inakage; Joanna Berzowska; Marcelo Coelho; Aisling Kelliher; Nicolas Henchoz; Makai Smith
affiliations: Concordia University; Design Intelligence Lab MIT Department of Architecture; Digiplasty; The Media Studio Inc.; University of Southern California; École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL)
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Computational design reshaped interactive media, product design, and architecture over the past two decades. What comes next?

This SIGGRAPH 365 session puts that question to the ACM SIGGRAPH Design Affinity Group, tool-makers and educators who have shaped computational design practice and its platforms. In a sequence of short provocations, panelists will stake out what supersedes computational design: where AI, generative methods, and open frameworks are taking creative practice, and how future tools can support human agency and collaborative forms of design.

The talks will open a discussion on bridging domains: interaction and UX designers, visual and graphic designers, industrial and product designers, as well as architects, have been part of SIGGRAPH since its inception, but have remained at its edges. The Design Affinity Group exists to move them closer to the center. We want your take on what advocacy for design within SIGGRAPH should look like.

title: Boundary-aware Neural Model Reduction for PDEs
speakers_or_contributors: Li Liao; Pengfei Shen; Yifan Peng
affiliations: The University of Hong Kong; The University of Hong Kong (HKU)
description: Neural eigenanalysis enables reduced-order modeling across shape families but is limited to Neumann boundaries. We extend it to Dirichlet, Robin, and mixed conditions by treating boundary configurations as inputs, forming a unified shape–boundary space for consistent spectral analysis and simulation across varying geometries and boundary settings.

title: FabricTryOn: Taming Image Editing Models for Garment Re-Texturing
speakers_or_contributors: Jun Ma; Qian He; Gaofeng He; Huang Chen; Chen Liu; Xiaogang Jin; Yin Yang; Huamin Wang
affiliations: State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University; Style3D Research; University of Utah; Zhejiang Sci-Tech University; Zhejiang University
description: We study fabric try-on as a garment re-texturing task, replacing fabric materials while preserving garment geometry and illumination. Leveraging modern image editing models and a real-image data curation pipeline, our framework performs fabric removal and application directly in image space, enabling coherent and photorealistic fabric try-on from a single image.

title: Frontiers Workshop: Quantum Machine Learning: Bridging Quantum Computing & Machine Learning
speakers_or_contributors: Wei Zhang; Tianming Liu; Yingfeng Wang; Xiang Li; James Hendler; Thilanka Munasinghe; Jennifer Wei
affiliations: Augusta University; Harvard Medical School; National Aeronautics and Space Administration; Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute; The University of Georgia; University of Tennessee at Chattanooga
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Quantum Machine Learning (QML) is emerging as a transformative field connecting quantum computing with modern machine learning and AI. This workshop explores how quantum-based approaches can support new learning paradigms, hybrid quantum–classical architectures, generative models, and computer graphics pipelines. Emphasizing both theory and practice, the workshop will examine near-term quantum hardware, deployable system design, and rigorous evaluation methods for identifying meaningful quantum advantage. Through three keynote talks, a panel discussion, open dialogue, and roadmap synthesis, the workshop aims to foster interdisciplinary collaboration and help shape the future of scalable, practical, and impactful QML.

title: Graphics4Science 2026: Graphics for Cross-Scale Reliable Scientific Instruments
speakers_or_contributors: Minghao Guo; Mina Konaković Lukovic; Qixing Huang; Yin Yang; Chenfanfu Jiang; Wojciech Matusik; Peter Yichen Chen; Wolfgang Heidrich; Ellen Zhong; Karsten Kreis
affiliations: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST); Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); NVIDIA; Princeton University; The University of British Columbia; The University of Texas at Austin; University of California Los Angeles; University of Utah
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Dynamics; Education; Fabrication; Generative AI; Geometry; Image Processing; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Physical AI; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing
description:
Computer graphics is increasingly central to modern scientific discovery, providing not only visualization but also physically grounded modeling, simulation, inverse problem formulations, and interactive systems for hypothesis generation. Building on the Graphics4Science course at SIGGRAPH 2025, Graphics4Science 2026 adopts a workshop-style format with fewer broadcast talks, deeper technical presentations, and more discussion. The program features four keynotes spanning computational imaging, scientific reconstruction for structural biology, generative modeling for molecular structures, and reduced-order modeling for physical simulation, complemented by two curated lightning-talk sessions highlighting advances from the community.

As graphics-based methods move from retrospective analysis toward guiding experiments and engineering decisions, a shared requirement across these areas is reliability. In this workshop, reliable scientific instruments refers to methods and systems that remain useful under scientific constraints, including limited or noisy measurements, distribution shift across instruments and conditions, and the need to communicate uncertainty.

This notion of reliability includes physical and geometric validity, numerical stability under perturbations, calibrated uncertainty estimates, and clearly stated assumptions and failure modes. It also includes practical concerns such as reproducible pipelines, transparent reporting, and interfaces that allow scientists to interrogate and stress-test results rather than treating models as black boxes.

Against this backdrop, Graphics4Science 2026 aims to move beyond a catalog of applications toward an actionable technical agenda for graphics-enabled science. A unifying theme of the workshop is translation: connecting computational methods to scientific constraints, evaluating progress when ground truth is limited, and packaging techniques into practical tools that domain experts can trust, use, and iterate on.

registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover

This presentation offers a focused update on the state of NVIDIA's graphics developer tools and a look at how agentic AI workflows are shaping their future direction. Covering recent advances in Nsight Graphics, Nsight Systems, and Nsight Aftermath, the session highlights key improvements to debugging, profiling, and crash analysis capabilities alongside an exploration of where AI-driven automation is heading next.

Attendees will come away with a grounded understanding of the current tooling landscape and practical insight into the agentic AI features being built for graphics developers. This presentation is paired with two hands-on labs where participants will directly use and explore AI-assisted workflows.

title: SIGGRAPH 2026 Opening Session & SIGGRAPH 2026 Keynote: Lanny Smoot, Inventions, Innovations, and Imagination: Lanny Smoot’s Prolific Path
speakers_or_contributors: Lanny Smoot
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
One of Disney’s most prolific inventors is taking the SIGGRAPH 2026 stage for an exciting presentation that will have attendees reflecting on the power of research, activating emerging technologies, and the value of cross-industry connections to drive technological advancements in one’s career.

Lanny Smoot will share some of the significant inventions and innovations he’s made during his long career in research and development, starting by working backward through his 27 years at Walt Disney Imagineering R&D and to his earlier 20-year career at the nation’s telecommunications giants: Bell Labs, Bellcore, SAIC, and Telcordia.

Along the way, Lanny will invite us into his lab and will describe the career lessons he’s learned — the importance of both research and new ideas and the necessity for collaboration between people with disparate talents. He will describe how he, and many engineers and scientists, are inspired to come into their fields and how young practitioners can find success.

As a long-time contributor to and friend of SIGGRAPH, Lanny will reflect on some of his professional contributions to the conference, including the Emerging Technologies systems he’s dreamed up: “The Electromagnetic Eye”, “Volumetric Display”, “The Interactive Holographic Zoetrope”, and the “Interactive Ping Pong Ball Zoetrope”.

Come for a story of meaningful advancements that revolutionized entertainment and communication, and be inspired by a unique career path built on imagination and taking technology to the next level.

title: Substance by Adobe | The AI Inflection Point: What It Actually Means for the Next Generation of 3D Artists
speakers_or_contributors: Alwyn Hunt
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Generative AI hasn't taken the 3D artist out of the picture, but it has changed what the job actually looks like.Generative AI hasn't taken the 3D artist out of the picture, but it has changed what the job actually looks like. The technical steps that used to take years to learn can now be handled or accelerated by AI tools that any student can access today.

So what does that mean for how 3D is taught?

This panel brings together four people working at the intersection of education, production, and industry to have an honest conversation about where things stand. A university professor, an independent creator who has built a massive self-taught online audience, a CG production veteran, and an Adobe industry strategist will dig into one core question: if AI is handling more of the technical execution, what should educators actually be focusing on?

The conversation won't be about which software is best. It's about how different approaches to 3D creation, procedural, generative, real-time, represent genuinely different ways of thinking about the craft, and what that means for students trying to build a career.

If you're a student wondering whether your degree is preparing you for the industry you're actually entering, this panel is for you.

Panellists
Matt Dombrowski — Professor of Emerging Media, UCF School of Visual Arts & Design; Creative Director, Limbitless Solutions.

title: Towards Co-design in Computer Graphics: Building Collaborations between Researchers and Artists
speakers_or_contributors: Emilie Yu; Ana Dodik; Claudia Davis; Laleh Mehran; Chris Coleman; Jennifer Jacobs
affiliations: Independent; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); The Ohio State University; University of California Santa Barbara
interest_area: Arts & Design; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
We invite researchers and artists to discuss co-designing computer graphics technologies.
Building collaborations between researchers and artists is essential to designing tools for visual production that use novel techniques to support human creative processes rather than supplant or degrade them. Drawing from the organizers’ experience in designing creative software in academic and industry settings, we will discuss concrete methods to build mutually beneficial collaborations with creative practitioners. We see co-design as a way to simultaneously foster scientific contributions and powerful creative workflows for artists, and more broadly as a way to collectively define meaningful directions for innovation in the field.

title: DreamActor-M2: Universal Character Image Animation via Spatiotemporal In-Context Learning
speakers_or_contributors: Mingshuang Luo; Shuang Liang; Yuxuan Luo; Zhengkun Rong; Tianshu Hu; Ruibing Hou; Hong Chang; Yong Li; Yuan Zhang; Mingyuan Gao
affiliations: ByteDance; Institute of Computing Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences; School of Computer Science and Engineer, Southeast University
description: We present DreamActor-M2, a universal character animation framework that regards motion conditioning as spatiotemporal in-context learning. It leverages video foundation model priors and enables end-to-end pose-free motion transfer from raw videos. Without explicit pose estimation, it achieves strong generalization and high-fidelity animation across complex scenes.

title: Learning Laplacian Eigenspace with Mass-Aware Neural Operators on Point Clouds
speakers_or_contributors: Zherui Yang; Tao Du; Ligang Liu
affiliations: Tsinghua University; University of Science and Technology of China
description: NEO is a neural framework for fast Laplace-Beltrami spectral analysis on 3D point clouds. Rather than computing eigenvectors with expensive iterative solvers, it predicts low-frequency eigenspaces directly from geometry, enabling near-linear scaling, robustness to irregular sampling, and accurate zero-shot transfer to substantially higher-resolution shapes and downstream spectral tasks.

title: Efficient Multiscale Lanczos Eigenpair Extraction
speakers_or_contributors: Theo Braune; Jérémie Dumas; Jean-Marc Thiery
affiliations: Adobe; Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique - Laboratoire d'informatique de l'École Polytechnique (LIX)
description: We extend the implicitly restarted Lanczos method to a multiscale context using arbitrary multigrids (algebraic, geometric) and we demonstrate the gain in performance and robustness on a variety of application scenarios.

title: MACE-Dance: Motion-Appearance Cascaded Experts for Music-Driven Dance Video Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Kaixing Yang; Jiashu Zhu; Xulong Tang; Ziqiao Peng; Xiangyue Zhang; Puwei Wang; Jiahong Wu; Xiangxiang Chu; Hongyan Liu; Jun He
affiliations: Alibaba Group; Malou Tech Inc; Renmin University of China; Tsinghua University; Wuhan University
description: MACE-Dance generates dance videos from music using cascaded motion and appearance experts. It first produces expressive 3D dance motion from music, then animates a reference image into a temporally coherent video. The paper also introduces MA-Data, a large-scale dataset and evaluation protocol for this task.

title: Can AI Judge Its Own Work? Self‑Assessment and Trust in Creative AI Pipelines
speakers_or_contributors: Tyler Varacchi
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Ethics and Society; Games; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Visual Effects
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Generative and agentic AI increasingly grades its own output. VLM and LLM judges decide when a result is good enough, confidence scores gate quality, and autonomous tools self‑terminate. But when can we trust an AI's assessment of its own work? This facilitated discussion gathers researchers and practitioners across graphics, vision, HCI, and production to compare notes on AI self‑assessment and calibration. We will discuss where self‑scores break down, how overconfidence hides in iterative loops, and which external‑validation strategies work (second‑model judges, geometric checks, human review). Bring your own failure cases. Researchers, engineers, and students welcome.

title: Component Modes Synthesis Method with Multiple Partitions for Large-scale Eigenvalue Problem
speakers_or_contributors: Chongyao Zhao; Junzhou Yin; Hujun Bao; Jin Huang
affiliations: Independent Researcher; State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University; Zhejiang University
description: To solve ultra-large-scale modal analysis problems, we extend Component Mode Synthesis (CMS) methods to use multiple partitions to generate more efficient local eigenbasis functions and reduce the computational cost of the low-frequency interface modes. We obtained the lowest 1500 eigenmodes of a model (30M DoFs) in 88 minutes.

title: Composing People Together: Iterative Pose-Image Generation for Multi-Person Interaction Scenes
speakers_or_contributors: Wenxuan Peng; Bharath Hariharan; Hadar Elor
description: We present a method for generating realistic multi-person interaction scenes from text. By modeling human pose and appearance together and composing scenes step by step, our approach produces images that better capture complex interactions, with improved accuracy and greater diversity compared to existing text-to-image models.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Debugging with Nsight Graphics (Second Offering)
speakers_or_contributors: Aurelio Reis
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Modern graphics applications are getting increasingly difficult to debug. Ray Tracing, advanced rendering pipelines with neural rendering, GPU crashes, and performance bottlenecks often require developers to navigate large amounts of data before identifying the true source of a problem. At the same time, AI-powered tools are beginning to transform how developers investigate and understand complex systems.

In this hands-on lab, participants will enter the Hall of Mysteries, an interactive graphics debugging experience built around a series of realistic rendering and performance challenges. Using a preview of the in-development Nsight Graphics MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, participants will study a Vulkan application to investigate visual artifacts, analyze renderer behavior, diagnose GPU crashes, and optimize performance using professional graphics debugging workflows. Rather than following a scripted tutorial or traditional use of the tooling, participants will gather evidence, formulate hypotheses, inspect captures and traces, and verify conclusions through AI-guided investigation. Along the way, they will learn how AI-assisted workflows can accelerate debugging while preserving the reasoning and verification skills required to solve real graphics problems.
By the end, participants will have solved multiple graphics debugging challenges and gained practical experience using AI-assisted workflows to investigate and resolve issues in modern graphics applications.

title: HumanFlow: Controllable Human Image Generation via Flow Matching
speakers_or_contributors: Wenzhuo Fan; Hongsheng Zheng; Jianchi Sun; Fei Fang; Hong Ding; Chunxia Xiao
affiliations: Guangxi University of Finance and Economic; Wuhan Textile University; Wuhan University
description: HumanFlow is a unified flow-matching framework for controllable full-body human image generation. It introduces a Control Encoder, Token-ControlNet, and topology-aware loss for structural consistency, along with the large-scale MiCoGen dataset, achieving improved controllability and fidelity over existing diffusion and flow-based methods.

description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. title: An Artist-Friendly Albedo Approximation for Transmissive Materials
speakers_or_contributors: Artem Kryzhanovskyi
description: Transmissive materials are difficult to parameterize in an artist-friendly way. This talk presents a practical albedo approximation derived from a simplified Kubelka–Munk model. Using thin- and thick-layer reflectance, absorption is recovered in closed form, avoiding iterative fitting and enabling efficient, intuitive control for real-time rendering applications.

title: Beyond the Dot: New Ways to Reduce Motion Sickness in Gaming
speakers_or_contributors: Adrian Ledda; Magalie Rosseeuw; Ewa Trusz
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Motion sickness remains a significant barrier to immersive games, affecting up to one-third of players. We present a player-centered mitigation approach developed within a kinetic first-person game, reframing motion comfort as accessibility and embedding inclusive design directly into gameplay systems, supported by empirical adoption and impact data.

title: Chem2Vid: Translating Chemical Equations into Videos for Chemistry Education via Chemically Constrained Diffusion
speakers_or_contributors: Baotong Qian; Qian Zhang; Xiaoming Chen; Yongquan Hu
affiliations: Beijing Technology and Business University (BTBU); National University of Singapore (NUS)
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Education; Generative AI
description: Chem2Vid introduces a multimodal framework translating chemical equations into dynamic, chemically faithful videos for education. It integrates LLMs and chemical simulators with diffusion models to generate stoichiometry-constrained reaction visualizations and synchronized property curves, creating a low-cost virtual laboratory to enhance chemistry understanding.

title: Production-Driven Data Management for a Service-Based Pipeline
speakers_or_contributors: Katie Vinson; Yaash Jain; Oliver Staeubli; Timothy Hoff
description: Walt Disney Animation Studios Technology developed a new automated data management system that migrates production resources between storage tiers and cleans up unneeded data throughout a film’s lifetime. The system ensures no disruption to end users and maintains data integrity while also aiming to be user-friendly, scalable, and observable.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH: Opportunities and Programs for Educators -– Part 2
speakers_or_contributors: Glenn Goldman; Richard Lewis; Glenn Goldman; Richard Lewis
affiliations: Middle Tennessee State University; New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT)
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This session is the second part of the kickoff for the 2026 SIGGRAPH Educators Program and will primarily be the screening of the annual show reel for the 2026 double-curated Faculty Submitted Student Work (FSSW) exhibit. Designed as a way for educators to share their project ideas across schools and disciplines, FSSW is an online archive of assignment and project briefs as well as a curated video emphasizing the variety of student work and schools submitted. The annual exhibition video is a taste of the content available on the Education Committee website and this event showcases and celebrates to the greater SIGGRAPH community the best examples of student work done for projects and assignments for the 2025-2026 school year. NOTE: Part 2 will begin immediately upon completion of other presentations by the ACM SIGGRAPH Education Committee that begins in Part 1.

title: The Connective Tissue: VFX Supervision Across Art, Camera, and Creatures | VES Women Who Lead Series
speakers_or_contributors: Tara DeMarco
keywords_tags: Animation; Diversity, Equity and Inclusion; Ethics and Society; Visual Effects
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Visual Effects Supervisor Tara DeMarco will host a discussion of VFX Supervision as a discipline of deep collaboration with production design and cinematography. We invite questions about how invisible, craft-driven VFX work serves story without overwhelming it. We can examine together how early cross-departmental integration can bring the most fantastical work to life in a grounded way.

title: Advancing Virtual Production: Technical Innovations and Collaborative Pipelines for Film, TV & Live Events
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Stein; Lon Grohs; Ryan Mayeda; Markus Hoell; Paul Franklin; Callum Macmillan
affiliations: BeloFX; Dimension Studio; Epic Games; NVIDIA; Technology Executive
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Industry Insight; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time
description: Exploring recent advances in virtual production resulting in higher performance, better data interchange, and realtime VP scalability for VFX supervisors and filmmakers. From lumenAOVs to frame generation with nDisplay and fast rendering Gaussian Splats, speakers discuss the integration of Epic and NVIDIA technologies, technical opportunities, pipeline impacts, and collaborative workflows.

title: Curves for Technical Artists
speakers_or_contributors: Thomas Stevenson
description: This course is an introduction to curves for technical artists and aspiring graphics engineers. We will build an understanding of curves used in computer graphics, especially rigging, from simple mathematical notation, using graphical visualisations, code implementation and relating them to their use in DCCs.

title: Game Audio Lasagna: Building Layers to Create New Sonic Worlds
speakers_or_contributors: Kole Hicks; Kole Hicks; Emmanuel Lagumbay
affiliations: Kole Hicks Music; Sword and Sound Audio
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Join a Composer and Sound Designer as they share a glimpse into how they build sonic palettes and craft unique audio experiences for games. Hear examples of how various layers of different sounds can create a fireball spell, which unique combination of instruments create an unsettling atmosphere, or even how to evoke a specific emotion within just a few seconds of audio.

In addition to diving into the details on how these sound effects and musical excerpts were made, we’ll also focus on the high level decisions that would inform the audio’s direction. Analyzing how to determine that which is most important for audio to represent, aligning across multiple teams on creative goals, and other essential considerations for the production of audio for games.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: Nsight Graphics in Action: Develop and Debug Modern Ray-Tracing Applications
speakers_or_contributors: Jacob Skarphol; Jeffrey Kiel; Jonathan Litt
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Dive into the world of high-performance graphics programming with this hands-on lab focused on developing and debugging real-time rendering and ray tracing applications built with Vulkan and Direct3D 12. This lab teaches how to effectively use NVIDIA Nsight Graphics to inspect and debug frames to identify and diagnose common rendering bugs and performance blockers.

By the end of the lab participants will understand the direct connection between the tools and the graphics runtime, and will be prepared to use Nsight Graphics with their own applications. Key tools covered include the Graphics Debugger, Ray Tracing Inspector, Shader Debugger, and GPU Crash Dump Inspector.

title: We're All In This Together: The Making Of Disney and Pixar's Hoppers
speakers_or_contributors: Beth Albright; Ian Megibben; Jon Reisch; Andrea Goh; Christina Garcia Weiland; Laurie Kim
description:
Creating the world of Hoppers required reconciling three potentially incompatible visual goals: the graphic clarity needed for broad comedy, the controlled lighting and staging required for suspense, and the rich, observational beauty of nature photography. Rather than choosing between stylization and realism, the filmmakers built a collaborative, cross-departmental pipeline that allowed physical behavior and artistic design to coexist in every shot.

This production session presents the making of that hybrid visual language and the teamwork that made it possible. Artists and technologists from production design, sets, layout, characters, crowds, FX, simulation, lighting, compositing, and tools worked in tight feedback loops to build a hybrid visual language where physical simulation anchors the world, and artistically directed stylization shapes how we see it.

title: AI-Driven Data Pipelines for Digital Twins and Simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Viv Devadas
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Digital Twins; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Simulation
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: AI is transforming how digital twins and simulations are built, fed, and maintained but connecting intelligent pipelines across fragmented tools and formats remains a hard, unsolved problem. This facilitated discussion brings together practitioners from film, games, architecture, and engineering to share real-world experience building AI-powered data pipelines: automated ingestion, anomaly detection, model-driven transformation, and live feedback loops between simulation and physical systems. What is working in production? Where does AI add genuine value versus hype? Come exchange honest lessons and push the conversation forward on what AI-driven pipeline infrastructure actually looks like at scale.

title: ‘Battlefield 6’: How We Brought The Franchise Vision To Life With Our Destruction System Debut
speakers_or_contributors: Senlei Deng; Bruno Brito; Rickard Antroia
keywords_tags: Art; Games; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Battlefield 6 redefines destruction as a tactical ecosystem rather than visual spectacle. Through ten interrelated destruction elements, systemic and pre-simulated workflows, consistent material damage classes, and shared pipelines, destruction reshapes gameplay spaces, enhances player agency, and delivers immersive, readable, and scalable battlefield experiences that fulfills franchise vision.

title: DAC & SIGGRAPH 2026 Creative Content Overview
speakers_or_contributors: Rebecca Xu; Victoria Szabo; Everardo Reyes; Vernelle A. A. Noel; Marina Antunes; Jesse Barker; Gerry Derksen; Mk Haley; Esen K. Tütüncü; Rebecca Xu
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH Art Advisory Group; ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community; SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery; SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Papers; SIGGRAPH 2026 Computer Animation Festival; SIGGRAPH 2026 Immersive Pavilion; SIGGRAPH 2026 Real-Time Live!; SIGGRAPH 2026 Spatial Storytelling; Unity
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Augmented Reality; Education; Games; Performance; Real-Time; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This session offers a preview of SIGGRAPH 2026’s creative programming. Chairs and Directors from across the conference will share highlights from their programs, including featured projects, upcoming events, and curatorial directions. Attendees will gain an early view of how this year’s conference is expanding the role of digital art, encouraging cross-disciplinary exchange, and supporting experimentation across media, technology, and artistic practice.

The session also introduces the ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community (DAC), a platform for global dialogue among artists, designers, and technologists. Through initiatives such as SPARKS Lightning Talks, curated digital exhibitions, and student competitions, DAC connects artistic practice with computer graphics, interactive media, and emerging technologies. More information is available at dac.siggraph.org.

title: Animation Theater 1: Open Worlds
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: What does it mean to build a world from nothing? This program brings together short films that take the art and philosophy of video game world-building seriously — from sweeping open-world epics to the quiet intimacy of indie landscapes.

title: We're All In This Together: The Making Of Disney and Pixar's Hoppers - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: DevOps in Production
speakers_or_contributors: Marc Salvati
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Production companies are often production-driven rather than engineering-driven. Because of this, they have a particularly strong need to leverage DevOps technologies and automation in order to efficiently deliver and maintain technologies within production pipelines.
This BOF will focus on recent advances in DevOps, from coding standards and workflow automation to the growing impact of AI-based workflows and tools. We are especially interested in how these technologies are being adopted in production studios and companies today.
The goal of this session is to share production-proven knowledge and tips and tricks that help improve the delivery of technologies in production environments.

title: Performance Capture at Scale: Building a Pipeline for Battlefield 6 Cinematics
speakers_or_contributors: Pasha Sol; Pasha Sol
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Film-grade virtual production workflows are increasingly finding their way onto game MoCap stages — but adapting them to the pace, scale, and constraints of AAA production is its own challenge. This talk details how EA Create Capture met that challenge for Battlefield 6: from 1-to-1 physical set construction and moving vehicle builds to real-time visualization that enabled key creative decisions on set and same-day editorial delivery.

title: Between the Frames
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Education; Games; Generative AI; Image Processing; Simulation
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education

title: Breathing Life into Illustrations - Building a look for Stylized Compositing
speakers_or_contributors: Megan Deane; Rebecca J. Lin
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work
description: The team at Sony Pictures Imageworks developed a scalable compositing workflow for GOAT to achieve a hand-painted aesthetic. Deconstructing illustrations into a "recipe" of plug-and-play modules helped balanced stylization with efficiency. New pipelines for asset-based FX curves and 2D-to-3D sketch translation addressed the show's unique look-of-picture needs.
