title: Frontiers Workshop: Graphics In Medicine: From the Dev Floor to the Operating Room 2
speakers_or_contributors: Enoch Omale; Enoch Omale; James Hu; Jacob Alford; Sina Soltanzadeh Zarandi; Brian Lien; Kotaro Tsutsumi; Anthony Le; Kevin Seals
affiliations: Cedars-Sinai Medical Center; Cleveland Clinic Foundation; Cook Children's Medical Center; Illuminant Surgical Inc.; USC; University of California Irvine
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Industry Insight; Real-Time; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
We're back for round 2 of "From the Dev Floor to the Operating Room"! Like last year, we hope to bring to light more of the wonderful applications of computer graphics in medicine. We'll be discussing current challenges with XR in medicine, digital twins, simulation, robotics and more.
We'll also expand on last years workshop activity with a brand new panel of physicians to help us practice designing graphics solutions for medicine that actually make an impact.

This will also be a great place to network with people interested the beautiful intersection of Medicine and Technology

title: In Conversation with Tyree Dillihay
speakers_or_contributors: Garth Burkhard; Tyree Dillihay
description:
Making his feature directorial debut with Sony Pictures Animation, Tyree Dillihay joins us to look back on the making of "GOAT," the story of a small goat with big dreams who gets a once-in-a-lifetime shot to join the pros and play roarball, a high-intensity, co-ed, full-contact sport dominated by the fastest, fiercest animals in the world.

Blending a unique, original visual style with the kinetic energy of modern basketball culture, the film is a love letter to the underdog spirit. In this conversation, Dillihay will discuss bringing his vision to life with an A-list voice cast led by Caleb McLaughlin and Gabrielle Union, working with producer Stephen Curry and other NBA legends including Andre Iguodala to achieve authentic roarball action, and what it took to make his feature debut at one of animation's most celebrated studios.

title: Lines and Minds: Visual Abstraction in Art, Psychology, and Computer Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Yael Vinker; Mia Tang; Kartik Chandra; Maneesh Agrawala; Judith Fan; Aaron Hertzmann; Barbara Tversky; Aaron Hertzmann; Rubaiat Habib; Scott McCloud
affiliations: Adobe Research; Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); N/A; Stanford University
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Education; Generative AI; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Rendering; Scientific Visualization
description:
Visual abstraction --- from sketches and diagrams to icons and comics --- enables people to communicate, express, and create by distilling complex visual information into simplified forms that preserve essential meaning. These simplified forms support perception, reasoning, and communication in ways that detailed depiction often cannot. Visual abstraction has been studied across cognitive science, art theory, and computer graphics, offering complementary perspectives on how humans perceive, produce, and interpret such representations.

This workshop brings together these perspectives to examine abstraction as a cognitive mechanism, a communicative tool, and a computational challenge, exploring questions such as: Why and how do people abstract? What makes a simplification effective? How can machines learn to abstract in ways aligned with human perception? And as AI excels at generating realistic images, what role might abstraction play in how humans and AI create together?

title: NVIDIA Graphics Developer Tools Roadmap: Agentic AI and Beyond (First Offering)
speakers_or_contributors: Aurelio Reis; Jonathan Litt
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
This presentation offers a focused update on the state of NVIDIA's graphics developer tools and a look at how agentic AI workflows are shaping their future direction. Covering recent advances in Nsight Graphics, Nsight Systems, and Nsight Aftermath, the session highlights key improvements to debugging, profiling, and crash analysis capabilities alongside an exploration of where AI-driven automation is heading next.

Attendees will come away with a grounded understanding of the current tooling landscape and practical insight into the agentic AI features being built for graphics developers. This presentation is paired with two hands-on labs where participants will directly use and explore AI-assisted workflows.

title: NVIDIA: Beyond 3D: From Real-World Objects to Digital Assets and Physical AI
speakers_or_contributors: Pallavi Mohan; Tomasz Bednarz; Frank Guan; Zoe Ryan
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Generative AI; Geometry; Physical AI; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation
description: Discover how scalable compute, advanced graphics and generative AI reshape scientific and cultural narratives. Leveraging AI and neural graphics, we guide attendees through the complete object lifecycle - Seeing, Reconstructing, Restoring, and Simulating. Learn to build active, physically grounded digital twins that unlock deep insights across scientific and cultural domains.

title: Roto-Lite: Transformer-based Automatic Rotoscoping with Splines
speakers_or_contributors: Sirak Ghebremusse
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Image Processing
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: Roto-Lite is a pixels-to-sequence transformer model that parses regions of portrait captures and outputs an efficient set of spline shapes. Our aim is to offer minimal friction between the outputs of our model and its adoption by artists, as compared to more traditional raster-based segmentation models.

title: Towards a Real-Time Workflow: An All-encompassing Pipeline for Natural History Filmmaking
speakers_or_contributors: He Sun
keywords_tags: Art; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Virtual Reality
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: This presentation explores an end-to-end virtual production workflow for natural history filmmaking, demonstrated through Surviving Earth (2026). It showcases real-time tools spanning pre-production, on-set In-Camera VFX, and post-production, revealing how hybrid pipelines enhance planning, creativity, and efficient delivery of scientifically accurate visual storytelling.

title: We Never Go Out of Style
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Industry Insight; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering

title: Early Career Development: Kickstart a Career in Computer Graphics – Resume, Reel & Industry Insights
speakers_or_contributors: Angela Holm; Angela Holm; Michael Tanzillo; Philip Luhn
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH – Early Career Development Committee; Adobe; HNTB
keywords_tags: Education; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The ACM SIGGRAPH Early Career Development Committee (ECDC) Resume and Reel Review program has long supported students and emerging professionals through mentorship, portfolio feedback, and year-round career development programming.

In this interactive panel discussion, ECDC brings together industry professionals from fashion, enterprise visualization, infrastructure, product development, and creative technology to discuss the many pathways available within modern 3D careers beyond traditional entertainment pipelines.

Panelists include leaders working in apparel visualization, immersive media, enterprise 3D workflows, AI-assisted production, and digital product creation. Together, they will share practical insights into resumes and demo reels, hiring expectations, portfolio strategy, networking, mentorship, and adapting creative skills across evolving industries and technologies.

Rather than a traditional lecture, this session is designed as an interactive conversation shaped by audience questions and discussion. Attendees will have the opportunity to engage directly with panelists on topics including career transitions, breaking into new industries, building sustainable creative careers, and navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of 3D tools and workflows.

Whether attendees are students, emerging professionals, or experienced artists exploring new directions, this session offers an opportunity to gain real-world insight from artists and technologists actively shaping the future of 3D across multiple industries.

title: Coloring Outside the Pipeline: Stylized Pastel Techniques in Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5
speakers_or_contributors: Nate Skeen; Peter Demarest; Patrick Yu Wang; Ravindra Dwivedi; Dave Quirus; Max Gilbert; Laura Grieve; Alexander Alvarado
description: Creating Toy Story 5's hand-drawn "Playtime" sequences ventured outside Pixar's traditional pipeline via collaborative non-linear workflows spanning effects, shading, and compositing. The team developed pre-lit geometric pastel assets, stylized character treatments, and layered compositing approaches that transformed CG animation into ethereal pastel worlds visualizing childhood imagination.

title: Simulating Superpowers: From Flailing to Flying in a Controller-Free CAVE
speakers_or_contributors: Joey Ziolkowski
description: How do you make someone truly feel like Superman? Discover how we blended markerless motion capture, 4D effects, cooperative game mechanics, and interactive storytelling in a 3D CAVE to transform physical movements into simulated superpowers for 'Superman Experience: Defenders Unite'.

title: Trajectory-Guided Frame Interpolation for Anime-Style Toon Effects
speakers_or_contributors: Julia Chylak
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI
description: Creating anime-style fluid effects relies on time-consuming manual drawing or hard-to-control physical simulations that fail to capture the distinctive anime-like movement. We address this with a machine learning pipeline that, using sparse motion trajectories and just the first and last frames, generates temporally coherent, stylized fluid animations efficiently.

title: Forging the Glacier at IO Interactive: Fireside Chat with Henrik Schlichter (Technical Director)
speakers_or_contributors: Henrik Schlichter; Henrik Schlichter; John Canning
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: As the game industry grapples with rising costs, shifting platforms, and an explosion of available tools, the question of how studios build their games has never been more consequential. IO Interactive Technical Director Henrik Schlichter sits down to discuss, debate, and unpack the realities of modern game development - what it takes to build the custom Glacier engine, innovate at scale, and how to build cheaper, faster and better without sacrificing quality. Equal parts technical deep-dive and industry reality check, this fireside chat sets the stage for bold conversations for Games at SIGGRAPH 2026.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Debugging with Nsight Graphics (First Offering)
speakers_or_contributors: Aurelio Reis
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Modern graphics applications are getting increasingly difficult to debug. Ray Tracing, advanced rendering pipelines with neural rendering, GPU crashes, and performance bottlenecks often require developers to navigate large amounts of data before identifying the true source of a problem. At the same time, AI-powered tools are beginning to transform how developers investigate and understand complex systems.

In this hands-on lab, participants will enter the Hall of Mysteries, an interactive graphics debugging experience built around a series of realistic rendering and performance challenges. Using a preview of the in-development Nsight Graphics MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, participants will study a Vulkan application to investigate visual artifacts, analyze renderer behavior, diagnose GPU crashes, and optimize performance using professional graphics debugging workflows. Rather than following a scripted tutorial or traditional use of the tooling, participants will gather evidence, formulate hypotheses, inspect captures and traces, and verify conclusions through AI-guided investigation. Along the way, they will learn how AI-assisted workflows can accelerate debugging while preserving the reasoning and verification skills required to solve real graphics problems.
By the end, participants will have solved multiple graphics debugging challenges and gained practical experience using AI-assisted workflows to investigate and resolve issues in modern graphics applications.

title: Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender - Hybrid Stylization A Contextual Approach to the Painterly Aesthetic
speakers_or_contributors: Chris Barischoff; Navneet Rao; Quentin Destais; Jerry Huynh; Guillem Ramisa De Soto
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
description: Flying Bark Productions introduces an NPR workflow for Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender (feature) that shifts final stylization to Compositing, integrating directly with background paintings. Using Local Position and Tangent Field Vectors, it generates and smears procedural strokes in 2D screen space, enabling painterly stylization, dynamic lighting, and temporal stability.

title: Behind ReVerie: Sense: Designing Interactive Fulldome Experiences with 3D Generative AI and Robotic Interfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Pinyao Liu
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Performance; Physical AI; Robotics
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: ReVerie: Sense is an interactive and co-creative fulldome installation that explores the use of 3D generative AI and a multisensory robotic sculpture for transmedia storytelling. In this talk, we present the creative R&D process, offering reflections on designing with generative AI for expanded storytelling with an immersive and somatic approach.

title: Workflow-friendly Anime In-betweening Using Video Generation Models
speakers_or_contributors: Akinobu Maejima; Sosui Koga; Kazuki Kumagai; Takuya Funatomi; Tatsuo Yotsukura; Yasuhiro Mukaigawa
affiliations: Kyoto University; Nara Institute of Science and Technology; OLM Digital Inc.
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Image Processing
description: We propose a framework which generates anime in-betweens while satisfying the technical standards of the traditional pipelines. This achieves state-of-the-art results, especially in complex out-of-plane motion scenarios. Our framework is unique and effective by filling the gap between existing video generation models and production-ready requirements.

title: Any Representation, Any Hardware, All Interactions: Accelerating Interactive Prototypes Over Cutting Edge AI & 3D Research
speakers_or_contributors: Maria Shugrina
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Scientific Visualization
description: This talk introduces a new public library, designed for rapid prototyping of interactive Web interfaces over a large class of emerging technologies in 3D representations and AI. We base our design on first-hand experience in developing multiple interactive tools presented at SIGGRAPH as technical papers, Real-Time Live presentations, and Labs.

title: Beyond Eyeballing: The Need for Standardized Assessment in AI-Driven Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Akshay Jindal; Anton Sochenov
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The shift from native graphics rendering to AI-driven neural rendering (NVIDIA-DLSS, AMD-FSR, Intel-XeSS) has fundamentally disrupted how the video game industry measures visual fidelity. Traditional objective metrics no longer suffice, leaving developers, QA testers, and reviewers to rely on subjective "eyeballing". Following the enthusiastic response to our 2025 BoF, we return to continue the conversation. This session will explore the fragmentation of quality assessment in modern game development workflows, the severe economic costs of manual visual QA, and how the interactive entertainment industry can learn from established telecommunications standards (such as ITU and VQEG) to build automated, objective quality metrics.

title: Brushing Up on Illumination: Temporal Coherence in Painterly Compositing in Disney and Pixar’s Hoppers
speakers_or_contributors: Greg Finch; Jon Reisch; Dan Enstrom; Chia-chi Hu
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
description: For Pixar’s "Hoppers", the artistic vision called for a distinctive painterly aesthetic that would give the film a handcrafted, illustrative quality while maintaining our sophisticated lighting and color work. This talk presents the Paintbrush Pipeline, a flexible asset and compositing approach developed to balance artistic control with technical requirements.

title: In Defense of Euler Angles in Game Programming
speakers_or_contributors: Matthew Whiting
description: When teaching 3D rotations, the prevailing rationale is to avoid Euler angles due to gimbal lock. In many game situations, quaternion slerp produces undesirable effects while Euler angle interpolation actually results in the correct behavior. Students should learn to select rotation representations that produce appropriate motion for the application.

title: SIGGRAPH for Beginners - Welcome to the Conference!
speakers_or_contributors: Diana Arellano; Diana Arellano; Mikki Rose; Jim Kilmer; Chris Redmann; June Kim
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH; Conference Advisory Group, SIGGRAPH; Filmakademie Baden-Württemberg GmbH
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Education; Games; Hardware; Scientific Visualization; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
New to SIGGRAPH? Returning after a SIGGRAPH-break? Just curious about what the conference has to offer? If your answer is YES to any of these questions, this session is your friendly introduction to the week ahead.

SIGGRAPH for Beginners will help you make the most of your time at the conference. You'll get practical tips, helpful context, and a chance to ask questions in a relaxed setting.

Topics include:
- How to navigate the conference and its program
- Where to find help and support during the week
- Top Programs you won’t want to miss
- An introduction to ACM SIGGRAPH, the organization behind the event

Bring your questions and curiosity—and stay until the end for our Meet & Greet, featuring drinks and snacks, to kick off the conference in style!

NOTE: The session will take place from 3:30 - 4:30 pm in 404 AB. The Meet & Greet from 4:30 - 5:00 pm in 409 AB.

title: An Introduction to Quantum Computing
speakers_or_contributors: Andrew Glassner
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Games; Generative AI; Image Processing; Math Foundations and Theory; Simulation
description: Quantum computing is a radically new and exciting approach to programming. By exploiting the unusual behavior of quantum objects, this new technology invites us to re-imagine the computer graphics methods we know and love in revolutionary new ways. This course is math-free and requires no technical background.

title: Don't Do it in Post
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Games; Geometry; Hardware; Image Processing; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education

title: Fluid Simulation with the Lattice Boltzmann Method
speakers_or_contributors: Wei Li; Chaoyang Lyu; Mengyun Liu; Yixin Chen; Mathieu Desbrun; Kui Wu; Xiaopei Liu
affiliations: INRIA / Ecole Polytechnique; LightSpeed Studios; Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Lab; Shanghai Jiao Tong University; ShanghaiTech University; University of Toronto
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: Lattice Boltzmann Method (LBM) offers a powerful alternative to traditional Navier-Stokes solvers, using mesoscopic particle distributions on Cartesian lattices to achieve far superior GPU performance and accuracy. This 1.5h course introduces LBM fundamentals and recent advances in multiphase flows, free-surface flows, high–Reynolds-number flows, moving boundaries, and robotics. We also highlight applications of LBM in industrial simulation, game production, and visual effects.

title: How To Build End-To-End Physical AI Systems for Robots
speakers_or_contributors: Akul Santhosh; Edith Llontop
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Hardware; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Robotics; Simulation
description: This course covers building end-to-end physical AI for humanoid and general robots. Covers data generation with NVIDIA Cosmos (Predict, Transfer) and synthetic data; training with Isaac Lab (RL/imitation) and GR00T VLAs; and deployment on the edge with Jetson Thor. Instructors: Edith Llontop, Akul Santhosh.

title: Production-Ready Camera Tracking via LiDAR-Constrained Deep Optical Flow
speakers_or_contributors: Srdjan Milosevic
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Geometry; Image Processing; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: Our system solves difficult VFX matchmoves by fusing LiDAR geometry with Deep Optical Flow. Unlike traditional corner-detection, it leverages dense geometric constraints to handle motion blur and dynamic occlusion. The system features automated actor masking and a novel re-anchoring architecture that eliminates drift, delivering accurate camera solves for production pipelines.

title: The OpenUSD Roundtable: Using an Open Source File Format in Film and Game Dev Pipelines
speakers_or_contributors: Robin-Yann Storm; Robin-Yann Storm; Ezra Goss
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
OpenUSD has long been used to great success in the movie and VFX industries. The game development world is starting to use it too, but is reckoning with workflows that were built for movies rather than games. How have studios reconciled these differences, if at all, and what can we learn from both film and games that helps us all make better 3D workflows?

This is a roundtable in which everyone who wants to can participate to discuss their experiences with OpenUSD in their pipelines, as well as those who want to ask questions about whether they should use OpenUSD in their pipeline.

Questions can be submitted during the roundtable. For example, these may include topics such as:
1. What are issues you have bumped into when implementing USD into your pipeline?
2. What would be required for USD to be more widely adopted?
3. How can studios, and vendors, best share Schemas?

title: On-Set Image-Based Asset Acquisition at LAIKA: The Interface Between Practical and Digital in Hybrid Filmmaking
speakers_or_contributors: Todd Harvey; Benjamin Gilbert
keywords_tags: Animation; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Hardware; Pipeline Tools and Work
description: LAIKA’s VFX look development artists create both digital doubles and CG assets that integrate into the artisan-made world of stop motion film. Our tools for on-set visual data acquisition drive look development, validate the congruity of the asset, and produce shot ready assets that fit LAIKA’s bespoke, handmade vision.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Profiling with Nsight Systems (First Offering)
speakers_or_contributors: Dan Binyamin; Uri Shomroni
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: This training lab offers graphics developers a look at the agentic future being built into Nsight Systems, with a focus on holistic analysis of CPU and GPU performance. Participants will work through hands-on scenarios where AI agents assist with performance analysis . The session bridges the gap between emerging model capabilities and the practical realities of real-time and offline graphics pipelines. Developers at all levels will gain actionable insight into how agentic AI workflows can be adopted today and what innovations are on the horizon.

title: Research to Reality: Scaling Generative AI for The Wizard of Oz at Sphere
speakers_or_contributors: Rudy Grossman; Irfan Essa; Kevin Mullican; Steven Hickson; Ben Grossmann; Wyatt Bartel
affiliations: Google DeepMind; Magnopus; Sphere Entertainment Co.
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Display; Education; Generative AI; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description:
Adapting the 1939 classic The Wizard of Oz for the 16K x 16K immersive canvas of Sphere presented a unique technical paradox: how to translate nearly century-old, 4:3 analog footage into a fully immersive environment. Initiated at a time when viable generative video models did not yet exist, and requiring bespoke solutions beyond the capabilities of emerging commercial tools, this session reveals the collaborative R&D workflow between Sphere Studios, Magnopus, and Google DeepMind.

Panelists will dissect the hybrid production pipeline used to solve the project's most complex challenges: Generative Super Resolution, Character Outpainting, and AI Performance Generation.

First, the panel will address the image fidelity challenge. On Sphere’s media plane, the original film grain appeared as massive visual noise. The team will detail the development of novel upscaling architectures designed to fundamentally modernize the footage. We will demonstrate how these custom models suppressed legacy artifacts and synthesized new high-frequency detail, creating sharpness and texture comparable to modern digital cinema, while maintaining the temporal coherence required for large-format playback.

Second, the session explores the integration of generative elements into a traditional VFX environment. While traditional vendor workflows handled the massive environmental extensions, the team utilized Generative AI to solve the complex character continuity challenges. We will detail the use of Character Outpainting to seamlessly complete characters partially cropped by the original 4:3 frame, alongside the generation of new AI Character Performances for those who walked completely out of view. The team will discuss the strategies used to ensure these generated assets matched the distinct physical language of the original actors.

Finally, the session covers the engineering hurdles of orchestrating this hybrid pipeline. We will examine how the team managed the ingest of disparate high-resolution generative elements to deliver a unified, temporally stable 16K master for the world’s largest high-resolution LED screen.

Since its debut, this pioneering effort has become one of the most commercially successful generative AI deployments in entertainment history, driving record-breaking audiences at Sphere. Validating the success of this hybrid R&D approach, the project earned the prestigious PGA Innovation Award and the VES Award for Outstanding Visual Effects in a Special Venue Project. Attendees will leave with a proven roadmap for navigating the intersection of generative AI research, traditional VFX, and next-generation immersive storytelling.

title: Stage Presence: Design and Testing of a Virtual Rehearsal Room Toolset
speakers_or_contributors: Kevin Laibson; David Gochfeld; Alex Coulombe
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Performance; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: We present Stage Presence, a multi-user XR rehearsal platform that treats theatrical rehearsal as an embodied spatial storytelling practice. Through practice-based evaluation with professional theatre makers, we show how immersive, role-aware tools can support distributed rehearsal, creative iteration, and spatial memory while surfacing new design tensions around agency and learning.

title: SIGGRAPH for Beginners - Meet & Greet
speakers_or_contributors: Diana Arellano; Diana Arellano
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The SIGGRAPH for Beginners - Meet & Greet is a continuation of the session that will help you learn and navigate the conference like a pro.

Featuring delicious snacks, this is the way to kick off the conference in style!

Special thanks to our donors: Das Element (Germany) and Animationsinstitut of Filmakademie Baden-Wuerttemberg (Germany).

title: Modernizing the Glacier Render Architecture for 007 First Light
speakers_or_contributors: Owen Angell
description: The talk covers transitioning to a render dependency graph implementation with automatic memory management, GPU synchronization, and async compute scheduling, along with a rich suite of debug tools — including a real-time frame analysis tool. Case studies include raytracing acceleration structures and terrain virtual texturing.

title: Machine Choreographies: AI, Dance, and the Future of Embodied Performance
speakers_or_contributors: Noshaba Cheema; Llogari Casas; Alexandra Schmucklermann; Ye Pan; Tomasz Bednarz; Philipp Slusallek; Kenny Mitchell
affiliations: DFKI; Independent; Max Planck Institute for Informatics; NVIDIA; Shanghai Jiao Tong University; Spatial; Walt Disney Animation Studios
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Machine Choreographies: AI, Dance, and the Future of Embodied Performance explores how artificial intelligence is reshaping movement, creativity, and the body in motion. This Birds of a Feather session invites artists, technologists, and researchers to exchange ideas, tools, and provocations around co-creation with machines. From live performance capture to GenAI video, generative choreography, responsive environments, and embodied interaction, we will discuss emerging practices and critical questions. How does AI change authorship, presence, and expression in dance? What new aesthetics and ethics emerge? Join this informal gathering to share projects, spark collaborations, and imagine future forms of hybrid human-machine performance.

title: XRHeads, IRL: Connecting Young Creators, Builders, and Researchers to Hack New Realities
speakers_or_contributors: Mehek Gosalia; Ashley Neall
affiliations: Limit Labs LLC; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Haptics; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Are you an 18- to 20-something interested in virtual, augmented, or extended reality? Do you want to build the future of spatial computing with your own hands and headset? Come join us for non-alcoholic drinks and discussion on experiential technology, from open-source to academia to industry. Mocktails on the table, no topic off the table. This event is sponsored by Intercollegiate XR (ICXR) and Reality Hack Inc.

title: Research to Reality: Scaling Generative AI for The Wizard of Oz at Sphere - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: SIGGRAPH Art Party - Joint Event by DAC, Art Paper & Art Gallery
location: Hotel Per La, Autograph Collection, 649 S Olive St
speakers_or_contributors: Rebecca Xu; Vernelle A. A. Noel; Everardo Reyes; Lex Dreitser
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community; ACM SIGGRAPH LA Professional Chapter; SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery; SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Papers
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Hosted by the ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community, the SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery, and Art Papers, in collaboration with the LA local art community, this gathering invites you into a warm, vibrant space to meet, share, and connect. Whether you are an artist, designer, researcher, student, or simply curious, come spend an evening with fellow creatives exchanging ideas, stories, and inspiration. Together, we’ll celebrate the diverse art practices that make SIGGRAPH 2026 so dynamic—welcoming new voices, reconnecting with old friends, and enjoying the energy of a shared creative community.

Don't forget to wear your conference badge!

Event Meeting Location: Hotel Per La, Autograph Collection, 649 S Olive St

The Pathfinders team can offer detailed explanations and descriptions of all SIGGRAPH programs or venues, and help you build a schedule based on your personal skills and interests.

title: Women of SIGGRAPH Conversations (WOSC) Breakfast Mixer
speakers_or_contributors: Kalina Borkiewicz; Mikki Rose; Eveline Falcão; Shanna Alcide; Mikki Rose
affiliations: Conference Advisory Group, SIGGRAPH; DECA Games; Guru Studio; University of Utah
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Join Women of SIGGRAPH Conversations (WOSC) for breakfast and an interactive, forward-looking session focused on capability building across creative and technical careers. Through dynamic ice breakers and collaborative discussion, we’ll explore how people develop range, build and signal competencies, and navigate growth through upskilling, lateral moves, and strategic pivots. We’ll talk honestly about generalist versus T-shaped paths, personal branding, and how tools like AI are reshaping how we learn, work, and expand what we can do. Bring your own questions, experiences, and ambitions, and leave with practical frameworks, concrete ideas, and a clearer sense of how to intentionally build capability for the next phase of your career.

title: AI for Creative Visual Content Generation Editing and Understanding (CVEU 2026)
speakers_or_contributors: Zheng Wei; Yuying Tang; Mia Tang; Anyi Rao
affiliations: Stanford University; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
description: This course introduces a multi-agent framework fusing Generative AI’s creativity with Computer Graphics’ precision. Addressing GenAI’s limitations in data accuracy, we demonstrate how agents orchestrate deterministic rendering (e.g., for charts or spatial logic) to guide video synthesis. Attendees will learn to build systems for controllable, precise cinematic and data storytelling.

title: An Introduction to Neural Shading
speakers_or_contributors: Benedikt Bitterli; Chris Cummings; Kai Zhang; Shannon Woods
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Math Foundations and Theory; Rendering
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Research & Education
description: Learn neural shading from the ground up. This hands-on course teaches attendees to build and train MLPs directly in Slang shaders, leverage GPU tensor cores for hardware acceleration, and implement neural materials — transforming complex visual phenomena into compact, high-fidelity learned representations.

title: Asiagraphics Workshop on Neural Graphics Processing
speakers_or_contributors: Young J. Kim; Hongbo Fu; Takeo Igarashi; Ligang Liu; Stefanie Zollmann; Lin Gao; Hao (Richard) Zhang; Qixing Huang; Ravi Ramamoorthi; Lingqi Yan
affiliations: Aarhus University; Brown University; Ewha Womans University; HKUST; Institute of Computing Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence; Simon Fraser University; The University of Texas at Austin; The University of Tokyo; University of California San Diego; University of Maryland; University of Pennsylvania; University of Science and Technology of China
description:
Neural graphics is transforming how we create, represent, and interact with visual content. This workshop brings together leading researchers across geometry, rendering, generative modeling, and embodied AI to present a unified view of this rapidly evolving field.Through a curated sequence of invited talks, the program will guide attendees from geometric foundations and 3D generation to neural rendering, real-time techniques, physics-aware reconstruction, and interaction with the physical world.

The primary objective is to explore how neural representations and generative models can be integrated with classical graphics pipelines to enable more scalable, controllable, and physically grounded visual computing. The workshop will feature the technical presentations, encouraging active engagement between speakers and attendees. Participants can expect lively exchanges, opportunities to ask questions, and insights into emerging research directions.

Designed for researchers, practitioners, and students in graphics, vision, and AI, this workshop offers a concise yet comprehensive overview of neural graphics and its future trajectory.

title: Fast, High-Volume Asset Imports and Exports Using Unreal Engine and OpenUSD
speakers_or_contributors: Tzung-da Tsai; Darian Wang; Manuel Agost Garcia; Jason Baldwin
keywords_tags: Animation; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time
description: We present a scalable workflow for importing and managing film production environment assets. By using Unreal Engine and OpenUSD, this workflow reduces import times, load times, and data footprints while improving framerate. This avoids traditional DCC geometry overhead and allows teams to rapidly import polygon-dense assets with increased interactive performance.

title: Frontier Workshops: Digital Twins for Science and Industry
speakers_or_contributors: Barton Fiske; Tomasz Bednarz; Elizabeth Baron; Andrea Townsend-Nicholson; Dan Isaacs; Jeff Adie; Nicholas Polys; Scott Greenwood
affiliations: Digital Twin Consortium; Immersionary Enterprises, LLC; NVIDIA; Oak Ridge National Laboratory; University College London (UCL); Virginia Tech University
keywords_tags: Digital Twins; Physical AI; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This workshop addresses the transformative role of digital twins across science and industry. Following the National Academies' 2024 standardized definition, we seek to foster a community of practice embracing this common understanding across diverse domains. We open with a 2026 state-of-the-art survey and Digital Twin Consortium perspectives on evolving standards, then examine societal impacts in healthcare, fusion energy, climate science, and sovereign AI. Technical sessions address rendering fidelity in virtual replicas, AI integration for prediction and optimization, and frameworks combining machine learning with physics-based simulation. Case studies span drug discovery, computational biomedicine, sustainable energy, entertainment production, and scientific visualization.

title: Optimal Transport for Fluid Simulation: New and Old
speakers_or_contributors: Bruno Lévy; Cyprien Plateau--Holleville
affiliations: Inria Saclay and Laboratoire de Mathématiques d'Orsay Université Paris Saclay; Institut national de recherche en informatique et en automatique (INRIA)
keywords_tags: Geometry; Math Foundations and Theory; Simulation
description: This course on Fluid Simulation focuses on Optimal Transport, a mathematical toolbox for creating a 3D mesh representation that follows the movement of the fluid while accurately ensuring volume conservation, fluid-boundary interactions and free-surface evolution. For a hands-on experience, it includes many bring-back-home programming examples, from simple to elaborate.

title: Playable Music at Runtime: Building a Stateful Audio System for Astral Planes
speakers_or_contributors: Clay Ewing
description: Astral Planes replaced loop-based playback with a Unity-native runtime music system where gameplay reshapes composition directly. This production case study explains tracks, bins, notes, shared timing, persistent loops, and how audio architecture changes when composition becomes part of interaction.

speakers_or_contributors: Mitch J. Prater

description: Stop-motion cameras and lenses are an order of magnitude closer to their real-world subjects and the real-world materials they're made from than is typical in live-action scale cinematography. This talk details the unique requirements of rendering realistic imagery at this scale so that it is indistinguishable from their real counterparts.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH: Opportunities and Programs for Educators - Part 1
speakers_or_contributors: Glenn Goldman; Richard Lewis; Glenn Goldman; Richard Lewis
affiliations: Middle Tennessee State University; New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT)
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The kickoff of the 2026 SIGGRAPH Educators Program will include an overview of activities and opportunities with, and sponsored by, the ACM SIGGRAPH Education Committee. Additionally, the winning entries from the SpaceTime competition will be shown along with a screening of the show reel for the 2026 double-curated Faculty Submitted Student Work (FSSW) exhibit. Designed as a way for educators to share their project ideas across schools and disciplines, FSSW is an online archive of assignment and project briefs as well as a curated video emphasizing the variety of student work and schools submitted. The annual exhibition video is a taste of the content available on the Education Committee website and this event showcases and celebrates to the greater SIGGRAPH community the best examples of student work done for projects and assignments for the 2025-2026 school year. Part 2 of this event will begin immediately upon the conclusion of presentations in Part 1.

Taiwan Night (a.k.a. the Taipei ACM SIGGRAPH Chapter Reunion) is an informal gathering for researchers, artists, designers, students, and professionals interested in Taiwan's computer graphics, XR, AI, digital art, and creative technology communities. Supported by Taipei ACM SIGGRAPH, it's a chance to connect with Taiwanese colleagues, exchange ideas, and learn about developments from academia, industry, and startups. All are welcome.

Join us at Prank Bar (1100 S Hope St, Los Angeles, CA 90015), starting at 21:15 (PT) for RSVP'd guests and open to everyone after 23:00 (PT).

title: Assets in Motion (AIM) - Injecting life into a static and linear asset building process
speakers_or_contributors: Hans Heymans; Chris Logan; Brian Cohen
affiliations: Sony Pictures Imageworks; Sony Pictures Imageworks, Canada
description: This talk introduces Assets in Motions (AIM) at Sony Pictures Imageworks. AIM is a strategic approach, designed to move character designs into well rounded production ready assets in a more cost effective and collaborative way.

title: LatentScore: Sketching Soundscapes with LLM-Distilled Retrieval for Procedural Synthesis
speakers_or_contributors: Prabal Gupta
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Games; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: LatentScore turns typed scene descriptions into procedural soundscapes you can steer — running locally on CPU. An open-source Python SDK maps language to structured synth parameters in under a second. See a live audiovisual demo where prompts become sound, palettes, and particles.

title: Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender - Strike Force A Hybrid In-House Production Model for a Modern 2D/3D Animated Feature Pipeline
speakers_or_contributors: Jerry Huynh; Patrick Kueking; Samantha Steyns; Karyn Buczek Monschein; Vijoy Gaddipati
description: Paramount Animation formed a multidisciplinary “Strike Force” during Avatar Aang: The Last Airbender (feature) to support a distributed, vendor‑based pipeline. The team paralleled work across global studios while building an in‑house pipeline for finaling, FX, layout, and compositing. This document details the group’s purpose, workflows, tools, and outcomes under tight constraints.

title: Eclipsed Bokeh: A Conceptual Model for Defocusing Through Defocused Edges
speakers_or_contributors: Christopher Berry
keywords_tags: Art; Computer Vision; Image Processing; Rendering
description: A defocused object viewed through the edge of another defocused object exhibits strange warping and reshaping effects, known as “eclipsed bokeh”. By studying what happens to the bokeh, we can see how to recreate the effect from 2D images.

title: Knit Everything: Surfaces, Systems, and the Future of Textiles
speakers_or_contributors: William Samosir
keywords_tags: Art; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Fabrication; Geometry; Modeling; Rendering
description: What if anyone who can draw could knit? This talk follows VARIANT3D’s journey building software, languages, and novel production systems that make 3D knitting a more democratic medium—enabling designers to create and manufacture curved, complex surfaces with unprecedented accessibility and control.

title: Art Gallery
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery explores computer graphics and interactive techniques as languages and materials to express artistic visions and experiences. The theme “In-Betweens” highlights the interactions that the twelve installations establish with space, time, and generations. Across media and approaches, including screens, projections, installations, sculptures, retro devices, wearables, bio art, data visualization art, robotics, and space art, the Art Gallery brings together creators whose works address large-scale questions and humanitarian concerns; investigate foundational components such as algorithmic methods and computing hardware; expand how we perceive an increasingly interconnected world; and re-examine and speculate about our place within the layered infrastructures of contemporary technology.

title: Crafting Feathers for the Bird Kingdom in Hoppers
speakers_or_contributors: Kiki Poh; Kris Campbell; Alan Browning; Michael Jefferies
keywords_tags: Animation; Dynamics; Geometry; Industry Insight; Modeling; Simulation
description: We present the feather system for Disney/Pixar's "Hoppers," covering multiple bird species. We detail customizing Houdini's hair grooming pipeline, integrating groomed feathers into Presto character rigs, developing a flexible shading framework supporting patterns and two-sided coloration, and creating procedural and simulated flutter effects for natural animation and wind response.

title: NVIDIA | The Making of Kök Börü: Where Untold Stories Find New Pipelines
speakers_or_contributors: Amina Ömirjan
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Kök Börü is a hybrid hand-drawn and AI-integrated animated short that brings a rare Central Asian story to life, one that traditional studio greenlight processes often overlook. In this panel, the creative team pulls back the curtain on their artist-first pipeline, commenting on how to meaningfully integrate AI tools to augment rather than replace traditional craftsmanship. They will share unfiltered budget, team, and timeline metrics, offering a transparent, honest account of what worked, what broke, and how they built an unconventional production model from the ground up.

title: No-Code Virtual Reality & Immersive Learning
speakers_or_contributors: Keith Hibbert
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Education; Games; Simulation; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: How are educators creating immersive learning experiences without coding? This Birds of a Feather session brings together educators, researchers, learning designers, and XR practitioners to share ideas, tools, and workflows for building virtual reality experiences, immersive 360° tours, and digital twins. Participants will discuss successful projects, challenges, and emerging opportunities while exploring platforms that make immersive content creation more accessible than ever. Featuring examples from RMIT University, Australia, this interactive meetup encourages knowledge sharing, networking, and collaboration. Join the conversation, showcase your work, discover new approaches, and help shape the future of immersive education.

title: Remote Production in 2026: What actually works?
speakers_or_contributors: Jean-Christophe Rodier; Marc Bourhis; Alexandre Henneuse; Stanislas Mako
keywords_tags: Industry Insight; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Remote production is no longer experimental. Across animation, VFX and real-time studios, distributed workflows are now central to production operations. Yet key questions remain: which remote and hybrid models are sustainable, and how can studios balance security, infrastructure costs, operational complexity and access to global talent?
This Birds of a Feather session brings together technology leaders from animation studios and the production technology ecosystem to share lessons learned from large-scale deployments. Topics include workstation access, multi-site production, cloud and hybrid infrastructure, pipeline operations, security and evolving remote technologies. Attendees will compare experiences and identify effective industry practices.

title: Dog Walk: Narrating Human-AI Alignment through Companion Robots
speakers_or_contributors: Robert Twomey; Jesse Fleming
affiliations: The Awareness Lab; University of California, San Diego
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Performance; Physical AI; Real-Time; Robotics; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: BFF: Dog Walk explores human-AI intimacy through co-parenting two robot dogs. With video and live performance, the artist-researchers challenge the mind-body divide, documenting the evolution of their dogs' LLMs and their life in the intimate everyday. Blending human and machine perspectives, this project seeks a hybrid language exploring the boundaries of authenticity, embodiment, and synthetic companionship.

title: Hands-on Course: NVIDIA: From 3D Captures to Simulated Digital Environments
speakers_or_contributors: Clement Fuji Tsang; Vismay Modi; Maria Shugrina
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Physical AI; Simulation
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
We introduce a novel capture-to-simulation pipeline for in-the-wild 3D Gaussian Splat scenes,
showing our research-based solutions for segmenting objects, predicting their volumetric mechanical properties using a recent learning-based method, and mixed splat-mesh physics simulations, all supported by recent features in the Kaolin Library.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community Roundtables
speakers_or_contributors: Victoria Szabo; Rebecca Xu; Everardo Reyes; Vernelle A. A. Noel; Victoria Szabo; Rebecca Xu; Everardo Reyes; Vernelle A. A. Noel
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH Art Advisory Group; ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community; SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery; SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Papers

description: Art Community Roundtables for Art Papers, Art Gallery, and Digital Arts Community members. Community members will be invited to join conversations related to the Art Gallery, Art Papers, and Digital Arts Community activities, as well as other themes related to the arts at SIGGRAPH.

title: Beyond the Score: Interpreting Graphics Benchmark Results for Better System Decisions
speakers_or_contributors: Brigit Valencia; Anthony Mansur; Rizu Jain
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Graphics benchmark scores are often used to compare systems, evaluate hardware, and guide purchasing decisions, but results are easier to quote than to interpret. Their meaning depends on the workload, system configuration, and bottlenecks behind the measurement. This talk explores how workload design, display resolution, system balance, driver behavior, and software optimization shape measured graphics performance, and how average scores can obscure behavior that affects real-world user experience. Attendees will learn how to ask better questions of benchmark data, recognize what results do and do not represent, and apply them more effectively when comparing systems and selecting the right configuration.

title: BoxCtrl: 3D-Aware Visual Prompting for Geometric Image Editing
speakers_or_contributors: Feifei Wang; Shiyuan Yang; Xiaoyu Li; Jing Liao
affiliations: City University of Hong Kong; Tencent
description: BoxCtrl introduces a 3D-aware visual prompting framework for precise image editing. Using RGB 3D bounding boxes to decouple geometry from appearance enables accurate translation, rotation, and scaling. A two-stage training of first SFT and then Reinforcement Learning achieves superior precision and photorealism in geometric editing across diverse real-world data.

title: Co-Optimization of Structure and Manufacturable Semi-Continuous Layers for Laminated Composites
speakers_or_contributors: Tao Liu; Aoran Lyu; Yongxue Chen; Yu Jiang; Michael James Petty; Charlie C.L. Wang
affiliations: The University of Manchester; University of Manchester
description: A field-based computational framework for designing fabric-reinforced composites with optimized topology and manufacturable semi-continuous layers, producing laminates up to 43.8% stiffer than conventional planar-ply designs.

title: Computational Design
Exact predicates, exact constructions and combinatorics for mesh CSG
speakers_or_contributors: Bruno Levy
affiliations: Inria Saclay and Laboratoire de Mathématiques d'Orsay Université Paris Saclay
description: This article introduces an algorithm that exactly constructs the so-called Weiler model (also called a 3D mesh arrangement) and that uses it to implement CSG with arbitrary multi-operand expressions. The main contribution is a 2D Constrained Delaunay Triangulation with exact coordinates and symbolic perturbations to disambiguate configurations with co-cyclic points.

title: Geometry Processing
Image Editing
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Generative AI; Virtual Reality

title: Path Guiding in Disney's "Zootopia 2"
speakers_or_contributors: Yining Karl Li; Lea Reichardt; Marco Manzi; Alexander Rath; Brian Green; Neelima Karanam
affiliations: Disney Research Studios; Walt Disney Animation Studios
description: Walt Disney Animation Studios' “Zootopia 2” was the first film to adopt Disney's Hyperion Renderer's second-generation path guiding system. Building on lessons we previously shared in “Path Guiding in Production and Recent Advancements”, this talk presents new advancements of our system that enabled large-scale adoption, improving workflows and rendering efficiency.

title: Progressing Level-of-Detail Animation for Volumetric Elastodynamics
speakers_or_contributors: Jiayi Eris Zhang; Doug James; Danny Kaufman
description: We extend Progressive Dynamics [Zhang et al. 2024, 2025] from cloth and shells to volumetric finite elements, enabling an efficient level-of-detail (LOD) animation-design pipeline with predictive coarse-resolution previews for rapid iteration toward a final high-resolution volumetric elastodynamics animation.

title: Rendering and Volumes
keywords_tags: Animation; Augmented Reality; Dynamics; Games; Geometry; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation; Virtual Reality
