title: Simulation
Trial by Fire: The VFX of The Lost Bus - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: Diceplay: A Modular Canvas for Physical Image Composition
speakers_or_contributors: Milin Kodnongbua; Zihan Jack Zhang; Shishi Xiao; Vivian Li; Heather Robertson; Rulin Chen; David Laidlaw; Adriana Schulz
affiliations: Beijing Normal-Hong Kong Baptist University; Brown University; University of Washington
description: Diceplay is a modular, physical, low-resolution canvas that uses identical geometric dice to create abstract visual compositions. We propose a gradient-based optimization approach to generate Diceplay configurations from text prompts by using a novel shape grammar and relaxing the purely discrete design space to a mixed-discrete-continuous problem.

title: Mixed Material Point Methods for Stiff Elastoplasticity
speakers_or_contributors: Gilles Daviet
description: Our variant of the Material Point Method discretizes both the velocity and stress fields over mixed finite elements, avoiding the need for repeated costly particle-to-grid transfers within the implicit elastoviscoplastic solve. Our method supports materials ranging from sand and snow to elastic solids, with two-way coupling to rigid-body solvers.

title: Surface chamfering for robust tetrahedral meshing
speakers_or_contributors: Lorenzo Diazzi; Daniele Panozzo; Jiacheng Dai; Marco Attene
affiliations: CNR IMATI; IMATI CNR Genova; NYU; New York University
description: We describe a new algorithm for conforming high quality tetrahedral meshing with 100% success rate. A novel input "chamfering" that eliminates all acute angles guarantees formal convergence of the underlying Delaunay refinement, while novel implicit Steiner points coupled with indirect geometric predicates ensure numerical robustness along the whole process.

title: ViewWeaver: Geometry-Grounded Generative Rendering for 3D-Aware Image Customization
speakers_or_contributors: Yaowei Li; Xiaoyu Li; Zhaoyang Zhang; Hongxiang Li; Long Chen; Ying Shan; Yuexian Zou
description: ViewWeaver is a geometry-grounded generative framework for 3D-aware image customization. It leverages multi-view references and explicit camera control to synthesize instruction-following images with consistent identity and structure, improving viewpoint accuracy and realism through efficient multi-view fusion and a scalable training and evaluation pipeline.

title: AI in Education: Continuing Conversations on Disruptive Trends
speakers_or_contributors: Nandhini Giri; Glenn Goldman
affiliations: New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT); Purdue University
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Ethics and Society; Games; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This BOF session builds on discussions from the 2025 Educators’ Forum panel, which examined the challenges educators face in addressing key industry trends.

The 2026 session will feature a facilitated discussion among organizers and attendees, revisiting major disruptors and trends in: (1) AI in education, (2) industry readiness and partnerships, and (3) evolving learning modalities.

Organizers will begin by sharing insights from the past year, followed by an interactive dialogue on pedagogical strategies, the integration of AI in classrooms, industry perspectives on academic preparation, and opportunities for continuous learning.

title: Access:VFX Networking + Recruiter AMA
speakers_or_contributors: Kathleen Ruffalo
interest_area: Arts & Design; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
ACCESS:VFX is a global non-profit of over 70 leading Animation and VFX studios. We focus on actively pursuing and encouraging inclusion, diversity, awareness and opportunity.

Step away from your screens and join Access:VFX IRL! While our monthly virtual networking sessions are great, nothing compares to connecting and engaging face-to-face with our amazing community.

This inclusive space is your opportunity to meet-up, share experiences, and build your network. Plus, we’re hosting an exclusive "Ask VFX Recruiters Anything" session. Bring your questions and get honest, real-world advice on how to stand out and navigate the complexities and challenges of the industry today.

title: NVIDIA | Making the Old Stories New: Innovative Dreams and Moses
speakers_or_contributors: Jon Erwin; Tim Moore
affiliations: Vū Technologies; WONDER PROJECT; INNOVATIVE DREAMS
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: The Old Stories: Moses revisits one of the most enduring stories ever told. In this talk, Innovative Dreams shares how its artists and filmmakers approached the making of Moses, using real-time hybrid filmmaking to move from imagination to image with greater speed and flexibility. The team connects performance, virtual environments, and real-time creative iteration to shape atmosphere, scale, and emotional clarity. The session is less about technology as spectacle, and more about the craft of making an ancient story feel immediate, intimate, and alive.

title: A Hybrid BVH Structure for Interactive GPU Ray Tracing
speakers_or_contributors: Andrew Bauer; Yining Karl Li
description: Choosing a BVH structure for accelerating ray tracing applications typically involves trading off between BVH build/update speed and BVH traversal speed. We introduce a hybrid BVH structure that combines the strengths of both the standard two-level structure and a monolithic single-level BVH, while minimizing the downsides of either approach.

title: A Unified Homogenization Framework for Straight- and Curved-Crease Origami Materials
speakers_or_contributors: Mingjie Li; Juan Montes Maestre; Emilien Ganier; Klara Mundilova; Mark Pauly; Bernhard Thomaszewski
description: We present a unified computational framework for numerical homogenisation of straight- and curved-crease origami materials with periodic crease patterns.

title: Continuous Control of Editing Models via Adaptive-Origin Guidance
speakers_or_contributors: Alon Wolf; Chen Katzir; Kfir Aberman; Or Patashnik
description: Diffusion editing models lack smooth control over edit intensity, and scaling standard Classifier-Free Guidance fails to provide it. We introduce Adaptive-Origin Guidance (AdaOr), which dynamically adjusts the guidance origin using an identity-conditioned prediction. This enables continuous, fine-grained control over both image and video models at inference time without specialized datasets.

title: DJM: Compact Base Meshes for Displacement Mapping using Triangle Jacobians
speakers_or_contributors: Congyi Zhang; Nicholas Vining; Yanhong Lin; Alireza Khatami; Ziyu Sun; Xiaohu Guo; Wenping Wang; Alla Sheffer
affiliations: NVIDIA; Texas A&M University; The University of British Columbia; TransGP; University of British Columbia; University of Texas Dallas
description: A new method for computing base meshes for displacement mapping, exploiting the Jacobian of the displacement function to guide base-mesh computation. DJM outperforms prior art in terms of reconstruction accuracy-to-size trade off and can be used for both traditional micromesh-based rendering and neural encoding.

title: MPM Lite: Linear Kernels and Integration without Particles
speakers_or_contributors: Xiang Feng; Yunuo Chen; Chang Yu; Hao Su; Demetri Terzopoulos; Yin Yang; Joe Masterjohn; Alejandro Castro; Chenfanfu Jiang
affiliations: Toyota Research Institute; University of California Los Angeles; University of California San Diego; University of Utah
description: MPM Lite is a hybrid Lagrangian/Eulerian method that removes particle-based quadrature at solve time. It achieves 15.9 times speedup over implicit MPM, and 1.88 times speedup over explicit MPM.

title: "Dear Upstairs Neighbors": Artist-Centered, AI-Assisted Expressionistic Animation
speakers_or_contributors: Connie He; Sarah Rumbley; Cassidy Curtis
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation
description:
A young woman named Ada, desperate for a good night’s sleep, is kept awake by her exceedingly noisy neighbors. As she struggles to imagine what could be causing the cacophony upstairs, reality drifts into fantasy, and an epic battle for peace and sanity ensues.

That’s the story of “Dear Upstairs Neighbors”, an animated short made by a hybrid team of animation veterans and AI researchers.

The artistic core of the film — storyboards, concept art, character animation — was lovingly hand-crafted by artists using traditional 2D and 3D techniques. Our technical artists then created custom generative image and video models, which they fine-tuned on our own concept art and used, much like a renderer, to unite these hand-crafted components into polished shots that respect all of the artistic choices made along the way.

The film’s expressionistic painterly style evolves, shot by shot, to reflect Ada’s intense and changing emotions. To achieve this evolving look by hand would have meant painting over 7,000 frames, with artists spending so much effort on execution that they would have no time to iterate beyond a first pass. To do it using computer graphics would have required developing dozens of custom new stylized rendering algorithms — a feature film’s worth of R&D for a 6-minute short. Not impossible, but far from practical!

Instead, we built artist-driven, AI-assisted video-to-video workflows that let us iteratively refine the look of each shot, homing in on the exact stylistic choices that best suited each moment in the story. That process was itself a direct conversation between artists: our technical artists would render shots, and receive feedback from our director and production designer, often in the form of new paintings, which they would use to improve the fine-tuning for the next iteration.

This video-to-video approach gave us unprecedented control and flexibility. Each artist could work in their comfort zone, using whatever tool was most natural and creative for them. We could also iterate on every shot, switching freely between our generative models and traditional tools like Premiere and After Effects, to bring the shot closer to perfection. Masked generation allowed us to specify exactly where we wanted the models to improvise.

We feel that generative tools can only be truly useful for making animated films when artists are in control, not only as users of the models, but as co-designers. This film was our opportunity to take the wheel, and guide the technology’s development in a more artist-centered direction.

title: Feature-Preserving Offset Meshing
speakers_or_contributors: Hongyi Cao; Gang Xu; Renshu Gu; Jinlan Xu; Xiaoyu Zhang; Rabczuk Timon; Yuzhe Luo; Xifeng Gao
affiliations: Bauhaus University Weimar; Beijing Institute of Spacecraft System Engineering; Hangzhou Dianzi University; LightSpeed Studios; State Key Laboratory of CAD&CG Zhejiang University
description: This paper introduces a novel method for generating offset meshes from 3D surfaces. It uniquely supports mitered joins and variable offset distances while robustly preserving sharp features. Evaluated on a broad dataset, our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in feature fidelity and element count.

title: HeadRouter: A Training-free Image Editing Framework for MM-DiTs by Adaptively Routing Attention Heads
speakers_or_contributors: Yu Xu; Fan Tang; Juan Cao; Xiaoyu Kong; Yuxin Zhang; Jintao Li; Oliver Deussen; Tong-Yee Lee
affiliations: Beihang University; Institute of Computing Technology Chinese Academy of Sciences; National Cheng Kung University; University of Chinese Academy of Sciences; University of Konstanz
description: HeadRouter is a training-free image editing framework tailored for Multimodal Diffusion Transformers (MM-DiTs). It addresses diminishing text guidance by adaptively routing attention heads based on semantic sensitivity and refining token representations. The method improves semantic alignment and structural preservation over state-of-the-art baselines through dynamic head-level control without additional training mechanisms.

title: The Instrument Is the Story: Ten Years of Spatial Music-Making with Paradiddle
speakers_or_contributors: Emre Tanirgan
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Games; Real-Time; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In this demo, we trace the ten-year development of Paradiddle, a VR drumming application, showing how changes in the XR landscape shaped its evolution. We explore how our studio's guiding design questions adapted to a rapidly changing medium and the users that changed with it.

title: Tube Maps: Fast SPH Boundary Handling with Tubular Coordinates
speakers_or_contributors: Daria Nogina; Silvia Sellán
description: We introduce Tube Maps, a drop-in replacement for SPH boundary density computation that achieves accuracy comparable to implicit methods while dramatically reducing their computational cost. Our key observation is that the boundary density integral is fully determined by the local surface geometry near a fluid particle's closest point.

title: mpcGear: Multi-Point Conjugation Gear Mechanisms
speakers_or_contributors: Ke Chen; Joshua John Shi Kai Lee; Jianmin Zheng; Ligang Liu; Peng Song
affiliations: Nanyang Technological University; Singapore University of Technology and Design; University of Science and Technology of China
description: This work presents a new class of gear mechanisms, called multi-point conjugation gear mechanisms, as well as computational techniques to modeling these gear mechanisms for exactly generating user-specified 3D motions under external loads.

title: A Short Among the Stars: Pipeline Adaptation when Everything is a Volume in Disney's "Versa"
speakers_or_contributors: Laura Grondahl; Archana Senthilkumar; Lea Reichardt
keywords_tags: Animation; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
description: Walt Disney Animation Studios' "Versa" is a beautifully emotional six minute short, where volumes make up everything across both environments and characters. This talk explores how we enabled new workflows and achieved cross-departmental collaboration with Effects to bring this unique short to life.

title: Kinematic Kitbashing
speakers_or_contributors: Minghao Guo; Victor Zordan; Sheldon Andrews; Wojciech Matusik; Maneesh Agrawala; Hsueh-Ti Derek Liu
affiliations: Clemson University; Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Roblox; Stanford University; École de Technologie Supérieure (ÉTS)
description: Kinematic Kitbashing synthesizes articulated 3D objects by assembling reusable parts according to an abstract kinematic graph. It preserves plausible attachments across motion using kinematics-aware geometric cues and can optimize for functional goals, enabling new articulated designs from existing part libraries.

title: LooseRoPE: Content-aware Attention Manipulation for Semantic Harmonization
speakers_or_contributors: Etai Sella; Yoav Baron; Hadar Averbuch-Elor; Daniel Cohen-Or; Or Patashnik
description: LooseRoPE enables prompt-free image editing by letting users crop and paste objects directly into new scenes. It improves diffusion-based harmonization by modulating RoPE with saliency, balancing identity preservation and contextual blending. This yields intuitive, spatially precise edits that maintain object appearance, pose, and placement without requiring text prompts or masks.

title: Low-Rank Koopman Deformables with Log-Linear Time Integration
speakers_or_contributors: Yue Chang; Peter Yichen Chen; Eitan Grinspun; Maurizio M. Chiaramonte
affiliations: Meta Reality Labs Research; The University of British Columbia; University of Toronto
description: We present a Koopman operator method for fast deformable simulation using Dynamic Mode Decomposition, enabling efficient long-term prediction without sequential time stepping. Our discretization-agnostic formulation generalizes across shapes and resolutions, making Koopman-based reduced models practical for geometry variation, control, and fast shape optimization tasks.

title: PR-Cage: Progressive Feasibility Relaxation for Tight Bounding Cage Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Huibiao Wen; Kaikai Qin; Xinxin Su; Jingcheng Mei; Shuangmin Chen; Chongyang Deng; Changhe Tu; Shiqing Xin; Wenping Wang
affiliations: Hangzhou Dianzi University; HoteamSoft; Qingdao University of Science and Technology; School of Computer Science and Technology Shandong University; Shandong Huayun Technology Co., Ltd.; Shandong University; Texas A&M University
description: PR-Cage is an automated framework for generating high-quality cages—simplified mesh proxies that tightly enclose complex geometry. Using a staircase relaxation of a thickness parameter and constrained QEM optimization, it achieves extreme simplification while preserving shape fidelity, enabling efficient contact simulation, deformation, and other graphics applications.

title: C^0 Generalized Coons Volumes over Arbitrary Polyhedra
speakers_or_contributors: Kaikai Qin; Zeqi Ge; Péter Salvi; Chenhao Ying; Huibiao Wen; Kepeng Xu; Shiqing Xin; Chongyang Deng
affiliations: Budapest University of Technology and Economics; Hangzhou Dianzi University; School of Computer Science and Technology Shandong University; Shandong University; Xidian University
description: In this paper, we generalize the Coons volume from hexahedral topology to arbitrary polyhedral topologies via generalized barycentric coordinates. We prove that the proposed generalized Coons volume possesses several desirable geometric properties and demonstrate its applications in computer graphics.

title: Computational Design of Coordinate-Motion Assemblies
speakers_or_contributors: Yukun Lu; Ke Chen; Ligang Liu; Peng Song
affiliations: Singapore University of Technology and Design; University of Science and Technology of China
description: We present a computational approach for designing contact-based coordinate-motion assemblies that meet user-specified target appearance and motion. One key enabler of our approach is that we established a theoretical connection between contact geometry within an assembly and unique coordinate motion of the assembly's parts.

title: MAOAM: Unified Object and Material Selection with Vision-Language Models
speakers_or_contributors: Jaden Park; Valentin Deschaintre; Jason Kuen; Kangning Liu; Iliyan Georgiev; Krishna Kumar Singh; Yong Jae Lee; Michael Fischer
affiliations: Adobe; Adobe Research; University of Wisconsin-Madison
description: MAOAM is a unified selection framework that enables precise object- and material- level selection across both text- and click-based interactions. MAOAM leverages a VLM which interprets the user's selection intent, and encodes information to the segmentation head. MAOAM achieves strong performance and generalization across varied objects, materials, and prompting scenarios.

title: Physics-Inspired Procedural Texturing of Extremely Deformable Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Aleksei Kalinov; Mickaël Ly; Christian Hafner; Chris Wojtan
affiliations: Institute of Science and Technology Austria; Institute of Science and Technology Austria (ISTA)
description:
Applying texture maps to dynamic deformable surfaces presents a significant challenge, due to ever-shifting differences in length scales involved.
We present two novel wave-based procedural texturing algorithms which reproduce common physical properties like advection and self-similarity, enabling physics-guided animation of deforming objects with extreme texture map distortions.

title: Computational Design of Terrestrial Robots with Anisotropic Friction
speakers_or_contributors: Hang Hu; Kangbo Lyu; Changyu Hu; Zihan Li; Peiwen Yang; Minchen Li; Shuguang Li; Tao Du
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; Shanghai Qi Zhi Institute; Tsinghua University
description: The interplay of morphology, control, and anisotropic friction makes optimal design for terrestrial locomotion challenging. We present a computational pipeline that co-designs friction and controllers across diverse robot morphologies, showing anisotropic friction is critical and achieving statistically superior performance over state-of-the-art methods.

title: Image Editing - Interactive Discussion
description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. Outside the room will be a series of poster boards for authors to gather around with the audience. Authors are invited to bring any material related to their paper that could instigate further conversation such as printouts, posters, demos, or other presentation aids. The interactive discussions provide attendees more interaction with the individual authors.

title: Texture-Aware Remeshing for Texture-aware Geometry Processing
speakers_or_contributors: Julian Knodt; Seung-Hwan Baek
description: This work demonstrates how to hoist textures to vertex color to allow for incorporating textures into geometry processing algorithms. The use of vertex colors for texture-aware results is then shown across a number of algorithms, including parameterization, clustering, remeshing, and others.

title: Volumetric FX for the 2D world of The Bad Guys 2
speakers_or_contributors: Zach Glynn; Nyoung Kim
description: The look for The Bad Guys 2 is a blend of rich 3D geometry and lighting, with traditional hand drawn 2D shapes and movement. To help embody this traditional 2D art style, novel tools and techniques were developed, allowing artist to expand their capabilities into traditional workflows.

title: Woodstock: Interactive Modeling of Fungal Wood Decay
speakers_or_contributors: Zhanyu Yang; Nikolas Schwarz; Bosheng Li; Dominik Michels; Bedrich Benes; Sören Pirk; Wojtek Palubicki
affiliations: Adam Mickiewicz University in Poznań; KAUST; Kiel University; Purdue University; Samsung
description: Woodstock is a novel biophysical simulation framework for fungal wood decay. By tightly coupling rot dynamics, wood states, and strand-based mechanics, our method reproduces characteristic decay phenomena such as fungal colonization, trunk hollowing, wood fracturing, and eventual structural collapse.

title: Hands-on Course: Advance World Simulation With 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Environment Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Zoë LaLena
keywords_tags: Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Physical AI; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Learn how to reconstruct a large scene for robotics testing using NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec Gaussian-based reconstruction technologies to perform multi-GPU training, object segmentation, and extraction. This lab will walk through core concepts with a step-by-step workflow for data capture, reconstruction, and object level-integration for robotics simulation in NVIDIA Isaac Lab.

title: Techspressionism: An Expressive Approach to Digital Art
speakers_or_contributors: Cynthia B. Rubin; Patrick Lichty; Gregory Patrick Garvey; Randi Matushevitz
affiliations: C B Rubin Studio; Independent Artist; Quinnipiac University; Winona State University
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Image Processing; Scientific Visualization; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Techspressionism: what is it? An artistic approach in which technology is utilized as a means to express emotional experience. Its practitioners form a global network of artists spanning over 45 countries. This presentation highlights current activities, including exhibitions at the LA Center for Digital Art (LACDA) and the Arda Khiva Museum in Uzbekistan, as well as past exhibitions and initiatives. We also discuss our monthly virtual salons, artist interviews, roundtables, and weekly coworking sessions. Additionally, we will share details about an exciting new collaboration between Techspressionism and SIGGRAPH DAC that is on display during SIGGRAPH2006.

title: The Return of the Utah Teapot
speakers_or_contributors: Cem Yuksel; Jim Blinn; Martin Newell
keywords_tags: Animation; Augmented Reality; Games; Geometry; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Real-Time; Rendering; Virtual Reality
description: The Utah Teapot has been a symbol of computer graphics, appearing in numerous papers, animations, and movies. It is included in many graphics applications and APIs. We tell the story of the Utah Teapot as we celebrate it on the 50th anniversary of its first appearance in SIGGRAPH. We also release the 2026 version of the Utah Teapot.

title: "Dear Upstairs Neighbors": Artist-Centered, AI-Assisted Expressionistic Animation - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: Animation Theater 4: Adventure Time
description: Pack light because this program has places to be! A collection of films that chase horizon lines, embrace the unexpected, and remind us why leaving is sometimes the whole point.

title: Educator's Townhall Session
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: After four days of education-focused presentations, attendees are invited to an invigorating, open discussion on current topics in computer graphics education and interactive techniques. Come to the town hall session to ask questions, share perspectives, and have a good time with the SIGGRAPH education community!

title: SIGGRAPH 2026 Technical Papers Town Hall
description: Town Hall discussion of the SIGGRAPH Technical Papers Program.

title: History: Fueling the Future by Preserving the Past - The ACM SIGGRAPH History Archive
speakers_or_contributors: Bonnie Mitchell; Bonnie Mitchell; Janice Searleman; Kai Yabis; Camille Leber; Jenna Mauch; Aliyah Wise; Lillian Aulls
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH History Committee; Bowling Green State University; Clarkson University
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Display; Dynamics; Education; Ethics and Society; Fabrication; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Haptics; Hardware; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Performance; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
If you are interested in the history of computer graphics, this session is for you. The ACM SIGGRAPH History Archive is both a physical collection and an online archive of SIGGRAPH organization leadership and events, conference presentations, demonstrations, animation screenings, art exhibitions, and more. The archive is a volunteer effort involving pioneers, professionals, students, and community members. This session will showcase the new additions to the archive and include short talks by student interns and volunteers. We will also focus on how this incredibly information-rich archive has fueled the future of computer graphics research and creative endeavors.

The archive has grown tremendously and now includes more than 75,000 pages of information. One of the most significant contributions to the community is opening up the archive so the AI bots could learn from 52 years worth of conference presentations, demonstrations, animation screening, etc. Today, Gemini, ChatGPT and other AI assistants often cite the ACM SIGGRAPH History Archive and the information related to the history of computer graphics is much more accurate. Another significant addition was the programming of an online inventory system that tracks the 4000+ physical artifacts in the collection. Eighteen years worth of SIGGRAPH Asia conference content was also added to the archive along with the unique programs from SIGGRAPH's past such as performances, competitions, and special sessions and events. Overall, this session is an exciting way to capture a glimpse into SIGGRAPH's past and speculate on how access to this information will alter computer graphics' future trajectory.

title: NVIDIA | Making ChikaBOOM!: AI, Animation, and the Artist-Centered Production Pipeline
speakers_or_contributors: Benjamin Michel; Tian Pei; c. Craig Patterson
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: ChikaBOOM! offers a timely case study in how generative AI is being used inside a real animation production pipeline. Written and directed by c. Craig Patterson, the short follows aspiring young magician Chika Batstone as she accidentally unleashes a chaotic magical creature into New York City. In this session, Patterson will unpack how AI supported the making of the film, from creative development and visual exploration through production iteration and final frames. The session will focus on the practical realities of AI-assisted production: what worked, how teams maintained creative control, and what lessons apply to studios, artists, technologists, and production leaders building AI into animation workflows.

title: Smells Like Memory: Reconstructing Virtual Autobiographical Spaces Through Scent in VR
speakers_or_contributors: Jiaxuan Yin
keywords_tags: Games; Haptics; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: An immersive VR experience investigates spatial storytelling through the integration of olfactory memory and photographic interaction. By framing photography as a tangible interface for memory recall, the work examines how scent-driven, embodied interaction enables non-linear autobiographical reconstruction and contributes to research on multi-sensory narrative design in virtual environments.

title: The Urgency of Now: Digital Art For Democracy!
speakers_or_contributors: isa gordon
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Education; Ethics and Society; Performance; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Even if you've never made protest art before: now is the time to step up. We'll discuss the recent surge of creative resistance in the face of tyranny, with reviews of both recent and upcoming pro-democracy art shows and emphasis on how we can all get more involved. We'll encourage SIGGRAPH artists to stand up and refuse to remain silent, to use creative practice as vital weapon of resistance and tool for civic awakening: celebrating the power of art to unite, heal, and defend our shared freedoms using bold visual dissent against authoritarianism. Contact: art4democracy@isagordon.net for more info.

title: 3D Gaussian Splatting II
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Geometry; Modeling; Rendering

title: Adapting Quality Metrics to Tone Mapping
speakers_or_contributors: Kenneth Chen; Dongyeon Kim; Yuta Asano; Alexandre Chapiro; Qi Sun; Rafał Mantiuk
affiliations: New York University; Reality Labs Research, Meta; University of Cambridge
description: This work provides a recipe for adapting existing image/video quality metrics for evaluating tone-mapping algorithms. We demonstrate across multiple datasets that when tone-mapped images and their HDR reference are represented in a perceptually-uniform space, existing metrics such as TOPIQ, DISTS, and ColorVideoVDP outperform tone-mapping-specific metrics, such as TMQI and FFTMI.

title: Frontiers Workshop: Form and Function: A Hands-On Workshop on Computationally Designing and Fabricating Barrier-Grid Animations
speakers_or_contributors: Ticha Sethapakdi; Maxine Perroni-Scharf; Ticha Sethapakdi
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Display; Fabrication; Image Processing
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This frontiers workshop equips participants with hands-on experience using a modern computational design workflow for creating and fabricating barrier-grid animations. Participants will author animations whose occlusion patterns are shaped by mathematical functions, preview them under multiple interaction modes, and fabricate take-home artifacts. By participating in this workshop, attendees will understand how to design expressive barrier-grid animations that balance form and function.

title: Garments
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Geometry; Modeling; Simulation

title: Graphical X Splatting (GraphiXS): A Graphical Model for 4D Gaussian Splatting under Uncertainty
speakers_or_contributors: Doga Yilmaz; Jialin Zhu; Deshan Gong; He Wang
affiliations: Baidu Research; The University of Hong Kong; University College London (UCL)
description: Graphical X Splatting (GraphiXS) is a novel probabilistic framework for systematically modelling data uncertainty in 4D Gaussian Splatting. It handles various types of spatial and temporal sparsity, including missing frames and sparse viewpoints, and generalizes across diverse primitives. Extensive evaluations show that GraphiXS outperforms existing methods across a range of sparse data scenarios.

title: Imaging, Video, and HDR
keywords_tags: Display; Generative AI; Hardware; Image Processing

title: Learning in Motion
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Modeling; Simulation

title: LoBoFit: Flexible Garment Refitting via Local Bone Mapping Blending
speakers_or_contributors: Meng Zhang; Yu Xin; Feiya Guo; Kaizhang Kang; Mengyu Chu; Ruizhen Hu
affiliations: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST); Nanjing University of Science and Technology; Peking University; Shenzhen University; University of Science and Technology of China
description: LoBoFit brings robust, high-fidelity garment refitting to challenging avatar changes. It preserves original design features, fine wrinkles, and fit style by optimizing garments in blended local bone coordinates, enabling stable convergence, efficient refinement, and reliable results across large body variations, arbitrary poses, and both single- and multi-layer clothing.

title: NVIDIA | The Making of Sentinel: How AI Infrastructure Tools are Reshaping Film Production
speakers_or_contributors: Gary Mundell; Phil Tippet; Remko Noteboom
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Sentinel, an upcoming stop-motion feature film by Academy Award-winning visual effects and animation pioneer Phil Tippett, will serve as a lens for exploring a surreal alternate universe within a soldier's mind during World War I. Drawing on Tippett's legendary visual storytelling virtuosity, the project reflects an agentic AI-first production workflow and creative process where ideas evolve through exploration and experimentation. In this talk, the artists behind Sentinel discuss how story, intuition, and collaboration guide the work, and how their partnership with LOFTAPPS' LOFTai AI infrastructure platform helps manage assets, provenance and resources while preserving creative intent as the film takes shape.

title: NVIDIA: A Step-by-Step Introduction to the ANARI Rendering API
speakers_or_contributors: Jefferson Amstutz; Ingo Wald
keywords_tags: Graphics Systems Architecture; Rendering; Scientific Visualization
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: This course introduces the Khronos ANARI rendering API using a highly practical, step-by-step approach in which attendees learn to write increasingly capable ANARI based applications. At the end of this course, users should be easily able to realize their own 3D applications using ANARI for both interactive and offline rendering use cases.

title: Performance, Rendering, and Interaction for Competitive Esports (PRICE)
speakers_or_contributors: Josef Spjut; Benjamin Watson; Madison Klarkowski; Rulon Raymond; Mark Claypool; Paul Ritaldato
affiliations: Epic Games; Infinity Ward; NVIDIA; North Carolina State University; Queensland University of Technology; Worcester Polytechnic Institute
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Display; Games; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering
description:
The Performance, Rendering, and Interaction for Competitive Esports (PRICE) workshop brings together cutting-edge research on topics essential to esports, including graphics and rendering, systems and performance, human factors and HCI, computer supported cooperative work (CSCW), data, and artificial intelligence.

Esports is no longer a niche hobby; it is a major cultural and economic force, with a global audience in the hundreds of millions and a market size exceeding $1 billion. The performance and experience of both players and spectators are critically dependent on the underlying technology. Success in competitive games hinges on a complex interplay between hardware capabilities, software optimizations, and the limits of human perception, performance, and experience.

The program includes a keynote, sketch presentations, and interactive panel discussions among sketch authors where the audience will be able to participate in the discussion. Additionally, a few short breaks in the agenda will allow for small group and one-on-one interactions.

title: Physics Is Easier Than You Think: From Classical to Neural Elastic Simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Davide Corigliano; Otman Benchekroun; Jernej Barbič
affiliations: ETH Zürich; USC; University of Toronto
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Research & Education
description: Through theory and reference code, attendees will learn the simple foundations of elastic simulation. Once the basics are established, the course further explores through the current research landscape of neural physics simulation, showing how newcomers can integrate modern physics simulation into their projects.

title: R-DMesh: Video-Guided 3D Animation via Rectified Dynamic Mesh Flow
speakers_or_contributors: Zijie Wu; Lixin Xu; Puhua Jiang; Sicong Liu; Chunchao Guo; Xiang Bai
affiliations: Huazhong University of Science and Technology; Tencent Hunyuan
description: We present R-DMesh, a novel feed-forward video-guided mesh animation framework that solves the pose misalignment dilemma between an initial mesh and a condition video through jump offset modeling. It generates high-fidelity, motion aligned 4D animations and enables downstream applications like pose retargeting and holistic 4D generation.

title: Remesher: Automate Character Pipelines with ComfyUI's API, Docker, and Agents
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Gold
description: Learn to build automated character generation pipelines using ComfyUI's REST/WebSocket APIs. Deploy state-of-the-art open source image generation and 3d generation models (Qwen, Hunyuan3D) via Docker and cloud GPU providers, then build Typer CLI tools that transform text prompts into rigged 3D characters through programmatic workflow execution: generate→turnaround→mesh→rig.

title: SYNAPTICON
speakers_or_contributors: Albert Barque-Duran
affiliations: IASLab (Interactive Arts & Science Lab) - La Salle Barcelona (Universitat Ramon Llull)
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Ethics and Society; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: SYNAPTICON is an artistic research artifact at the intersection of neuro-hacking, brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), and foundation models, probing new territories of human expression, aesthetics, and AI alignment.

title: The Pipeline Conference - Organizer Meetup
speakers_or_contributors: Fran Zandonella Benjamin; Ryan Bottriell
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The Pipeline Conference will be in-person in September in Vancouver, Canada. We will discuss future pipeline focused events and the organization as a whole. Come meet The Pipeline Conference organizers. Get involved. Share your dream pipeline ideas for upcoming events. Join us and participate in the conversation.

title: World Models: When Digital Reality Thinks, Adapts and Responds
speakers_or_contributors: Benjamin Lock
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Simulation; Visual Effects
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: World models could redefine how games, animation, film and television are imagined, explored and created. Emerging across creative media, simulation and robotics, world models transcend fixed environments and programmed rules by learning how reality behaves, maintaining context, predicting outcomes and responding to creative or user input. This facilitated discussion with developers, filmmakers, artists, researchers and production technologists examines the opportunities, limitations and practical requirements of this emerging technology. We will explore persistence, memory, causality, directability, consistency and control, asking what skills are needed and what new forms of creation become possible when digital reality can think, adapt and respond.

title: LUCID: Learning Unified Control for Image Deflaring and Exposure Mastery in Nighttime Photography
speakers_or_contributors: Tingyu Yang; Yuan Cheng; Xiaoyun Yuan
description: Master the night with LUCID: Learning Unified Control for Image Deflaring and Exposure Mastery in Nighttime Photography. Merging flare disentanglement and generative diffusion, our framework conquers entangled degradations. LUCID unlocks continuous control over global illumination and light source scale, enabling the brightening of deep shadows while strictly preventing light pollution.

title: Object-Space Analysis of Local Contrast Sensitivity for Hierarchical Representations of 3D Gaussians
speakers_or_contributors: Naoto Yoshii; Suguru Saito; Masataka Sawayama; Yoshinori Dobashi
affiliations: Hokkaido University; Institute of Science Tokyo
description: We present an object-space contrast sensitivity analysis for 3D Gaussian representations that estimates spatial frequency responses without explicit rasterization using analytical Fourier transforms and the projection-slice theorem. This enables contrast-sensitivity-based perceptual evaluation and efficient foveated rendering via hierarchical Gaussian level selection, preserving visual quality while reducing rendering cost.

title: PatternGSL: A Structured Specification Language for Template-Free and Simulation-Ready 3D Garments
speakers_or_contributors: Zhenyang Li; Lutao Jiang; Yizhou Zhao; Weikai Chen; Ying-Cong Chen; Xin Wang; Yifan Peng
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; LIGHTSPEED; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou); The University of Hong Kong (HKU)
description: We present PatternGSL, a structured and template-free representation for simulation-ready 3D garments. Given a single garment image, our VLM-based method predicts editable sewing patterns with explicit panel, edge, and stitch topology. Trained on the large-scale PatternGSLData dataset, it enables accurate reconstruction, cloth simulation, and downstream garment design workflows.

title: TopoCap: Learning Topology-Agnostic Motion Priors for Monocular Video-to-Animation
speakers_or_contributors: Chengfeng Pu; Jia-Peng Zhang; Meng-Hao Guo; Yan-Pei Cao; Shi-Min Hu
affiliations: CS Dept, Tsinghua University; Tsinghua University; VAST
description: TopoCap is a unified framework for motion capture and retargeting from monocular video, supporting arbitrary skeletal topologies without test-time optimization. Leveraging a two-stage generative pipeline, TopoCap achieves motion transfer across diverse creatures, backed by Mobjaverse—a dataset with over 5,000 skeletal topologies and 2 million frames for robust zero-shot retargeting.

title: Garment Particles: A 2D–3D Symmetric Garment Representation for Generation and Editing
speakers_or_contributors: Kiyohiro Nakayama; I-Chao Shen; Ruofan Liu; Yiming Wang; Gordon Wetzstein; Takeo Igarashi
affiliations: ETH Zürich; Institute of Science Tokyo; Stanford University; The University of Tokyo
description: Garment Particles is a 5D point-cloud representation that jointly encodes 2D sewing patterns and 3D geometry. This representation enables Garment Particles Flow (GPF) and Particles-to-Pattern Flow (PPF), a rectified flow framework for both simulation-ready sewing pattern generation from text and images and intuitive 2D/3D editing via diffusion posterior sampling.

title: Learning View-Dependent Splatting Kernels
speakers_or_contributors: Huakeng Ding; Zhanpeng Liu; Fan Pei; Kun Zhou; Hongzhi Wu
affiliations: State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University; Zhejiang University
description: We present a differentiable framework to automatically learn view-dependent 2D kernels in a splatting-based pipeline to improve reconstruction quality and representation efficiency for novel 3D view synthesis.

title: Lucky High Dynamic Range Smartphone Imaging
speakers_or_contributors: Baiang Li; Ruyu Yan; Ethan Tseng; Zhoutong Zhang; Adam Finkelstein; Jiawen Chen; Felix Heide
description: Stage 3 revisions add a DNG-to-sRGB pipeline description and ISP-limitation caveat, promote six supplement figures/tables to the main paper, clarify use of unseen handheld captures for real-world evaluation, added a failure case, limitation and future work in the main paper, and add supplementary motion statistics and a no-motion HDR+ comparison.

title: Stylized Text-to-Motion Generation via Hypernetwork-Driven Low-Rank Adaptation
speakers_or_contributors: Junhyuk Jeon; Seokhyeon Hong; Junyong Noh
affiliations: KAIST, Visual Media Lab; Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)
description: We propose a hypernetwork-driven LoRA framework for stylized text-to-motion generation that dynamically modulates a pretrained diffusion model using style embeddings extracted from reference motions. By structuring the style latent space with supervised contrastive learning and incorporating style-guided sampling, the method improves stylization quality, efficiency, and generalization to unseen motion styles.

title: CAdam: Context-Adaptive Moment Estimation for 3D Gaussian Densification in Generative Distillation
speakers_or_contributors: SeungJeh Chung; Geonho Park; Misong Kim; HyeongYeop Kang
description: CAdam resolves the mismatch between reconstruction-native densification and stochastic generative guidance in optimization-based 3DGS. By reinterpreting densification as statistical signal verification via momentum and context-adaptive selection, it reduces Gaussian primitives by 85%–97% across diverse backbones while preserving comparable perceptual quality, making generative 3DGS far more compact and practical.

title: Learning Sewing Patterns via Latent Flow Matching of Implicit Fields
speakers_or_contributors: Cong Cao; Ren Li; Corentin Dumery; Hao Li
affiliations: EPFL; MBZUAI; Mohamed Bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence
description: We introduce an implicit representation for sewing patterns using distance fields. Combined with latent flow matching, the model learns distributions over panel geometry and stitching, enabling generation, image-based estimation, and optimization. The framework supports flexible topology and improves accuracy over prior methods.

title: MUSIC: Learning Muscle-Driven Dexterous Hand Control
speakers_or_contributors: Pei Xu; Yufei Ye; Shuchu Sun; Yu Ding; Elizabeth Schumann; C. Karen Liu
description: We present a data-driven framework for physics-based, muscle-driven dexterous control that enables musculoskeletal hands to perform piano playing for novel musical scores. Our framework adopts a hierarchical architecture, combining high-frequency muscle control with low-frequency coordination. The method achieves coordinated bimanual motions and produces human-like activation patterns, generalizing beyond reference data.

title: Soft Anisotropic Diagrams for Differentiable Image Representation
speakers_or_contributors: Laki Iinbor; Zhiyang Dou; Wojciech Matusik
affiliations: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); MIT
description: Soft Anisotropic Diagrams (SAD) is a fast, differentiable image representation based on a soft anisotropic additively weighted Voronoi partition (i.e., an Apollonius diagram) with learnable per-site temperatures. It reconstructs images with sharp, content-aligned boundaries, compact storage, fast random access, and substantially faster encoding and higher quality than prior methods.

title: Learned Universal Interoperable Virtual Try-ON
speakers_or_contributors: Cong Cao; Xianhang Cheng; Jingyuan Liu; Yujian Zheng; Zhenhui Lin; Ren Li; Meriem Chkir; Hao Li
affiliations: MBZUAI; Mohamed Bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence; The University of Tokyo
description: We introduce a fully automated 3D virtual try-on system that fits diverse multilayer garments onto arbitrary humanoid characters. Using SMPL as an intermediate proxy, it handles unrigged, non-manifold assets, supports garment resizing, and produces high-quality draping without manual correspondence, shared rigs, or 2D sewing patterns.

title: MOCHI: Motion Enhancement of Collaborative Human-object Interactions
speakers_or_contributors: Jiye Lee; Yonghun Choi; Jungdam Won
description: Our method enhances noisy multi-human object interaction (MHOI) data by automatically generating plausible hand and finger motions and refining noisy body movements, all while preserving the original interaction semantics.

title: Radiance Fields from Photons
speakers_or_contributors: Sacha Jungerman; Aryan Garg; Mohit Gupta
description: Neural radiance fields and Gaussian splats often produce artifacts in low light, high dynamic range, or with rapid motion. Instead, by training at the granularity of individual photons, as enabled by single-photon cameras, we can achieve sharp, reliable reconstructions even in these challenging conditions.

title: Single-shot HDR using conventional image sensor shutter functions and optical randomization
speakers_or_contributors: Xiang Dai; Kyrollos Yanny; Kristina Monakhova; Nicholas Antipa
affiliations: Cornell University; UC Berkeley; UC San Diego
description: We present a single-shot HDR imaging method using a global reset release (GRR) sensor and optical shuffling via a random fiber bundle. Our prototype enables HDR recovery with a simple prior and flexible exposure control, demonstrating a dynamic range increase from 48 dB to 73 dB in validation.

title: ACT: A Unified Framework for Rigging and Animating Characters with Arbitrary Topologies
speakers_or_contributors: Pengyu Long; Weirui Wang; Qingcheng Zhao; Xiaoyang Guo; Xiaoyu Pan; Qixuan Zhang; Jiaqing Zhou; Tianlei Hu; Wei Yang; Lan Xu; Jingyi Yu
affiliations: ByteDance Games; Huazhong University of Science and Technology; ShanghaiTech University; University of Toronto
description: ACT is a unified AI framework that effortlessly brings static 3D characters to life. By integrating rigging, skinning, and motion generation into a single diffusion-based pipeline, ACT automatically animates characters of any shape, delivering high-quality, physically plausible motions that overcome the limitations of traditional, multi-step animation workflows.

title: FIT: A Large-Scale Dataset for Fit-Aware Virtual Try-On
speakers_or_contributors: Yuanhao Wang; Johanna Karras; Yingwei Li; Ira Kemelmacher-Shlizerman
description: We introduce FIT (Fit-Inclusive Try-on), a virtual try-on dataset of over 1M samples covering diverse garment fits, each annotated with precise body/garment measurements. Our novel data generation pipeline leverages synthetic garment simulation and photorealistic retexturing. Then, we leverage FIT to train a state-of-the-art fit-aware virtual try-on model.

title: Finding Fast Filters
speakers_or_contributors: Karima Ma; Andrew Adams; Jonathan Ragan-Kelley
affiliations: Adobe Inc; Massachusetts Institute of Technology - EECS
description: Image and audio processing applications want to use large FIR filters while adhering to strict runtime and latency requirements. Prior methods for fast filter approximation offer limited speed versus quality tradeoffs. We unify these approximation techniques as primitives within an automatically searchable space for fast 1D and 2D filters.

title: Ref-DGS: Reflective Dual Gaussian Splatting
speakers_or_contributors: Ningjing Fan; Yiqun Wang; Dong-Ming Yan; Peter Wonka
affiliations: Chongqing University; Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences; King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
description: Ref-DGS is a dual Gaussian splatting framework that decouples surface reconstruction and specular reflection, modeling near- and far-field specular reflections without ray tracing, achieving efficient, high-quality surface reconstruction and novel view synthesis.

title: Archiving Media Art and Preserving Obsolete Technologies
speakers_or_contributors: Zeynep Abes; Joey Heinen; Russell Zych; Stephanie Becker
keywords_tags: Art; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Education; Hardware; Pipeline Tools and Work; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: How do museums, archives, and libraries steward media artworks built on obsolete or rapidly aging technologies? From CD-ROMs and Flash to early VR headsets, custom hardware, and platform-dependent installations, much of recent media art history runs on systems no longer manufactured or supported. This Birds of a Feather, convened with the LA Media Art Archives Network, brings together archivists, conservators,curators, artists, and technologists to share institutional approaches. Topics may include emulation, migration, documentation, artist interviews, exhibition and archival standards, and the realities of long-term stewardship of media art. Come share challenges and solutions from your own practice. All levels welcome.

title: An Introduction to the Fourier Transform
speakers_or_contributors: Andrew Glassner
keywords_tags: Audio; Computer Vision; Education; Image Processing; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Rendering; Scientific Visualization
description: The Fourier Transform is fundamental to computer graphics, explaining topics from aliasing and sampling to image compression and filtering. This friendly course explains the principles in words, pictures, and animation, rather than math. The concepts are the important thing. We show that they are comprehensible, useful, and beautiful.

title: Gaussian Reconstruction
GeoQuery: Geometry-Query Diffusion for Sparse-View Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Xiao Cao; Yuze Li; Youmin Zhang; Jiayu Song; Cheng Yan; Wen Li; Lixin Duan
affiliations: Rawmantic AI; Tianjin University; University of Electronic Science and Technology of China
description: GeoQuery is a geometry-guided single-step diffusion framework for mitigating query contamination in existing multi-view self attention mechanism. By retrieving proxy features from clean reference views via geometric correspondences, it bypasses corrupted query features and enables high-fidelity restoration for sparse-view 3D Gaussian Splatting.

title: HydraOps: Replacing an In-House Lighting and Rendering Runtime with Hydra 2.0
speakers_or_contributors: Alexander Kalyuzhnyy; Maksim Tokarev
keywords_tags: Animation; Graphics Systems Architecture; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
description: HydraOps is the first production, artist-facing lighting framework built on Hydra 2.0 within OpenDCC, unifying interactive and final rendering from a single USD scene. Replacing a legacy in-house engine with Hydra’s modular scene index architecture, it streamlines evaluation, reduces complexity, and lowers maintenance costs.

title: Implicit Minimal Surfaces for Bijective Correspondences
speakers_or_contributors: Etienne Corman; Yousuf Soliman; Robin Magnet; Mark Gillespie
affiliations: CNRS, Inria, LORIA; INRIA; INRIA, Saclay; Side Effects Software Inc
description: We introduce an implicit representation of continuous, bijective, orientation-preserving maps between genus zero surfaces with or without boundary. The distortion of these maps can easily be minimized by optimizing the Ginzburg-Landau functional---a ubiquitous model in physics and differential geometry---leading to a simple algorithm for computing bijective correspondences using only standard tools of the tangent vector field toolbox. The method avoids combinatorial mesh modifications and does not require barrier functions to enforce bijectivity making it more robust to noise and simpler to implement.

title: Mapping, Untangling & Summarizing Surfaces
Multimodal Learning
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Modeling; Simulation

title: Pipeline Under the Hood
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Graphics Systems Architecture; Image Processing; Lighting; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering

title: PointLLM-R: Enhancing 3D Point Cloud Reasoning via Chain-of-Thought
speakers_or_contributors: Chaoqi Chen; Qile Xu; Wenjun Zhou; Hui Huang
description: PointLLM-R introduces chain-of-thought reasoning for 3D point cloud understanding via a data-centric pipeline. It builds a large reasoning dataset (PoCoTI) and trains a multimodal model that achieves state-of-the-art performance, improving accuracy, interpretability, and robustness across synthetic and real-world 3D tasks.

title: Reciprocal Latent Fields for Precomputed Sound Propagation
speakers_or_contributors: Hugo Seuté; Pranai Vasudev; Etienne Richan; Louis-Xavier Buffoni
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Simulation
description: Wave-coding methods for sound propagation in games are very memory-intensive. We present Reciprocal Latent Fields leveraging Riemannian metric learning to reduce memory usage by orders of magnitude. Our method infers relevant acoustic parameters nearly instantaneously and results in perceptually identical sound quality to the ground truth wave coding.

title: Untangling Surfaces via Shape and Mesh Repulsion
speakers_or_contributors: Jiří Minarčík; Michael Liu; Keenan Crane; Minchen Li
description: We present an energy-based framework for removing self-intersections from surface meshes. A shape-level Gaussian energy resolves global entanglement while a mesh-level Minkowski penalty handles local discrete degeneracies. Together, they eliminate all self-intersections across benchmark datasets, outperforming prior state-of-the-art methods.

title: VidSplat: Gaussian Splatting Reconstruction with Geometry-Guided Video Diffusion Priors
speakers_or_contributors: Jimin Tang; Wenyuan Zhang; Junsheng Zhou; Zian Huang; Kanle Shi; Shenkun Xu; Yu-Shen Liu; Zhizhong Han
affiliations: Kuaishou Technology; Tsinghua University; Wayne State University
description: We present VidSplat, a generative reconstruction framework that leverages powerful video diffusion priors to synthesize novel views that compensate for missing input coverage, and thereby recover complete 3D scenes from sparse inputs. VidSplat performs robustly to sparse input and even a single image.

title: Immediate 3D Gaussian Splat Reconstruction of Unordered Input with Global Consistency
speakers_or_contributors: Andreas Meuleman; Linus Franke; Boris Zhestiankin; Camille Montemagni; George Drettakis
affiliations: Inria Université Côte d'Azur; Inria, Université Côte d'Azur; Inria, Université de Rennes
description: We propose a 3D Gaussian Splatting reconstruction method with immediate feedback that handles unordered image captures and very large scenes. Through fast place-recognition-driven matching, cluster-based loop closure with graph-propagated correction, and a progressive Gaussian hierarchy construction, it achieves robust, scalable reconstruction at a fraction of offline processing times.

title: MAViD: A Multimodal Framework for Audio-Visual Dialogue Understanding and Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Youxin Pang; Jiajun Liu; Lingfeng Tan; Yong Zhang; Feng Gao; Xiang Deng; Zhuoliang Kang; Xiaoming Wei; Yebin Liu
description: MAViD is a multimodal framework for interactive audio-visual dialogue. Its Conductor-Creator architecture integrates multimodal understanding and reasoning into response generation. By combining AR and diffusion models, MAViD enables synchronized, long-duration audio-video generation with consistent identity and timbre, delivering vivid and contextually coherent digital human interactions.

title: Quasi-Medial Distance Field (Q-MDF): A Robust Method for Approximating and Discretizing Neural Medial Axes
speakers_or_contributors: Jiayi Kong; Chen Zong; Jun Luo; Shiqing Xin; Fei Hou; Hanqing Jiang; Chen Qian; Ying He
affiliations: Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Nanyang Technological University; S-Lab Nanyang Technological University; School of Computer Science and Technology Shandong University; SenseTime Research
description: We introduce an implicit method for medial axis extraction from point clouds and meshes, addressing challenges in noisy or incomplete data. By connecting the signed distance field and the medial field, we reformulate the problem as implicit reconstruction, offering a novel, robust solution for accurate medial axis computation.

title: Stranger Things: Season 5 - Optimising Workflows Through Advanced Optical Flow And Automated Lighting
speakers_or_contributors: Masahiro Teraoka; Indah Maretha
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Image Processing; Lighting
description: We present Thistle (an optical flow pipeline) and Marama (an AI-driven automated lighting optimization framework)—two tools enabling scalable, consistent, and high-quality integration of CG characters into live-action plates under demanding production conditions. This presentation details their technical design, pipeline integration, and measured impact in large-scale VFX production.

title: Audio-Omni: Extending Multi-modal Understanding to Versatile Audio Generation and Editing
speakers_or_contributors: Zeyue Tian; Binxin Yang; Zhaoyang Liu; Jiexuan Zhang; Ruibin Yuan; Hubery Yin; Qifeng Chen; Chen Li; Jing Lyu; Wei Xue; Yike Guo
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Peking University; Tencent
description: Audio-Omni is the first unified framework for multi-modal audio generation and editing across sound, music, and speech. By combining a frozen multimodal language model with a trainable diffusion transformer, it achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmarks while exhibiting remarkable inherited capabilities.

title: MoonSplat: Monocular Online Gaussian Splatting with Sim(3) Global Optimization
speakers_or_contributors: Guo Pu; Yixuan Han; Haofeng Li; Yao Zhang; Hui Zhou; Zhouhui Lian
affiliations: Beijing Hydrogen Intelligent Tech. Co., Ltd.; Peking University
description: MoonSplat is a robust and efficient online voxelized 3DGS reconstruction framework integrated with global Sim(3) optimization, which enables reliable camera tracking and efficient global loop closure for both camera poses and voxelized 3DGS. MoonSplat is equipped with a color residual learning strategy for real-time online optimization of voxelized 3DGS.

title: Structural MAT: Clean and Scalable Medial Axis Simplification via Explicit Surface Correspondence
speakers_or_contributors: Pengfei Wang; Shuangmin Chen; Dongming Yan; Ying He; Shiqing Xin; Changhe Tu; Wenping Wang
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Nanyang Technological University; Qingdao University of Science and Technology; School of Computer Science and Technology Shandong University; Shandong University; Texas A&M University
description: We present Structural MAT, a fast and scalable medial axis simplification framework that explicitly tracks the correspondence between medial vertices and surface regions throughout simplification. The method produces clean, structure-aware medial axes with accurate feature alignment and high mesh quality for both organic shapes and CAD models.

title: Just-Dub-It: Video dubbing via Joint Audio-Visual Diffusion
speakers_or_contributors: Anthony Chen; Naomi Ken Korem; Tavi Halperin; Matan Ben Yosef; Urska Jelercic; Ofir Bibi; Or Patashnik; Daniel Cohen-Or
affiliations: Lightircks; Lightricks; Peking University; Tel Aviv University
description: JUST-DUB-IT is a unified audio-video diffusion model for automatic video dubbing. It jointly synthesizes translated speech and synchronized lip movements via a lightweight LoRA adapter, replacing traditional pipeline approaches. Trained on synthetically generated multilingual data with strategic inpainting, it achieves superior visual quality and lip sync across varied motion scenarios.

title: Persistence-guided Prescribed Topological Simplification
speakers_or_contributors: Linxuan Rong; Tao Ju
description: We developed a new algorithm to simplify the topology of a 3D shape to user-prescribed numbers of connected components, handles, and voids, while minimizing geometric changes.

title: RaDe-GS: Rasterizing Depth in Gaussian Splatting
speakers_or_contributors: Baowen Zhang; Chuan Fang; Rakesh Shrestha; Yixun Liang; xiaoxiao Long; Ping Tan
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Nanjing University; Simon Fraser University
description: Gaussian Splatting enables high-quality, real-time novel-view synthesis, but extracting detailed 3D geometry remains challenging. We introduce a rasterized approach to render the depth and normal maps for general 3D Gaussian primitives. Our method significantly enhances shape reconstruction accuracy while maintaining the computational efficiency of Gaussian Splatting.

title: The Proactive Pipeline: Liquid Production Metadata for CG Pipelines
speakers_or_contributors: Alok Gandhi
description: The Proactive Pipeline introduces two production-proven data-modeling innovations for CG pipelines: schema-flexible production metadata using JSONB in a relational database, and live dependency graphs projected from relational state. Deployed across productions, an event backbone delivers reliable real-time data to artists and workflows, eliminating refresh-and-wait cycles and achieving 20× lower latency.

title: VfxDB: A Visual Effects Volume Dataset and Benchmark for VDB-Native Generative Modeling
speakers_or_contributors: Junwei Shu; Hantang Liu; Dawei Miao; Wenzheng Song; Mingyang Yuan; Wenjie Liu; Changgu Chen; Yang Li; Changbo Wang
affiliations: East China Normal University; Independent Researcher; KunByte
description: VfxDB introduces a pioneering 1-million-sample dataset tailored for VDB-native generation of sparse volumetric VFX sequences, alongside a reproducible benchmark suite. Validated by a scalable diffusion baseline featuring our Atomic-Continuous prior, this comprehensive pipeline establishes essential infrastructure for future 3D volume generative modeling.
