title: Pashboard: Modernizing Rez for Animation
speakers_or_contributors: Ryan Bland; Jacob Melvin; Chethna Kabeerdoss
description: Netflix Animation Studios presents Pashboard, a cloud-native evolution of the Rez package manager. By integrating with Netflix’s streaming infrastructure and zero-trust security, Pashboard solves the latency and access hurdles of traditional on-premise pipelines. This novel system enables a high-performance, globally distributed software workflow for artists and developers across six continents.

title: Technical Papers Closing Session
Frontier Talk: UNIX V4 and the Utah Teapot
speakers_or_contributors: Thalia Archibald
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: We recently recovered UNIX V4 from a 1974 magnetic tape at the University of Utah. This version of the UNIX operating system, thought to have been lost, was the 19th copy distributed to the public, just months after the first public announcement. It was originally acquired by Martin Newell while managing the computer graphics laboratory, and it was likely connected to his foundational research in procedural modeling and the famous Utah teapot. This talk shows the unique insight this artifact provides into the dawn of computer graphics and the history of UNIX.

title: Women of SIGGRAPH Conversations (WOSC): Meet and Greet
speakers_or_contributors: Mikki Rose; Mikki Rose; Shanna Alcide
affiliations: Conference Advisory Group, SIGGRAPH; Guru Studio
keywords_tags: Diversity, Equity and Inclusion; Ethics and Society; Industry Insight; Networking
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Come meet the WOSC team and share your thoughts on what we should focus on in the coming year! We want to hear from you on the types of events, topics, speakers, and types of community engagement we can help build within the SIGGRAPH community.

title: Blazing a New Trail of Curls
speakers_or_contributors: Jacob Brooks; David Eberle; Alan Browning
description: For Disney/Pixar’s Toy Story 5 character Blaze, we developed a grooming structure for tightly coiled curls, enabling rapid artistic iteration. We deployed Fizt Strands, built on our Fizt cloth solver, offering superior hair collision and stability. These advancements enabled believable curl simulation, complimenting the character’s ethnicity and personality.

title: Constant Mean Curvature Surfaces from Discrete Harmonic Maps
speakers_or_contributors: Yousuf Soliman; Peter Schröder; Ulrich Pinkall
affiliations: Side Effects Software Inc; Technical University of Berlin; University of Bonn
description: We present a simple discretization of constant mean curvature surfaces based on the classical observation that their Gauss maps are harmonic. Our construction is elementary---requiring only discrete Dirichlet energy minimization and a Poisson solve---yet it exactly mirrors this aspect of the smooth theory.

title: Divide and Truncate: A Penetration and Inversion Free Framework for Coupled Multi-physics System
speakers_or_contributors: Anka H. Chen; Jerry Hsu; Youssef Ayman; Miles Macklin
affiliations: NVIDIA; The American University in Cairo; ZeroMatter
description: Divide and Truncate (DAT) is a unified framework for penetration-free coupling of multi-physics systems, including rigid bodies, volumetric soft bodies, thin shells, rods, and animated objects. DAT eliminates artificial damping and deadlock issues. Material- and solver-agnostic, DAT plugs into any iterative optimizer as a post-processing step.

title: Feathers, Scales and Curls
keywords_tags: Animation; Dynamics; Geometry; Industry Insight; Modeling; Simulation

title: From Human Vision to Machine Vision
speakers_or_contributors: Kenneth Chen; Niall Williams; Colin Groth; Jenna Kang; Alexandre Chapiro; Qi Sun; Aaron Hertzmann; Laurie Wilcox; Richard Zhang; Rafał Mantiuk
affiliations: Adobe Research; Barnard University; New York University; Reality Labs Research, Meta; University of Cambridge; York University
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Computer Vision; Display; Image Processing; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
description:
Visual computing encompasses the full range of technologies and methodologies involved in rendering, displaying, reconstructing, modeling, and recording visual data. Among the many subfields within visual computing, there has been a growing interest in two subfields in particular: human vision and machine vision. Human vision research focuses on understanding how human vision and cognition functions and using that knowledge to improve the user experience through realism, immersion, and responsiveness. In contrast, machine vision research focuses on building computational systems that complete a visual perception task, and may aim to mimic the structure or functionality of biological vision systems.

This workshop aims to facilitate dialogue and collaboration between researchers and practitioners in machine and human vision. By exploring common challenges, methodologies, and philosophical approaches, ranging from perceptually grounded models to data-driven deep learning frameworks, we hope to spark interdisciplinary discussion that drives innovation across the broader field of visual computing.

title: Frontiers Workshop: Dronevision, Holodecks, and Spatial Computing Using Swarms of Flying Light Specks
speakers_or_contributors: Shahram Ghandeharizadeh; Shahram Ghandeharizadeh; Hamed Alimohammadzadeh; Shuqin Zhu; Jernej Barbic; Heather Culbertson
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Digital Twins; Display; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Haptics; Hardware; Networking; Performance; Physical AI; Real-Time; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This timely SIGGRAPH 2026 Frontiers workshop explores Flying Light Specks (FLSs), including their applications, current capabilities, and future research directions. An FLS is a miniature drone equipped with a lighting apparatus that serves as a building block for next-generation display systems. These systems range from desktop Dronevision platforms to room-scale holodecks and outdoor spatial-computing environments. Swarms of FLSs can illuminate graphics and animations visible to the naked eye, enabling users to interact with them using their bare hands. Topics include the classification of FLS devices with LightBender as a representative example, end-to-end architectures spanning content generation, illumination, and bare-hand interaction.

For more information visit: https://www.flsworkshop.com

title: Geospatial and 3D Tiles
speakers_or_contributors: Sean Lilley; Tamarat Belayneh
keywords_tags: Graphics Systems Architecture; Pipeline Tools and Work; Scientific Visualization; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In this session we'll cover how glTF is being used for runtime-efficient streaming of 3D geospatial data and digital twins. This includes a wide range of content types like Gaussian splats, point clouds, vector data, volumetric data, and AEC/CAD/BIM. We'll also look at glTF 2.1's new complex scenes capabilities and how this scales to massive datasets.

title: How to Get Your Resume & Reel Noticed
speakers_or_contributors: Craig Slagel; Shannon Wiggins; Mikki Rose; Gracie Arenas Strittmatter; AJ Christensen; Craig Slagel
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH; Electronic Arts; Lucasfilm / ILM; NASA Scientific Visualization Studio; Skydance Animation
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Education; Games; Lighting; Modeling; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The ACM SIGGRAPH Early Career Development Committee’s “Resume and Reel Review” program has long offered students and early career professionals the chance to have their work reviewed by industry experts at SIGGRAPH conferences.

This session will feature a panel of industry professionals who will discuss and review a selection from this year’s program live. This unique opportunity allows attendees to see real-time reviews, learn what makes a great resume and reel, and ask questions directly to experts.

Register for a one-on-one session at https://ecdc.siggraph.org.

title: Inverse Rendering
Inverse Rendering for Discrete X-Ray Computed Tomography
speakers_or_contributors: Lovro Nuic; Ziyi Zhang; Korbinian Sager; Wenzel Jakob
affiliations: Carl Zeiss AG; Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne; Google
description: We propose a gradient-based discrete tomography method that models each voxel as a probability distribution over known materials, minimizing an object-space loss for projection consistency. Inspired by inverse rendering, it outperforms classical methods, supports scattering, and excels in sparse and limited-angle scenarios.

title: Lifting Lines and Tone: Image-Space Stylization in Path-Space
speakers_or_contributors: Rex West; Sayan Mukherjee; Yonghao Yue
affiliations: Aoyama Gakuin University; The University of Tokyo
description: We present a framework for lifting image-space stylizations into path-space rendering, and demonstrate it on line and tone rendering. By constructing geometric mappings between image and path space, our method preserves line and tone structure under complex light transport, enabling coherent, expressive non-photorealistic rendering within the stylized rendering equation.

title: NPR, Style & Abstraction
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Fabrication; Generative AI; Modeling; Rendering

title: NVIDIA | The Making of The Great Galactic War: Directing Generative Video in 3D
speakers_or_contributors: Dade Orgeron; Pinar Seyhan Demirdağ
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
The Great Galactic War, a short-form streaming series from Nick Montori, Co-Founder and Creative Director of AI production house Anima Studios, has surpassed 120 million views. This session shows how a small team created a consistent, cinematic world at streaming scale, and the virtual cinematography tool that made it possible.
Like many teams working with generative video, Anima faced a core challenge: control and consistency. Foundation models can produce striking frames, but camera movement, composition, lighting, and scene continuity can shift unpredictably between renders, making it difficult to direct a coherent series.
Using real production footage, Pinar Seyhan Demirdağ and Dade Orgeron will show how the team used EVRN, a 3D directing layer for generative video, to solve that problem. They will demonstrate how a flat image becomes an editable 3D scene where characters, props, and environments become independent objects with accurate spatial relationships. The team could then place cameras, light scenes, compose shots, and lock layouts before sending them to the generative model, replacing prompt-driven guesswork with deterministic references for repeatable renders.
Attendees will learn how spatial direction fits into existing AI production pipelines and preview EVRN’s upcoming support for USD, GLB, OBJ, Unreal Engine, Unity, Blender, and Houdini.

title: NVIDIA: Advanced Real-Time Volume Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Stefan Zellmann; Alper Sahistan; Ingo Wald
affiliations: NVIDIA; University of Cologne; University of Utah
description: This course teaches attendees how to write sci-vis volume renderers on modern architectures. Since the last course at Eurographics 2006 the hardware and algorithmic landscape have changed tremendously. We show how this has influenced the state-of-the-art in real-time volume graphics in the last years.

title: Preventing Collision
Quads & Singularities
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Fabrication; Geometry; Modeling

title: Abstraction in Style: Beyond Texture and Color
speakers_or_contributors: Min Lu; Yuanfeng He; Anthony Chen; Jianhuang He; Pu Wang; Daniel Cohen-Or; Hui Huang
affiliations: Peking University; Shenzhen University; Tel Aviv University
description: Artistic styles often involve shape reinterpretation, not merely a change of surface appearance. Conventional style transfer methods miss this deeper abstraction behavior. We introduce Abstraction in Style (AiS), a two-stage generative framework that learns and transfers style at shape level, beyond texture and colors, enabling more expressive and controllable stylization.

title: Ambient-robust Inverse Rendering using Robot-assisted RGB-NIR Imaging
speakers_or_contributors: Hoon-Gyu Chung; Jinnyeong Kim; Hyunwoo Kang; Seung-Hwan Baek
description: This paper presents an ambient-robust inverse rendering method using robot-assisted RGB–NIR imaging. By combining multi-view RGB images with NIR flash images, we reconstruct accurate geometry and reflectance under multiple lighting conditions. We also introduce a robot-assisted imaging system and multi-view RGB–NIR dataset collected across multiple ambient illumination settings.

title: Robust and Efficient Penetration-Free Elastodynamics without Barriers
speakers_or_contributors: Juntian Zheng; Zhaofeng Luo; Minchen Li
description: We introduce a barrier-free optimization framework for nonpenetration elastodynamic simulation using a smooth augmented Lagrangian formulation and efficient active-set exploration. Unlike IPC barriers, our method avoids TOI locking, maintains a compact anticipatory constraint set, and delivers faster, robust, visually faithful penetration-free simulation for demanding contact-rich applications.

title: Synchronizing Fields with Singularities
speakers_or_contributors: Natalia Pacheco-Tallaj; Mattéo Couplet; Edward Chien; David Palmer
affiliations: Boston University; Institute of Science and Technology Austria (ISTA); Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
description: We cast a variety of field-processing problems into the language of synchronization, unlocking a unified algorithm based on semidefinite relaxation. Our method produces better optima, enables new boundary conditions, and comes with a geometric interpretation in terms of lifting topological defects (singularities).

title: EAG-PT: Emission-Aware Gaussians and Path Tracing for Diffuse Indoor Scene Reconstruction and Editing
speakers_or_contributors: Xijie Yang; Mulin Yu; Changjian Jiang; Kerui Ren; Tao Lu; Jiangmiao Pang; Dahua Lin; Bo Dai; Linning Xu
affiliations: Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory; Shanghai Jiao Tong University; The Chinese University of Hong Kong; The University of Hong Kong; Zhejiang University
description: EAG-PT reconstructs indoor scenes with 2D Gaussians for editable diffuse global illumination. By separating emissive and non-emissive components and combining efficient reconstruction with path tracing, it enables more natural, physically consistent scene editing than radiance-field methods, while avoiding the geometry limitations of mesh-based inverse rendering.

title: Global Discrete Optimization for Quad-Dominant Mesh Reduction
speakers_or_contributors: Yuzhe Luo; Jingchen Gao; Zherong Pan; Kui Wu; Xuebo Ji; Xiaogang Jin; Xifeng Gao
affiliations: Hangzhou Dianzi University; LIGHTSPEED; LightSpeed Studios; State Key Laboratory of CAD&CG Zhejiang University; The University of Hong Kong; Zhejiang University
description: We propose a global optimization framework for simplifying quad-dominant meshes used in game and animation production. By formulating edge selection as an Integer Linear Programming problem with partial poly-chord segmentation and curvature-aware constraints, our method preserves clean edge flows and quad topology far better than existing greedy approaches.

title: High-Order Continuous Geometrical Validity
speakers_or_contributors: Federico Sichetti; Zizhou Huang; Marco Attene; Denis Zorin; Enrico Puppo; Daniele Panozzo
affiliations: IMATI CNR Genova; New York University; Università di Genova
description: We propose a conservative algorithm to test the geometrical validity of polynomial finite elements as they deform linearly in time. In elastodynamic simulation, our algorithm guarantees that the system remains physically valid during the entire trajectory, not only at discrete time steps, even when implemented using floating point arithmetic.

title: Practical Methods for Kinky-Coily Hair in Animation Production
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Davison; Guillaume Pernin; Curtis Andrus
description: The talk presents fast, modular approximations of Curly‑Cue’s methods for kinky‑coily hair—switchbacks, phase locking, and period skipping—integrated into Netflix Animation Studio’s Houdini-based grooming pipeline, prioritizing artistic control, simplicity, and interactivity over strict physical accuracy while achieving natural-looking hairstyles.

title: Progressive Photorealistic Simplification
speakers_or_contributors: Adi Rosenthal; Dana Berman; Yedid Hoshen; Ariel Shamir
affiliations: Google; Hebrew University of Jerusalem; Reichman University
description: We introduce a method for simplifying photorealistic scenes by progressively removing elements based on semantic importance. Our framework systematically removes elements in a hierarchical order, using generative inpainting to maintain photorealism throughout the process. This generates plausible photograph sequences, enabling applications like content-aware decluttering, layer decomposition, and interactive editing.

title: Floating-Point Robustness in Parametric Surface Continuous Collision Detection: From Algorithm to Benchmarking
speakers_or_contributors: Xuwen Chen; Junyu Wang; Cheng Yu; Xingyu Ni; Meng Zhang; Bin Wang; Mengyu Chu; Baoquan Chen
affiliations: Independent; Nanjing University of Science and Technology; Peking University; SIST, Peking University; Southeast University; The University of Hong Kong
description: We present solutions to the floating-point–induced decision errors arising in CCD for parametric surfaces, including an error-resistant CCD method and a data construction pipeline with known GT for algorithm evaluation under floating-point perturbations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed methods are effective and robust.

title: GimmBO: Interactive Generative Image Model Merging via Bayesian Optimization
speakers_or_contributors: Chenxi Liu; Selena Ling; Alec Jacobson
description: GimmBO helps users interactively explore combinations of customized image generation styles to create desired results. It replaces manual slider tuning with a tailored Preferential Bayesian Optimization approach, outperforming alternatives in simulations and user studies. It further extends to combining content and scaling to large model collections by integrating model retrieval.

title: Radiance Caching for Differentiable Path Tracing
speakers_or_contributors: Ziyi Zhang; Delio Vicini; Sebastian Winberg; Stephan Garbin; Wenzel Jakob
affiliations: Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne; Google
description:
We introduce a spatially blended radiance-caching framework for differentiable path tracing that improves speed and robustness in material recovery under unknown lighting.
By jointly optimizing cache, materials, and blending, the method produces physically meaningful materials that generalize to new lighting conditions.

title: SQuadGen: Generating Simple Quad Layouts via Chart Distance Fields
speakers_or_contributors: You-Kang Kong; Yang Liu; Yue Dong; Xin Tong; Heung-Yeung Shum
affiliations: Anuttacon; International Digital Economy Academy; Microsoft Research Asia; Tsinghua University
description: SQuadGen introduces a diffusion-based approach to simple quad layout generation based on Chart Distance Fields, a continuous representation that encodes discrete quad structures on surfaces. This formulation avoids direct mesh connectivity prediction and enables the synthesis of simple, artist-friendly quad layouts that consistently outperform existing automatic retopology methods.

title: Trial by Fire: The VFX of The Lost Bus
speakers_or_contributors: Zoe Cranley; Phil Bonner
description:
In this Production Session, beloFX takes audiences inside the making of the Oscar-nominated visual effects of The Lost Bus, where real-time workflows, large-scale simulation, and cinematic storytelling came together.

Built around director Paul Greengrass’ visceral handheld style, the film demanded an immersive and grounded approach to recreating California’s devastating 2018 Camp Fire. Using Unreal Engine, the team developed a large-scale “digital twin” of Northern California for previs, virtual scouting, and production planning, while game-inspired workflows powered everything from dense traffic simulations to reactive handheld camera movement.

The session will explore how these real-time systems helped the production rapidly adapt when several sequences unexpectedly shifted into full CG midway through filming due to real world wildfires. Attendees will gain insight into scalable previs, resilient pipelines, and the evolving role of game-engine technology in modern VFX filmmaking.

title: AGIPC: Adaptive In-Solve Algebraic Coarsening for GPU IPC
speakers_or_contributors: Xuan Wang; Zhaofeng Luo; Minchen Li; Taku Komura; Kemeng Huang
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; The University of Hong Kong; The University of Hong Kong (HKU)
description: We propose a GPU-friendly adaptive algebraic coarsening method guided by Green strain increments and combined with affine embedding to avoid topological changes and irregular memory access, yielding the first fully GPU-optimized adaptive IPC solver with up to 3× speedup over state-of-the-art GPU IPC while preserving identical visuals.

title: Creating the scales for Gary De'Snake in Disney's "Zootopia 2"
speakers_or_contributors: Jennifer Stratton; Jesse Erickson; Andy Milne; Brian Missey; Jose Velasquez
description: Capturing the look and performance of Gary De’Snake’s scales in an appealing way was a key challenge in Disney’s “Zootopia 2”. New procedural workflows for creating and deforming scales made it possible for artists to efficiently achieve the desired look/motion while allowing for specific art-direction and exaggerated animation performance.

title: Sample Matching for Joint Extinction Gradient Estimation in Differentiable Volume Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Ruihan Yu; Yu-Chen Wang; Jingwang Ling; Feng Xu; Shuang Zhao
affiliations: The University of Tokyo; Tsinghua University; University of California Irvine; University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
description: We propose sample matching, a variance reduction principle for differentiable volume rendering. By jointly estimating scattering and transmittance gradient components at shared sample positions, our method reduces gradient variance by up to 80%, significantly improving convergence speed and reconstruction quality in volumetric inverse rendering.

title: Semantic-Structural Alignment for Generative Pictorial Charts
speakers_or_contributors: Zhida Sun; Yulin Zhang; Zheng Gu; Min Lu; Bongshin Lee; Daniel Cohen-Or; Hui Huang
affiliations: Shenzhen University; Tel Aviv University; Yonsei University
description: We introduce a generative framework that transforms traditional statistical graphics into memorable, engaging pictorial charts. It utilizes structural alignment to anchor spatial layouts and semantic alignment to transfer expressive textures. This ensures the resulting visuals are artistically compelling and structurally consistent, advancing data-driven visual storytelling.

title: Surface Power Diagrams for Knit Singularity Placement
speakers_or_contributors: Rahul Mitra; Mattéo Couplet; Ruichen Liu; Jonathan Ng; Ruza Markov; William Batara Jeremiah Samosir; Megan Hofmann; Edward Chien
description: We present an algorithm for knit singularity placement that leverages a generalization of power diagrams to surfaces. By optimizing singularity positions in a global fashion, we achieve faster and more optimal placement, allowing for denser knit graphs. Our framework robustly produces helix-free knit graphs with a variety of user constraints.

title: Gradient Descent in the ALPS: Abstracted Low-Poly Stylization and Fabrication
speakers_or_contributors: Ruben Wiersma; Alexandre Binninger; Peizhuo Li; Annika Oehri; Aviv Segall; Tanguy Magne; Danielle Luterbacher; Marcel Padilla; Jing Ren; Olga Sorkine-Hornung
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Fabrication; Generative AI; Modeling
description: We generate 2D low-poly meshes that abstract images using score distillation sampling. We focus on ensuring the validity of the output mesh, quantized palette colors and semantic similarity to the input. The resulting vector images are easy to edit and fabricated as mosaics, embroidery, crocheting, patchwork and stencils.

title: Learning Sparse Singularities for Cross Field Design
speakers_or_contributors: Xiaohu Zhang; Hujun Bao; Jin Huang
description: Problem-aware quad mesh design is challenging due to the choice of neural representation. We propose a two-stage strategy: we first learn sparse singularities, follwed by an analytical step to convert the singularities into cross field and the final quad. Thanks to the hybrid neural-analytical strategy, we can achieve a problem-aware quad style, which is beneficial to downstream animation or simulation.

title: Robust Computation of Boundary Path Integrals Using Kernel-Density Estimation
speakers_or_contributors: Peiyu Xu; Lifan Wu; Benedikt Bitterli; Ravi Ramamoorthi; Shuang Zhao
affiliations: NVIDIA; University of California San Diego; University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
description: We present a simple, robust, and consistent estimator for boundary path integrals in physics-based differentiable rendering. By reformulating boundary integration with kernel-density estimation, our method avoids fragile measure-zero sampling and costly reparameterization. It matches finite-difference derivatives and outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in synthetic inverse-rendering benchmarks with improved efficiency and lower variance.

title: YASPS: A Symbolic Framework for Extensible, High-Performance IPC Simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Xuan Tang; Kemeng Huang; Gilbert Bernstein; Minchen Li; Tzumao Li
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; The University of Hong Kong; University of California San Diego; University of Washington
description: YASPS is a programming system for automatically computing derivatives of your physical simulation energy from symbolic definitions, specializing to contact-rich scenarios. YASPS captures the structure of the simulation data, parameterization, and sparsity, and produces modular and efficient GPU code with performance on par with manually written code.

title: 3D Assets: New features, Tooling, and What Comes Next
speakers_or_contributors: Ken Jakubzak; Henrik Edstrom
affiliations: Bentley / Metaverse Standards Forum; Institution Jason Moore Productions / The MetaMovie
keywords_tags: Animation; Digital Twins; Games; Graphics Systems Architecture; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Interoperable assets are foundational to spatial computing, whether it be representing people, places, or things. This BoF takes a look at some of the interoperable asset work that's gone on between various SDOs in the last year; their ratification status, real-world implementations, available tooling, and sample assets. These are standards that are continuing their development into the next year; this is your opportunity to shape the directions of the standards.

registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience

This is an informal meet-up for Gov/DoD/Mil artists and storytellers to discuss common challenges in our unique work environments.

The target audience is individuals with .mil, .gov email addresses.

title: Art Gallery
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery explores computer graphics and interactive techniques as languages and materials to express artistic visions and experiences. The theme “In-Betweens” highlights the interactions that the twelve installations establish with space, time, and generations. Across media and approaches, including screens, projections, installations, sculptures, retro devices, wearables, bio art, data visualization art, robotics, and space art, the Art Gallery brings together creators whose works address large-scale questions and humanitarian concerns; investigate foundational components such as algorithmic methods and computing hardware; expand how we perceive an increasingly interconnected world; and re-examine and speculate about our place within the layered infrastructures of contemporary technology.

title: Chaos | Ray-Traced Gaussian Splats in Production: The June July Pipeline
speakers_or_contributors: Chris Nichols
keywords_tags: Animation; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Building on the VidViz workflow introduced at SIGGRAPH 2025, this talk documents the next phase of the June July virtual production pipeline. We describe how VidViz planning tools informed the live-action shoot, and how captured location data was transformed into an HDR Gaussian splat using a combination of the XGrids scanner and the Arri Alexa 35. We detail the process of integrating that asset into Chaos Arena and the advantages of working with ray-traced splats in a flexible, real-time stage environment, including lighting evaluation, camera planning, and interactive iteration before a single frame of principal photography is committed.

title: Edges of Immersion: Data Translation into Ecoaesthetic Experience
speakers_or_contributors: Rasa Smite; Raitis Smits
affiliations: Art Academy of Latvia; ZHdK Zurich University of the Arts
keywords_tags: Art; Audio; Capture/Scanning; Scientific Visualization; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Climate knowledge is increasingly shaped by complex data that remain experientially distant. This paper examines how immersive ecoaesthetic practices translate environmental data into situated sensory experience. Through a case study, it introduces edges of immersion, showing how artistic strategies make mediation perceptible and enable new ways of engaging with climate imaginaries.

registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
EgoForce: Forearm-Guided Camera-Space 3D Hand Pose from a Monocular Egocentric Camera

EgoForce enables real-time 3D hand pose, shape, and absolute position recovery from a single head-mounted RGB camera. Designed for lightweight smart glasses, it uses forearm cues and camera-aware ray-space lifting to provide precise tracking across diverse camera models and device setups.

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | OpenUSD for AI Agents, Model-Driven Workflows, and 3D Graphics Pipelines
speakers_or_contributors: Aaron Luk
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Graphics Systems Architecture; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: OpenUSD is becoming a foundation for how developers build, connect, and validate 3D graphics pipelines for physical AI. As AI agents and open models begin helping teams create simulation environments, developers need scene data they can direct, inspect, and trust. This session covers how the latest OpenUSD standard work supports modular, interoperable 3D worlds, and how NVIDIA is using OpenUSD across agentic physical AI workflows.

title: Pioneers Featured Speaker - Don Greenberg
speakers_or_contributors: Edward Kramer; Don Greenberg
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Education; Ethics and Society; Games; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Industry Insight; Pioneers; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Computer Graphics legend Don Greenberg is the ACM/SIGGRAPH Pioneers Featured Speaker for 2026! Greenberg's astonishingly multi-disciplinary career started in architecture, and has spanned the computer graphics revolution from its genesis in the 1960s. He has taught in the fields of architecture, engineering and computer science, while also being an elite athlete and life-long advisor to the tennis and soccer teams at Cornell. Greenberg’s teaching has covered computer-aided design in architecture as well as computer animation in the Department of Art, and he has simultaneously taught courses in computer science and technology strategy. Greenberg received the 1987 ACM SIGGRAPH Steven A. Coons Award for Outstanding Creative Contributions to Computer Graphics, its highest honor, and the 1989 NCGA Academic Award, the highest educational award given by the National Computer Graphics Association. He is a member of the National Academy of Engineering; Founding Fellow, American Institute of Medical and Biological Engineering; Fellow, Association for Computer Machinery; Fellow, American Association for the Advancement of Science; and the founding director of the 11-year National Science and Technology Center for Computer Graphics and Scientific Visualization.

Don will be sharing his thoughts on THE FUTURE OF SIGGRAPH, in what is sure to be one of the most memorable, pivotal and inspirational keynote conversations in our organization's history. You do not want to miss this!

Please note: Due to a format change, this session will be delivered via live stream for the onsite audience. The talk will be followed by a moderated Q&A.

speakers_or_contributors: Joe D'Amato; Jeff Rochlin
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Hardware; Industry Insight
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This is an annual discussion of Large-Scale Rendering. With render farm infrastructure, both on-premise and hybrid cloud solutions have software, queue management, reliability, and performance woes. Industry experts and new entrants are all welcomed, for a sharing of knowledge, experience, and best practices. Mail joe@jdamato.com for any questions or comments. This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the VFX Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud Native, Pipeline, Studio Storage, and Agentic Workflows

speakers_or_contributors: Nia Bickford; Chris Hebert

keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Education; Performance; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | Building SimReady 3D Worlds for Physical AI
speakers_or_contributors: Jonathan Stephens; Steven Ren
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Physical AI; Rendering; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Physical AI needs more than realistic 3D graphics. Developers need simulation-ready 3D worlds with the semantics, scale, materials, physics, and validation required for robotics, AI training, and world model development. This session will explore how teams can use SimReady and OpenUSD-based assets to prepare 3D worlds for downstream physical AI workflows.

title: Nebius | World Models: Where Games, VFX, and AI Collide
speakers_or_contributors: Jeff Kember; Josh Liss; Mala Tejwani
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: World models can already simulate environments in real time, making them playable, editable, and generated on demand. Are they the next game engine, the future of VFX, or a new medium that encompasses both? Join the COO of World Labs, NVIDIA's creative technology lead, and the Head Of AI Media at Nebius for a straight-talk look at what's working now, what's still hype, and what studios and toolmakers need to prepare for.

title: 3D Generation
Autodesk Vision Series | How to give soul to a faceless alien, question? Animating Rocky in Project Hail Mary with Framestore
speakers_or_contributors: Aulo Licinio; Bob Winter
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
At the core of Project Hail Mary is an unlikely friendship between an astronaut, Grace, and a faceless alien, Rocky. In this presentation, VFX Supervisor Bob Winter and Animation Supervisor Aulo Licinio break down Framestore’s collaboration with directors Phil Lord and Chris Miller to deliver the emotional heartbeat of the story.

Bringing Rocky to life meant inventing a completely unique physical language for a creature with no eyes, no face, and no human expressions. The team will detail how they navigated the unique challenges of controlling a five-legged anatomy, turning strict biological constraints into endearing character quirks.

Discover how Framestore blended cutting-edge keyframe animation with physical on-set puppetry to build a tactile, believable connection between the two leads, and see if you can spot where the puppet ends and the digital animation begins.

title: Chasing De’Snake! - Developing the Worlds and Characters of Disney’s “Zootopia 2” - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: Controllable Texture Tiling via Diffusion Transformers with Transformed Rotary Embeddings
speakers_or_contributors: Junrong Huang; Zhiyuan Zhang; Rui Tang; Hongbo Fu; Jing Liao
affiliations: City University of Hong Kong; HKUST; Manycore Tech Inc.
description: We introduce a novel Diffusion Transformer framework for high-fidelity, controllable texture tiling. By leveraging Coordinate-Transformed Rotary Embeddings, our method enables precise manipulation of texture frequency, scale, and orientation. It seamlessly integrates reference patterns into images while perfectly preserving the original scene's lighting and geometry.

affiliations: National Chiao Tung University; national yang ming chiao tung university
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This work presents Gesture Flux, an immersive performance system integrating mixed-reality gesture recognition, Budaixi (traditional Taiwanese glove puppetry), and contemporary dance. Through three interaction modes, hidden puppeteering gestures are transformed from direct articulation to perceptual extension and energetic radiation, visualized as dynamic traces and extended into full-body performance, proposing a cross-disciplinary approach to cultural reinterpretation and embodied interaction.

title: In Conversation with Brad Bird
speakers_or_contributors: Brad Bird; Steven Argula
description:
Join two-time Academy Award-winning filmmaker Brad Bird (“The Incredibles,” “Ratatouille”, “The Iron Giant”) for an exclusive SIGGRAPH conversation about “RAY GUNN,” his highly anticipated animated detective, neo-noir coming to Netflix.

Set in Metropia—a dazzling retro-futuristic metropolis imagined as the future through the lens of 1939—RAY GUNN follows Raymond Gunn, the last human private detective, as he becomes entangled in a mystery involving aliens, murder, and a celebrity whose fame may conceal dangerous secrets. Described by Bird as “”The Maltese Falcon” meets “Buck Rogers,”” the film blends classic detective storytelling with expansive science-fiction world-building.

For decades, Bird has pushed the boundaries of what animation can achieve, creating richly realized worlds that are as emotionally engaging as they are visually groundbreaking. With “RAY GUNN,” he brings that signature style to an ambitious new universe, combining noir atmosphere, Art Deco-inspired design, and cutting-edge animation to create a world unlike anything seen on screen before.
Featuring never-before-seen insights into “RAY GUNN”'s development and production, this session offers a rare opportunity to hear one of animation’s most influential storytellers discuss the art, technology, and creative vision behind his latest cinematic achievement.

title: MTPano: Multi-Task Panoramic Scene Understanding via Label-Free Integration of Dense Prediction Priors
speakers_or_contributors: Jingdong Zhang; Xiaohang Zhan; Lingzhi Zhang; Yizhou Wang; Zhengming Yu; Jionghao Wang; Wenping Wang; Xin Li
affiliations: Adobe; Northeastern University; Texas A&M University
description: MTPano is a robust multi-task foundation model for panoramic scene understanding. Trained via a label-free pipeline using perspective dense priors, it adopts a dual-stream architecture that resolves feature conflicts between depth, segmentation, and surface normal estimations, achieving state-of-the-art performance on spherical images.

title: Materials & Textures
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Modeling; Real-Time; Rendering

title: Nexus: Native Mesh Generation with Diffusion
speakers_or_contributors: Hanxiao Wang; Ying-Tian Liu; Yuan-Chen Guo; Qi-Yuan Feng; Zi-Xin Zou; Ding Liang; Biao Zhang; Yan-Pei Cao
affiliations: CS Dept, Tsinghua University; Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences; VAST; Xi'an Jiaotong University
description: Nexus is a revolutionary two-stage diffusion framework for generating high-fidelity 3D polygon meshes. By replacing slow sequential modeling with geometry structure and topology diffusion, Nexus effortlessly handles complex, non-manifold geometries and achieves more controllable and faster mesh generation. It delivers significantly superior artistic meshes compared to current state-of-the-art methods.

title: Phased & Viscous Fluids
Practical Applications
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Education; Games; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Simulation; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience

title: Spatiotemporal FLIP for Fast Free-Surface and Two-Phase Simulation With Very Large Time Steps
speakers_or_contributors: Bernhard Braun; Rene Winchenbach; Jan Bender; Nils Thuerey
affiliations: RWTH Aachen University; Technical University Munich
description: We present ST-FLIP, a spatiotemporal extension of the Fluid-Implicit Particle (FLIP) method for free-surface and two-phase simulation. ST-FLIP augments particles with 4D spatiotemporal coordinates and supports time steps up to an order of magnitude larger than in existing solvers, delivering several-fold speedups for multi-billion-particle simulations at very high grid resolutions.

title: Buoyancy-driven Phase Separation in the Material Point Method
speakers_or_contributors: Mehrnaz Ayazi; Craig Schroeder; Tamar Shinar
description: The Material Point Method struggles with separating materials due to a shared background grid that couples velocities. We introduce a hybrid approach using separate velocity grids and a unified pressure model, enabling buoyancy-driven phase separation for immiscible fluids.

title: Matérn Noise for Triangulation-Agnostic Flow Matching on Meshes
speakers_or_contributors: Tianshu Kuai; Arman Maesumi; Daniel Ritchie; Noam Aigerman
description: This paper tackles the task of learning to generate signals over triangle meshes in a triangulation-agnostic manner, meaning the trained model can be applied to different mesh triangulations effectively. It proposes a specific noise distribution which is triangulation agnostic on meshes, to be used inside the Flow Matching's denoising process.

title: SmoothMotionVectors: Optimizing Your Content for Video Codecs in Free View Video Compression
speakers_or_contributors: Mingyang Song; Yang Zhang; Siyu Tang; Tunc Ozan Aydin
description: Conventional video codecs exploit spatial and temporal self-similarity to compress structured signals. We bring this principle to dynamic scene reconstruction with 3D Gaussian Splatting by organizing deformation outputs through a tailored pipeline. The resulting motion vectors are smoother across space and time, allowing greater storage reduction without sacrificing visual quality.

title: VideoNeuMat: Neural Material Extraction from Generative Video Models
speakers_or_contributors: Bowen Xue; Saeed Hadadan; Zheng Zeng; Fabrice Rousselle; Zahra Montazeri; Milos Hasan
affiliations: NVIDIA; University of California Santa Barbara; University of Manchester
description: VideoNeuMat extracts reusable neural materials from video diffusion models. It fine-tunes a large video model as a virtual gonioreflectometer and uses a Large Reconstruction Model (LRM) to reconstruct compact neural materials in a single pass. The resulting materials are realistic, diverse, and generalize to novel viewing and lighting conditions.

title: ANARI in Action: Open Rendering for Visualization, Simulation, and Discovery
speakers_or_contributors: Jefferson Amstutz; Bill Sherman
keywords_tags: Digital Twins; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Industry Insight; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: ANARI is helping make advanced rendering more accessible by decoupling scene description from rendering implementation, enabling developers to take advantage of modern visualization technologies without deep graphics expertise. Join the Khronos Group and the ANARI community for an interactive look at the latest advances in ANARI, including new capabilities, tooling, real world adoption, and integration across scientific visualization, simulation, digital twins, HPC, and industrial workflows. Bring your questions, share your experiences, and help shape the future of open, scalable rendering.

title: Going Cloud Native
speakers_or_contributors: Doug Sherman; Jesse Lehrman
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Join a participant-driven discussion with key representatives from the graphics community, comparing experiences and exploring techniques related to pushing the production pipeline and resources toward cloud native architecture and deployment.

This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud, Pipeline, and Storage. Attendees will receive invites to our 'Beers of a Feather' event, the same evening.

title: The Continuous Production Layer: When Do Previs, Techvis, and Postvis Just Become Viz?
speakers_or_contributors: Benjamin Lock
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Previs, techvis, and postvis have traditionally described separate production moments: planning the shot, engineering the shoot, and supporting editorial or VFX after photography. But real-time engines, virtual production, performance capture, simulation, asset-aware workflows, and generative AI are making visualization more continuous across development, production, and post. This Birds of a Feather asks whether visualization is becoming a persistent production layer: carrying creative intent, technical constraints, shot truth, iteration, and review across the pipeline. Participants will discuss what should carry forward, what should stay flexible, and how visualization can remain controllable, traceable, and artist-led.

title: Tool UX Design & Workflow: From Tool Pain to Workflow Gain
speakers_or_contributors: Robin-Yann Storm
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
What features does a material editor need? How do we make a good warning dialog? Why do we need better translate gizmos? How do we get our internal users to use the new tool instead of sticking to the old tool?
Ask these questions, and any others you can think of, in this sessions. We will discuss tool UX design & workflow questions that anyone in the room brings up, find solutions and experiences as a group, and find the best answers that allow you to move forward and make the best tools possible for 3D content creation.

title: What's the latest at Chaos? Realtime, world-building, Gaussian splats and artist-friendly AI meet together
speakers_or_contributors: Vladimir Koylazov
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Education; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: In this talk we will explore the latest additions to the Chaos M&E products including V-Ray for Houdini, Maya, 3ds Max and Blender, as well as Vantage and Arena.

title: CAGS: Color-Adaptive Volumetric Video Streaming with Dynamic 3D Gaussian Splatting
speakers_or_contributors: Daheng Yin; Yili Jin; Jianxin Shi; Isaac Ding; Miao Zhang; Fangxin Wang; Zhaowu Huang; Cong Zhang; Jiangchuan Liu; Fang Dong
affiliations: Fuzhou University; McGill University; Nankai University; Simon Fraser University; Southeast University; The Chinese University of Hong Kong
description: CAGS is a novel color-adaptive system for 3D Gaussian Splatting that enables high-quality, immersive volumetric video streaming. By utilizing vector quantization and reference images for color restoration, CAGS overcomes bandwidth limitations, delivering significantly faster speeds and superior visual quality compared to existing solutions under fluctuating networks.

title: Expanded Voices: Voice Cloning, Latent Spaces, and Embodied Translation
speakers_or_contributors: Tomas Andrade Weber; Maria Arnal Dimas; Raquel Barrachina Llobet; Sol Bucalo Mana; Jerónimo Calderón; Fernando Cucchietti; Thaleia Ntiniakou; Adrià Espinoza Gómez; Paula Fernández Vergara; David Garcia-Povedano; Alex Gil; Roger Gonzalez March; Othmane Hayoun Mya; Marc Heras Villacampa; Míriam Herrero-Valea; Guillermo Marín; Paula Méndez Gordillo; Sara Tolosa Alarcón
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Geometry; Scientific Visualization; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This work treats AI models not only as technical systems, but as perceptual, embodied, and cultural artifacts. By combining voice cloning and latent space visualization in an interactive installation, the project demonstrates how AI representations can become accessible and critically examined. It opens new perspectives on translation, embodiment, and authorship, offering artists, researchers, and technologists a shared framework to reflect on how computational representations shape creative practice and human identity.

title: Learning Computer Graphics via Authentic Engineering Challenges
speakers_or_contributors: LEI YANG
keywords_tags: Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Education; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This short article introduced our experiences in teaching computer graphics through authentic engineering challenges to student members in an academic makerspace at the University of Hong Kong. It highlights the importance of computer graphics technologies in solving emerging engineering challenges.

title: PureSample: Neural Materials Learned by Sampling Microgeometry
speakers_or_contributors: Zixuan Li; Zixiong Wang; Jian Yang; Miloš Hašan; Beibei Wang
description: PureSample is a neural BRDF representation that learns material appearance directly from forward random walks on microgeometry. It enables efficient BRDF evaluation, importance sampling, and pdf evaluation using a flow-based sampler and a lightweight view-dependent albedo network, supporting complex homogeneous and spatially varying materials.

title: Tempo3D: Efficient Temporal-Aware Fine-Tuning and Multi-View Latent Aggregation for 3D Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Huizhi Zhu; Jiongming Qin; Yusen Wang; Chunxia Xiao
affiliations: DFRD, Dongfeng Motor Corporation Research&Development Institute; Wuhan University
description: Tempo3D is a resource-efficient framework for high-fidelity 3D generation from a single image. By innovatively enhancing detail learning and resolving single-view ambiguities without expensive retraining, it delivers state-of-the-art geometric fidelity. Additionally, Tempo3D empowers users with natural language-guided 3D editing.

title: Volume-Preserving LBM-MPM Coupling for Air-Water-Sand Mixtures
speakers_or_contributors: Xiaoyu Xiao; Haoxiang Wang; Xiaokang Yang; Mathieu Desbrun; Wei Li
affiliations: Department of Automation, Tsinghua University; INRIA / Ecole Polytechnique; Shanghai Jiao Tong University
description: We present a physically-based framework for simulating sand–water–air mixtures by coupling LBM fluids with MPM granular sand under a unified formulation. A water retention model with built-in volume conservation enables stable, realistic simulation of mixtures across diverse, multiscale scenarios.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - LightOpt: Lights Optimization for Real-time Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Luyan Cao
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
LightOpt: Lights Optimization for Real-time Rendering

LightOpt is a differentiable optimization framework that automatically reduces the number of lights in real-time game scenes while preserving visual appearance. By optimizing and restructuring real-time light sources, it lowers lighting cost, reduces overlap, and improves cross-platform performance for modern game rendering.

Paper Presentation Presented:
Monday, July 20, 3:45-5:15 pm; Paper Session: Real-Time Rendering; Room 408 A

title: A Nonlocal Monolithic Variational Framework for Free Surface Flows
speakers_or_contributors: Shusen Liu; Yuzhong Guo; Lixin Ren; Ying Qiao; Xiaowei He
affiliations: Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: We propose a unified nonlinear optimization framework that achieves a monolithic coupling of incompressibility, viscosity, and surface tension within a single solver. This first particle-based solver resolves their interdependence via position-based nonlocal viscosity formulations, enhancing stability beyond operator-splitting methods.

title: Fast and Accurate Gaussian Process Modelling of Real-World Materials
speakers_or_contributors: Arnau Colom; Christian Bouville; Julien Pettre; Kadi Bouatouch; Ricardo Marques
affiliations: Institut National de Recherche en Informatique et en Automatique (INRIA) Rennes University, CNRS, IRISA; Institut de Recherche en Informatique et Systèmes Aléatoires (IRISA); Pompeu Fabra University Interactive Technologies Group (GTI)
description: We propose a BRDF modeling method that provides accurate and compact representations for isotropic and anisotropic BRDFs. We propose new theoretical developments enabling tractable Gaussian Process BRDF regression, leading to analytical BRDF representations. State-of-the-art methods can be outperformed in most BRDF cases by using a small training set of observations.

title: PEGAsus: 3D Personalization of Geometry and Appearance
speakers_or_contributors: Jingyu Hu; Bin Hu; Ka-Hei Hui; Haipeng Li; Zhengzhe Liu; Daniel Cohen-Or; Chi-Wing Fu
affiliations: Autodesk Research; Lingnan University; Tel Aviv University; The Chinese University of Hong Kong; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; The University of Hong Kong
description: PEGAsus enables personalized 3D shape generation by learning reusable geometry and appearance concepts from a single reference shape, supporting both global and region-wise personalization. The learned concepts can be combined with text to synthesize novel personalized 3D assets outperforming existing state-of-the-art solutions.

title: TSMC: Time-varying 4D Scene Mesh Compression
speakers_or_contributors: Guodong Chen; Libor Váša; Amrita Mazumdar; Mallesham Dasari
affiliations: NVIDIA; Northeastern University; University of West Bohemia
description: TSMC is a compression framework for time-varying scene meshes that separates static and dynamic regions, leverages mesh-enclosed volumes to create reference meshes for motion prediction, and efficiently compresses dynamic displacements, enabling bandwidth-efficient 4D scene compression and real-time decoding, with a Unity plugin for VR headset playback.

title: Visual Instruments for Live Cinema: Turning Process into Performance
speakers_or_contributors: Kevin Peter He
keywords_tags: Art; Education; Fabrication; Games; Hardware; Industry Insight; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Simulation; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Virtual production pipelines (LED volumes, camera tracking, motion capture, and game engines) are deeply interactive systems, yet the final render often conceals the labor and live decision-making behind them. Part audiovisual performance and part artist talk, this session imagines the production process as a live cinema performance, using custom visual instruments to make calibration, mapping, and real-time control visible on stage.

title: AniGen: Unified $S^3$ Fields for Animatable 3D Asset Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Yi-Hua Huang; Zi-Xin Zou; Yuting He; Chirui Chang; Cheng-Feng Pu; Ziyi Yang; Yuan-Chen Guo; Yan-Pei Cao; Xiaojuan Qi
affiliations: CS Dept, Tsinghua University; The Chinese University of Hong Kong; The University of Hong Kong (HKU); VAST
description: AniGen generates animatable 3D assets from a single image by jointly producing geometry, skeletons, and skinning weights. Its unified S³ field representation enables consistent, high-quality rigged asset creation across diverse categories, including animals, humans, cartoon characters, and articulated objects.

title: From Studio Set to Render: A Collaborative Framework for Hybrid Media Education
speakers_or_contributors: James Porto
keywords_tags: Animation; Education; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: As the tools of photography and computer graphics become inseparable, the 'hybrid artist' is the new industry standard. This talk demonstrates how to bridge the lens-to-render gap by teaching students to navigate the collaborative methodologies of modern VFX and commercial production."

title: Sparse-to-Complete: From Sparse Image Captures to Complete 3D Scenes
speakers_or_contributors: Yiyang Shen; Yin Yang; Kun Zhou; Tianjia Shao
affiliations: State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University; University of Utah; Zhejiang University
description: We introduce S2C-3D, a novel sparse-view 3D reconstruction framework for high-fidelity, complete scene reconstruction. It features three components: a specialized diffusion model for scene-specific image restoration, a training-free view-consistency conditioned sampling process in the diffusion model for refined Gaussian optimization, and a camera trajectory planning scheme for comprehensive scene coverage.

title: Stochastic geomorphological transport for terrain erosion simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Nicholas McDonald; Guillaume Cordonnier
affiliations: INRIA, Université Côte d'Azur; erosiv Studio GmbH
description: Geomorphological transport is the long-distance transport of quantities which are key elements of terrain erosion. We propose a new stochastic algorithm for the efficient simulation of geomorphological transport with momentum conservation, which enables us, for the first time, to model dynamic emergent deltas, meanders and debris fans in eroded terrains.

title: Successive Height Preintegration for a Height-Interdependent Path Formulation of Multiple Bounces in Smith Microfacet BRDFs
speakers_or_contributors: Siyuan Zhang; Takuya Funatomi; Yuki Fujimura; Yasuhiro Mukaigawa; Hiroyuki Kubo
affiliations: Chiba University; Kyoto University; Nara Institute of Science and Technology
description: Classic microfacet models for rendering account only for single-bounce light, leading to energy loss. We present a novel multiple-bounce Smith microfacet BRDF with a height-interdependent formulation, successive height preintegration, and an improved BRDF evaluation method. Our model can conserve energy well and achieves low variance with negligible bias.

title: The Imagery from Calligraphy: Encoding and Decoding Pictographs Via Interactive Artistic Exploration
speakers_or_contributors: Jiayang Huang; Kang Zhang; David Yip
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Digital Twins; Games; Performance; Real-Time; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The Imagery from Calligraphy is a two-part new media artwork that explores how Chinese seal script can encode and decode cultural meaning. Through interactive writing and immersive performance film, it links calligraphy, embodied action, and Xuan-paper craft into a participatory experiential archive of visual language and heritage knowledge.

title: Faceform | NanoSkin: Human Skin Super-Resolution and Material Acquisition for Photorealistic Digital Doubles at Scale
speakers_or_contributors: Andrei Krovopuskov; Kirill Klimov
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
We will present NanoSkin, our approach for increasing the resolution of actor-specific skin microdetail by a factor of 4 to 16. NanoSkin is a neural network trained on our proprietary skin dataset to generate displacement textures up to 16K.
Skin microdetail is one of the key factors that contributes to the realism of digital characters. However, one of the most common production approaches is still to use pre-made skin detail maps captured from other people. We will give a high-level overview of the approach we use to enhance the details of a specific actor, rather than inventing them from scratch or transferring them from another person.
We will also discuss our work on lookdev automation and automatic material map acquisition, where texture maps are estimated in a way that allows the rendered character to match the actor’s reference photographs as closely as possible.

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | How to Develop a Real-Time 3D Digital Twin for Rapid Engineering Design
speakers_or_contributors: Abigail Breazeale
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Digital Twins; Physical AI; Real-Time
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Learn how to build a real-time 3D digital twin for rapid engineering design using NVIDIA Omniverse and CUDA-X libraries, AI physics models, and agentic engineering tools. This session will explore how leading simulation providers are leveraging NVIDIA technologies to enable real-time digital twins that accelerate design iteration and improve engineering workflows. We will highlight how Rivian is leveraging Siemens technologies accelerated by NVIDIA and AI physics to achieve rapid automotive design.

title: Articulate That Object Part (ATOP): 3D Part Articulation from Text and via Motion Personalization
speakers_or_contributors: Aditya Vora; Sauradip Nag; Kai Wang; Hao (Richard) Zhang
description: We present a few-shot method for articulating static 3D objects using text-guided motion personalization. Leveraging diffusion models, we generate plausible part motions and adapt them to input objects via image prompting. Differentiable rendering with score distillation transfers multi-view motion into 3D, enabling optimization of articulation parameters under piecewise rigidity assumptions.

title: MeshFlow: Mesh Generation with Equivariant Flow Matching
speakers_or_contributors: Qi Sun; Kiyohiro Nakayama; Jing Yan; Qixing Huang; Alexander Rush; Leonidas Guibas; Gordon Wetzstein; Jing Liao; Guandao Yang
affiliations: City University of Hong Kong; Cornell Tech; Stanford University; The University of Texas at Austin
description: MeshFlow is a powerful diffusion-based framework utilizing equivariant modeling to transform random triangle soups into high-quality meshes in less than one second.

title: Mixwell: Sharp 2D Fluid Brushes for Progressive Physics-Based Mixing
speakers_or_contributors: Doug James; Ethan James
description: Mixwell introduces sharp 2D fluid brushes and GPU-accelerated analytical methods for progressive, resolution-independent physics-based mixing. Derived from potential flow around cylindrical tines, Mixwell evaluates drift per sample without grids or intermediate resampling, enabling real-time, arbitrary-resolution fluid mixing and rendering with negligible numerical dissipation.

title: Robust In-Engine Texture Optimization from Inconsistent Generative Targets
speakers_or_contributors: Taejoon Kim; Seung-Uk Yoon; Seong-Jae Lim; Bon-Woo Hwang; Kinam Kim; Seung Wook Lee
description: We present a practical method for optimizing textures directly inside production rendering engines from inconsistent AI-generated images. By handling geometric mismatch and unreliable details, our approach avoids blur and ghosting, enabling robust texture refinement without requiring differentiable rendering or changes to existing pipelines.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH Cartographic Visualization Birds of a Feather
speakers_or_contributors: Theresa-Marie Rhyne; Theresa-Marie Rhyne
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The ACM SIGGRAPH Cartographic Visualization (Carto) session explores how viewpoints and techniques from the computer graphics community can be effectively applied to cartographic and spatial data sets. Speakers demonstrate their latest tools and application efforts.

title: BruSHŪ: Cross-Modal Translation of Implicit Micro-Actions in Chinese Calligraphy
speakers_or_contributors: Tiancheng LIU; Shumeng Zhang; Nicolò Merendino
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: BruSHŪ is a sensor-embedded calligraphy brush that translates fleeting micro-actions, contact boundaries, turns, and hesitations into sparse sound events and an evolving ink-like visual field. Rather than scoring or reconstructing strokes, it makes tacit embodied decisions perceptible and discussable in real-time, advancing non-normative, cross-modal feedback for reflective creative practice.

title: Rolling Through the Pipeline: A Course Model for Virtual Production Education
speakers_or_contributors: Flip Phillips; David Long; Aidan Montag; Chao Peng
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This paper presents an introductory virtual production (VP) course built around an LED-wall stage, where students learn VP through embodied, pipeline-centered practice. By integrating cinematography, real-time rendering, tracking, and collaborative workflows, the course develops ecosystem fluency and workflow literacy across film, animation, and game design disciplines.

title: Strips as Tokens: Artist Mesh Generation with Native UV Segmentation
speakers_or_contributors: Rui Xu; Dafei Qin; Kaichun Qiao; Qiujie Dong; Huaijin Pi; Qixuan Zhang; Longwen Zhang; Lan Xu; Jingyi Yu; Wenping Wang; Taku Komura
affiliations: ShanghaiTech University; Texas A&M University; The University of Hong Kong (HKU); University of Hong Kong
description: We propose Strips as Tokens (SATO), a novel framework with a token ordering strategy inspired by triangle strips. By constructing the sequence as a connected chain of faces that explicitly encodes UV boundaries, our method naturally preserves the organized edge flow and semantic layout characteristic of artist-created meshes.

title: 3D Generation - Interactive Discussion
description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. Outside the room will be a series of poster boards for authors to gather around with the audience. Authors are invited to bring any material related to their paper that could instigate further conversation such as printouts, posters, demos, or other presentation aids. The interactive discussions provide attendees more interaction with the individual authors.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | What's Next for Animation at Autodesk
speakers_or_contributors: Evan Atherton; Frederik Brudy; Lance Thornton; Noa Kaplan
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Character animation workflows are evolving rapidly, creating new opportunities for artists to create more easily, explore ideas, and stay focused on performance and storytelling. Join Autodesk’s Animation and Research teams for an inside look at what’s next in animation at Autodesk and the tools we're building to give you more freedom to create the way you want to create.

Through demos and research previews, you'll see new AI workflows for motion generation, alongside new approaches to story development and planning. You'll also see how Autodesk is building more flexible workflows that support artist choice, control, and interoperability.

title: Hands-on Course: NVIDIA: Hands-On Machine Learning with Slang: Building High-Performance GPU Inference Pipelines
speakers_or_contributors: Christopher Hebert
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Real-Time
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Learn to build GPU ML inference pipelines with Slang. Use a lightweight, open set of pre-written kernels to implement an MNIST CNN from scratch: load SafeTensors weights, run inference, and apply kernel fusion for better performance. Hands-on, cross-platform, and highly customizable—ideal for games, tools, and research.

title: LA Local Artists Showcase
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The Spatial Storytelling LED Wall Showcase features a curated collection of short-form digital works by Los Angeles–based artists. Spanning immersive media, experimental film, generative art, and digital storytelling, the showcase highlights creative practices that explore new relationships between narrative, technology, and space.

Join us between sessions and during the dedicated lunch hour to experience these works at architectural scale and celebrate the vibrant artistic community of Los Angeles within the Spatial Storytelling program.

title: Creativity ≠ Generativity: A Case Study of Attentive Machine Learning in Dance Performance
speakers_or_contributors: Ziyu Xu; Zhuodi Cai
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Display; Dynamics; Education; Ethics and Society; Hardware; Lighting; Performance; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Dance expresses through translation. It transforms intention and story into movement through tacit and relational knowledge. When computational systems enter, they interpret movement as machine-legible forms, shaping how dance is perceived. This paper presents a case study of human–machine co-performance translation through a movement recognition system in a twenty-minute dance work. Using wearable IMU sensors and a MiniRocket-based classifier, our system translates performers’ movements into real-time audiovisual responses, while elevating agency, care, and meaning over accuracy.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - MUSIC: Learning Muscle-Driven Dexterous Hand Control
speakers_or_contributors: Pei Xu
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
MUSIC: Learning Muscle-Driven Dexterous Hand Control

We present a data-driven framework for physics-based, muscle-driven dexterous control that enables musculoskeletal hands to perform piano playing for novel musical scores. Our framework adopts a hierarchical architecture, combining high-frequency muscle control with low-frequency coordination. The method achieves coordinated bimanual motions and produces human-like activation patterns, generalizing beyond reference data.

Paper Presentation Presented: Thursday, July 23, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: Learning in Motion; Room 403 A

title: Animation Theater 2: Historically Speaking
description: The past is never just the past, it's an argument about the present. This program brings together works that mine history, memory, and inherited struggle to illuminate the fault lines we're still standing on.

speakers_or_contributors: Doug Sherman; Ryan Bottriell; Jesse Lehrman
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Pipeline Tools and Work
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
A participant-driven discussion on leveraging AI agents to enhance studio technology and workflow throughput. The goal is to reduce friction in current established studio pipelines by using AI for intervention, answering questions, and solving 'slow' issues, without requiring the removal or changing of existing pipelines. The discussion will explicitly avoid creative and pixel generation topics, focusing solely on augmenting Studio pipelines.

This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud, Pipeline, and Storage. Attendees will receive invites to our 'Beers of a Feather' event, the same evening.

title: Ancestral Craft and Emerging Technologies: Designing Futures from Place
speakers_or_contributors: gabriela bila advincula; Jonny Cohen
affiliations: Media Lab, Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
keywords_tags: Art; Ethics and Society; Fabrication; Real-Time; Robotics; Spatial Computing

Through XR installations and robotic artworks developed in the Amazon rainfores, it shares methods for building non-generic futures grounded in local knowledge, material practices, collaboration and real-time graphics.

We also invite faculty to join as project advisors. So come, hear about our projects, and share your experiences in research at the undergraduate level.

title: Computer Graphics History: SIGGRAPH Stories and more…
speakers_or_contributors: Niall Williams; Bonnie Mitchell

keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Display; Dynamics; Education; Ethics and Society; Fabrication; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Performance; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing
