description: Love SIGGRAPH’s history? Curious about the people, projects, and milestones that shaped modern computer graphics? Join the ACM SIGGRAPH History Committee Birds of a Feather for a fun, informal gathering where attendees swap stories and share memories about SIGGRAPH, past and present. Whether you have a favorite conference moment, a behind-the-scenes anecdote, or a burning question about the early days of the field, this is the place to hear it and tell it. Come meet fellow history enthusiasts, learn what the committee has been working on and pitch ideas for new projects to help preserve SIGGRAPH's history.

title: LTX | From Pixels to Physics: Building AI's Right Brain in the Open
speakers_or_contributors: Ofir Bibi
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Physical AI; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Simulation; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Foundational models aren't new, but building LTX taught us their scope is expanding faster and further than anyone predicted. LTX is our multimodal generative world model, and in this talk Ofir shows how a system trained to do one deceptively simple thing, generate pixels across time, a.k.a. video, becomes a physically grounded foundation that reasons about light, physics, and causality. If language models are the left brain of AI, LTX is a bet on the right: the intuitive, perceptual, physical side of intelligence.
We'll trace LTX's journey from a state-of-the-art open-weights model to core infrastructure across domains, from media and entertainment to physical AI. Expect real applications spanning robotics and simulation, a look under the hood at the architectural shifts that made it possible (MoE, stronger text encoders), and the conviction driving the project: when a model is open and production-ready, it compounds, for researchers, developers, enterprises, and the applications this room will build next.

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | Career Growth in Physical AI: Bringing 3D Graphics Skills into Robotics and Digital Twins
speakers_or_contributors: Anthony Okuchi; Ashley Goldstein; Fatima Anes; Laurie Koenig
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Physical AI; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Creative 3D skills are becoming increasingly relevant to physical AI. Join this career growth panel to hear how industry leaders have moved from 3D graphics and content creation into roles across robotics, AI, simulation, and digital twins. Panelists will share what carried over, what they had to learn, and practical advice for others looking to make the same move.

title: OMBI: Open Standards for the Metaverse Browser
speakers_or_contributors: Neil Trevett; Dean Abramson; Yinch Yeap
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Digital Twins; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: AR glasses are arriving. Airports, hospitals, logistics centers, and universities are already planning spatial deployments, but the open infrastructure layer that makes it all work doesn't exist yet. The Open Metaverse Browser Initiative (OMBI), a Metaverse Standards Forum initiative seeded by RP1's operational prototype, is building that layer: an open-source Metaverse Browser Engine (Sneeze) that connects any device to spatial services on demand, on open standards from Khronos, W3C, and OGC, with no proprietary lock-in. This BOF brings together industry contributors to walk through the architecture, share progress, and ensure the AR hardware race produces a genuinely open spatial web.

title: Real-Time Shading BOF: Building a Community Around Shaders, Shading Languages, and Tech Artist Workflows
speakers_or_contributors: Randi Rost; Jesse Barker; Shannon Woods; Ken Perlin; Marc Olano
affiliations: Khronos; NVIDIA; NYU; Unity; University Of Maryland, Baltimore County
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Games; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The Real-Time Shading BOF, with Ken Perlin and Marc Olano, aims to foster a vibrant, cross-disciplinary community of researchers, technical artists, hardware vendors, compiler developers, and practitioners working with shaders and shading languages. Building on the 2026 Shading Languages Symposium, this session will focus on practical pain points faced by technical artists in production environments—such as shader authoring, debugging, portability, performance, and integration with modern real-time pipelines—while welcoming insights from language designers and hardware experts. The BOF will serve as an informal forum for discussion, idea exchange, and networking, acting as a forerunner to the Real-Time Shading Symposium in 2027.

title: SIGGRAPH for Health: Convergence of Trends and Techniques
speakers_or_contributors: Diana Arellano; Nicolas Henchoz; Diana Arellano; Davide Corigliano; Jernej Barbic; Enoch Omale; Yi-Chin Lee; Tim McGraw
affiliations: ETH Zürich; Filmakademie Baden-Württemberg GmbH; Illuminant Surgical Inc.; National Taiwan University; Purdue University; USC; École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL)
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Education; Ethics and Society; Games; Performance; Physical AI; Real-Time
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
SIGGRAPH is the perfect platfom to create impact in a world that increasingly needs care!
This session is the most desired follow-up to the conversations on how the convergence of AI, computer graphics, and immersive media is transforming healthcare. To ensure adoption, these innovations must combine science and human-centered perspectives. Gathering research, technology and culture, SIGGRAPH is a core player in this domain.
Join the opening session to hear inputs from leading experts sharing strategies for obtaining international impact, behind the scenes of innovation ecosystems, real case studies, and human centered approach. We will have a networking cocktail, followed by the interactive workshop—help shape SIGGRAPH’s strategy for health innovation!

title: Autodesk Vision Series | AI in the Production Pipeline: A Conversation with Cinesite's CTO
speakers_or_contributors: Kamal Mistry; Michele Sciolette
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Join Cinesite CTO Michele Sciolette for a fireside chat with Kamal Mistry, Sr. Director of Product for Autodesk Media & Entertainment, on how one of the world’s leading animation and VFX studios are approaching AI in production. The conversation will explore where AI can remove friction from creative workflows, how teams evaluate emerging tools for real pipeline use, and what it takes to introduce new capabilities without disrupting artists’ craft, collaboration, or creative control.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - Sketch2Arti: Sketch-based Articulation Modeling of CAD Objects
speakers_or_contributors: Changjian Li
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
Sketch2Arti: Sketch-based Articulation Modeling of CAD Objects

Sketch2Arti is the first sketch-based articulation modeling system for CAD objects. Given a CAD model and simple 2D sketches, it identifies movable parts and predicts motion parameters. It can also complete missing internal structures revealed by motion, enabling controllable, iterative, and generalizable articulation modeling for diverse objects.

Paper Presentation Presented: Wednesday, July 22, 10:45 am-12:35 pm; Paper Session: Vectors Graphics, Strokes & Sketches; Room 403 A

title: 8DNA: 8D Neural Asset Light Transport by Distribution Learning
speakers_or_contributors: Liwen Wu; Haolin Lu; Bing Xu; Miloš Hašan; Ravi Ramamoorthi
affiliations: NVIDIA; University of California San Diego
description: We pre-bake complex light scattering in 3D assets into neural 8D light transport functions by learning distributions from forward path-traced samples. Unlike previous far-field methods, our representation remains accurate under near-field illumination, while delivering lower variance and faster inference across challenging scenes than path tracing.

title: A Robust and Efficient Intersection Algorithm for NURBS Surfaces: Handling Small Loops and Tangent Intersections
speakers_or_contributors: Jieyin Yang; Xiaohong Jia
affiliations: State Key Laboratory of Mathematical Sciences Academy of Mathematics and Systems Science Chinese Academy of Sciences University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: This paper presents a robust and efficient method for computing NURBS surface intersection curves, with emphasis on small loops and degenerate cases. By combining winding number theory with subdivision on the parametric domain, the method reliably detects starting points of all branches, improving topological correctness and computational efficiency in CAD.

title: Advances in Real-time Rendering in Games, Part II
speakers_or_contributors: Natalya Tatarchuk; Alexander Muller; Sergey Makeev; John Hable
description:
Modern video games employ a variety of sophisticated algorithms to produce groundbreaking 3D rendering pushing the visual boundaries and interactive experience of rich environments. This course brings state-of-the-art and production-proven rendering techniques for fast, interactive rendering of complex and engaging virtual worlds of video games.

This is the course to attend if you are in the game development industry or want to learn the latest and greatest techniques in the real-time rendering domain!

title: Chaos | From Game Engine to Production Engine
speakers_or_contributors: Ivan Reel
keywords_tags: Art; Games; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: For many virtual production facilities, game engines became the foundation upon which modern LED volume workflows were built. Along the way, teams developed new disciplines, new processes, and new technical expertise to bridge the gap between real-time rendering and production requirements. This session shares StradaXR’s perspective on that journey and examines emerging approaches that seek to reduce workflow complexity, preserve creative intent, and bring production-ready content closer to the stage.

title: Computational Photography
Efficient 3D Content Creation for Immersive Experiences
speakers_or_contributors: Shaohui Jiao; Yuheng Jiang; Sheng Li; Zhuo Su; Jiaming Sun; Lan Xu; Lan Xu; Jiaming Sun; Shuo Wang; Di Wu
affiliations: 4DV.ai; ByteDance Inc.; Deemos; Peking University; Pico Inc.; ShanghaiTech University
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Virtual Reality
description:
This workshop is dedicated to democratizing 3D content creation, encompassing both static and dynamic 3D assets, by exploring cutting-edge advances in volumetric video built on 4D Gaussian Splatting, fast 3D objects and scenes reconstruction, AI generated 3D content, and real-time immersive video generation with diffusion models.
We bring together both academic and industrial researchers and practitioners to discuss scalable pipelines that lower the barrier to immersive content creation. What's more, we will explore the 3D/4D content experience on both VR headsets as well as consumer mobile platforms.
A key feature of this workshop is a hands-on demonstration: participants will experience 1) volumetric video of industrial demonstration in both video-on-demand applications and in real-time streaming of
live-broadcasting, 2) interactive experience of AI generated 3D content and 3D reconstruction, and also 3) real-time interactive and high-resolution video generation via diffusion models.
The workshop seeks to bridge the gap between cutting-edge 3D content creation in academic research and in real-world industrial applications.

title: GPC: Large-Scale Generative Pretraining for Transferable Motor Control
speakers_or_contributors: Yi Shi; Yifeng Jiang; Chen Tessler; Xue Bin Peng
description: We present Generative Pretrained Controllers, a framework for training reusable generative controllers for physically simulated characters. GPC leverages FSQ discretization with end-to-end RL to learn reusable motor skills from large-scale motion data. Once trained, the generative controller can be finetuned and adapted to perform diverse downstream tasks using naturalistic behaviors.

title: Global-Scale Photorealism with Google Immersive View XR
speakers_or_contributors: Se-Joon Chung; Chun-Po Wang; John Bates; Daniel Filip; Azin Mehrnoosh; Dominik Käser
keywords_tags: Performance; Real-Time; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This talk chronicles the engineering and design journey of bringing Google Maps Immersive View to standalone XR. We detail the unique challenges of rendering this fully navigable, global-scale environment on constrained headsets. Highlighting rendering optimizations for 3D Tiles and 3D Gaussian Splatting, intuitive hands-only gestural navigation, and adaptive spatial audio, we reveal how to achieve performant photorealism and active user presence on next-generation XR hardware.

title: Guidestar-Free Adaptive Optics with Asymmetric Apertures
speakers_or_contributors: Weiyun Jiang; Haiyun Guo; Christopher A. Metzler; Ashok Veeraraghavan
affiliations: Rice University; University of Maryland, College Park
description: We introduce and demonstrate the first closed-loop adaptive optics system capable of optically correcting aberrations in real-time without a guidestar or a wavefront sensor. We enable these capabilities by combining asymmetric apertures, machine learning–based PSF estimation and phase retrieval algorithms, and a high resolution spatial light modulator.

title: Light Architecture: Translating the AI Black Box into Immersive Experience
speakers_or_contributors: Yiyun Kang
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Physical AI; Real-Time
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: As AI becomes an opaque "Black Box," Light Architecture physically "translates" invisible algorithmic structures instead of merely exploiting generative outputs. Synthesizing kinetic light and spatial audio into a Gesamtkunstwerk, this "Experiential AI" offers a sensory alternative to traditional Explainable AI (XAI), providing a critical framework for demystifying the "Hyperobject" of modern computation. Reclaiming agency requires a visceral, embodied encounter with technological systems rather than just intellectual analysis.

Starting from an existing 3D application, Damien will show how developers can connect OpenUSD scenes, RTX rendering, physics, simulation, and UI capabilities into a working prototype. Lexi, an AI agent co-host, will help show how agents can inspect scenes, call tools, and speed up iteration while Omniverse libraries provide the foundation for repeatable physical AI workflows in 3D graphics and simulation.

title: RigX: Rethinking How Characters Are Rigged
speakers_or_contributors: Marko Šašić; Miroslav Radmanović
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Games; Industry Insight; Modeling; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Marko Š. presents RigX, a next-generation character rigging and deformation pipeline designed to rethink how digital humans are built for high-end games and cinematic production. Unlike traditional approaches that rely on heavy muscle simulations or complex dependency chains, RigX introduces a lightweight logic-driven deformation system that produces muscle-like behavior with full artistic control and significantly reduced computational overhead.

In this session, attendees will see how RigX enables the creation of both full-body and facial systems with consistent, production-ready performance across a wide range of character styles, from hyper-realistic digital doubles to stylized or cartoony characters. The system is designed for responsiveness, stability, and scalability in real-time pipelines.

The presentation includes a short rendered breakdown followed by a live demonstration of facial animation, body deformation, and rig behavior in real time. Marko will walk through core design principles, integration strategies for advanced production pipelines, and practical considerations for adopting RigX in studio environments.

The session concludes with a focus on production impact, demonstrating how RigX enables significantly faster rigging workflows, simplified setup and iteration, and substantially higher-quality deformation results compared to traditional muscle simulation-based systems.

title: Studio Storage: This is an annual discussion of VFX & Animation studios' Large-Scale Storage experiences
speakers_or_contributors: Joe D'Amato; Amandeep Singh; Michele Sciolette
affiliations: CDW StudioCloud; Cinesite; Pixar Animation Studios
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Rendering; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Studio Storage: Storage for VFX & Animation has extreme requirements and only a limited number of proven solutions are available or you can build your own. Industry experts and new entrants are all welcomed, for a sharing of knowledge, experience, and best practices. Mail joe@jdamato.com for any questions or comments. This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the VFX Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud Native, Pipeline, Studio Storage, and Agentic Workflows.

title: USD in production
speakers_or_contributors: Pallav Sharma; Nick Porcino; Tabatha Hickman; Martin Davies; Sean Donnelly
affiliations: Autodesk; LAIKA; Pixar Animation Studios; Walt Disney Animation Studios
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Games; Geometry; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation
description: This hands-on course focuses on debugging USD composition in production. Participants gain practical experience authoring data across composition arcs and diagnosing issues in complex scenes. Building on USD in Production, the course emphasizes interactive use of modern debugging and visualization tools to understand how opinions, overrides, and data resolve.

title: “Live from LA!”: Facilitating Personal and Political Storytelling through Immersive Youth Theater
speakers_or_contributors: Ana Cardenas Gasca; Payton Croskey; Xin Xin; Jennifer Jacobs
affiliations: Processing Foundation; University of California Santa Barbara
keywords_tags: Art; Augmented Reality; Education; Ethics and Society; Performance
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: “Live from LA!” is an immersive youth-theater production blending dance, poetry, and projection-mapping. We discuss how we scaffolded this production across disciplines, locations, and age groups to support youth political storytelling through personal narratives. We highlight how p5.js tools supported youth authorship while navigating practical constraints and safety.

title: A Generalizable Light Transport 3D Embedding for Global Illumination
speakers_or_contributors: Bing Xu; Mukund Varma T; Cheng Wang; Tzu-mao Li; Lifan Wu; Bartlomiej Wronski; Ravi Ramamoorthi; Marco Salvi
affiliations: NVIDIA; University of California San Diego
description: We introduce a scalable 3D embedding that learns global illumination directly from 3D scene configurations. This design scales to environments with millions of triangles, enabling the first generalizable GI learning on complex, high-fidelity indoor scenes, far beyond prior limits. It bypasses per-scene retraining and screen-space artifacts to deliver realistic rendering.

title: MAROON: A Dataset for the Joint Characterization of Near-Field High-Resolution Radio-Frequency and Optical Depth Imaging Techniques
speakers_or_contributors: Vanessa Wirth; Johanna Bräunig; Nikolai Hofmann; Martin Vossiek; Tim Weyrich; Marc Stamminger
description: Robust perception benefits from complementary sensors, yet close-range setups remain scarce. We characterize optical and near-field radar imagers via multimodal calibration, collecting data from RGB-D sensors and one mmWave MIMO radar. We evaluate depth sensing across materials, geometries, and distances, release MAROON, and show applications in inverse rendering and reconstruction.

title: SMP: Reusable Score-Matching Motion Priors for Physics-Based Character Control
speakers_or_contributors: Yuxuan Mu; Ziyu Zhang; Yi Shi; Dun Yang; Minami Matsumoto; Kotaro Imamura; Guy Tevet; Chuan Guo; Michael Taylor; Chang Shu; Pengcheng Xi; Xue Bin Peng
affiliations: National Research Council Canada; Simon Fraser University; Snap; Sony Interactive Entertainment; Stanford University
description: SMP builds reusable and modular reward models for training motor controllers. Once constructed from a motion dataset, the priors can be reused across diverse tasks while preserving the behaviors in the data, without requiring access to the original dataset or retraining.

title: Stress-Aware Panelization of Freeform Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Xinzhuo Hu; Roi Poranne; Julian Panetta
affiliations: University of California Davis; University of Haifa
description: We present a variational surface rationalization algorithm for creating structurally robust polyhedral approximations of freeform surfaces. Coupling a continuous panelization flow with differentiable shell stress analysis, our method lets structural performance guide the panel decomposition, achieving substantial stress reductions and producing more robust designs than minimizing stress under predetermined decompositions.

title: Free-form Surface Approximation Using Rotational Patches
speakers_or_contributors: Yuanpeng Liu; Yi Min Xie; Ting-Uei Lee; Ziqi Wang; Nico Pietroni
affiliations: Hohai University; RMIT University; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; University of Technology Sydney
description: We propose a method to approximate free-form surfaces using rotational patches for architectural rationalization. By segmenting the input surface and optimizing B-spline-based patch layouts, followed by post-processing, we generate smooth, seamless quad meshes with locally repeated building elements (including beams, panels, and nodes), offering great potential for reducing fabrication costs.

title: Light Field Cinematography (and the Birth of a Volumetrics Lab)
speakers_or_contributors: C. K. Olsen
keywords_tags: Art; Capture/Scanning; Education; Modeling; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This talk explores Light Field Cinematography through the development of an interdisciplinary Volumetrics Lab supporting faculty-led undergraduate research. Bridging computer science, real-time graphics, cinematography, and embodied performance, it demonstrates how motion-based volumetric capture enables new forms of cinematic, computational, and conceptual problem solving through spatial engagement within real-world educational constraints.

title: Machine Civilization: Exploring Machine Alterity through a Generative Semiotic Installation
speakers_or_contributors: Jia-Qi Shi; Yixiong Wang; Jiawen Zheng; Hourun Wang; Yufeng Zeng; Chen Liang
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Guangzhou
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Real-Time
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Machine Civilization offers SIGGRAPH community a critical lens for examining the gap between explainability and intelligibility in the age of intelligence. This work explored non-human meaning-making and comprehension limits in human-machine interaction. It raises an ethical question: can we engage with machine intelligence as genuine alterity rather than tools? This research proposes that recognizing the alterity of machine intelligence and rethinking de-anthropocentric innovation may be essential for AI's next stage.

title: MotionBricks: Scalable Real-Time Motions with Modular Latent Generative Model and Smart Primitives
speakers_or_contributors: Tingwu Wang; Olivier Dionne; Michael De Ruyter; David Minor; Davis Rempe; Kaifeng Zhao; Mathis Petrovich; Ye Yuan; Chenran Li; Zhengyi Luo; Brian Robison; Xavier Blackwell; Bernardo Antoniazzi; Xue Bing Peng; Yuke Zhu; Simon Yuen
affiliations: ETH Zürich; NVIDIA; Simon Fraser University
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Robotics; Simulation
description: MotionBricks is a real-time generative framework that transforms interactive motion control for animation and robotics. By combining a large-scale latent backbone with intuitive "smart primitives," it delivers high-quality, zero-shot motion synthesis at 15,000 FPS, allowing users to effortlessly build complex and fine-grained animations and robotic applications.

title: Non-line-of-sight imaging with arbitrary relay surface geometries via 3D Gaussian Transient Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Yi Wang; Ziyu Zhan; Yuran Wang; Hao Wang; Qiang Liu; Zuoqiang Shi; Lingyun Qiu; Xing Fu
affiliations: City University of Hong Kong; Tsinghua University
description: We present a LOS-guided NLOS imaging pipeline for arbitrary relay surface geometries, representing hidden scenes with 3D Gaussian primitives and developing an efficient differentiable transient renderer. Real experiments on complex relay surfaces captured with our custom system show improved robustness and broaden the practical applicability of NLOS imaging.

title: Rao-Blackwellized Markov chain Monte Carlo Light Transport
speakers_or_contributors: Sascha Holl; Gurprit Singh; Hans-Peter Seidel
affiliations: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD); Max Planck Institute for Informatics
description: We introduce a novel Rao-Blackwellization technique for variance reduction in Markov chain Monte Carlo light transport. We improve both Metropolis Light Transport and the current state-of-the-art, Jump Restore Light Transport, consistently outperforming traditional variance reduction, accelerating convergence, and establishing a new state-of-the-art in MCMC-based rendering under equal-time and equal-sample evaluations.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Clumsy, Deadly, Unforgettable: Creating the Rad Roach Massacre in Fallout Season 2
speakers_or_contributors: Fred Ruff; Stephen Lauser
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Join Refuge VFX's founder Fred Ruff and Lead Look Development Artist Stephen Lauser as they break down the creation of the rad roach massacre sequence from Fallout Season 2. From early creative challenges and budget-driven decisions to creature animation, environmental interaction, and final compositing, we'll reveal how a swarm of giant insects became one of the sequence's most memorable and terrifying moments. Through behind-the-scenes footage and shot breakdowns, you'll see how the team transformed clumsy creatures into deadly predators while balancing artistic goals with production realities.

title: Broadband Hyperspectral 3D Imaging using Dispersed Structured Light
speakers_or_contributors: Suhyun Shin; Yunseong Moon; Ryota Maeda; David Lindell; Kyros Kutulakos; Seung-Hwan Baek
affiliations: POSTECH; University of Hyogo; University of Toronto
description: We present a broadband hyperspectral 3D (BH3D) imaging system spanning visible to SWIR (450–1500 nm). Using a single-spectrograph stereo setup, our method jointly reconstructs accurate 3D geometry and material properties, enabling robust analysis of real-world scenes beyond the visible spectrum.

title: Continuity-Enhancing Degree Elevation and Splits
speakers_or_contributors: Cem Yuksel
description: We present two novel methods for automatically enhancing the continuity of piecewise-polynomial curves with negligible computational overhead. They provide the first practical interface for modeling curves with curvature (or higher) continuity using the familiar Bezier handles of piecewise cubic curves, the most popular curve modeling interface that previously offered no mechanism to achieve curvature continuity. Our solutions can also be used for defining new interpolating curve formulations with desirable properties. Furthermore, they can be used with higher-dimensional curves or surfaces, which we demonstrate by fixing the curvature discontinuities of the famous Utah Teapot model.

title: Deep Motion Warping via Phase-Conditioned Diffusion Autoencoder
speakers_or_contributors: Bowen Zheng; Linjun Wu; Xinwei Jiang; Yujin Chai; Zijiao Zeng; He Wang; Xiaogang Jin
affiliations: Tencent VISVISE; University College London (UCL); Zhejiang University
description: We present a deep motion warping framework utilizing a phase-conditioned diffusion autoencoder. By explicitly disentangling motion into root velocity, phase, and style, it enables intuitive, high-level character animation editing including root warping, exaggeration, time warping, and style transfer while strictly preserving structural consistency.

title: Deploying Radiance Fields Across Real-time and Web: Bridging Academia and Industry
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Rubloff; Lukasz Mirocha, PhD
affiliations: Hong Kong Baptist University | Opus Lab; Radiancefields.com
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Education; Games; Generative AI; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality; Visual Effects
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The session is for attendees interested in information exchange between the research and industry/application communities working with different implementations and applications of radiance fields.

The third community-driven forum features a panel composed of leading researchers presenting at 2026 and industry representatives, plus an audience Q&A session. It aims to foster knowledge exchange, identify future directions, and promote new collaborations, connecting the research and user communities within computer graphics and interactive techniques in applying NeRF/3DGS for visualization, digital twins, interactive media (XR/3D/web) design. The discussion will dive into data compression, web-based hosting, game engine integration, & dynamic techniques (4DGS).

title: Ragged Neighborhood Attention for Spatiotemporal Neural Denoising of Deep Monte Carlo Renderings
speakers_or_contributors: Xianyao Zhang; Gerhard Röthlin; Tunc Ozan Aydin; Farnood Salehi; Marios Papas
description: RaNAD is the first spatiotemporal neural denoiser for deep Monte Carlo renderings. It handles semi-structured deep images with variable bin counts per pixel through ragged neighborhood attention, and exploits temporal context across up to seven frames, greatly advancing in both quality and temporal stability for denoising deep images.

title: Sponsored Keynote by Bolt Graphics: Darwesh Singh, Founder, CEO/CTO, Building a GPU for the Future of Computer Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Darwesh Singh
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: What happens when you design a GPU to be very efficient at path tracing and physics simulations? It ends up looking different from any other GPU. GPUs were originally designed to be proficient at rasterization. However, the holy grail of computer graphics is real-time path tracing and simulations, which so far has been difficult to achieve with GPUs built around rasterization. Learn how Bolt Graphics is building its very own GPU, Zeus, to achieve real-time path tracing and simulations for creators and researchers around the world.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - A LoD of Gaussians: Out-of-Core Training and Rendering for Seamless Ultra-Large Scene Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Felix Windisch
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
A LoD of Gaussians: Out-of-Core Training and Rendering for Seamless Ultra-Large Scene Reconstruction

Gaussian Splatting struggles with large scenes due to memory limits and chunking artifacts. We introduce "A LoD of Gaussians," a new framework enabling ultra-large scene training and rendering on a single consumer GPU. Through out-of-core streaming and Level-of-Detail, it achieves seamless multi-scale reconstruction without relying on scene partitioning.

Paper Presentation Presented: Monday, July 20, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: 3D Gaussian Splatting I; Room 408 A

title: GenPIE: A Time-Resolved Plenoptic Imager
speakers_or_contributors: Ziheng Wang; Siyuan Shen; Huanyu Xu; Kaichun Qiao; Longwen Zhang; Qixuan Zhang; Qilin Sun; Shiying Li; Jingyi Yu
affiliations: ShanghaiTech University; The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
description: We present a physically grounded generative imaging system to resolve high-dimensional, time-resolved plenoptic light transport from sparse measurements. Using decoupled laser-detector capture, 3D foundation-model priors, and differentiable transient rendering, we reveals indirect light transport for applications including multi-bounce separation, time unwarping, and time-resolved relighting.

title: Gorgon Loop: An Interactive Art Installation Revealing Algorithmic Judgment through Machine Vision and Generative Language
speakers_or_contributors: Shuang Li; Patrick Hartono
affiliations: RMIT University Vietnam; 伦敦大学金史密斯学院
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Display; Ethics and Society; Generative AI; Hardware; Image Processing; Physical AI; Real-Time; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Gorgon Loop makes algorithmic judgement in public space visible as a collective, embodied process. Through machine vision, language models, and group commentary, it exposes how limited data, bias, and automation shape social evaluation, offering a critical lens on the cultural consequences of AI perception.

title: MultiAct: Text-to-Motion Generation from Composite Text via Tailored Attention Guidance
speakers_or_contributors: Nathan Sala; Ofir Abramovich; Ariel Shamir; Daniel Cohen-Or; Andreas Aristidou; Sigal Raab
affiliations: Reichman University; Tel Aviv University; University of Cyprus
description: Text-to-motion models struggle with composite prompts, often collapsing to a single action. We present MultiAct, an inference-time, unpaired framework that enhances pretrained generators by amplifying cross-attention for underrepresented tokens. A lightweight decision scheme selects optimal parameters per prompt, improving semantic coverage while preserving motion realism without retraining or architectural changes.

title: NeuPPS: Neural Piecewise Parametric Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Lei Yang; Yongqing Liang; Xin Li; Congyi Zhang; Guying Lin; Cheng Lin; Alla Sheffer; Scott Schaefer; John Keyser; Wenping Wang
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; Center for Robotics and Embodied Intelligence (CREO); Macau University of Science and Technology; Texas A&M University; The University of Hong Kong; University of British Columbia; University of Texas Dallas
description: We present Neural Piecewise Parametric Surfaces (NeuPPS) that models complex surface geometries with a coarse patch layout containing arbitrary n-sided surface patches, offering enhanced flexibility, high fitting precision, and ensured continuity between adjacent patches. Applications to surface fitting and shape space learning tasks demonstrate its advantages over existing methods.

title: Photons × Force: Differentiable Radiation Pressure Modeling
speakers_or_contributors: Charles Constant; Santosh Bhattarai; Elizabeth Bates; Marek Ziebart; Tobias Ritschel
affiliations: Alan Turing Institute; University College London (UCL)
description: We present a system for optimizing spacecraft designs under radiation pressure, the force that photons exert on objects. It combines a fast Monte Carlo simulation, a neural proxy of it, and gradient-based optimization to find spacecraft geometry, or operational parameters that minimize travel time or maximize proximity to a target.

title: Stranger Forces: Enhancing Virtual Production with Gen-AI in Real-World College Sponsored Studio Collaborations
speakers_or_contributors: Christopher Maraffi; Jedidiah Gant; Ryan Khan
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Education; Generative AI; Real-Time; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In spring 2025, NCSU MADTech faculty began teaching our students the targeted use of generative AI tools to solve real-world design problems in sponsored studio projects within our College of Design Virtual Production Lab. This presentation will show how we are teaching MADTech students the ethical integration of these gen-AI tools throughout our virtual production pipeline to better support human artists in achieving their creative visions and prepare students for a changing industry where such technologies are quickly becoming embedded.

title: Hands-on Course: Hands-on Programming and Running Quantum Teleportation
speakers_or_contributors: Andrew Glassner
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Education; Image Processing; Math Foundations and Theory; Performance; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In this hands-on programming course you'll learn how to write and run quantum computing code. Then you'll program the quantum teleportation algorithm, and run it on a high-quality simulator to transfer the state of a quantum bit to your partner, who will decode it to get your secret message.

title: Autoregressive Diffusion with Hybrid Representation for Interactive Human Motion Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Kaifeng Zhao; Mathis Petrovich; Haotian Zhang; Tingwu Wang; Siyu Tang; Davis Rempe
description: ARDY is a real-time autoregressive diffusion model for interactive 3D human motion generation. Utilizing a novel hybrid motion representation, ARDY empowers users to interactively synthesize high-fidelity animations directed by dynamic text prompts and flexible spatial constraints, such as full-body keyframes, trajectories, and end-effector joints.

title: Differentiable Neutron Transport
speakers_or_contributors: Xi Deng; Maosen Tang; Michael Czekanski; David Bindel; Steve Marschner
description: We present a differentiable Monte Carlo framework for multi-energy group neutron transport, extending ideas from differentiable rendering to nuclear engineering. The method enables gradient-based sensitivity analysis and inverse design.

title: NeuBase: Spline Surfaces with Neural Basis Functions
speakers_or_contributors: Anshul Mendiratta; Lei Yang; Xin Li; John Keyser; Scott Schaefer; Wenping Wang
affiliations: Texas A&M University; The University of Hong Kong
description: We introduce NeuBase, a neural parametric surface representation that both accurately fits target surfaces with fine geometric detail and supports intuitive real time surface deformation.

title: Policy-based Foveated Imaging and Perception
speakers_or_contributors: Howard Xiao; Jan Ackermann; Boyang Deng; Gordon Wetzstein
description: Ultra-high-resolution sensors capture fine details critical for visual perception, but acquiring all pixels at full resolution is often infeasible under realistic constraints. We introduce a real-time, task-aware foveated imaging framework that learns a sensor attention policy to dynamically allocate pixel bandwidth, extensively validated in simulation and on a 200-megapixel prototype.

title: Chaos | Cloud Arena: Bringing Real-Time Ray-Traced Virtual Production to the Global Cloud
speakers_or_contributors: Eric Pinkel
keywords_tags: Games; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
As Virtual Production continues to transform filmmaking, the demand for scalable, accessible infrastructure is growing beyond the walls of traditional studios. In this session, Eric Pinkel of CenterGrid will present Cloud Arena, a collaborative research initiative exploring how real-time ray-traced Virtual Production workflows can be deployed across globally distributed cloud infrastructure.

Using CenterGrid VFX Datacenter and Chaos Arena, the team successfully validated cloud-hosted rendering, cloud-hosted Arena services, and international operator workflows spanning North America and Europe. The presentation will walk through the project's milestone-based development process, system architecture, technical challenges, and key findings, including why synchronization and deterministic frame delivery have emerged as the next major frontier for cloud-native Virtual Production.

Attendees will gain insight into how cloud-based GPU workstations, remote rendering, and globally connected creative teams can expand access to Virtual Production technologies while enabling new workflows for studios, educators, and content creators worldwide.

speakers_or_contributors: Aline Normoyle; Bronwen Densmore
keywords_tags: Capture/Scanning; Education; Math Foundations and Theory; Virtual Reality

The projects involve hands-on construction and puppetry and also allow students to show their creativity.

title: From Computer Vision to the Vision of Law: Human Overs[a]ight
speakers_or_contributors: Kristina Tica; Joaquín Santuber
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Ethics and Society; Image Processing; Real-Time
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience

By bridging these disciplines, this translation aims to open a broader discourse on the topic from an innovative standpoint in media art that integrates legal matters, computer science, and a critical media theory approach.

speakers_or_contributors: Ryan Bottriell; Fran Zandonella Benjamin
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation

A casual discussion on production pipeline development. Come talk shop about the latest trends, common concerns, and best practices.

This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud, Pipeline, and Storage. Attendees will receive invites to our 'Beers of a Feather' event, the same evening.

title: Integrating Neural Graphics into Vulkan Rendering Pipelines on Arm: A Fireside Conversation with the Teams Behind Neural Dawn - Arm x Sumo
speakers_or_contributors: Lukas Medek; Peter Hodges; Sergio Alapont
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Industry Insight; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
"Neural graphics techniques have moved into production rendering workflows in games and this is becoming true in mobile. This fireside-style session brings together engineers and developers from Arm and Sumo Digital to discuss the development of Neural Dawn, a production-quality Unreal Engine 5 mobile title built using Arm Neural Technology.

The discussion focuses on the practical realities of integrating neural graphics into Vulkan rendering pipelines for mobile hardware. Topics include workload scheduling, frame graph integration, memory bandwidth constraints, debugging, performance validation, scalability across device classes, and balancing visual quality against demanding performance targets.

Panelists will also examine how Arm Vulkan extensions and neural technologies influenced rendering and engine decisions during development, alongside broader ecosystem considerations such as portability, tooling, and preparing for emerging neural graphics hardware capabilities.

Drawing on perspectives from engine developers, artists, and production teams, the session provides a grounded view of what it takes to move neural graphics from prototype to shipping content. Attendees will leave with practical insight into the technical tradeoffs and rendering architecture decisions involved in building real-world neural graphics workflows on Arm platforms."

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day: Autonomous Vehicle Simulation Technologies with NuRec, 3D Gaussian, Cosmos-Dreams
speakers_or_contributors: Darian Hogue; Itai Zadok; Jialiang Wang
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Physical AI; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Last year generating five seconds of photorealistic simulated driving took minutes of compute. Today, you can interactively drive through a world that has never existed in real time, rendered frame by frame on a single GPU. This talk covers two complementary approaches that make this possible: neural scene reconstruction from real-world captures with NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec and generative scenario synthesis with NVIDIA Cosmos-Dreams. It will cover open problems and solutions in scene representation, temporal consistency, and controllable generation, all core computer graphics challenges.

title: Running large-scale, multi-site VFX productions in the real world: what's working & what to fix?
speakers_or_contributors: David Conley; Simon Hedges; Aleks Pejic
keywords_tags: Animation; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This BOF session is a candid conversation about navigating today’s global VFX landscape. We’ll move beyond tax incentive headlines to focus on what truly makes geographically-distributed productions succeed: robust creative pipelines, effective cross-site collaboration, and empowering technology.

We’ll discuss practical realities coordinating work across timezones and cultures, examining how to harness the diverse strengths of global teams to turn geographical distribution into an advantage. We'll address client expectations, how studios balance tax incentive pressures with maintaining creative quality, and how technology helps companies prepare for a future without boundaries.

Together, let’s build the roadmap for our industry’s next chapter.

title: The Future of Web3D: Standards, Performance, and Immersive Experiences
speakers_or_contributors: ANITA HAVELE
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Digital Twins; Real-Time; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing

Whether you're building immersive web experiences, developing 3D tools, or shaping the future of open standards, this is your opportunity to connect with peers, share insights, and influence the direction of Web3D technologies. Come prepared to discuss what's working, what needs improvement, and where we go next.

speakers_or_contributors: Maia Kayser

speakers_or_contributors: Brittany Morago

title: Orchestrating Multimodal Control for Unified Audio-Visual Synthesis
speakers_or_contributors: Naomi ken korem; Urska Jelercic
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Generative AI; Rendering

Beyond text prompts, we demonstrate precise control via audio, video, imagery, and spatial data. Attendees will learn dual-stream transformer architectural innovations and practical techniques for orchestrating high-fidelity, aligned multimodal generation

title: The Previs Battleground: Precision Planning vs Creative Freedom
speakers_or_contributors: Megha Davalath; Todd Jansen; Adam Holmes; Karl Herbst; Jon Teer; Dave Clayton; Kyle Winkelman
affiliations: Blizzard Entertainment; Industrial Light & Magic; Netflix Animation Studios; Sony Pictures Animation; Sony Pictures Imageworks; Wētā FX
description: This panel examines how previs is evolving across Animation, VFX, and Games, and the trade-offs between fast, exploratory workflows and high-fidelity planning. Industry leaders share how different previs strategies shape creative iteration, technical pipelines, budgets, and downstream production decisions in real-world environments.

title: What Makes AI Slop (Or Not)
speakers_or_contributors: Lauren Morrison
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
description:
Audiences have shown a clear disdain for AI generated imagery, calling anything that’s recognizably AI “slop”. But it’s undeniable that AI opens up new possibilities for artistic pipelines, and the technology isn’t going away.

What is AI actually capable of, and how can we balance production constraints with artistic integrity?

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - AGILE: Hand-object Interaction Reconstruction from Video via Agentic Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Hao Chen
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
AGILE: Hand-object Interaction Reconstruction from Video via Agentic Generation

AGILE reconstructs 3D hand-object interactions from monocular videos via agentic generation. It produces complete, simulation-ready object meshes and robustly estimates object pose without SfM, enabling accurate and stable results on challenging real-world data.

Paper Presentation Presented: Monday, July 20, 3:45-5:35 pm; Paper Session: Humans & Hands - Reconstruction; Room 403 A

title: Building the Interactive Future: Inside Tripo's Full-Stack 3D Generation Ecosystem
speakers_or_contributors: Tripo AI Research Team
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Generative AI; Modeling
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
While text and image AI have seamlessly integrated into daily workflows, 3D generation remains uniquely challenging. In this session, the Tripo AI research team examines why 3D demands specialized architectures, and demonstrates the full-stack generative ecosystem built to address it.

Moving beyond the generation of unstructured meshes and baked textures, the team will detail Tripo's latest foundational models, highlighting H-series for high-fidelity geometry and texturing, alongside P-series for extremely fast, production-grade mesh topology. Through live demonstrations, attendees will see how these architectures output clean assets complete with semantic segmentation and automatic rigging, i.e., capabilities that are directly integrated into pipelines for gaming, 3D printing, and interactive media.

The session concludes by shifting focus from object generation to environment simulation. Leveraging massive-scale architectures and multimodal integration, the Tripo team will preview their ongoing R&D in Generative World Models, demonstrating how the field is advancing toward dynamic environments, agentic systems, and world modeling

title: Montage as Pedagogical Scaﬀold for Reasoning Beyond Language
speakers_or_contributors: Xiaodi Tang; Robert Rapoport; David Lin
keywords_tags: Art; Education; Ethics and Society; Image Processing; Pipeline Tools and Work
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Multimodal reasoning models have introduced a disruptive, still-insufficiently elaborated shift in how students learn and create–functioning as what some might call a "collaborative partner." The crux of the issue, however, is that this human-machine interaction carries an inherent asymmetry: the machine holds a partial view of context, stakes, and tacit/embodied knowledge involved.

This BoF gathers practitioners to share strategies for working and teaching in algorithmically opaque creative environments. As an opening provocation, we'll explore possibilities latent-space visualization tools bring for latent-space navigation, then open the floor: What tacit knowledge manifests here? What pedagogies are emerging in your practice?

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | Introduction to Newton: Open-Source GPU Physics for Robotics
speakers_or_contributors: Louis Lian; Miles Macklin
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Researchers and developers need faster, physically grounded simulation to model complex interactions and close the sim-to-real gap across diverse robot embodiments. Newton is an open-source, GPU-accelerated physics engine built on NVIDIA Warp. With OpenUSD integration, Newton enables real-time simulation of rigid and soft bodies, contact, friction, and actuators for advancing robot learning and simulation research

title: AI, The End of Hollywood, and What Comes Next
speakers_or_contributors: Charlie Fink
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: Dramatic changes in audience behavior, platforms, and viewing habits combined with runaway production to create an existential crisis in Hollywood, even before the arrival of Cinematic AI. While AI is both an accelerant and a solution, the long-term reality is that the algorithm will transform media as we know it.

title: Animating Engagement: Connecting Technology, Artistry, and Purpose in a Small Public University
speakers_or_contributors: Damian Thorn-Hauswirth
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This talk presents four project-based learning experiences from a small public university animation program, demonstrating how connecting technology, artistry, and purpose fosters student growth and engagement. Through classroom, collaborative, and community-based projects, the talk offers transferable strategies for supporting diverse student populations in evolving creative fields.

title: Blurring the Line Between Real and Digital Water in Avatar: Fire and Ash
speakers_or_contributors: Sam Cole; Nicholas Illingworth; Alexey Stomakhin; Sean Flynn
keywords_tags: Animation; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Simulation
description: Avatar: Fire and Ash required geometric consistency between photographed and simulated water in chaotic tank environments over long frame sequences, with characters interacting directly with the surface. We describe a feature-driven approach for recovering temporally coherent water motion to support stable parametric modeling and simulation coupling.

title: Bridging Artistic Expression and Game Design through Cross-Cultural Collaboration: An Educational Framework from a Seven-Year Japan–U.S. Program
speakers_or_contributors: Nahomi Maki; Mitsuko Okamoto; Takashi Kiriyama; Peter Brinson; Andreas Kratky
affiliations: Tokyo University of the Arts; University of Southern California
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This paper reports an eight-year Japan–U.S. collaborative game-making program between an art university and a game program. We present a transferable educational framework that bridges artistic expression and system-oriented game design. Qualitative observation identifies three strategies: role-fluid teams, intent-driven interaction design goals, and the pedagogical use of cross-cultural differences.

title: Thick Foam and Floating Debris in Avatar: Fire and Ash
speakers_or_contributors: Job Guidos; Joel Wretborn; Sean Flynn; Jason Lazaroff; Tanē MacDonald; Ari Ross
description: In this talk we discuss advancements in foam and floating debris simulation made to our in-house water system, Pahi, to deliver close-up fidelity of those effects in Avatar: Fire and Ash.

title: Creating the Elemental Vortex in Avatar: Fire and Ash
speakers_or_contributors: Nicholas Illingworth; Alexey Stomakhin; Murali Ramachari; James Robinson; John Edholm; Sam Cole
keywords_tags: Animation; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Simulation
description: The Flux Devil, an elemental vortex in the climactic sequence of Avatar: Fire and Ash, is made of plasma, water and fire. To create an art-directable effect, while adhering to the laws of nature, we implemented specialized emission tools, warped gravity fields for buoyant updrafts and spatially varying Coriolis forces.

title: From “Is It Real?” to “Is It Defensible?”: Governing AI-Assisted Media Production
speakers_or_contributors: Nira Bozkurt
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Ethics and Society; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: Professional GenAI production lacks auditable decision-making, failing legal scrutiny. Shifting from authenticity to "defensibility," Verifiable Story Production (VSP) operationalizes responsibility via Phase 0 (Human Intent) and four pillars: Sovereign Ingest, Constitutional Guardrails, Persistent Provenance, and Human Validation. VSP is an open standard now being evaluated in film school projects.

title: CANCELED: AI3D Frontiers: AI for 3D BOF #4
speakers_or_contributors: Yosun Chang
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Fabrication; Games; Graphics Systems Architecture; Pipeline Tools and Work; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Your favorite non-commercial, honestly live-demo-focused BOF continues as a place for polymath hackers, artists, researchers, and independent creators to show what they are actually building — from XR to SOTA gen AI 3D agents to the strange, early, hybrid things that do not yet fit neatly into a paper, product category, or formal session.

Come share what you are working on, compare notes from the frontier, and help define what AI for 3D is becoming.

title: Sustainable Research in Computer Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Ruben Wiersma; Chenxi Liu; Emilie Yu
affiliations: Adobe Research; University of California Santa Barbara; University of Toronto
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
From climate change to biodiversity loss and resource exhaustion, human activities are impacting Earth’s limits and Computer Graphics is no exception. How can our research practices and organizations evolve to respect these limits?

After the success of last year’s BoF, we continue building a community of people who want to think about the broader impacts of our research and how to collectively work towards a more sustainable future.

This interactive meetup session will allow attendees to share experiences and questions on related topics in small groups, regardless of their current levels of involvement or expertise in sustainable approaches to research.

title: Critical Labor History of Generative Models
speakers_or_contributors: Ana Dodik
keywords_tags: Art; Ethics and Society; Generative AI
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Research & Education
description: We introduce SIGGRAPH to critical theories of generative models, focusing on socioeconomic relations to research and art. We introduce critical theory through computing's relationship to commodification and labor and contrast two views of generative models: one as the automation of labor and another examining their role in altering human sociality.

title: From Spark to Inferno: Scalable Stylized Fire in Disney and Pixar’s Hoppers
speakers_or_contributors: Greg Gladstone; Max Gilbert; Linhan Li; Alexis Angelidis; Krzysztof Rost; Jon Reisch
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Dynamics; Lighting; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Simulation
description: In the film Hoppers, a forest fire rages and engulfs the glade which Mabel swore to protect. We describe how we crafted this fire through our production strategy, looks and rig development, fire ‘taxonomy’ and dressing, lighting and compositing workflows, and optimizations such as an optimized fire lighting representation.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH Pioneers Reception
speakers_or_contributors: Edward Kramer; Edward Kramer
description: Yearly Pioneers social gathering with light appetizers and a cash bar. No agenda, just schmoozing with old friends and colleagues. Admission by Pioneer membership as printed on your badge.

title: Real-Time Live!
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Dynamics; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Modeling; Performance; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation

description:
Real-Time Live! Meet and Greet

Location: Hall K Lobby

title: Pathfinders
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: FIRST-TIME ATTENDEE RESOURCE: Pathfinders will be available to help you find your way. Pathfinders is a volunteer group dedicated to mentoring first-time conference visitors. The Pathfinders team can offer detailed explanations and descriptions of all SIGGRAPH programs or venues, and help you build a schedule based on your personal skills and interests.

title: Real-Time Live! Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: From Splats to Worlds: Advanced 3DGS Applications
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Rubloff; Ian Williams; Amanda Morgan; Daniel Zhong; Dr. Cai
affiliations: Bentley Systems; Cesium; Huawei; NVIDIA; Radiancefields.com
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Generative AI; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation; Spatial Computing
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
"Gaussian splatting has rapidly evolved from a novel radiance field reconstruction technique into a versatile foundation for real-time rendering, volumetric media, and spatial computing. This BoF session brings together researchers, engineers, and industry practitioners to explore the progression from 3D to 4D Gaussian representation.
Discussions will cover compression; animated Gaussian splatting; volumetric media from capture through distribution; 3DGS as a building block for world models bridging neural scene understanding with real-time engines; and the potential for 3DGS as a real-world archival format.
Come share progress, surface interoperability challenges, and help align where the ecosystem is heading.

title: 3D Jobs Outside of Entertainment - A Panel by The 3D Artist Community
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Tanzillo
keywords_tags: Animation; Digital Twins; Games; Industry Insight
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Ever thought about taking your 3D skills beyond entertainment but had no idea where to start?
Join a panel of working 3D artists who've built careers outside of VFX, gaming, and animation. They'll share how they made the leap into industries like fashion, product visualization, automotive design, motion graphics, and more.

Whether you're curious about a pivot or just want to know what's out there, this conversation will open your eyes to how far your skills can take you.

Come with questions!

title: Advances in Real-time Rendering in Games, Part I
speakers_or_contributors: Natalya Tatarchuk; Jon Greenberg; Daniel Craig; Michał Olejnik
affiliations: Activision; EA SEED; Sony Interactive Entertainment
description:
Modern video games employ a variety of sophisticated algorithms to produce groundbreaking 3D rendering pushing the visual boundaries and interactive experience of rich environments. This course brings state-of-the-art and production-proven rendering techniques for fast, interactive rendering of complex and engaging virtual worlds of video games.

This is the course to attend if you are in the game development industry or want to learn the latest and greatest techniques in the real-time rendering domain!

title: Code & Dev 1
keywords_tags: Education; Games; Image Processing; Modeling; Real-Time
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience

title: Fluid Reconstruction & Optimization
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Real-Time; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation

title: GauSmoke: Hybrid Physics-Optical Gaussian Splatting for Sparse Smoke Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Wenran Zhang; Yuxiang Cai; Letian Huang; Dongwei Ye; Jie Guo; Ren Bo
affiliations: Nanjing University; Nankai University; State Key Lab for Novel Software Technology, Nanjing University
description: We present a physics-aware method for reconstructing dynamic fluids from sparse-view videos. By integrating volumetric rendering with physically guided Gaussian optimization, it enforces consistency in density and motion, reducing artifacts and improving realism. The approach achieves accurate, stable 3D smoke reconstruction with high visual and physical fidelity.

title: Generative 3D (2)
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Geometry; Modeling; Rendering; Robotics

title: Human–AI Co-Creation in Generative Art: Graphics Methods, Systems, and Applications
speakers_or_contributors: Ying Jiang; Jiayin Lu; Yin Yang; Hongbo Fu; Chenfanfu Jiang; Yunuo Chen; Masha Shugrina; Adriana Schulz; Yael Vinker; Zeyu Wang
affiliations: Brown University; Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); HKUST; NVIDIA; Stanford University; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou); University of California Los Angeles; University of California Santa Barbara; University of Utah
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Virtual Reality
description: Recent advances in generative AI are transforming how visual and spatial content is created, enabling new forms of collaboration between humans and intelligent systems. This workshop explores human–AI co-creation in generative art, where AI acts not merely as an automation tool but as a creative partner supporting exploration, iteration, and artistic expression. The workshop brings together researchers, artists, and engineers from computer graphics, machine learning, human–computer interaction, and computational design to examine emerging systems for collaborative creativity. Topics include interactive creative tools, generative models for visual and 3D art, and fabrication-aware design systems that connect digital creation with real-world production. The workshop will feature keynote talks and interactive discussions exploring questions such as artistic control in generative systems, effective interfaces for co-creation, and integrating fabrication constraints into generative design, aiming to inspire new directions for human–AI collaboration in generative art and design. More details in: https://human-ai-cocreation.github.io/

title: Hydra in Production
speakers_or_contributors: Thomas Cauchois; Joe Longson; Anna Reid; Brett Levin; Björn Siegert; David Lanier; Paolo Selva; Raja Bala
affiliations: 880 Productions NZ; Autodesk; Pixar Animation Studios; Walt Disney Animation Studios
keywords_tags: Animation; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
description: Based on real production examples, Hydra in Production will present best practices and case studies of how to integrate OpenUSD's Hydra library into a content creation tool or pipeline, and leverage it to solve complex pipeline challenges and enable powerful workflows. This is a companion course to USD in Production.

title: Infant Vision: Reconfiguring Perception in Mixed Reality
speakers_or_contributors: Wei Chen Yen; Seth Riskin; Chun-Cheng Hsu
affiliations: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); national yang ming chiao tung university
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This work interweaves vision science with perceptual experience in art. Leveraging MR technology, it renders the infant's mysterious vision into a first-person experiential artwork. For the SIGGRAPH community, the project serves as a case study of how scientific theories and domain knowledge can be transformed into an embodied, explorable experience. Through exhibition, we found that Infant Vision opens other senses and encourages participants to reimagine the world.

title: LLM-enhanced Scene Graph Learning for Household Rearrangement
speakers_or_contributors: Wenhao Li; Shilong Zou; Zhinan Yu; Zheng Zhou; Wenxuan Li; Chenyang Zhu; Ruizhen Hu; Kai Xu
affiliations: Institute of AI for Industries Chinese Academy of Science; National University of Defense Technology; Shenzhen University
description: We present an LLM-enhanced scene graph framework for autonomous household rearrangement. By transforming scene graphs into affordance-enhanced graphs that encode scene-grounded object functionality and user preferences, our method detects misplaced objects and plans correct placements. We demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with an end-to-end robotic system in simulated and real-world environments.

title: Layouts & Procedural
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Geometry; Modeling; Rendering

title: Meet Mingle and Make a Difference
speakers_or_contributors: Mashhuda Glencross; Alain Chesnais; Nick Shelton; Alex Bryant
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Display; Dynamics; Education; Ethics and Society; Fabrication; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Haptics; Hardware; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Performance; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Join us for a relaxed and informative mingling event designed for those interested in exploring volunteering opportunities within the ACM SIGGRAPH organisation and in SIGGRAPH Conferences. This is a great chance to meet current key volunteers and leadership, learn about the various roles and committees that you could get involved in, and discover how your skills and passions could make a meaningful impact to our community. Whether you're looking to expand your professional network, gain new experiences, or give back to the community, this event provides a valuable opportunity to get involved with SIGGRAPH!

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | World Models : The Frontier of Physical AI, Unlocked By Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Ming-Yu Liu
keywords_tags: Computer Vision; Generative AI; Physical AI; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Physical AI begins with the ability to see, understand, generate, and simulate the world. In this opening address, Ming-Yu Liu will explore why world models are becoming one of AI’s most exciting frontiers, and why 3D graphics is central to the shift. The session will dive into the latest open frontier world model, NVIDIA Cosmos 3, exploring its breakthrough architecture built to accelerate vision reasoning, synthetic data generation, and policy training. Attendees will also learn how generative AI, simulation technologies, and agentic AI are converging to advance physical AI.

title: No Engine Required: Teaching Game Programming (Mostly) From Scratch
speakers_or_contributors: Sanjay Madhav; Matthew Whiting
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This talk discusses the course philosophy, design, and implementation details of the undergraduate "Video Game Programming" course at the University of Southern California, which has students program both 2D and 3D games largely from scratch.
