title: SegviGen: Repurposing 3D Generative Model for Part Segmentation
speakers_or_contributors: Lin Li; Haoran Feng; Zehuan Huang; Haohua Chen; Wenbo Nie; Shaohua Hou; Keqing Fan; Pan Hu; Sheng Wang; Buyu Li; Lu Sheng
affiliations: Bambu Lab; Beihang University; Beijing Jiaotong University; Renmin University of China; Tsinghua University
description: SegviGen is a framework for 3D part segmentation which unifies three settings in one architecture: interactive part segmentation, full segmentation, and 2D segmentation map–guided full segmentation. It improves over sota by 40% on interactive segmentation and by 15% on full segmentation, while using only 0.32% of the training data.

title: State of Animation Tools in the industry
speakers_or_contributors: Stuart Bryson; Rob Jensen
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
We invite animators, riggers, and engineers to discuss animation tools (third party and
proprietary), workflows and technology trends in the industry.

title: VFX Reference Platform - A Common Platform for VFX Software
speakers_or_contributors: Francois Chardavoine; Jean-Francois Panisset
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Hear the latest on the VFX Reference Platform (vfxplatform.com) and discuss software versioning challenges and opportunities with your peers from both studios and software vendors.

title: Video Analysis and Generation via a Semantic Progress Function
speakers_or_contributors: Gal Metzer; Sagi Polaczek; Arash Mahdavi-Amiri; Raja Giryes; Daniel Cohen-Or
affiliations: Simon Fraser University; Tel Aviv University
description: This paper introduces a Semantic Progress Function - a one-dimensional representation capturing the semantic pace of a given video. Building on this, a semantic linearization procedure is introduced to retime sequences and control the rate at which semantic change unfolds, yielding smoother, more coherent transitions.

title: Video Gen & Camera Control
CasLayout: Cascaded 3D Layout Diffusion for Indoor Scene Synthesis with Implicit Relation Modeling
speakers_or_contributors: Yingrui Wu; You-Kang Kong; Mingyang Zhao; Weize Quan; Dongmin Yan; Yang Liu
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Microsoft Research Asia; State Key Laboratory of Mathematical Sciences, Academy of Mathematics and Systems Science, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Tsinghua University
description: CasLayout is a cascaded diffusion framework that decomposes 3D indoor scene generation into four interpretable stages. This design reduces data requirements while enabling flexible, controllable synthesis and seamless integration with LLMs and VLMs for zero-shot tasks.

title: Go-with-the-Track: Video Compositing and Motion Control with Point Tracking
speakers_or_contributors: Koichi Namekata; Yash Kant; Zhizheng Liu; Ryan Burgert; Yuancheng Xu; Kuan Heng Lin; Emmett Steven; Julien Philip; Li Ma; Andrea Vedaldi; Paul Debevec; Ning Yu
affiliations: Eyeline Labs; Netflix; University of Oxford
description: We present Go-with-the-Track, a video generation framework conditioned on multiple reference images and point-tracks that jointly anchor the generated frames and the references. Our approach enables precise control over motion and multi-reference compositing, unlocking a wide range of applications, including video restylization, keypoint- and mesh-driven compositing, and camera motion control.

title: LagrangianSplats: Divergence-Free Transport of Gaussian Primitives for Fluid Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Ningxiao Tao; Mengyu Chu; Baoquan Chen
affiliations: Peking University; School of Intelligence Science and Technology, Peking University
description: LagrangianSplats reconstructs physically plausible 3D fluid velocity fields from sparse video by combining divergence-free kernels with Lagrangian Gaussian transport. A sliding-window optimization enables efficient long-range supervision, producing state-of-the-art transport consistency and physical accuracy on synthetic and real fluid datasets.

title: SimArt: Decomposing Monolithic Meshes into Sim-ready Articulated Assets via MLLM
speakers_or_contributors: Chuanrui Zhang; Minghan Qin; Yuang Wang; Baifeng Xie; Hang Li; Ziwei Wang
affiliations: ByteDance Seed; Nanyang Technological University, Singapore
description: SIMART is a unified multimodal large language model framework that converts static 3D meshes into simulation-ready articulated assets. By jointly modeling part decomposition and kinematics with a sparse 3D VQ-VAE, it reduces token complexity and enables scalable, high-fidelity object generation for robotics and physics-based simulation.

title: Fast VEM Fluid Simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Runze Zhang; Bo Ren
description: FastVEM is an efficient boundary-conforming fluid simulation framework. It combines Virtual Element discretization, a simulation-friendly body-fitted grid construction strategy, and a tailored geometric multigrid method to achieve robust, high-fidelity, and efficient fluid–boundary interaction.

title: Neural Cellular Automata: From Cells to Pixels
speakers_or_contributors: Ehsan Pajouheshgar; Yitao Xu; Ali Abbasi; Alexander Mordvintsev; Wenzel Jakob; Sabine Süsstrunk
affiliations: EPFL; Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne; Google Research
description: We pair Neural Cellular Automata with a lightweight Neural Field that decodes coarse cell states and local coordinates into high-resolution appearance, decoupling lattice size from output resolution. Trained end-to-end with task-specific losses, our model synthesizes textures and grows morphologies at arbitrary resolution across 2D/3D/mesh in real-time while preserving self-organizing behavior.

title: Raster2Seq: Polygon Sequence Generation for Floorplan Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Hao Phung; Hadar Averbuch-Elor
description: Raster2Seq is an anchor-based autoregressive formulation that frames floorplan reconstruction as scalable, sequential polygon prediction, transforming rasterized floorplan images into vectorized format. Through using our proposed labeled polygon sequence representation, the method jointly encodes spatial and semantic attributes, overcoming fixed-query budget limitations while significantly improving robustness as floorplan complexity increases.

title: ReRoPE: Repurposing RoPE for Relative Camera Control
speakers_or_contributors: Chunyang Li; Yuanbo Yang; Jiahao Shao; Hongyu Zhou; Katja Schwarz; Yiyi Liao
description: ReRoPE is a plug-and-play framework for controllable video generation that achieves precise camera control by injecting relative pose information into the underutilized low-frequency bands of standard Rotary Positional Embeddings (RoPE).

title: Resonance: Meditative Neural Rhythms as Collective Spatial Experience
speakers_or_contributors: Ruipeng Wang; Yuxiang Cheng; Zhiyan Xing; Behnaz Farahi
affiliations: Harvard University; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Media Lab, Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
keywords_tags: Art; Dynamics; Hardware; Lighting; Performance; Real-Time; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Resonance transforms a traditionally solitary meditative practice into a shared spatial experience by externalizing a meditator's neural rhythms as architectural-scale light and motion. Rather than treating neurofeedback as private data, the work situates cognition within an immersive environment that can be collectively sensed and inhabited. Bridging contemplative traditions with computational design, it expands bio-responsive systems beyond subjective interaction toward shared, embodied engagement and collective awareness.

title: Teaching Building Game Engines: An entry-level course on game programming without pre-built Game Engines
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Shah
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In this paper, we present our singular course on teaching fundamental game programming skills by building game engines from scratch. The course is designed to provide a pathway for students in game development as an entry level course, while providing students a pathway to learning industry specific technologies later on.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Animating Creatures of the Upside Down with Wētā FX: The Creepy Realism of Stranger Things Season 5
speakers_or_contributors: Dave Clayton
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
In this talk, Dave Clayton, five-time Academy and BAFTA awards-nominated Animation Supervisor at Wētā FX, will break down some of the techniques used to imbue the creatures and characters of Stranger Things with a new level of creepy realism. Coming on board for the iconic show’s final season, Wētā FX contributed more than 1,100 shots across all eight episodes, for which they received 4 VES nominations.

Starting with Vecna, Dave will review how the team built the villain on concept art from the client, and extended his look and build from prior seasons. From his deadly vines coiling and extending from within his body, to the surprising methods used to arrive at Vecna's ‘charred’ makeover.

He’ll also explain the process of bringing the Demogorgons and Demodogs to new scary-real creative heights though the use of mocap, rag-doll dynamics, muscle simulations and keyframe animation — making them feel a natural part of the Stranger Things creature menagerie.

Finally he’ll dive into the team’s work on the ever-looming Mindflayer: how they animated continuous action to help bring the grand battle finale to a pulse-pounding climax, while crafting its motion to reinforce its scale, weight, and terror. This is a talk that any fan of cutting-edge animation and creature work, or of Stranger Things, shouldn't miss!

title: Chasing De’Snake! - Developing the Worlds and Characters of Disney’s “Zootopia 2”
speakers_or_contributors: Jack Fulmer; Adam Green; Kira Lehtomaki; David Hutchins; Tyler Kupferer; Daniel Rice
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Geometry; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Simulation
description:
When building upon a beloved franchise like Zootopia, the artists of Walt Disney Animation Studios had to consider every detail that makes the world unique and its characters memorable. By combining the famous bunny and fox duo with a herd of new animated animals and environments, artists developed new rules and techniques to expand the world in genuine and exciting ways.

In this session, the “Zootopia 2” leadership team will take you on a wild journey through the film’s worldbuilding. They will explore what it took to visualize marshes, mountains, deserts, and tundra, and discuss the development of new characters like Gary De’Snake and the Lynxley family. They will also share new workflows and tools throughout the discussion that allowed for collaboration across multiple departments, and enabled the crew to tackle an adventurous and expansive body of work.

title: DiffSurFlow: Efficient and Robust Differentiable Fluid Optimization via Surrogate Strategy on Flow Map
speakers_or_contributors: Yuhao Quan; Hui Wang; Weile Lian; Zhi Wang; Xubo Yang
description: We present DiffSurFlow, an efficient and robust framework for long-horizon and vortex-rich fluid optimization. Powered by a physics-informed surrogate gradient strategy that exploits flow map "gradient highways" while pruning computationally intensive high-order gradient passes, it achieves significant speedups and memory reductions while maintaining near-identical gradient accuracy.

title: FreeOrbit4D: Training-Free Arbitrary Camera Redirection for Monocular Videos via Foreground-Complete 4D Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Wei Cao; Hao Zhang; Fengrui Tian; Yulun Wu; Yingying Li; Shenlong Wang; Ning Yu; Yaoyao Liu
affiliations: Eyeline Labs; University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign; University of Pennsylvania
description: FreeOrbit4D is a training-free framework for camera redirection from monocular videos. By decoupling foreground and background reconstruction and completing foreground geometry via multi-view diffusion, it builds a foreground-complete 4D proxy that guides video generation, enabling faithful and temporally coherent novel views under large-angle camera trajectories.

title: Neural Particle Automata: Learning Self-Organizing Particle Dynamics
speakers_or_contributors: Ehsan Pajouheshgar; Hyunsoo Kim; Sabine Süsstrunk; Wenzel Jakob; Jinah Park
affiliations: EPFL; Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne; Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)
description: Neural Particle Automata extend Neural Cellular Automata from fixed grids to dynamic particles. Each particle carries a continuous position and internal state, updated by a shared neural rule with SPH-based local perception. Backed by optimized CUDA kernels, NPA learn scalable self-organizing dynamics for morphogenesis, particle texture synthesis, and point-cloud classification.

title: Toward Richer Material Generation via Procedural Data Enhancement
speakers_or_contributors: Yunchen Yu; Jacob Munkberg; Jon Hasselgren; Chris Cummings; Steve Marschner; Andrea Weidlich
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Modeling
description: Generative material models are limited by simple PBR data. We augment single-lobe GGX materials into layered, multi-lobe models capturing richer effects (e.g., dust, clearcoat). These are encoded as neural materials in a shared 6D latent space. The resulting dataset enables generative models to produce more expressive materials.

title: Generic Variational Spacetime Optimization of Vortex Core Manifolds
speakers_or_contributors: Xingdi Zhang; Peter Rautek; Markus Hadwiger
affiliations: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
description: We introduce a highly efficient variational framework for computing optimal vortex cores in 3D unsteady flows. By unifying diverse detection criteria through time-preintegrated Lagrangians, our method accurately reconstructs spacetime vortex manifolds by solving Euler-Lagrange equations in just one time step, enabling rapid and robust flow analysis.

title: InfiniteDiffusion: Bridging Learned Fidelity and Procedural Utility for Open-World Terrain Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Alexander Goslin
description: We introduce InfiniteDiffusion, a training-free algorithm enabling diffusion models to generate spatially infinite outputs with the core properties of procedural noise: seed-consistency (determinism) and constant-time random access. We incorporate a novel architecture for terrain generation to produce Terrain Diffusion, a learned, real-time, infinite terrain generator with a procedural noise-like interface.

title: PixTex: Consistent 3D Texturing via Pixel-Space Multi-View Diffusion
speakers_or_contributors: Yuqing Zhang; Yan-Pei Cao; Hao Xu; Yiqian Wu; Sirui Lin; Yuqing Wang; Ding Liang; Yuan-Chen Guo; Xiaogang Jin
affiliations: State Key Lab of CAD&CG, Zhejiang; State Key Lab of CAD&CG, Zhejiang University; VAST; Zhejiang University
description: We propose PixTex, the first pixel-space multi-view diffusion framework for texture generation. By bypassing latent compression and utilizing a coarse-to-fine strategy, it ensures lossless geometric guidance and superior pixel-level multi-view consistency, achieving state-of-the-art, high-fidelity, and seamless results.

title: Sew and Code! Understanding How to Construct Bezier Curves
speakers_or_contributors: Brittany Morago
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The Sew and Code assignment is designed to fuse programming, math, algorithms, art, and design and provide motivation to Computer Graphics students learning how to construct Bezier curves using the DeCastlejau Algorithm. Students cycle between sewing then programming steps of the algorithm, building up String Art and interactive programs simultaneously.

title: The Adaptation Threshold in Mixed Reality Theater: Cognitive Integration and Narrative Reality
speakers_or_contributors: Nils Gallist; David Gochfeld; Lino Brunmayr
affiliations: Agile Lens: Immersive Design; Media Interaction Lab, University of Applied Sciences Upper Austria, Hagenberg; University of Applied Sciences Upper Austria
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Augmented Reality; Performance; Real-Time; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Mixed-reality technologies increasingly enter theatrical and artistic practice, and questions of perception, presence, and staging become as important as technical feasibility. This paper focuses on audience experience, stylization, and artistic intention. The project demonstrates that mixed reality can function as a meaningful dramaturgical tool rather than as a spectacle. The concept of an adaptation threshold offers artists a practical lens for understanding when technology supports narrative engagement.

title: UCM: Unified Modeling of Camera Control and Memory with Time-aware Positional Encoding Warping for World Models
speakers_or_contributors: Tian-Xing Xu; Zi-Xuan Wang; Guangyuan Wang; Li Hu; Zhongyi Zhang; Peng Zhang; Bang Zhang; Song-Hai Zhang
affiliations: Alibaba Group; CS Dept, Tsinghua University; Tsinghua University; University of Science and Technology of China
description: We propose UCM, a novel framework for the unified modeling of long-term scene consistency and camera controllability in video generation-based world models. Trained on over 500K monocular videos, UCM achieves high-fidelity world exploration capability with excellent generalizability to open-world environments.

title: CameraSquad: Achieving Content Consistency in Parallel Multi-Trajectory Camera-Controlled Video Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Zhufeng Xu; Xuan Gao; Bailin Deng; Yikang Ding; Xiaoqiang Liu; Haoxian Zhang; Pengfei Wan; Hongbo Fu; Lin Gao
affiliations: HKUST; Institute of Computing Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Kling Team, Kuaishou Technology; School of Computer Science and Informatics, Cardiff University
description: CameraSquad is a multi-trajectory camera control framework that supports both single-trajectory and parallel multi-trajectory video generation. By decoupling content and camera control mechanisms and using a dual-mode cross-view attention mechanism, it ensures viewpoint consistency and camera control precision in multi-trajectory mode.

title: Generative 3D Gaussians with Learned Density Control
speakers_or_contributors: Runjie Yan; Yan-Pei Cao; Peng Wang; Ding Liang; Yuan-Chen Guo
affiliations: Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences, Tsinghua University; VAST
description: DeG is an image-to-3D generative framework for Gaussian splatting that learns to place detail adaptively. It evaluates each Gaussian’s effect on rendering loss, then reinforces density in important regions and suppresses less useful ones, enabling variable-resolution, high-quality 3D asset generation.

title: RCGP: Resource Contracts for Graphics Programming
speakers_or_contributors: Venkataram Sivaram; Sai Praveen Bangaru; Ravi Ramamoorthi; Tzu-Mao Li; Jonathan Ragan-Kelley; Fredo Durand
affiliations: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Massachusetts Institute of Technology - EECS; NVIDIA; University of California San Diego
description: RCGP introduces a type system for graphics programming that statically enforces resource contracts, the agreements on type, layout, binding, and synchronization between shaders, pipelines, and host code. Through contracts, modules, combinators, and witnesses, RCGP catches descriptor mismatches, layout drift, and missing barriers at compile time, eliminating common classes of errors.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - GauSmoke: Hybrid Physics-Optical Gaussian Splatting for Sparse Smoke Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Ren Bo
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
GauSmoke: Hybrid Physics-Optical Gaussian Splatting for Sparse Smoke Reconstruction

We present a physics-aware method for reconstructing dynamic fluids from sparse-view videos. By integrating volumetric rendering with physically guided Gaussian optimization, it enforces consistency in density and motion, reducing artifacts and improving realism. The approach achieves accurate, stable 3D smoke reconstruction with high visual and physical fidelity.

Paper Presentation Presented: Tuesday, July 21, 9-10:30 am; Paper Session: Fluid Reconstruction & Optimization; Room 408 A

title: Prana Labs | Seeing is Believing, Grasping is Physics: Generative Environments with Physically-Grounded Robot Manipulation
speakers_or_contributors: Fan Shi
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Physical AI; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
This demo is a collaboration between Tripo and Prana that pairs generative 3D content with physically grounded simulation for robot manipulation. Tripo generates the functional 3D assets and drives the generative rendering, producing convincing scenes equipped with simulation-ready geometry. Prana's simulation engine, FLASH, governs the physics of these environments, so contact, grasping, and object dynamics behave in a physically consistent way rather than only looking correct.
Scenarios center on robotic manipulation, such as a robot arm picking up and handling everyday objects. Every interaction is resolved by the simulator, so the same scene that looks real also responds correctly when a gripper makes contact, including objects made of composite materials that combine rigid and flexible parts within a single body. The simulator is fully differentiable, so its physical behavior can be fit to real observations rather than tuned by hand.
Functional 3D generation and physical accuracy are distinct but complementary problems. Tripo's generation provides the precise 3D structure and visual diversity to generalize; Prana's physically grounded simulation makes it behave right with the accuracy contact demands. Together they produce environments both visually rich and physically valid, which is what embodied tasks like grasping require.
We hope to prompt discussion on how generative content and differentiable simulation can build interactive, physically accurate virtual worlds.

title: Affine Bodies, Shells & Rods
Autodesk Vision Series | Not Just Big, Alive: The Animation of Kong in Monarch Season 2
speakers_or_contributors: Christophe St. Pierre Paradis
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
In Monarch: Legacy of Monsters Season 2, Rodeo FX’s animation team faced a massive challenge: make a 300-foot titan feel like a character. Kong had to emote and move with the weight of something real, not just big.

Animation Supervisor Christophe St-Pierre Paradis walks through the creative and technical process behind Kong's performance in Autodesk Maya, from the motion capture sessions that formed the foundation of his movement, to the animator-driven refinement that transformed raw mocap data into a creature performance with genuine scale and emotional presence. The talk covers locomotion at an impossible scale, fight choreography rooted in intention, the micro-movements that give a giant an inner life, and the pipeline realities of preserving a performance through fur, cloth, and simulation.

title: M-ABD: Scalable, Efficient, and Robust Multi-Affine-Body Dynamics
speakers_or_contributors: Zhiyong He; Dewen Guo; Minghao Guo; Yili Zhao; Wojciech Matusik; Hao Su; Chenfanfu Jiang; Peter Yichen Chen; Yin Yang
affiliations: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); The University of British Columbia; USC; University of California Los Angeles; University of California San Diego; University of Utah
description: We present a multibody simulation framework leveraging affine body dynamics with a co-rotational formulation, enabling pre-factorized system matrices under implicit integration. Mapping primal coordinates onto a dual space via KKT, our method exactly enforces joint constraints, achieving interactive rates for hundreds of thousands of bodies on a single CPU core.

title: Mix3R: Mixing Feed-forward Reconstruction and Generative 3D Priors for Joint Multi-view Aligned 3D Reconstruction and Pose Estimation
speakers_or_contributors: Siyou Lin; Zhou Xue; Hongwen Zhang; Liang An; Dongping Li; Shaohui Jiao; Yebin Liu
affiliations: Beijing Normal University; ByteDance Inc.; Tsinghua University
description: We present Mix3R, a method that mixes feed-forward reconstruction and generative 3D priors using a Mixture-of-Transformer architecture for multi-view aligned generative 3D reconstruction from sparse views.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: SimReady or Not, Here Comes Gravity with OpenUSD for Simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Ashley Goldstein
keywords_tags: Generative AI; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Physics is at the heart of building reliable physical AI systems, and this course in the NVIDIA Physical AI Agent Bootcamp explores how to move from visual inspection into simulation readiness.

Workflows in physical AI need assets that are structured, validated, and physically meaningful. Building on the prior OpenUSD and RTX viewport lab, this hands-on course moves from visual inspection into simulation readiness.

Attendees will validate OpenUSD assets for simulation-ready (SimReady) workflows, convert CAD and Gaussian splat content to USD, create a physics stage, and assemble environments such as stairs, ramps, or other test layouts. They will enable physics, make scene objects interactable, and run simulations to see how assets behave under gravity and user input.

By the end, attendees will have a runnable physics scene with validated assets, converted USD content, and an interactive environment ready for robotics testing or physical AI workflows.

title: Robust Planar Maps for 3D Vectorization
speakers_or_contributors: Robert Fuchs; Keenan Crane
description: Vectorizing 3D scenes into 2D vector images requires planar maps to define solid non-overlapping regions. Existing planar map methods are slow and susceptible to numerical instability. We introduce a robust and efficient method for constructing planar maps, using a spatial hierarchy as the fundamental representation of the planar map.

title: Surface Reconstruction from Unoriented Points via Green’s 3rd Identity
speakers_or_contributors: Zhonghao Wu; Dong Xiao; Renjie Chen
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China
description: G3R reconstructs surfaces from unoriented point clouds by using Green's 3rd identity to recover globally consistent normals by solving a well-conditioned linear system. It removes the tangential nullspace that weakens prior winding-number-based methods and delivers accurate orientations and higher-quality reconstructions.

title: Teaching OpenGL 4.5/4.6 in Introductory Computer Graphics: A Hidden Sweet Spot in Graphics API Education Accessibility
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Shah
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In this paper, I discuss my effort and philosophy on how to balance the tension of teaching beginners introductory computer graphics programming with the appropriate API. The solution I have found is by teaching OpenGL 4.5 as the minimum graphics API and and introducing Vulkan-like programming abstractions.

title: The Fate of Humanity Rests with Project Hail Mary. ILM's Contribution to Visual Effects Behind the Film - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: Vectors Graphics, Strokes & Sketches
2D Gaussian Splatting for Bézier Spline Line Art Vectorization
speakers_or_contributors: Tianhao Chen; Clara Fernandez; Marteinn Oskarsson; Chuck Tappan; Olga Sorkine-Hornung; Markus Gross; Christopher Schroers; Abdelaziz Djelouah
affiliations: Disney Research Studios; ETH Zurich; Walt Disney Animation Studios
description: We introduce a state-of-the-art vectorization method using depth and semantic features to extract strokes. By modeling Bézier curves via differentiable 2D Gaussian splatting, we efficiently optimize geometry and texture. The framework extends to video for temporal consistency, offering high-quality, user-adjustable results for image and animation reconstruction.

title: A Bayesian Approach for Task-Specific Next-Best-View Selection with Uncertain Geometry
speakers_or_contributors: Jingsen Zhu; Silvia Sellán; Alexander Terenin
description: We introduce a Bayesian framework for task-specific next-best-view selection in 3D reconstruction. Unlike general uncertainty-reduction methods, we selectively reduce uncertainty only in regions critical to the downstream task via task-specific acquisition functions on posterior distributions over implicit surfaces, achieving superior performance with fewer views across classification, segmentation, and physics simulations.

title: Distance Field Rasterization for End-to-End Mesh Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: Jinkai Cui; Kaiwen Song; Chumeng Niu; Juyong Zhang
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China
description: SDFRaster combines the efficiency of rasterization with signed distance fields for end-to-end mesh reconstruction from multi-view images. By optimizing a tetrahedral SDF and extracting meshes during optimization, it produces accurate, complete, and compact surfaces without heuristic post-processing.

title: Heterogeneous Subspace Corrections for GPU Deformable Multibody Dynamics
speakers_or_contributors: Dewen Guo; Zhendong Wang; Minchen Li; Sheng Li; Guoping Wang; Huamin Wang; Chenfanfu Jiang; Yin Yang
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; Peking University; Style3D Research; University of California Los Angeles; University of Utah
description: Heterogeneous Subspace Corrections (HSC) accelerates GPU simulations of complex multibody systems. By decoupling deformable and rigid/affine components into separate Neumann-preconditioned Krylov iterations and utilizing an Adaptive Cross Approximation (ACA) for low-rank coupling, it overcomes ill-conditioning to achieve direct-solver convergence speeds for stiff, high-resolution models.

title: Motion Capture Society
speakers_or_contributors: Troy McFarland
keywords_tags: Animation; Capture/Scanning; Games; Hardware; Visual Effects
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Open to everyone who has an interest in Motion Capture. We'll discuss various hardware and software solutions, pipelines and techniques for getting the best data, performance, shoot day, and production pipeline. This is not specific to any one vendor or technology. All are invited!

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Faster Creative Iteration with Autodesk Arnold
speakers_or_contributors: Sebastien Blaineau-Ortega
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Creative iteration remains one of the most expensive challenges in modern content production. In this session, Autodesk will present recent advances in the Academy Award-winning Arnold renderer aimed at reducing technical friction in the creative process. The session will cover architectural improvements to interactive viewport rendering and GPU optimizations that improve responsiveness in complex scenes. Attendees will learn how these advances help artists and technical directors iterate more fluidly while maintaining the robustness, predictability, and image quality expected from a physically based renderer. Autodesk will also share lessons learned from real-world production deployments

title: Art + Code: Case Study in Expanding Access with p5.js
speakers_or_contributors: Alex Avila; Xin Xin; Roxana Hadad; Kit Kuksenok
affiliations: Los Angeles Unified School District; Processing Foundation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Widely used for making and teaching algorithmic art, p5.js prioritizes access and expressiveness. What impact does p5.js have on students? This presentation reflects on an educator program that grounds web-based interactive graphics in students' contexts, expanding imagination of what is possible with resources at hand, and suggesting new professional possibilities.

title: CaRaFe: Camera-Radar Radiance Fields for Scene Reconstruction
speakers_or_contributors: David Borts; Julian Ost; Shamik Basu; Tim Broedermann; Andrea Ramazzina; Christos Sakaridis; Mario Bijelic; Felix Heide
affiliations: ETH Zürich; INRIA; Mercedes-Benz; Princeton University
description: CaRaFe is the first method to jointly reconstruct 3D scenes from both camera and radar data using a multi-modal shared neural geometry representation. It can reconstruct large-scale urban environments from a single drive-through and synthesize measurements from both sensors at novel viewpoints, leveraging their complementary strengths to improve geometric fidelity.

title: Distributed Affine Body Dynamics with Adaptive Consensus
speakers_or_contributors: Jiafeng Liu; Wenhui Zhou; Xinming Pei; Yifan Peng; Huamin Wang; Yin Yang; Lei Lan; Weiwei Xu
affiliations: State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University; Style3D Research; The University of Hong Kong (HKU); University of Utah
description: Affine Body Dynamics within Incremental Potential Contact enables accurate simulation of near-rigid, extremely stiff solids with strict non-penetration. We present a distributed ABD solver based on consensus ADMM, where nodes solve local subproblems in parallel and synchronize shared bodies through global consensus, achieving robust convergence, non-penetration, and scalable multi-node performance.

title: Monte Carlo Rendering of Biharmonic Diffusion Curves
speakers_or_contributors: Paul Himmler; Tobias Günther
description: Biharmonic diffusion curves are lightweight vector graphics primitives that offer artistic control over color gradients. We present their first Monte Carlo renderer, which builds upon recent advances in Monte Carlo geometry processing. We phrase the biharmonic problem as two coupled second-order problems and recover boundary data using variational energy minimization.

title: Topology Type Estimation of Simulated 4D Image Data by Combining Downscaling and Convolutional Neural Networks
speakers_or_contributors: Khalil Hannouch; Stephan Chalup
description: This project integrates a 4D camera model, synthetic 4D datasets, and CNNs trained on downscaled data to analyse its 4D topology. The results suggest that advanced 4D vision pipelines, particularly when paired with significant computational resources, could enable scientific breakthroughs unattainable with traditional two and three dimensional methods.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - MotionBricks: Scalable Real-Time Motions with Modular Latent Generative Model and Smart Primitives
speakers_or_contributors: Tingwu Wang
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
MotionBricks: Scalable Real-Time Motions with Modular Latent Generative Model and Smart Primitives

MotionBricks is a real-time generative framework that transforms interactive motion control for animation and robotics. By combining a large-scale latent backbone with intuitive "smart primitives," it delivers high-quality, zero-shot motion synthesis at 15,000 FPS, allowing users to effortlessly build complex and fine-grained animations and robotic applications.

Paper Presentation Presented: Tuesday, July 21, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: Motion Generation, Warping & Control; Room 403 A

title: Better Bending: Analysis, Construction and Verification of Discrete Bending Models for Kirchhoff-Love Shells
speakers_or_contributors: Zhen Chen; Danny Kaufman; Etienne Vouga
affiliations: Adobe Inc.; Adobe Research; The University of Texas at Austin
description: While thin shells have been studied for decades, there is little consensus on how to discretize them. We systematically study bending models for Kirchhoff-Love shells. We develop new models and methods to address discovered simulation gaps, and make practical recommendations of when and how these models should be used.

title: NILE: Nested Interleaving of Low-Dimensional Elements
speakers_or_contributors: Abdalla Ahmed; Matt Pharr; Victor Ostromoukhov; Hui Huang
affiliations: NVIDIA; Shenzhen University; Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1
description: We introduce a novel modular modular meta-sampler architecture that bridges local subspace and global sampling, allowing integrator designers to employ specialized low-dimensional samplers while still achieving high-dimensional uniformity.

title: Pixels2Peaks: Converting Terrain Images to Heightmaps
speakers_or_contributors: Aryamaan Jain; James Gain; Guillaume Cordonnier
affiliations: INRIA, Université Côte d'Azur; University of Cape Town
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Geometry; Modeling; Simulation
description: Pixels2Peaks automates the creation of 3D bare-earth terrain heightmaps from a single 2D photograph. Our method first estimates camera parameters and visible geometry; then uses a guided diffusion model to plausibly synthesize occluded regions. The result maintains geomorphological, hydrological, and view consistencies.

title: Real-Time GPU Vector Graphics SDF Generation Based on Quadratic Stroke Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Xuhai Chen; Guangze Zhang; Juan Cao; Zhonggui Chen
affiliations: Fujian Key Laboratory of Urban Intelligent Sensing and Computing, Xiamen University; School of Informatics, Xiamen University; School of Mathematical Sciences, Xiamen University
description: We introduce a GPU-based algorithm for real-time signed distance field generation from quadratic vector graphics. By treating SDF computation as parallel stroke rendering, the method achieves substantial speed improvements over CPU approaches, enabling dynamic text, interactive user interfaces, and real-time distance-based visual effects.

title: Toys meet Tech: The Making of Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5
speakers_or_contributors: Thomas Jordan; Athena Xenakis; Mariana Galindo; Stephen Marshall; Paul Kanyuk; Jesse Hollander
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Dynamics; Geometry; Lighting; Modeling; Rendering; Simulation
description:
Howdy folks! Hop onto your saddle and hold on tight because this is going to be a wild ride! Jessie is the new sheriff in town at Bonnie’s house, who is now eight years old. Ever since Bonnie’s parents gifted her with LilyPad, a grating, tablet tech toy shaped like a frog, playtime has dried up; Bonnie’s a full-on screen zombie! THE AGE OF TOYS IS OVER!!

In this Production Session, you’ll learn how Toy Story 5 was made including new characters, new environments, new looks and new technology. We’ll show you how we created FIFTY Buzz Lightyears, new tech toys, a perky Sticky Hand, a glamorous collection of horse figurines, a haunting toy shed, and a child’s imagination brought to life on the big screen in a way the audience has never seen before. Join us on this adventure and learn how the world of Toy Story is taken to the next level of visual richness and technical innovation. Yeehaw!!!

title: VR Biosecurity Surveillance Training for Indigenous Rangers
speakers_or_contributors: Michela Ledwidge
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Education; Games; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In northern Australia, biosecurity is everybody's business. Learn how Mod designs a hybrid live action video / game production using graph data science to create an hour-long VR training experience for Indigenous rangers and stakeholders, identifying pests, viruses and diseases out in the field while remaining "croc-wise" in croc country!

title: Efficient B-Spline Finite Elements for Cloth Simulation
speakers_or_contributors: Yuqi Meng; Yihao Shi; Kemeng Huang; Zixuan Lu; Ning Guo; Taku Komura; Yin Yang; Minchen Li
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; The University of Hong Kong; The University of Hong Kong (HKU); University of Utah; Zhejiang University
description: We present a quadratic B-spline FEM approach for cloth simulation that captures smoother, more accurate deformations with rich wrinkle detail and few artifacts. Our optimized reduced integration scheme enables better performance compared to traditional linear FEM, while supporting robust, high-quality cloth simulation for realistic garment animation.

title: Gradient-based Design of Non-Uniform Low-Discrepancy Samples
speakers_or_contributors: Xiangyu Li; Ege Ciklabakkal; Daniel Ritchie; Toshiya Hachisuka
description: This paper introduces a first numerical optimization method for approximately generating non-uniform low-discrepancy point sets with an arbitrary target distribution. It is based on a new differentiable estimator of non-uniform L2-discrepancy for a given point set.

title: MegaNorm: Local Patches Embedding for Efficient and Robust Point Normal Orientation at Super-Large Scale
speakers_or_contributors: Zhuodong Li; Zengke Liu; Fei Hou; Xuhui Chen; Wencheng Wang; Ying He
affiliations: Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Nanyang Technological University
description: MegaNorm is a fast, accurate, and robust method for orienting normals in non-watertight, scene-scale point clouds, scaling to ~50M points in under 10 minutes. It uses two feed-forward networks: one orients local patches in parallel, while a lightweight MLP evaluates pairwise consistency. Finally, a 0–1 integer program enforces global consistency.

title: Mixing Colors: Exploring Digital Images through the Bayer Filter
speakers_or_contributors: Brittany Morago
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This Bayer Filter assignment introduces digital image formation concepts in undergraduate Computer Graphics courses while playing with different components of light and color. Students get visual feedback as they take a deep, step-by-step dive into working with digital image data structures combining flashlights, worksheets, code, and photographs.

title: Stroke of Surprise: Progressive Semantic Illusions in Vector Sketching
speakers_or_contributors: Huai-Hsun Cheng; Siang-Ling Zhang; Yu-Lun Liu
affiliations: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University
description: This work introduces Progressive Semantic Illusions, a novel vector sketching task where a single sketch undergoes a dramatic semantic transformation through the sequential addition of strokes, and presents Stroke of Surprise, a generative framework that optimizes vector strokes to satisfy distinct semantic interpretations at different drawing stages.

title: ANARI: Advancing Open Rendering Standards
speakers_or_contributors: Jefferson Amstutz; Bill Sherman
keywords_tags: Digital Twins; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Industry Insight; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Get the latest updates directly from the Khronos ANARI Working Group as the ecosystem continues to expand across visualization, simulation, engineering, and high-performance computing. This session will cover recent technical progress, roadmap discussions, interoperability efforts, implementation updates, and opportunities for community participation. Learn how ANARI is evolving to support modern rendering workflows, scalable deployment across diverse hardware and software environments, and closer integration with adjacent Khronos technologies. Join the discussion, connect with contributors, and hear what is next for open, portable rendering standards.

title: Miris | OpenUSD streamed to any device, anywhere.
speakers_or_contributors: Sean Looper
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Digital Twins; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Simulation; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Fully path-traced, OpenPBR-compliant OpenUSD, streamed to any consumer device, anywhere.

Reaching low-powered hardware has always meant reducing fidelity, downgrading geometry and texture sizes. That is exactly where photorealism dies. Miris doesn't work that way. We use AI to condition each 3D asset once upstream, optimizing across devices, network conditions, and content characteristics, then stream it adaptively to any device. Our radiance field technology, built leveraging NVIDIA fVDB software and CoreWeave infrastructure enables Miris to deliver high-fidelity content at global scale.

Join Sean Looper, CTO of Miris, for a look at how the pipeline is built and how Playbook, Evercast, CGAxis, and Voxel51 are putting it to work across digital twins, physical AI, media and entertainment, and digital asset marketplaces.

If you build with 3D, come talk shop. We are looking for partners.

title: Beyond Positional Encoding: A 5D Spatio-Directional Hash Encoding
speakers_or_contributors: Philippe Weier; Lukas Bode; Philipp Slusallek; Adrián Jarabo; Sébastien Speierer
description: This paper introduces two new compact neural encodings, the hash-sphere and hash-grid-sphere, which represent all-frequency signals using hierarchical geodesic grids. While we demonstrated their effectiveness in neural path guiding, radiance field reconstruction, and incident radiance caching, these versatile "drop-in" replacements offer significant potential for various applications across computer graphics.

title: Interactive Yarn-level Knitwear with Nested Douglas-Rachford Splitting
speakers_or_contributors: Chun Yuan; Zixuan Lu; Haoyang Shi; Dewen Guo; Huamin Wang; Chenfanfu Jiang; Zherong Pan; Kui Wu; Yin Yang
affiliations: LIGHTSPEED; LightSpeed Studios; Style3D Research; University of California Los Angeles; University of Utah
description: We propose a novel framework utilizing a generalized Douglas-Rachford Splitting (DRS) scheme and hierarchical decomposition for high-resolution knitwear simulation. By convexifying complex yarn dynamics, it ensures robust, optimal convergence. With matrix-free GPU parallelization, our method achieves interactive simulation rates for multi-million DOF garments.

title: NeuralSketch2Surf: Fast Neural Surfacing of Unoriented 3D Sketches
speakers_or_contributors: Hongsheng Ye; Anandhu Sureshkumar; Zhonghan Wang; Stefanie Hahmann; Marie-Paule Cani; Georges-Pierre Bonneau; Amal Dev Parakkat
affiliations: Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique - Laboratoire d'informatique de l'École Polytechnique (LIX); LTCI - Telecom Paris; University Grenoble Alpes, CNRS, INRIA, Grenoble INP, LJK
description: We introduce NeuralSketch2Surf, the first fast and robust neural surfacing solution, capable of processing arbitrary unoriented sketches at interactive rates.

title: Resolution Where It Counts: Hash-based GPU-Accelerated 3D Reconstruction via Variance-Adaptive Voxel Grids
speakers_or_contributors: Lorenzo De Rebotti; Emanuele Giacomini; Giorgio Grisetti; Luca Di Giammarino
description: We propose a variance-adaptive voxel grid that adjusts size based on local SDF variance. Using a flat spatial hash table, it achieves constant-time access, memory efficiency, and full GPU parallelism. Additionally, a parallel quad-tree structure supports GPU-accelerated Gaussian Splatting rendering, enabling effective control over splat density.

title: ArtiFixer: Enhancing and Extending 3D Reconstruction with Auto-Regressive Diffusion Models
speakers_or_contributors: Riccardo De Lutio; Tobias Fischer; Yen-Yu Chang; Yuxuan Zhang; Zhangjie Wu; Xuanchi Ren; Tianchang Shen; Katarína Tóthová; Zan Gojcic; Haithem Turki
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Rendering
description: ArtiFixer enhances and extends 3D reconstructions efficiently and scalably. Given an initial reconstruction and reference views, it autoregressive generates novel content consistent with existing observations. ArtiFixer can produce hundreds of novel views in a single inference pass or serve as pseudo-supervision to improve the underlying 3D reconstruction.

title: CollabXR: an open-source platform for immersive spatial communication of learning materials
speakers_or_contributors: George Takahashi; Jake White
keywords_tags: Augmented Reality; Digital Twins; Education; Real-Time; Scientific Visualization; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: CollabXR: a collaborative, multi-user XR platform that enables instruction, training and research through visualization and interaction with immersive three-dimensional materials. Originally designed for sharing astrophysics visualizations of supernova remnants in an 'XR classroom,' the platform has expanded to incorporate other 3D data from STEM, arts, humanities and cultural heritage.

title: Hands-on Course: NVIDIA: Simulating a Dextrous Hand For Robotics With OpenUSD
speakers_or_contributors: Ji Yuan Feng; Alexandra Kissel
keywords_tags: Digital Twins; Physical AI; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Hands-on course on preparing robot USD assets for simulation, covering why physics tuning is foundational to robot learning, inspecting and configuring colliders and joints with OpenUSD/PhysX, and tuning control gains for stable, realistic motion. Participants work with a robot hand asset and apply best practices for simulation-ready authoring.

title: Hi-SPAD: Video-Rate Hyperspectral Imaging and Inference with Single-Photon Cameras
speakers_or_contributors: Haejoon Lee; Mohit Gupta; Vijayakumar Bhagavatula; Aswin Sankaranarayanan
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University; University of Wisconsin-Madison
description: This work develops a hyperspectral imager using a SPAD image sensor that achieves a spatial resolution of 1024 × 768 pixels across 123 spectral bands spanning visible and near-infrared wavelengths, at a temporal rate of 26 frames per second.

title: Sketch2Arti: Sketch-based Articulation Modeling of CAD Objects
speakers_or_contributors: Yi Yang; Hao Pan; Yijing Cui; Alla Sheffer; Changjian Li
affiliations: Tsinghua University; University of British Columbia; University of Edinburgh
description: Sketch2Arti is the first sketch-based articulation modeling system for CAD objects. Given a CAD model and simple 2D sketches, it identifies movable parts and predicts motion parameters. It can also complete missing internal structures revealed by motion, enabling controllable, iterative, and generalizable articulation modeling for diverse objects.

title: SymX: Energy-based Simulation from Symbolic Expressions
speakers_or_contributors: José Fernández-Fernández; Fabian Löschner; Lukas Westhofen; Andreas Longva; Jan Bender
description: SymX is an open-source framework that turns succinct symbolic energy expressions into optimized code, delivering automatic first- and second-order derivatives and parallel assembly for Newton-style optimization time integrators. SymX facilitates building complex simulations, including high-order FEM, rigid-deformable coupling, and frictional contact, accelerating simulation research.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | AI for Spatial Storytelling: Going Beyond Generation with 3D World Models
speakers_or_contributors: Ben Mildenhall; Nikola Todorovic
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Physical AI; Simulation; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Discover how 3D world models are poised to transform animation and visual effects by moving beyond visual generation toward systems that can understand, simulate, and reason about space and time. In this panel, Nikola Todorovic, Co-Founder of Wonder Dynamics, an Autodesk company, and Ben Mildenhall, Co-Founder of World Labs will explore why spatial intelligence and simulation matter for the next generation of creative tools. The conversation will connect long-term research ambitions with real-world production workflows.

title: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage
speakers_or_contributors: Jimmz Zhang
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage is an interactive, generative art installation that re-imagines Los Angeles as a living, ever-evolving digital twin. Conceived for SIGGRAPH 2026, the project bridges LA’s rich cinematic legacy with the frontier of spatial computing. By seamlessly integrating Kinect motion tracking and AI-generated point clouds, it invites participants to co-author a collaborative digital monument. As users input their personal memories and desires of LA, individual landmarks dynamically merge, overlap, and fold into a singular, massive 3D cyber environment—blurring the boundaries between physical presence, collective memory, and cybernetic space.

Credit：
Visual & Interactive Director: Jimmz ZHANG, Digital FUN & TEA Community
Technical Director: Ruokun CHEN, TEA Community & FLARE Lab
Sound Artist: Louzhang, USC School of Cinematic Arts

title: Poster Sessions
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: New ideas start here. Join us to hear from authors as they discuss their work.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - LooseRoPE: Content-aware Attention Manipulation for Semantic Harmonization
speakers_or_contributors: Etai Sella
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
LooseRoPE: Content-aware Attention Manipulation for Semantic Harmonization

LooseRoPE enables prompt-free image editing by letting users crop and paste objects directly into new scenes. It improves diffusion-based harmonization by modulating RoPE with saliency, balancing identity preservation and contextual blending. This yields intuitive, spatially precise edits that maintain object appearance, pose, and placement without requiring text prompts or masks.

Paper Presentation Presented: Thursday, July 23, 10:45 am-12:15 pm; Paper Session: Image Editing,; Room 408 B

title: Animation Theater 3: The Other Side of Quiet
description: Some stories don't announce themselves; they arrive in whispered confessions, long silences, and small acts of survival. This program gathers intimate works about the private weight of struggle and the unlikely ways people find their way through it.

title: Berthouzoz Women in Research Lunch
speakers_or_contributors: Ticha Sethapakdi; Fan Feng
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Chapters; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Display; Diversity, Equity and Inclusion; Dynamics; Education; Ethics and Society; Fabrication; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Haptics; Hardware; History; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Networking; Performance; Physical AI; Pioneers; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality; Visual Effects
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The Berthouzoz Women in Research Event is an annual networking event organized by WiGRAPH for researchers, faculty, and students. Floraine Berthouzoz started this event as an informal gathering.
After her passing in 2015, Floraine's mentees and colleagues built upon her efforts to create an event that aims to broaden the network of women researchers and provide a friendly and personal environment where graduate students can interact with senior researchers. We will be hosting a panel of women in graphics who will share with us some of their research experiences. The event is open to all researchers, regardless of gender.

title: Toys Meet Tech: The Making of Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5 - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: Chapters: West Coast Meetup
speakers_or_contributors: Amira Malcom; Joan Collins; Sunil Rampersad
affiliations: LA ACM SIGGRAPH; Los Angeles Professional ACM SIGGRAPH Chapter; Professional and Student Chapters Committee
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Display; Dynamics; Education; Ethics and Society; Fabrication; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Haptics; Hardware; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Performance; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
In conjunction with the Professional and Student Chapters Committee (PSCC), leaders from chapters on the West Coast of North America welcome you to join a social hour to connect with other students and professionals. This session will give information on chapters in the area, the activities they host, and ways to get involved.

More information about the PSCC, chapters near you and how to get involved can be found at https://siggraph.org/chapters.

title: Fulbright Program International Exchange Opportunities
speakers_or_contributors: David Thornton
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Are you interested in international exchange? Come be inspired and informed as a Fulbright alumnus describes his personal experience. Find out how you can develop a strong application for this prestigious academic and professional exchange program, now celebrating its 80th year. The Fulbright Program is the flagship international exchange program sponsored by the United States Government. In partnership with more than 160 countries worldwide, Fulbright offers an expansive suite of life-changing international academic and cultural exchange opportunities. Learn more at https://fulbrightprogram.org/apply/

title: Machine Learning, Neural Rendering, and the Future of Open Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Markus Tavenrath
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Industry Insight; Physical AI; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: As AI and real time graphics continue to converge, the Khronos Group is helping build an open, cross platform foundation for scalable machine learning and neural graphics. Join us for an interactive discussion on insights from the Khronos AI Ecosystem Research Project, emerging industry trends, and the growing role of Vulkan and Vulkan ML in enabling portable AI acceleration. Explore use cases including neural shaders, on device inferencing, neural rendering, and efficient graphics pipelines. Bring your questions, share your perspective, and help shape the future of open AI for interactive 3D.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Practical Advances in AI-Accelerated Ray Tracing
speakers_or_contributors: David Hart; Tony Kanell
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Modern rendering is rapidly evolving through advances in GPU ray tracing and AI-assisted workflows. NVIDIA engineers will share recent OptiX and ray tracing developments shaping interactive and production renderers. Topics include AI-assisted denoising, DLSS integration, curve rendering, debugging and profiling, compile-time workflows, and support for larger, more dynamic, and more programmable rendering systems. The talk also examines evolving areas such as neural rendering, differentiable workflows, Gaussian splats, and memory-scalable scene management, with an emphasis on what is practical today and what is coming next.

title: Neurodiversity in Computer Graphics, Games, and Interactive Media
speakers_or_contributors: Cheryl Briggs; Shoko Kimura
affiliations: Kyoto University; University of Central Florida
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Following community discussions at SIGGRAPH 2024 and 2025, this Birds of a Feather session invites educators, industry professionals, students, researchers, and advocates to discuss neurodiversity, accessibility, and inclusive practices across computer graphics, games, animation, and interactive media. Topics may include communication, collaboration, mentorship, workplace culture, learning environments, executive functioning, and sustainable support strategies. This session also provides an opportunity to discuss ongoing efforts toward building a Neurodiversity Affinity Group within the SIGGRAPH community and fostering year-round connection, advocacy, and visibility.

title: Virtual Theatre: Producing Live Theatre on Virtual Stages
speakers_or_contributors: Joe Geigel; Stephen Butchko; Yosun Chang; Alex Coulombe; Jason Moore
affiliations: AI3D.Foundation; Agile Lens: Immersive Design; Ferryman Collective; Institution Jason Moore Productions / The MetaMovie; Rochester Institute of Technology
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Performance; Real-Time; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: In this panel, we explore the creation of live performance on virtual stages with participants in different physical locales. With a focus on production, we tap into the extensive experience of panelists working directly in this space and explore workflows, participant engagement, and the future of this kind of entertainment.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | A Small Team’s Journey to a VES Award-Winning Animated Film
speakers_or_contributors: Damien Lagadeuc; Mathis Giraudeau
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Meet the young filmmakers behind Azimuth, a graduation short that went on to win the 24th Visual Effects Society Award for Outstanding Student Project, recognized for its artistry and innovation in animation.

At a time when conversations around AI and humanity are everywhere, the film tells a deeply human story, asking: if technology could recreate someone you love, would it ever truly be enough?
The young filmmakers will share how trust, problem-solving, and a shared love of storytelling helped them create something they never imagined possible together, and how they used Autodesk Maya and Arnold to layout, animate, and render the film under a tight timeline and with limited resources.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - Abstraction in Style: Beyond Texture and Color
speakers_or_contributors: Hui Huang
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
Abstraction in Style: Beyond Texture and Color

Artistic styles often involve shape reinterpretation, not merely a change of surface appearance. Conventional style transfer methods miss this deeper abstraction behavior. We introduce Abstraction in Style (AiS), a two-stage generative framework that learns and transfers style at shape level, beyond texture and colors, enabling more expressive and controllable stylization.

Paper Presentation Presented: Thursday, July 23, 9-10:30 am; Paper Session: NPR, Style & Abstraction; Room 408 B

title: Slang in Action: Modern Shading for Graphics, Compute, and AI
speakers_or_contributors: Shannon Woods
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Join the Khronos Group, the Slang project team, and the developer community for an interactive look at the latest advances shaping Slang and modern programmable graphics pipelines. Explore recent project updates, emerging capabilities, real world workflows, and opportunities spanning graphics, compute, AI, and next generation rendering. Bring your questions, share your experiences, and engage directly with the developers and community helping shape what comes next for Slang.

title: Hands-on Course: Debugging USD composition
speakers_or_contributors: Pallav Sharma; Nick Porcino; Joan Panis
keywords_tags: Animation; Games; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This hands-on course focuses on debugging USD composition in production. Participants gain practical experience authoring data across composition arcs and diagnosing issues in complex scenes. Building on USD in Production, the course emphasizes interactive use of modern debugging and visualization tools to understand how opinions, overrides, and data resolve.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: Blender, Oranges, and OVRTX (Not Your Typical Smoothie)
speakers_or_contributors: Max Bickley
keywords_tags: Generative AI; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Blender is where many 3D ideas first take shape, and this course in the NVIDIA Physical AI Agent Bootcamp shows how to extend that familiar workspace with Omniverse libraries instead of moving the work somewhere else. Attendees will use AI agents to scaffold a Blender integration that connects ovrtx, ovphysx, and ovstage into a live creative workflow.
The session walks through bringing GPU-accelerated path-traced rendering into Blender’s viewport.. Along the way, attendees will see how Blender’s scene graph can connect to Omniverse-powered rendering and simulation services.
By the end, participants will have an RTX-rendered, physics-enabled scene running inside Blender, plus a clearer model for how composable Omniverse libraries can extend existing tools used by artists, technical directors, and simulation teams.

title: 4D Humans
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Geometry; Modeling; Rendering; Simulation

title: Art in the Time of A.I.
speakers_or_contributors: Sunil Rampersad
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: A discussion about ML/AI technology from an artist's perspective. Some of the issues that exist and where the new technology can help or hinder. Ultimately we will have to ask: what;s art, who;s an artist, and what's good/bad art? How does the use of ML/AI help or hinder the goal of making good art? Are we seeing an exciting new development in art or the downfall of creativity? Let's discuss: Not to convince others to think like we do but to learn from different points of view.

title: BodyReLux: Temporally Consistent Full-Body Video Relighting
speakers_or_contributors: Li MA; Mingming He; Xueming Yu; David George; Ahmet Tasel; Paul Debevec; Julien Philip
description: BodyReLux is a video diffusion framework for relighting full-body human performances with photorealistic, temporally consistent results. Trained on video relighting pairs captured on large LED Sphere using digital bi-pack technology, it enables precise, dynamic lighting control and generalizes robustly to in-the-wild videos.

title: Cheap and Cheerful: Low-Fidelity Prototyping for Spatial Stories
speakers_or_contributors: Ethan Shaftel
keywords_tags: Art; Games; Real-Time; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This talk explores "cheap and cheerful" prototyping in immersive storytelling through the evolution of THE FOOTAGE, a VR narrative horror game. It traces how low-fidelity, no-code, and provisional methods surfaced crucial spatial insights, guided design decisions, enabled play-testing, and helped secure funding before full-scale production.

title: Differentiable Geometry Processing & Flow Maps
Film/VFX Production
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Lighting

title: Generative AI Meets Computer Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Lingjie Liu; Minghao Guo; Yin Yang; Chenfanfu Jiang; Wojciech Matusik; Rana Hanocka; Jiajun Wu; Xin Tong; Yuanming Hu; Yifei Li
affiliations: Anuttacon; Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Meshy AI; Stanford University; University of California Los Angeles; University of Chicago; University of Pennsylvania; University of Utah
description:
Generative AI is rapidly becoming a unifying interface for computer graphics, connecting large language models (LLMs), visual language models (VLMs), and video foundation models with geometry- and physics-aware representations. Led by experts from the University of Pennsylvania, MIT CSAIL, University of Utah, and UCLA, this workshop explores how modern generative models are reshaping the classical graphics pipeline, spanning 3D content creation, physics simulation, and animation. The program features a distinguished lineup of confirmed speakers from Stanford, the University of Chicago, Anuttacon, and Meshy AI, representing both academic pioneers and industry leaders.

The primary objective is to address the fundamental challenges of deploying generative methods in real-world production, specifically focusing on the controllability and editability of generated content, physical plausibility and consistency over time, and practical integration into professional workflows for film, games, and CAD. Attendees can expect a highly interactive session structure that moves beyond theory into practical application. The workshop includes five 30-minute invited talks and two 15-minute lightning talks highlighting emerging directions like garment synthesis and character animation. Most importantly, the session concludes with an 85-minute moderated panel and open audience discussion designed to synthesize themes, identify actionable open problems, and foster direct engagement between researchers and practitioners.

title: Implicit Surface Compression -- with Good Old Discrete Cosine Transform and Motion Compensation
speakers_or_contributors: Tao Jin; Shengxi Wu; Tianshu Huang; Mallesham Dasari; Srinivasan Seshan; Anthony Rowe
description: We present a real-time 4D compression framework for volumetric capture using the Truncated Signed Distance Field (TSDF) as coding domain. Combining DCT-based spatial coding with motion-compensated temporal prediction, our training-free GPU-accelerated system enables real-time encoding and decoding while substantially reducing bitrate for dynamic 3D content.

title: Iskra: A System for Inverse Geometry Processing
speakers_or_contributors: Ana Dodik; Ahmed Mahmoud; Justin Solomon
affiliations: MIT CSAIL; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
description:
Iskra is a system for differentiating through solutions to geometry processing problems.
It differentiates a broad class of algorithms, exploiting existing fast problem-specific schemes common to geometry processing, including local-global and ADMM solvers. It is compatible with machine learning frameworks, opening doors to new classes of inverse geometry processing applications.

title: Memory and Heritage
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Image Processing; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience

title: Moving Mobile Graphics
speakers_or_contributors: Jesse Barker; Ralph Potter; Liam O'Neil; Rodrigo Holztrattner Reis; Sebastian Aaltonen; Jasper de Winther
affiliations: Arm; HypeHype; Qualcomm Inc; Samsung; Traverse Research; Unity
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Hardware; Lighting; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering
description: A technical introduction to mobile graphics spanning the spectrum of hardware, software and developer experience, exploring the state of the art. We look at how neural graphics, GPU-driven rendering, global illumination and ray tracing techniques are being brought to mobile.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | GPU Rendering at Film Scale: Karma XPU in Production at Rodeo FX
speakers_or_contributors: Julien Héry; Mark Elendt; Scott Coates
keywords_tags: Industry Insight; Lighting; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Deploying GPU rendering across large-scale film pipelines requires balancing performance, scalability, and visual fidelity. In this session, SideFX and Rodeo FX will discuss Karma XPU’s capabilities in high-end production rendering. Mark Elendt will provide an overview of Karma XPU’s architecture and its integration of NVIDIA RTX technology, while Julien Héry and Scott Coates will examine practical applications from recent major productions. The session will cover GPU rendering at scale within an OpenUSD-based pipeline, from look development through final frame delivery.

title: New Generations
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Lighting; Modeling; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education

title: OpenXR: From XR Portability to Spatial Computing Platform
speakers_or_contributors: Ron Bessems; Leonard Daly
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Digital Twins; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Industry Insight; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Join the Khronos Group, industry leaders, and the OpenXR community for an interactive session exploring how OpenXR is evolving beyond device interoperability to help power the next generation of intelligent, context aware spatial experiences. We will cover the Spatial Entity Framework, new spatial extensions, OpenXR 1.1, and the expanded Extension Matrix, now including middleware support visibility across Unity, Unreal, Godot, and more. Plus best practices for shipping across headsets, AR glasses, Android XR, mobile, and edge deployments. Bring your questions, share your perspective, and help shape what comes next for OpenXR.

title: Relit-LiVE: Relight Video by Jointly Learning Environment Video
speakers_or_contributors: Weiqing Xiao; Hong Li; Xiuyu Yang; Houyuan Chen; Wenyi Li; Tianqi Liu; Shaocong Xu; Chongjie Ye; Hao Zhao; Beibei Wang
affiliations: Beihang University; Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence; HKUST; Huazhong University of Science and Technology; Nanjing University; The Chinese University of Hong Kong; Tsinghua University; University of Chinese Academy of Sciences China
description: Relit-LiVE produces physically consistent and temporally stable relighting results without requiring prior knowledge of the camera pose. It utilizes raw reference images to ensure realistic lighting effects and introduces a joint prediction framework generating relighting videos with frame-level warped environment maps, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art methods across benchmarks.

title: Shading, Relighting and Volumes
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Lighting; Modeling; Real-Time; Rendering

title: The Universal Scribe: A Single Model for Every Writing System
speakers_or_contributors: Ilya Makarov; Maksim Golyadkin; Innokentiy Humonen
affiliations: HSE University; Trusted AI Center, RAS
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Ethics and Society; Generative AI; Image Processing
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Can one generative model synthesize images of text in every writing system, from Latin and Han to endangered minority scripts and ancient undeciphered writing? This informal meetup gathers people building generative models for text and document images: synthetic handwriting and calligraphy, font and glyph generation, training data for OCR and handwriting recognition, and digitizing the world's manuscripts. A central question: can one model generalize to scripts it never saw? Computer vision and generative AI researchers, type designers, document analysis and digital heritage folks, and underserved language communities are all welcome. Come argue, share data, and find collaborators.

title: Toward Embodied Collective Memory by Remediating Vernacular Photographs into Spatial Experience
speakers_or_contributors: Minzhi Shen; Mengtai Zhang; Yuan Zhang; Gen Mai; Zhigang Wang; Wenbo Wang
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: December 2022 Beijing remediates vernacular photographs of China's COVID-19 reopening into immersive VR experiences that evoke presence, empathy, and reflection.
