Through an embodied artistic and technical practice, the work opens new perspectives on representing collective memory, distinct from traditional documentary media.

title: When Diffusion Hits the Wall: Scaling Generative Workflows for Architectural Projection Mapping
speakers_or_contributors: Guillaume Borgomano; Conner Tozier; Nelly Roxanne Karimpour-Harvey; Julien Bigeault
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
description: "When Diffusion Hits the Wall" explores what happens when generative AI meets architectural-scale projection mapping. Through real-world production, workshops, and experimentation, this talk shares techniques and lessons learned in turning diffusion outputs into ultra-high-resolution spatial material, using an artist-centered modular workflow that balances creative intent with real-world projection constraints.

title: Women at the Forefront: Discussing Storytelling in the Age of AI Production
speakers_or_contributors: Eric Pinkel; Jess Loren
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Ethics and Society; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Women at the Forefront is an interactive Birds of a Feather session built around open dialogue, networking, and shared exploration within the SIGGRAPH community. Through audience discussion, live Q&A, and candid industry conversation, attendees will engage directly with creative women shaping storytelling through AI, cloud production, and emerging digital workflows. Each panelist shares personal experiences, practical insight, and honest perspectives on navigating today’s rapidly evolving production landscape. Featuring Jieyi Lee, Jess Loren, Kim Landers, Liz Sloan Watts, and Jennette Vasiljevic, the session creates space for connection, collaboration, mentorship, and meaningful exchange across studios, brands, and the wider creative technology community.

title: World Wide Technology | Augmented, Not Automated
speakers_or_contributors: Kyle Lindsey; Rick Champagne
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Generative AI does not require creative studios to throw away decades of tools, talent, and pipeline investment. The bigger opportunity is augmentation, not automation: keep the tools artists already know, keep the artists in creative control, and add agents as helpers inside the existing pipeline to take on the repetitive and technical work. This session shows how WWT, NVIDIA, and Cisco make that hybrid pipeline real and safe inside a real production stack, so AI accelerates the creative work without replacing the creator at the center of it.

title: 4D Human-Scene Reconstruction from Low-Overlap Captures
speakers_or_contributors: Minhyuk Hwang; Sangmin Kim; Seunguk Do; Daneul Kim; Jaesik Park
description: We reconstruct dynamic 4D scenes with multiple people from just 4 sparse cameras with minimal overlap. Our method separately handles backgrounds using video generation and humans using body model priors, then harmonizes the result with a temporally consistent enhancement step, achieving high-quality novel-viewpoint video from practical, everyday camera setups.

title: GR3EN: Generative Relighting for 3D Environments
speakers_or_contributors: Xiaoyan Xing; Philipp Henzler; Junhwa Hur; Runze Li; Jonathan T. Barron; Pratul P. Srinivasan; Dor Verbin
affiliations: DeepMind; Google Research; University of Amsterdam
description: GR3EN enables relighting 3D environments to user-specified lighting conditions. It provides full control over every light source in a 3D scene — enabling adding new light sources, changing the color of existing ones or turning them off entirely. Shadows, reflections, and interreflections all update realistically.

title: Pixel Cube: Diffusion-based Portrait Video Relighting Through Realistic Lighting Reproduction
speakers_or_contributors: Yufan Zhang; Yu Ji; Ayo Ajiboye; Rundi Wu; Yu Guo; Changxi Zheng; Jinwei Ye
affiliations: Columbia University; George Mason University; LightThought LLC
description: Pixel Cube is a novel lighting system for realistic lighting emulation and high-speed video relighting data acquisition. With a high-quality lighting-aware dataset, we train a diffusion model for dynamic portrait relighting. Our model produces photorealistic, temporally consistent portrait videos under new lighting conditions that faithfully preserve the subject's facial features.

title: SharpNet: Enhancing MLPs to Represent Functions with Controlled Non-differentiability
speakers_or_contributors: Hanting Niu; Junkai Deng; Fei Hou; Wencheng Wang; Ying He
affiliations: Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences; Nanyang Technological University
description: We propose SharpNet, an enhanced MLP architecture that augments the input with a feature function to produce controllable derivative jumps. We show that SharpNet can preserve the sharpness in the intended regions while maintaining smoothness elsewhere, and that it outperforms baseline methods in sharpness-critical tasks such as CAD reconstruction.

title: Building and Transitioning the Rainbow Affinity Group
speakers_or_contributors: AJ Christensen; Jeffrey D. Weekley; Victor Zordan; AJ Christensen
affiliations: Clemson University; NASA; University of California Santa Cruz
keywords_tags: Diversity, Equity and Inclusion; Education; Ethics and Society
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
The ACM SIGGRAPH Rainbow Affinity Group was formally established in June 2025 to support LGBTQ+ individuals in computer graphics and interactive techniques. Participants throughout the years of the Rainbow Meetup Birds of a Feather articulated strong interest in mentorship, networking, and inclusive programming. This session continues that discussion and provides a framework for action.

This session presents the Rainbow Affinity Group as an active organizational design and transition effort. We reflect on early community input, examine the gap between ideation and execution, and introduce a structured model for forming a working committee to support future programming and leadership development.

Through a facilitated discussion, attendees will be invited to help define priorities and identify pathways for participation. The session will initiate the formation of a new contributor cohort aligned with defined roles in programming, mentorship, communications, and industry engagement. This cohort will form the foundation for the group’s next phase and future leadership, in alignment with ACM SIGGRAPH governance practices.

title: A Walled City: An Interactive AI Artwork for Decentralized Urban Memories and Collective Presence
speakers_or_contributors: Weidi Zhang; lijiaozi Cheng; Yi Wang; Jieliang Luo
affiliations: Arizona State University; Midjourney; Minus AI; The University of Sheffield
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Image Processing; Real-Time
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: It proposes a new artistic model of collective virtual presence grounded in historical urbanism and decentralized networks. Drawing from the spatial logic and aesthetic influence of the Kowloon Walled City, the project provides a speculative approach to participatory spatial memory systems through interactive AI art experience.

title: ATGS: Anchored Temporal Gaussian Splatting for Long Volumetric Video Representation
speakers_or_contributors: Jiahao Wu; Jie Liang; Die Hu; Jiayu Yang; Kaiqiang Xiong; Xiang Li; Xiaoyun Zheng; Chao Wang; Ronggang Wang
affiliations: Peking University; Peng Cheng Lab; Pengcheng Lab; Pengcheng Laboratory; Pengcheng lab; School of Computer Science, Peking University
description: ATGS is a volumetric video reconstruction method that handles long sequences with complex motions and supports rendering from arbitrary viewpoints, enabling immersive experiences.

title: EasyVFX: Frequency-Driven Decoupling for Resource-Efficient VFX Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Yue Ma; Xu Ye; Qinghe Wang; Yucheng Wang; Hongyu Liu; Yinhan Zhang; Xinyu Wang; Yuanpeng Chen; Shanhui Mo; Paul Liang; Fangneng Zhan; Qifeng Chen
affiliations: Dalian University of Technology; Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; Independent; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Tsinghua University
description: EasyVFX is a resource-efficient framework for high-fidelity VFX synthesis. It leverages frequency-domain decomposition to disentangle spatial detail and motion dynamics, reducing learning complexity. A frequency-aware MoE enables efficient training, while a test-time adaptation strategy refines unseen effects. The approach achieves realistic, consistent results with minimal data and computation.

title: Schrödinger Bridges on Discretized Geometric Domains
speakers_or_contributors: Leticia Mattos Da Silva; Mohammad Sina Nabizadeh; Justin Solomon
affiliations: MIT CSAIL; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
description: We introduce a spatially discrete formulation of the Schrödinger bridge problem on meshes and grids that enables structure-preserving interpolation between probability distributions. We demonstrate our approach across mesh- and grid-based applications, including displacement interpolation, shape interpolation, and color histogram manipulation, highlighting its ability to achieve geometric fidelity with computational efficiency.

title: Taming optimization variance in compact neural shading networks
speakers_or_contributors: Benedikt Bitterli; Petrik Clarberg; Chris Cummings; Aaron Lefohn; Steve Marschner; Jan Novák; Fabrice Rousselle; Andrea Weidlich; Tizian Zeltner
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Modeling; Real-Time; Rendering
description: We present a training strategy for small networks that improves stability, reducing run-to-run variance by 88% and average loss by 38%. Multiple instances train in parallel on different batch slices, while weaker ones are periodically removed until only the best-performing instance remains, improving reliability for neural shading models.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Animating a Multiverse of Horror for Marvel Zombies
speakers_or_contributors: Bert Van Brande; Jordan Vanderzalm; Nate Barnard
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Pipeline Tools and Work; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
Inside the making of Marvel Zombies, Stellar Creative Lab reveals the animation pipeline behind Marvel’s darkest animated universe yet. From eerie environments to stylized characters, discover the end-to-end production pipeline behind the #1 Disney+ series – spanning asset creation, layout, animation, lighting, lookdev, and final rendering for a fast-paced episodic workflow.

Learn how the team used Maya for layout and animated performances, Arnold to achieve the show’s stylized horror look through toon shading, and Flow Production Tracking (formerly ShotGrid) to connect artists, assets, and production data across the studio. And discover how OpenUSD helped unify the pipeline, streamline collaboration, and support scalable production across the studio.

Alongside exclusive production insights from Marvel Zombies, the team will discuss how Autodesk’s connected creative ecosystem enabled an artist-friendly workflow built for modern animated storytelling — and how emerging AI-powered workflows may help shape the future of animation production.

title: Explicit flows for implicit surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Camille Buonomo; Julie Digne; Raphaëlle Chaine
description: Our paper presents a method for shape-morphing and deformation using an exact mathematical flow. We introduce a neural architecture guaranteed to encode a flow, leveraging implicit supervision of explicit flows, ensuring topological consistency and efficient forward/inverse computation without ODE solvers. Applications include shape-morphing, editing, and transitions via a reference shape.

title: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models
speakers_or_contributors: Cheng-De Fan; Chun-Wei Tuan Mu; Chen-Wei Chang; Chin-Yang Lin; Kun-Ru Wu; Yu-Chee Tseng; Yu-Lun Liu
affiliations: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University
description: This work introduces long-horizon event-based video generation, a challenging task prone to severe temporal inconsistency, and presents LongE2V, a novel approach leveraging video diffusion priors to jointly handle video reconstruction, prediction, and frame interpolation, recovering high-quality videos from sparse event streams.

title: Multi-feature Radiance Baking Neural Networks for Instant Volumetric Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Jiaming Liang; Hongliang Yuan; Meng Gai; Guoping Wang; Sheng Li
description: We propose MRBNN that delivers real-time photorealistic volume rendering. We leverage reformulation of in-scattering integral and design an efficient neural representation and sampling strategy. Our method achieves rendering in a few milliseconds with the ability to handle complex cases such as spatially varying albedo and heterogeneous phase.

title: Sponsored Keynote by Tripo AI: Dr. Yanpei Cao, Chief Scientist, The Teapot Test: First It Tested Showing. Now It Tests Making.
speakers_or_contributors: Yanpei Cao
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
The future of interactive media requires a fundamental shift: Moving from manually crafted environments to worlds generated on the fly by the imaginations of millions. Tripo’s core mission is to make this massive-scale, user-generated creation a reality. But to unlock this, we must rethink the entire generative pipeline, spanning from robust individual asset generation to the simulation of completely dynamic environments.

In this deep-dive keynote, Tripo’s chief scientist and research scientists will share the unvarnished journey of building a generative ecosystem from the ground up. We will explore how Tripo scaled from preliminary fundamental research to a robust infrastructure currently serving millions of creators.

The panel will first trace the rapid evolution of the generative landscape by dissecting Tripo’s full-stack approach to 3D generation. Through live demonstrations, the team will highlight key breakthroughs in geometry, topology, texturing, and rigging. These showcases will detail how we are bridging the gap between fundamental AI and graphics research and production-ready pipelines.

However, generating individual assets is only half the equation. To unlock truly interactive user-generated content, the field is rapidly expanding into Generative World Models. The team will share our latest research in this space and explain why we believe World Models will serve as the core engine for next-generation content. Because they simulate physics, dynamics, and continuous interaction without being strictly bound by traditional spatial constraints, these models offer a fundamentally new way to create. Through our latest demos, we will showcase what happens when AI steps beyond creating isolated objects and begins simulating living realities.

Speakers:
Dr. Yanpei Cao, Chief Scientist, Tripo AI

Dr. Yuanchen Guo, Research Scientist, Tripo AI; Dr. Zixin Zou, Research Scientist, Tripo AI; Dr. Yingtian Liu, Research Scientist, Tripo AI; Dr. Biao Zhang, Research Scientist, Tripo AI are lead research scientists at Tripo, with Dr. Zhang also serving as a professor at Xi’an Jiaotong University. Together, they architect the fundamental science and production infrastructure behind Tripo’s full-stack Generative AI platform. Their collective research focuses on overcoming the industry’s hardest generative bottlenecks, directly translating foundational AI breakthroughs into robust, production-ready creator tools. As active open-source contributors and frequent authors in top-tier venues like SIGGRAPH, their work drives the core models that serve millions of creators globally.

title: VFAvatar: Feed-Forward 3D Avatar Reconstruction from Casual Image Collections
speakers_or_contributors: Shuo Huang; Zixuan Wang; Xiaoyu Qin; Shikun Sun; Jiayi Li; Jia Jia
description:
We introduce Visual-Fusion-Avatar (VFAvatar), a novel end-to-end feed-forward framework that reconstructs 3D avatars by fusing visual cues from casual image collections in just a few seconds.
VFAvatar integrates a pose-free reconstruction foundation model with a pretrained human generation prior in a mutually reinforcing manner and achieves SOTA performance.

title: A Few-Step Generative Model on Cumulative Flow Maps
speakers_or_contributors: Zhiqi Li; Duowen Chen; Yuchen Sun; Bo Zhu
description: We propose a unified few-step generative framework based on cumulative flow maps for long-range transport in probability space. By connecting instantaneous updates with finite-time transport, it applies broadly to diffusion and flow models, supports few-step and one-step generation, requires minimal modifications, preserves quality, and reduces inference cost across diverse tasks.

title: A Hybrid Rendering Approach to Scalable Synthetic Data Generation
speakers_or_contributors: Alexander Glavin; Ryusuke Villemin; Alex Feldman; Anton Gribovskiy
description: We present a scalable hybrid rendering system that addresses the scarcity of rare training data for autonomous vehicles by automatically compositing radiometrically calibrated synthetic objects into real-world HDR image.

title: Autoregressive Modeling of Film with Applications in Video Montage
speakers_or_contributors: Marcelo Sandoval-Castañeda; Fabian Caba Heilbron; Shiry Ginosar; Bryan Russell; Josef Sivic; Alexei Efros; Gregory Shakhnarovich
affiliations: Adobe; Adobe Research; TTIC; University of California Berkeley
description: This work introduces an autoregressive transformer designed for video montage, transforming raw footage into cinematic sequences. We train it on large corpora of movies to learn film grammar. Instead of generating pixels, we introduce a footage-constrained decoding algorithm to select shots from the input raw footage.

title: Bounding Stratified Bernoulli Impulses for Ray Marching Gaussian Process Implicit Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Junjie Chen; Zhimin Fan; Ling-Qi Yan; Junqiu Zhu; Yanwen Guo; Kun Zhou; Jie Guo
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence; Nanjing University; Shandong University; Zhejiang University
description: In this paper, we present an optimized algorithm for rendering Gaussian Process Implicit Surfaces (GPISes). By using stratified Bernoulli impulses, we precompute point-level bounds stored in a bound map. We also represent region-level bounds with a spatial data structure. Combining these bounds, we achieve significant improvements in rendering performance.

title: High-Fidelity 4D Cloth Capture Pipeline with a Two-Level Pattern
speakers_or_contributors: Ziheng Liu; Anka Chen; Shu Chen; Yin Yang; Cem Yuksel; Jenny Lin
description: We present a 4D (spatio-temporal) cloth capture system and a two-level pattern that achieves 1mm spatial resolution using only 16 RGB cameras. Our method produces temporally coherent sequences with no self penetration that faithfully capture fine wrinkles and folds even during complex motions with self-contact.

title: Unfinished Decay: Animating Historical Images from Gigapixel Digitizations via Fragment-Based AI Workflows
speakers_or_contributors: Juergen Hagler; Celine Pham
affiliations: University of Applied Sciences Upper Austria
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Generative AI
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Unfinished Decay explores AI-assisted animation of historical images using gigapixel digitizations. Through a fragment-based workflow, microscopic surface details are animated to foreground materiality and decay. The project reframes AI as an interpretive collaborator, offering a material-sensitive alternative to low-resolution, reconstruction-driven approaches in cultural heritage visualization.

title: 4D Humans - Interactive Discussion
description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. Outside the room will be a series of poster boards for authors to gather around with the audience. Authors are invited to bring any material related to their paper that could instigate further conversation such as printouts, posters, demos, or other presentation aids. The interactive discussions provide attendees more interaction with the individual authors.

title: Adaptive Ray Marching for Rendering Gaussian Process Implicit Surfaces
speakers_or_contributors: Zhiqian Zhou; Dario Seyb; Shuang Zhao
affiliations: University of California Irvine; University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign; Valve
description: This paper accelerates Gaussian Process Implicit Surface (GPIS) rendering by introducing an online algorithm for incrementally sampling conditional Gaussian distributions along rays and an adaptive ray marching scheme that minimizes steps. Together, these techniques reduce rendering error by up to 46× at equal time compared to prior methods.

title: Film/VFX Production - Interactive Discussion
description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. Outside the room will be a series of poster boards for authors to gather around with the audience. Authors are invited to bring any material related to their paper that could instigate further conversation such as printouts, posters, demos, or other presentation aids. The interactive discussions provide attendees more interaction with the individual authors.

title: Generative Modeling with Orbit-Space Particle Flow Matching
speakers_or_contributors: Sinan Wang; Jinjin He; Shenyifan Lu; Ruicheng Wang; Greg Turk; Bo Zhu
description: Orbit-Space Geometric Probability Paths (OGPP) is a flow-matching framework for generative modeling of particle systems that exploits shared geometric structure and uses probability path geometry to encode geometric information. It enables high-quality, efficient generation of particle configurations and point-cloud surfaces with normals, achieving state-of-the-art performance across diverse generation tasks.

title: Differentiable Geometry Processing & Flow Maps - Interactive Discussion
description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. Outside the room will be a series of poster boards for authors to gather around with the audience. Authors are invited to bring any material related to their paper that could instigate further conversation such as printouts, posters, demos, or other presentation aids. The interactive discussions provide attendees more interaction with the individual authors.

title: Exploring Quantum Computing for Texture Generation
speakers_or_contributors: João S. Ferreira; Spencer Topel; Pierre Fromholz; James R. Wootton
keywords_tags: Art; Games; Industry Insight; Lighting; Modeling; Physical AI; Rendering; Scientific Visualization; Simulation
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Research & Education
description: This talk introduces a novel framework that bridges ray tracing and quantum physics. We use Quantum Collision Models to simulate materials with optical properties that dynamically evolve in response to light. This enables the synthesis of new materials with complex optical signatures, suitable for production pipelines and near-term quantum hardware.

title: Higgsfield AI | Kök Börü: An Artist-First Approach to AI in Animation
speakers_or_contributors: Amina Ömirjan
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Diversity, Equity and Inclusion; Generative AI; Networking; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Kök Börü is a hybrid hand-drawn and AI-integrated animated short that tells a rare Central Asian story, the kind the traditional greenlight system tends to overlook. In this session, the team behind the film gives you the full picture in brief, covering why this story needed to exist, a condensed look at the hybrid pipeline that made it possible, and the real budget, team, and timeline numbers behind it. At its heart, though, this is just an honest conversation about AI and animation. We will look at where these tools genuinely help, where they fall short, and how they can open doors for underrepresented voices without pushing the artist out of the center of the work.

title: Large-Scale Photogrammetric Documentation of St. John's Co-Cathedral: A Workflow for Cultural Heritage Preservation
speakers_or_contributors: Matthew Kenely; Mark Bugeja; Andre Grima; Peter Pullicino; Matthew Pullicino; Dylan Seychell
affiliations: SeyTravel Ltd; Stargate Studios; University of Malta
keywords_tags: Art; Capture/Scanning; Computer Vision; Image Processing; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Cultural heritage sites face threats from climate change, conflict, and degradation. Digital documentation has become a preservation necessity, demonstrated by Notre-Dame's 2019 fire. However, academic photogrammetry research remains disconnected from practical implementation by heritage institutions.

This project demonstrates architectural digitization is achievable using commercial tools. By documenting our methodology we provide professionals with replicable approaches to preserve cultural sites. Computer graphics techniques can directly serve the preservation of cultural heritage.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Real-Time Short Form Content: Behind The Scenes of the ILM Minis
speakers_or_contributors: Landis Fields
keywords_tags: Animation; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: In the fast paced world of short-form content, traditional rendering bottlenecks can stifle creativity, slow iteration, and limit use across media types. ILM’s Landis Fields and his team break down their real-time production pipeline that leverages GPU-accelerated workflows across each phase. Designed for creatives and technical directors alike, this session will walk through ILM’s process of creating stylized short form content. Everything from designing to executing a high-fidelity project entirely within a real-time ecosystem, drastically reducing render times, enabling live collaborative reviews, and empowering small teams to make creative decisions using a process that would otherwise be cost prohibitive.

title: REfact: Growing with Movement – A Hybrid Performance
speakers_or_contributors: Zelia ZZ Tan
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
REfract is a hybrid performance where a dancer and avatars coexist, evolve, and shift agency
through motion capture and live rendering. In a 15-minute performance, set in a minimalist
desert landscape with floating letters, the piece explores transformation, spatial perception, movement-driven storytelling, presence, autonomy, physical-digital interaction.

title: Shading, Relighting and Volumes - Interactive Discussion
description: After the summary presentations, attendees will participate in an interactive discussion. Outside the room will be a series of poster boards for authors to gather around with the audience. Authors are invited to bring any material related to their paper that could instigate further conversation such as printouts, posters, demos, or other presentation aids. The interactive discussions provide attendees more interaction with the individual authors.

title: Memory and Heritage - Q&A
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: After all the presentations, attendees are invited to participate in a Q&A.

title: A New Media Techspressionist Architectures Experiment: An AR Mediated Speculative Expression in an Audio/Visual 21st century - LA World
speakers_or_contributors: Gustavo Rincon; Gustavo Rincon; Joshua Dickinson; Tuna Bora
affiliations: ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community; Tuna Bora Art; Unfiltered Audio
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Augmented Reality; Computer Vision; Display; Education; Generative AI; Graphics Systems Architecture; Image Processing; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
As part of ACM SIGGRAPH DAC’s host city community outreach initiative, this project expands the New Media Architectures series through a collaborative cultural partnership within the City of Los Angeles and the Los Angeles Center for Digital Art (LACDA). Developed for SIGGRAPH LA 2026, the project positions Los Angeles itself as an experimental civic platform for artistic research, public engagement, and interdisciplinary collaboration across art, science, technology, and urban culture.

This co-curated Augmented Reality experimental exhibition brings together architects, artists, composers, computer scientists, media artists, musicians, designers, and technologists to collaboratively develop responsive site-specific works. By engaging the city's cultural histories and communities, the project transforms LA into a living laboratory.

The exhibition investigates how AI, autonomous systems, algorithmic governance, surveillance infrastructures, and machinic economies are reshaping contemporary urban life locally and globally. Through geospatial interventions and hybrid physical-digital experiences, the project proposes new curatorial and research models that rethink public and private space as an augmented networked fabric for artistic, and performative inquiry.

Framed through the concept of “Techspressionism,” the project explores emerging expressive forms shaped by cinematic culture, computational media, immersive environments, and layered digital realities. Central to this initiative is a dialogue between artists, researchers, and local arts communities exploring how creative practice can critically engage and reimagine the future of urban experience.

This DAC session invites the SIGGRAPH community to explore new collaborative models of cultural expansive production that transform Los Angeles into a distributed platform for collective imagination, experimentation, public discourse, and community-centered technological futures.

title: From Cloud to Set: Discussing the Future of Cloud, Adaptive, and AI-Native Production
speakers_or_contributors: Eric Pinkel
keywords_tags: Capture/Scanning; Digital Twins; Generative AI; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Visual Effects
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Cloud to Set: The Future of Adaptive and AI-Native Production is an exciting BOF session at SIGGRAPH featuring an award-winning panel of filmmakers, technologists, and creative innovators shaping the future of production. Panelists Erik Weaver (USC ETC), Erick Geisler (Global Objects, G2 Immersive Studios), and Christopher Nichols (Chaos Innovation Lab, Monstrous Moonshine) bring decades of combined experience across Emmy-winning work, groundbreaking VFX, and pioneering AI-native and cloud-based production pipelines. Designed as a BOF, the session emphasizes open dialogue, networking, and shared exploration, with dedicated Q&A and industry insights exchanged between panelists and attendees through discussion.

title: Hands-on Course: Sponsored by Autodesk: From Footage to Previs: AI Mocap Workflows with Autodesk Flow Studio and Maya
speakers_or_contributors: Duane Rutkowski
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This hands-on course is designed for professional animation and VFX artists with experience using Autodesk Maya who want to explore new approaches to previsualization and early production.

Using Autodesk Flow Studio, participants will work with live-action footage to generate editable exports such as AI mocap, camera tracking, character passes, clean plates, full 3D scenes, and more. These elements will be used as a foundation for exploring performance, camera movement, and spatial storytelling before heavy asset development begins. The workshop focuses on how early creative exploration can inform layout, shot design, and production planning.

Through practical examples and guided walkthroughs, participants will understand how Flow Studio can support early iteration and integrate with Maya to establish creative intent and a production-ready foundation.

Learning Objectives
By the end of the hands-on course, participants will be able to:
· Use Flow Studio to generate editable mocap data, camera tracking, character passes, clean plates and full 3D scenes from live-action footage
· Apply Flow Studio outputs to previsualization and layout workflows in Maya
· Explore performance, camera movement, and scene layout during early production
· Export and refine AI-generated elements using USD and FBX
· Identify opportunities to incorporate Flow Studio into early-stage animation and VFX pipelines

Target Audience
Animation and VFX artists, technical directors, and Maya users working in visual effects, animation, and games, as well as students with Maya experience.

Experience Level
intermediate (prior experience with Maya and VFX pipelines recommended.)

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: Isaac Sim and the Fine Art of Making Your Robot Pick Things Up
speakers_or_contributors: Rishabh Chadha
keywords_tags: Generative AI; Physical AI; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Simulation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description:
This hands-on course in the NVIDIA Physical AI Agent Bootcamp turns an empty USD stage into a working Isaac Sim robot scene, with AI agents acting as simulation setup partners. Attendees will import a robot manipulator from URDF, compose the scene using prior lab assets or user-selected environments, and bring SimReady content into Isaac for testing.
The session focuses on the practical path from “nothing is in the scene” to “the robot picked up the cube.” Participants will configure the robot, prepare the object, validate physics behavior, tune the scene, and execute a basic pick-and-place task. Agent skills handle repeatable setup steps so attendees can focus on scene intent, simulation checks, and task behavior.
By the end, attendees will have a composed Isaac Sim scene with a physics-validated robot, interactive assets, and a successful pick-and-place task that connects OpenUSD scene building to robotics simulation.

title: The State of Vulkan: Updates, Tools, and Developer Resources
speakers_or_contributors: Ralph Potter; Hai Nguyen; Spencer Fricke; Steve Winston
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Graphics Systems Architecture; Hardware; Industry Insight; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Join the Vulkan Working Group for an update on the latest developments across the Vulkan ecosystem. Vulkan Working Group Chair Ralph Potter will discuss the group's direction, recent accomplishments, and upcoming work. Additional presentations will cover descriptor heaps, Vulkan debugging and developer tools, and an overview of available Vulkan tutorials and learning resources. Whether you are new to Vulkan or an experienced developer, this BOF is an opportunity to learn about current best practices, new capabilities, and the future of the API directly from the experts helping shape it.

title: World Models for Robotics: Bridging Graphics, Simulation, and Physical Intelligence
speakers_or_contributors: Junfan Zhu
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Physical AI; Robotics; Simulation; Spatial Computing
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
World models are rapidly emerging as a unifying paradigm across graphics, simulation, spatial intelligence, embodied AI, and robotics. This Birds of a Feather brings together researchers, engineers, founders, and students to discuss the latest advances in generative world models, digital twins, neural simulation, 3D foundation models, active perception, and robot learning. Through short introductions and open discussion, participants will explore how graphics and simulation communities can help accelerate the development of scalable physical AI systems. All perspectives and backgrounds are welcome.
Organized by Saturday Robotics, an open forum connecting researchers and practitioners across embodied AI, world models, and physical intelligence.

title: Culture & Heritage
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Augmented Reality; Digital Twins; Education; Games; Generative AI; Real-Time; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience

title: Dichoptic Foveation
speakers_or_contributors: Henry Kam; Colin Groth; Jenna Kang; Pratham Saraf; Qi Sun; Kenneth Chen
description: This paper presents a psychophysical study of dichoptic foveation, applying blur to one eye and sharpening to the other, in virtual reality. A perceptual model of interocular frequency differences across the visual field is developed and applied to enhance foveated rendering quality in VR headsets.

title: Echoes of the Prior: A Computational Phenomenology of Forgetting
speakers_or_contributors: Gege Gao; Bernhard Schölkopf; Andreas Geiger
affiliations: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems; University of Tuebingen; University of Tübingen
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Echoes of the Prior visualizes entropy within neural networks, turning machine forgetting into an interactive 4D experience. Unlike traditional CG simulating physical reality, this work reveals the aesthetics of algorithmic degradation, transforming technical failure into a new artistic medium bridging AI explainability with philosophical inquiry. It challenges the community to look beyond high-fidelity reconstruction and embrace the fragility of intelligence, proposing that data dissolution is as significant as its preservation.

title: Materiality and Computation
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Display; Education; Fabrication; Generative AI; Geometry; Hardware; Image Processing; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Performance; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Robotics
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience

title: MeshFEM: A Block-accelerated Solver for Nonlinear Finite Elements
speakers_or_contributors: Haleh Mohammadian; Xinzhuo Hu; Roi Poranne; Julian Panetta
affiliations: University of California Davis; University of Haifa
description: We present a high-performance Newton-type solver designed for the nonlinear finite element problems common in simulation and geometry processing. By leveraging the inherent block structure of spatially discretized systems across all solver phases, we achieve significant speedups over existing direct-solver-based frameworks without compromising accuracy, flexibility, or ease of use.

title: Numerical Methods for Geometry Processing
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Fabrication; Geometry; Modeling; Simulation

title: Perception
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Rendering; Simulation; Virtual Reality

title: Predator: Badlands – The Hunt for Emotions
speakers_or_contributors: Sheldon Stopsack; Karl Rapley
description: For Predator: Badlands, Wētā FX VFX Supervisor Sheldon Stopsack and Animation Supervisor Karl Rapley present how they developed Dek, a fully digital facial performance anchored in a practical on-set suit, to sustain a nuanced emotional arc without compromising the Predator’s franchise-defining silhouette and ferocity.

title: Probe-based Walk on Spheres for Efficient Path Reusing
speakers_or_contributors: Wanchao Huang; Yutian Zhu; Qing Fang; Ligang Liu
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China
description: A Walk on Spheres-based Monte Carlo PDE solver that reuses full random walk paths via fixed probes, achieving lower variance and faster convergence on mixed-boundary Laplace, Poisson, and screened Poisson problems.

title: Role-Aware Virtual Agents for Navigational Interaction guided by a Multimodal Large Language Model
speakers_or_contributors: Minyoung Kim; Changyang Li; Cuong Nguyen; Lap-Fai Yu
affiliations: Adobe Research; George Mason University; Goertek Alpha Labs
description: We present a role-aware virtual agent navigation system that generates consistent, role-aligned behaviors. Using multimodal large language models (MLLMs), our approach interprets scene context, user state, and role-context instructions to produce navigation decisions and stylized paths, enabling agents to respond dynamically and behave coherently in immersive environments such as AR.

title: Show Your Feelings
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Computer Vision; Performance

title: Simulating Memory: Teaching Gullah-Geechee Heritage through Virtual Performances by Community Leaders at a Reconstruction-Era Historical Site
speakers_or_contributors: Christopher Maraffi; Iyare Oronsaye; Ryan Khan
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Augmented Reality; Education; Generative AI; Real-Time; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: NCSU MADTech faculty collaborated with Historic Mitchelville Freedom Park on a sponsored studio course that taught students Gullah-Geechee heritage through the development of virtual scenes that featured digital doubles of community leaders in simulations of park landmarks. Students gained valuable design skills in applying these emerging technologies to a real-world digital heritage research project and earned College global experience credits by learning about the Gullah-Geechee roots of American Black culture from the descendants of the Mitchelville community.

title: VR, Haptics & Hardware
keywords_tags: Animation; Fabrication; Haptics; Simulation; Virtual Reality

title: Walk This Way: Monte Carlo Geometry
Emerging Technologies Demo - A Generalizable Light Transport 3D Embedding for Global Illumination
speakers_or_contributors: Bing Xu
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper:
A Generalizable Light Transport 3D Embedding for Global Illumination

We introduce a scalable 3D embedding that learns global illumination directly from 3D scene configurations. This design scales to environments with millions of triangles, enabling the first generalizable GI learning on complex, high-fidelity indoor scenes, far beyond prior limits. It bypasses per-scene retraining and screen-space artifacts to deliver realistic rendering.

Paper Presentation Presented: Tuesday, July 21, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: Light Transport; Room 408 A

title: EgoRelight: Egocentric Human Capture and Illumination Recovery for Relightable and Photoreal Avatar Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Jianchun Chen; Yinda Zhang; Rohit Pandey; Thabo Beeler; Marc Habermann; Christian Theobalt
affiliations: Google; Max Planck Institute for Informatics
description: To enable seamless mixed-reality telepresence in the wild, we present EgoRelight, a robust human performance capture and HDR illumination recovery approach using a single portable head-mounted device. This dual capability drives the photorealistic and relightable full-body 3D avatars, blending users naturally into surrounding environments.

title: Fast Sparse Matrix Permutation for Mesh-Based Direct Solvers
speakers_or_contributors: Behrooz Zarebavani; Ahmed Mahmoud; Ana Dodik; Changcheng Yuan; Serban Porumbescu; John Owens; Maryam Mehri Dehnavi; Justin Solomon
affiliations: MIT CSAIL; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Texas A&M University; University of California Davis; University of Toronto
description: We present a fast sparse matrix permutation algorithm tailored to mesh-based direct solvers. By exploiting mesh structure through patch-based nested dissection, our method reduces permutation overhead while preserving high-quality orderings, leading to substantial end-to-end speedups in sparse Cholesky factorization on both CPUs and GPUs.

title: Gradient Domain Reconstruction for Monte Carlo PDE Solvers
speakers_or_contributors: Jiaqi Wu; Xuejun Hu; Shuang Zhao; Kun Xu
affiliations: CS Dept, Tsinghua University; Tsinghua University; University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
description: We present a gradient-domain Monte Carlo framework for solving Poisson equations on complex domains. The method directly estimates solution differences between query points and reconstructs the final solution efficiently, reducing variance and improving convergence without introducing additional bias beyond the underlying WoS-based solver.

title: Reality Check: How Avatar and Face Representation Affect the Perceptual Evaluation of Synthesized Gestures
speakers_or_contributors: Haoyang Du; Yinghan Xu; John Dingliana; Brian Keegan; Rachel McDonnell; Cathy Ennis
affiliations: Maynooth University; Technological University Dublin; Trinity College Dublin
description: This paper investigates how avatar and face representation influence the perceptual evaluation of synthesized co-speech gestures. Across three controlled experiments, it shows that visual presentation systematically biases judgments of motion quality and provides practical recommendations for fairer, more reliable benchmarking of gesture generation systems.

title: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage
speakers_or_contributors: Jimmz Zhang
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage is an interactive, generative art installation that re-imagines Los Angeles as a living, ever-evolving digital twin. Conceived for SIGGRAPH 2026, the project bridges LA’s rich cinematic legacy with the frontier of spatial computing. By seamlessly integrating Kinect motion tracking and AI-generated point clouds, it invites participants to co-author a collaborative digital monument. As users input their personal memories and desires of LA, individual landmarks dynamically merge, overlap, and fold into a singular, massive 3D cyber environment—blurring the boundaries between physical presence, collective memory, and cybernetic space.

Credit：
Visual & Interactive Director: Jimmz ZHANG, Digital FUN & TEA Community
Technical Director: Ruokun CHEN, TEA Community & FLARE Lab
Sound Artist: Louzhang, USC School of Cinematic Arts

title: MiniMax | New Frontier in Multimodality
speakers_or_contributors: Leanna Ren
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Computer Vision; Games; Generative AI; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Driven by the mission to co-create intelligence with everyone, MiniMax is dedicated to accelerating the path to AGI. Through independently developed foundation models, MiniMax enables the seamless integration of text, audio, image, video, and music. This session will unveil a new frontier in multimodality, exploring how these cross-modal capabilities redefine the future of next-generation creative workflows and intelligent experiences.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Building a GPU Production Renderer with OptiX at Netflix Animation Studios
speakers_or_contributors: Luke Emrose
keywords_tags: Animation; Graphics Systems Architecture; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Maintaining real-time interactivity while navigating complex, large-scale animation environments is a major challenge for production pipelines. In this session, Netflix Animation Studios will share its journey building Glimpse, a custom, fully OpenUSD-based ray tracing viewport using NVIDIA OptiX, designed to replace standard viewports such as Maya Viewport 2.0. The presentation will cover how the team addresses large-scale scene complexity, optimizes USD memory sharing, and supports native instancing for large production scenes. Attendees will learn how this high-performance, GPU-accelerated viewport was designed for fast adoption in production workflows.

title: Coding Wagara: Fostering Motivation through Generating Japanese Patterns using SF Symbols
speakers_or_contributors: Shinya Nishide
affiliations: Senri International School of Kwansei Gakuin
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Bridging visual and syntax-based programming, this Art-Thinking curriculum fosters intrinsic motivation. By combining Apple’s SF Symbols to procedurally generate original Japanese patterns (Wagara), students master abstract concepts like coordinates and loops. This culturally responsive approach transforms syntax anxiety into "productive struggle," yielding tangible, creative outcomes.

title: Dynamic Visual Dominance in Stereoscopic Foveation
speakers_or_contributors: Daniel Jiménez Navarro; Colin Groth; Kenneth Chen; Qi Sun; Karol Myszkowski; Hans-Peter Seidel; Ana Serrano
affiliations: Max Planck Institute for Informatics; New York University; Universidad de Zaragoza
description: This work investigates visual dominance as a dynamic property across the FOV. Using a blur detection task, we confirm dynamic dominance in the fovea and calibrate monocular dominance in the periphery. The resulting gaze-independent peripheral dominance map can be integrated into different rendering pipelines to enhance foveation in non-dominant regions.

title: Electrospun Fields: 3D Nano-Fiber Material Computation as Design Method
speakers_or_contributors: Justin Wan; Ayah Mahmoud; Sergio Mutis; Avantika Velho; Zhiyan Xing; Behnaz Farahi
affiliations: Harvard University; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Media Lab, Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Electrospun Fields advances SIGGRAPH’s interest in material computation by framing electric fields as programmable boundary conditions and fiber deposition as a material rendering of invisible forces. It contributes a reproducible UR20 robotic workflow for non-planar electrospinning, enabling ultra-light 3D membranes difficult to achieve with planar setups. By linking conductive scaffold topologies to material behaviors using bio-compatible polymers, this framework opens novel pathways for emergent and expressive computational art and fabrication.

title: Monte Carlo PDE Solvers for Nonlinear Radiative Boundary Conditions
speakers_or_contributors: Anchang Bao; Enya Shen; Jianmin Wang
affiliations: School of Software and BNRist, Tsinghua University
description: This paper extends Monte Carlo PDE solvers to handle nonlinear radiative boundary conditions using a Picard-style fixed-point iteration framework. It introduces a heteroscedastic regression-based denoising method for boundary estimates and demonstrates the approach on heat radiation simulations with complex geometries, achieving higher accuracy than standard linearization strategies.

title: Rigs2Emotion: Automatic emotion classification from facial rigs
speakers_or_contributors: Michaël Bernier; Bastien Arcelin
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Computer Vision
description: We present Rigs2Emotion, a ML tool based on rig controllers values to classify emotions of animations frames accurately. It can be used on any production, is character- and rig-specific and is much more data-efficient than more usual method using audio or image fine-tuned pre-trained models.

title: Statistical Gradient Filtering for Geometry Optimization Under Limited Observations
speakers_or_contributors: Wonjong Jang; Gwangjin Ju; Seungyong Lee
description: We present a novel technique that leverages statistical shape space for accurate and robust 3D geometry optimization under limited observations, such as non-rigid registration on partial scans and single-view inverse rendering.

title: Voronoi Rooms: Dynamic Visibility Modulation of Overlapping Spaces for Telepresence
speakers_or_contributors: Taehei Kim; Jihun Shin; Hyeshim Kim; Hyuckjin Jang; Jiho Kang; Sung-Hee Lee
description: We propose a multi-user Mixed Reality telepresence system that aligns rooms to maximize shared space while visualizing non-shared spaces. Using Voronoi-based dynamic visibility modulation based on user proximity, our approach conveys spatial context of each user's environment, enabling seamless interaction across dynamically connected multi-room environments.

title: A Two-Millisecond Passthrough Headset for Perceptual Studies
speakers_or_contributors: Eric Penner; Josephine D'Angelo; Clinton Smith; Nathan Matsuda; Neethan Siva; Phillip Guan
affiliations: Reality Labs Research, Meta; Reality Labs, Meta
description: We present a nearly perspective-correct video passthrough headset with just 2 milliseconds of end-to-end latency. We use this headset to study perceptual requirements for low-latency passthrough in head-mounted displays.

title: Atmospheric Haptics: Rendering Airflow and Temperature as Tactile Fields for Interactive Experiences in Virtual Reality
speakers_or_contributors: Shengyi Zhan; Xueting Wu; Ning Zou
affiliations: College of computer science and technology, Zhejiang University,Hangzhou, Zhejiang; School of software technology, Zhejiang University
description: Atmospheric Haptics treats airflow and temperature as programmable, non-contact tactile fields for VR. Four perceptual primitives—Intensity, Temperature, Spatiality, and Dynamics—drive a three-layer rendering pipeline implemented in a handheld prototype. A user study confirms enhanced realism, improved navigation precision, and convincing wetness illusions without liquid contact.

title: Bringing The Mandalorian and Grogu to Life: Virtual Production, Creatures, and VFX Innovations
speakers_or_contributors: John Knoll; Hal Hickel; Rebecca Forth
keywords_tags: Animation; Lighting; Modeling; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering; Virtual Reality
description: Industrial Light & Magic invites you behind the scenes of The Mandalorian and Grogu for an in-depth exploration of the visual effects that brought the latest Star Wars feature to life. In this panel, the filmmakers and artists will unpack how groundbreaking real-time technology, virtual production workflows, and time-tested VFX techniques came together to create immersive worlds, unforgettable characters, and epic action on the big screen. From on-set innovation to final pixels, join the ILM team as they share creative insights, technical breakthroughs, and lessons learned while pushing the boundaries of modern visual storytelling.

title: GMT: A Geometric Multigrid Transformer Solver for Microstructure Homogenization
speakers_or_contributors: Yu Xing; Yang Liu; Tianyang Xue; Lin Lu
affiliations: Microsoft Research Asia; Shandong University; The University of Hong Kong
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Fabrication; Simulation
description: GMT is a Geometric Multigrid Transformer that significantly accelerates microstructure homogenization. Embedding Transformers into the multigrid hierarchy to capture multi-scale physical interactions delivers a 160x speedup over SOTA GPU solvers while maintaining engineering-grade accuracy at high resolutions. GMT enables real-time material screening, multi-scale simulations, and rapid inverse design of metamaterials.

title: Walk on Decomposed Subdomains: A Hybrid Monte Carlo–Deterministic Solver for Elliptic PDEs
speakers_or_contributors: Clément Jambon; Mohammad Sina Nabizadeh; Mina Konaković Luković
affiliations: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
description: We introduce a hybrid Monte Carlo-deterministic solver for elliptic PDEs on complex geometries. Our method decomposes the domain into simple subdomains, estimates local solution operators with Monte Carlo and couples them via a sparse linear system, delivering low-variance solutions orders of magnitude faster than pure Monte Carlo, without volumetric meshing.

title: Beaver World: Integrating Indigenous Wisdom and Ecological Simulation in a 3D Educational Game
speakers_or_contributors: Yuanyuan Xu; Chi Zhen; Obiajulu Nwosisi; Amir Rahsaz; Tanhab Sarker; Miles Thorogood; Patricia Lasserre; Aleksandra Dulic
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Beaver World uses beaver-based simulation to teach watershed restoration, integrating Indigenous ecological knowledge with climate education.

title: Computational Visual Semiotics: The “L” Programming Language by Frieder Nake
speakers_or_contributors: Andres Burbano
keywords_tags: Art; Education; Image Processing; Math Foundations and Theory
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: This study investigates Frider Nake's 1970 programming language "L" to explore the intersection of code and art. By examining how "L" translates programming languages into imaginative and intellectual explorations, we gain insights into the cultural dimensions of code and its potential as a medium for artistic production. This analysis deepens our understanding of how programming languages historically engaged with images, graphics and visual semiotics.

title: Igniting Performance in Avatar: Fire and Ash - a Case Study in Varang's Facial Performance
speakers_or_contributors: Stuart Adcock; Rebecca Leybourne
description: This talk explores how Wētā FX’s Anatomically Plausible Facial System enabled Varang’s ferocious, emotionally authentic performance in Avatar: Fire and Ash. Using Neytiri as a case study, we examine the motivations behind a revolutionary shift in facial technology and end-to-end development challenges required for Varang to stand alongside legacy characters.

title: Retrofitting Existing 3D Objects with Surface-Conforming Capacitive Sensing
speakers_or_contributors: Andela Ilic; Junpeng Gao; Zhipeng Li; Yijing Jiang; Rachel Schuchert; Manuel Meier; Philipp Herholz; Christian Holz
description: We present a computational fabrication pipeline for retrofitting 3D objects with real-time multi-touch sensing without modifying their interiors. From a 3D scan, our method routes and optimizes surface-conforming electrode layouts under geometric, fabrication, and hardware constraints. We guide assembly via projection to enable precise spatial input sensing on curved objects.

title: Robust Biharmonic Skinning Using Geometric Fields
speakers_or_contributors: Ana Dodik; Vincent Sitzmann; Justin Solomon; Oded Stein
affiliations: MIT CSAIL; Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT); University of Southern California
description: A mesh-free approach for computing bounded biharmonic skinning weights. Our method works well even in presence of severe mesh quality degradation and can incorporate artistic control via weight-painting. It relies on a differentiable field and incorporates boundary handling via hardware accelerated ray-tracing.

title: StyleID: A Perception-Aware Dataset and Metric for Stylization-Agnostic Facial Identity Recognition
speakers_or_contributors: Kwan Yun; Changmin Lee; AYeong Jeong; Youngseo Kim; Seungmi Lee; Junyong Noh
affiliations: KAIST, Visual Media Lab; Visual Media Lab, KAIST
description: StyleID is a perception-aware dataset and evaluation framework for measuring facial identity consistency under artistic stylization. By aligning identity similarity with human judgments across styles and strengths, it improves robustness beyond natural-photo face encoders and better supports stylized portrait generation and evaluation.

title: Walking on Spheres and Talking to Neighbors: Variance Reduction for Laplace's Equation
speakers_or_contributors: Michael Czekanski; Benjamin Faber; Margaret Fairborn; Adelle Wright; David Bindel
affiliations: Columbia University; Cornell University; University of Wisconsin-Madison
description: We introduce a novel variance reduction scheme for Walk on Spheres with Laplace's equation, demonstrate performance, and provide analytic guarantees on performance.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Advancing LED Virtual Production with Neural Rendering
speakers_or_contributors: Markus Hoell
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Display; Performance; Real-Time; Rendering
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience; Discover
description: Achieving high frame rates and visual fidelity on LED stages requires continuous pipeline innovation. This session explores how NVIDIA DLSS technologies, including Frame Generation, Super Resolution, and Ray Reconstruction, can deliver performance and quality gains for Unreal Engine-based virtual production. Markus Hoell will discuss how real-time AI inference can offload heavy rendering workloads, along with real-world developer stories covering engineering challenges, pipeline hurdles, future DLSS architecture considerations, and lessons learned from adapting neural rendering technologies for LED environments.

title: Generalized Aberrations for Processing-Aware Optical Design
speakers_or_contributors: Geoffroi Côté; Ethan Tseng; Felix Heide
description: Optimal imaging performance requires designing optics with the downstream processing in the loop—especially as AI pipelines grow in importance. Yet the scalar nature of loss functions in the processing-aware setting breaks industry-standard lens design solvers. We generalize classical ray aberrations to task-driven objectives, bringing robust solvers to end-to-end design.

title: JGS2-GQ: Training-free 2nd Jacobi with Gaussian Quadrature
speakers_or_contributors: Dewen Guo; Zixuan Lu; Zhiyong He; Yuqi Meng; Bohan Wang; Lei Lan; Weiwei Xu; Chenfanfu Jiang; Yin Yang
affiliations: National University of Singapore; State Key Lab of CAD and CG, Zhejiang University; University of California Los Angeles; University of Utah
description: JGS2-GQ replaces data-driven Cubature with training-free Gaussian Quadrature, enabling stable, high-resolution GPU simulation. It handles frictional contact, maintains Newton-like convergence, and outperforms JGS2 by 50% on novel deformations without training.

title: Neural Quadrature Rule and Autoregressive Adaptive Sampling
speakers_or_contributors: Haolin Lu; Liwen Wu; Zimo Wang; Tzu-Mao Li; Ravi Ramamoorthi
description: Learnable adaptive sampler and integrator using Transformer.

title: Quantifying reality of ultra-realistic 3-D displays - the effect of resolution and contrast
speakers_or_contributors: Joseph Gerard March; Dounia Hammou; Simon J. Watt; Rafał K. Mantiuk
description: We built an ultrarealistic display system capable of reproducing 3D scenes with such fidelity that they are indistinguishable from their real counterparts. Then, we used the display to measure how reducing resolution and contrast affects perceived realism.

title: It’s Not Just a Phase: Creating Phase-Aligned Peripheral Metamers
speakers_or_contributors: Sophie Kergaßner; Piotr Didyk
description: We present a method for enhancing the perceived quality of foveated images by extrapolating local image statistics, such as intensity, orientation and especially phase, from coarse to fine scales. In particular, we demonstrate that correct phase alignment across space and scales is crucial for achieving perceptually convincing image quality.

title: To Perform/ To Live: Decolonizing of Digital Music Instruments and Feminism with Human-AI Co-Created Embodied Experience of Daily Objects

description: To Perform/To Live brings decolonial, China-situated feminist perspectives into sonic art. It repurposes domestic objects and routines as interface logic, making gendered labor audible without romanticizing it and challenging interaction defaults. Performances are sensed and shaped live, then captured as traces. Audio analysis generates a terrain-like form, 3D-printed as a tactile disk for re-performance, linking decoloniality, embodied interaction, human-AI co-creation, and fabrication.

Panelists will discuss real-world applications — from AI-assisted spec review and conformance testing to smarter developer outreach and feedback analysis. Attendees are invited to join the conversation and share how AI is influencing their own standards workflows. Come ready to ask questions and help shape what's next.

keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Education; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Image Processing; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing

title: Everyone Is an Obscure Artist: Living Lineages in Computational Creativity
speakers_or_contributors: Yosun Chang
keywords_tags: Animation; Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Augmented Reality; Capture/Scanning; Chapters; Computer Vision; Digital Twins; Display; Diversity, Equity and Inclusion; Dynamics; Education; Ethics and Society; Fabrication; Games; Generative AI; Geometry; Graphics Systems Architecture; Haptics; Hardware; History; Image Processing; Industry Insight; Lighting; Math Foundations and Theory; Modeling; Networking; Performance; Physical AI; Pioneers; Pipeline Tools and Work; Real-Time; Rendering; Robotics; Scientific Visualization; Simulation; Spatial Computing; Virtual Reality; Visual Effects
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description:
Fame is an edge case. Obscurity is the normal condition of creative work.

Important creative technology often appears first as demos, tools, performances, installations, prototypes, virtual worlds, and interactive systems — not papers. Even when formally presented or archived, these works can disappear from the field’s working memory because research culture transmits papers better than living artifacts.

This BOF invites artists, researchers, tool-builders, educators, and independent creators to discuss how experimental work becomes legible, transmissible, and buildable across computer graphics, AI, XR, animation, games, and computational performance.

The goal is continuity: a living support network for work carried forward.

title: Lost Spirits: An Interactive Encounter with a Fractured Soul
speakers_or_contributors: Angela Wu
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Generative AI; Real-Time
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Lost Spirits is an interactive game prototype exploring emotionally resonant local AI characters on edge devices. The player acts as a medium, helping a fractured ghost reconstruct her identity. Built via an AI-augmented workflow, the experience allows players to guide the spirit’s evolution toward final redemption or condemnation.

title: RoboSax Melody Sl0t Machine
speakers_or_contributors: Masatoshi Hamanaka; Gou Koutaki
keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Augmented Reality; Performance; Physical AI; Real-Time; Virtual Reality
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Melody Slot Machine has dials with the staves, which can be rotated to change melody variations which are generated by AI-based melody-morphing method. The microcomputer on RoboSax receives MIDI notes from Melody Slot Machine and move servomotors so that the fingering corresponds to the note number.

speakers_or_contributors: Annette Lapham
keywords_tags: Animation; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Augmented Reality; Education; Ethics and Society; Games; Generative AI; Performance; Real-Time; Spatial Computing

Source-grounded historical personas speak with users while a turn-level Conversation Meter reads conversational quality, unlocks deeper disclosure, remembers topic progress across sessions, and surfaces a Tapestry of related ideas that guides curiosity through history, philosophy, science, and justice over time.

title: Vibing Art: AI-Generated Interactive Visuals Reacting to Sound and Movement in Your Pocket
speakers_or_contributors: Mingxuan Xie; Tianzi Hou; Bingyu Yu; Yunyi Chen
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Audio; Generative AI; Real-Time
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies

Powered by cloud AI generating custom WebGL/WebGPU shaders under 15s while on-device AI handles audio, gesture, and pose reactivity in real time. Perform with your hands, mirror to any screen, share and remix with friends!

title: Data Materialization: Principles and Practices in Artistic Research
speakers_or_contributors: Courtney Starrett; Susan Reiser
affiliations: Texas A&M University; University of North Carolina Asheville
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: Defining data materialization is relevant because it is an emerging practice that like SIGGRAPH lies at the intersection of art and technology. Furthermore, as data collection explodes in every facet of society, data materialization offers a new form of epistemic translation--the transformation of abstract datasets into physical forms that generate meaning through material–data connections rather than informational communication.

title: Printing the Underdetermined: Materializing Multi-solutionness in Figurative Paintings
speakers_or_contributors: Yutao Ming; Teng Xu; Youjia Wang; Yunyang Liu; Fengmin Yang; Fuqiang Zhao; Jingyi Yu; Yanjun Zhou
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Display; Generative AI; Modeling; Pipeline Tools and Work
interest_area: Arts & Design; New Technologies; Production & Animation
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: As generative models enter graphics pipelines, we need ways to see what gets assumed, invented, or discarded when an image becomes a 3D scene. Figurative paintings are a clear test case. Our workflow keeps this non-uniqueness explicit by sampling plausible completions, reconstructing each via 3D Gaussian Splatting, and fabricating them with volumetric 3D Printing. These artifacts expose where evidence constrains geometry and where it doesn't, opening a shared language for uncertainty across art and graphics.

title: Electronic Theater (separate ticket required)
description:
Every year the Electronic Theater highlights a selection of the very best in computer animation and this year's edition continues in this tradition.

The prestigious, Oscar® qualifying festival is your ticket to this best, and soon to be buzziest, short film projects. Opening night also includes the presentation of the festival awards!

title: Frontier Talk: Towards a Graphics-Silicon Gymnasium to Shape the Future of Micro-Embodied AI
speakers_or_contributors: Christopher Torng
registration_category: Full Conference Supporter; Full Conference; Experience
description: The graphics community thrives on standardized APIs and accessible GPU silicon. However, a parallel frontier awaits in micro-embodied AI, where tiny units capture images, perform mental geometric simulations, and intelligently act under constraints. However, the traditional graphics research abstraction breaks down in this regime. GPUs do not fit, and diverse custom silicon lacks common APIs. Confined to simulation, graphics researchers cannot access physical experimentation to explore their native challenges. This talk envisions a graphics-silicon gymnasium to bridge the gap and unlock fundamental questions: What new graphics algorithms emerge under the unique constraints of tiny, specialized silicon? What minimum properties are required for mental geometric simulations at miniature scales? We walk through semiconductor history to confirm absolute physical constraints, explore an illustrative camera-equipped smart ingestible pill, and discuss the merits of a gymnasium environment that can empower the graphics community to shape the future of micro-embodied AI.

title: Pathfinders
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education

title: AI for Creative Visual Content Generation, Editing and Understanding
speakers_or_contributors: Songlin Yang; Gaurav Parmar; Ozgur Kara; Jiaju Ma; Lvmin Zhang; Or Patashnik; Fabian Caba Heilbron; Jun-Yan Zhu; Daniel Cohen-Or; Maneesh Agrawala
affiliations: Adobe Research; Carnegie Mellon University; Eonix AI; Lightricks; Stanford University; Tel Aviv University; The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign; d1n0
keywords_tags: Art; Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Computer Vision; Generative AI; Image Processing
interest_area: Arts & Design; Gaming & Interactive; New Technologies; Production & Animation; Research & Education
description: The 10th installment of the CVEU workshop series marks a transformative shift toward the Next-Generation Creator-Machine Co-Learning Paradigm. Organized by leading academic researchers and the technical architects behind current video generation models, this workshop explores a future where generative AI evolves from a passive tool into a collaborative partner that internalizes both physical laws and artistic intent. Our primary objective is to bridge the "semantic gap" between raw visual tokens and the sophisticated language of cinema, focusing on how World Models can balance physical factuality with creative license. We will investigate how machine learning translate abstract user intent into precise model behavior, effectively reconstructing traditional production pipelines for professional filmmakers and artists alike. Attendees can expect a high-energy program featuring technical demos of AI-native creative workflows, provocative keynote sessions, and an open "Research-meets-Art" roundtable designed to spark dialogue between model builders and visual storytellers. This forum offers a unique opportunity to engage directly with the creators of the world’s most advanced visual AI systems to define the next era of human-machine synergy in the digital arts.

keywords_tags: Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning; Games; Graphics Systems Architecture; Performance; Real-Time; Spatial Computing

description: Join the Khronos Group, browser developers, and the web graphics community for an interactive look at the latest advances in WebGL and WebGPU. Explore new capabilities for real time rendering, compute, AI, tooling, and cross platform deployment, along with the evolving ecosystem across browsers, frameworks, game engines, and immersive web experiences. Hear from developers building the future of 3D on the web, ask questions, share feedback, and help shape what comes next for web graphics.

Attendees taking this course should be able to write their own GPU ray tracers, even without having to become experts in PTX/IR embedding or Shader Binding Tables.

It introduces key Vulkan concepts and provides comprehensive guidance for writing and modernizing Vulkan applications in 2026, focusing on resource, pipeline, draw, and synchronization management.

speakers_or_contributors: Ling-Hao Chen; Zixin Yin; Duomin Wang; Xianfang Zeng; Gang Yu
